Genesis1:1 When God created the heavens and the earth," 
Genesis1:2 the earth was completely formless and empty, with nothing but darkness draped over the deep. God’s Spirit hovered over the face of the waters." 
Genesis1:3 And then God announced, “Let there be light,” and light burst forth!" 
Genesis1:4 And God saw the light as pleasing and beautiful; he used the light to dispel the darkness. 
Genesis1:5 God called the light “Day,” and the darkness “Night.” And so, evening gave way to morning—the first day." 
Genesis1:6 And God said, “Let there be a dome between the waters to separate the water above from the water below.”" 
Genesis1:7 He made the dome and called it “Sky,” and separated the water above the dome from the water below the dome. Evening gave way to morning—day two." 
Genesis1:8  
Genesis1:9 And God said, “Let the water beneath the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry ground appear.” And so it happened." 
Genesis1:10 God called the dry ground “Land,” and the gathered waters he called “Seas.” And God saw the beauty of his creation, and he was very pleased." 
Genesis1:11 Then God said, “Let the land burst forth with growth: plants that bear seeds of their own kind, and every variety of fruit tree, each with power to multiply from its own seed.” And so it happened." 
Genesis1:12 The land flourished with grasses, every variety of seed-bearing plant, and trees bearing fruit with their seeds in them. And God loved what he saw, for it was beautiful." 
Genesis1:13 Evening gave way to morning—day three. 
Genesis1:14 And God said, “Let there be bright lights to shine in space to bathe the earth with their light. Let them serve as signs to separate the day from night, and signify the days, seasons, and years.” And so it happened." 
Genesis1:15  
Genesis1:16 God made two great lights: the brighter light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night. He also spread the tapestry of shining stars 
Genesis1:17 and set them all in the sky to illuminate the earth," 
Genesis1:18 to rule over the day and to rule over the night, and to separate the light from darkness. God loved what he saw, for it was beautiful." 
Genesis1:19 Evening gave way to morning—day four. 
Genesis1:20 God said, “ Let there be life! Let the waters swarm with sea life, and let the sky be filled with soaring birds of every kind.”" 
Genesis1:21 God created huge sea creatures and every living creature that moves of every kind—swarming in the water and flying in the sky, according to their species. God loved what he saw, for it was beautiful." 
Genesis1:22 God blessed them, saying, “Reproduce and be fruitful! Fill the waters of the sea with life, and the earth with flying birds!”" 
Genesis1:23 Evening gave way to morning—day five. 
Genesis1:24 God said, “Let the earth produce every class and kind of living creature: livestock, crawling things, wild animals, each after its kind.” And so it happened." 
Genesis1:25 God made the wild animals according to their species, livestock according to their species, and all the creatures that creep along the ground according to their species. And God loved what he saw, for it was beautiful." 
Genesis1:26 Then God said, “Let us make a man and a woman in our image to be like us. Let them reign over the fish of the sea, the birds of the air, the livestock, over the creatures that creep along the ground, and over the wild animals.”" 
Genesis1:27 So God created man and woman and shaped them with his image inside them. In his own beautiful image, he created his masterpiece. Yes, male and female he created them." 
Genesis1:28 And God blessed them in his love, saying, “Reproduce and be fruitful! Populate the earth and subdue it! Reign over the fish of the sea, the birds of the air, and every creature that lives on earth.”" 
Genesis1:29 And God said, “I give you every seed-bearing plant growing throughout the earth, vegetables, and every fruit-bearing tree with its seed within itself. They will be your food." 
Genesis1:30 They will also be food for every animal and bird, and every creature that moves on the ground—every creature with the breath of life.” And so it happened." 
Genesis1:31 God surveyed all he had made and said, “I love it!” For it pleased him greatly. Evening gave way to morning—day six." 
Genesis2:1 And so the creation of the heavens and the earth were completed in all their vast array. 
Genesis2:2 By the seventh day, God had completed creating his masterpiece, so on the seventh day, he rested from all his work." 
Genesis2:3 So God blessed the seventh day and made it sacred, because on it, he paused to rest from all his work of creation." 
Genesis2:4 This is the account of the heavens and the earth after they were created. At the time Yahweh -God created earth and heaven," 
Genesis2:5 there was yet no vegetation, grains of the field, or shrubs sprouting on the earth, for there was no one to cultivate the land, and Yahweh -God had not yet sent rain." 
Genesis2:6 In those days a mist arose from the soil and watered the whole face of the ground. 
Genesis2:7 Yahweh -God scooped up a lump of soil, sculpted a man, and blew into his nostrils the breath of life. The man came alive—a living soul!" 
Genesis2:8 Then Yahweh -God planted a lush garden paradise in the East, in the Land of Delight, and there he placed the man he had formed." 
Genesis2:9 Yahweh -God made all kinds of beautiful trees to grow there—fruitful trees to satisfy the taste. In the middle of the garden, he planted the Tree of Life and the Tree of the Knowledge of Good and Evil." 
Genesis2:10 Flowing from the Land of Delight was a river to water the garden, and from there, it divided into four branches." 
Genesis2:11 The first river, Overflowing Increase, encircles the gold-laden land of Havilah." 
Genesis2:12 The gold of that land is pure, with many pearls and onyx found there." 
Genesis2:13 The second river, Gushing, flows through the entire land of Cush." 
Genesis2:14 The third river, Swift Flowing, flows east of Assyria. And the fourth is the river Fruitfulness." 
Genesis2:15 Yahweh -God took the man and placed him in the garden of Eden to work and watch over it. 
Genesis2:16 And Yahweh -God commanded him, “You may freely eat of every fruit of the garden." 
Genesis2:17 But you must not eat of the Tree that gives the knowledge of good and evil, for when you eat from it you will most certainly die.”" 
Genesis2:18 Then Yahweh -God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. Therefore, I will fashion a suitable partner to be his help and strength.”" 
Genesis2:19 For Yahweh -God had formed from the soil every animal of the field and every bird of the air and brought them before the man to see what he would call them. So, whatever the man called the living creature, that was its name." 
Genesis2:20 He gave names to all the various cattle, birds, and wild animals, but Adam could not find a fitting companion that corresponded to him." 
Genesis2:21 So Yahweh -God caused Adam to fall into a deep trance, and while he slept, he took a portion of Adam’s side and closed its place with flesh." 
Genesis2:22 Then Yahweh -God used the portion of Adam’s side and skillfully crafted a woman and presented her to him. 
Genesis2:23 Then Adam said: “At last! One like me! Her bones were formed from my bones, and her flesh from my flesh! This one will be called ‘Woman,’ for she was taken from man.”" 
Genesis2:24 For this reason, a man leaves his father and his mother to be unselfishly attached to his wife. They become one flesh as a new family!" 
Genesis2:25 The man and his wife felt no shame, unaware that they were both naked." 
Genesis3:1 Now the snake was the most cunning of all living beings that Yahweh -God had made. He deviously asked the woman, “Did God really tell you, ‘You must not eat fruit from any tree of the garden—?’ ”" 
Genesis3:2 But the woman interrupted, “—We may eat the fruit of any tree in the garden," 
Genesis3:3 except the tree in the center of the garden. God told us, ‘Don’t eat its fruit, or even touch it, or you’ll die.’ ”" 
Genesis3:4 But the snake said to her, “You certainly won’t die." 
Genesis3:5 God knows that the moment you eat it, your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing both good and evil.”" 
Genesis3:6 When the woman saw that the tree produced delicious fruit, delightful to look upon, and desirable to give one insight, she took its fruit and ate it. She gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he also ate it." 
Genesis3:7 Immediately, their eyes were opened, and they realized they were naked, vulnerable, and ashamed; so they sewed fig leaves together for coverings." 
Genesis3:8 Then Adam and his wife heard the sound of Yahweh -God passing through the garden in the breeze of the day. So, they hid among the trees concealing themselves from the face of Yahweh -God." 
Genesis3:9 Then Yahweh -God called Adam’s name and asked, “Where are you?”" 
Genesis3:10 Adam answered, “I heard your powerful presence moving in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; so I hid.”" 
Genesis3:11 “Who told you that you were naked?” God said. “Did you eat the fruit of the tree that I commanded you not to eat?” 
Genesis3:12 Adam pointed to the woman and said, “The woman you placed alongside me— she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate it.”" 
Genesis3:13 So Yahweh -God said to the woman, “What have you done?” The woman pointed to the snake and said, “The shining snake tricked me, and I ate.”" 
Genesis3:14 Yahweh -God then said to the snake, “Because you have done this, you are cursed above every wild animal, condemned above every creature of the field! You will slither on your belly and eat dust all the days of your life!" 
Genesis3:15 And I will place great hostility between you and the woman, and between her seed and yours. He will crush your head as you crush his heel.”" 
Genesis3:16 Then God said to the woman, “I will cause your labor pains in childbirth to be intensified; with pain you will give birth to children. You will desire to dominate your husband, but he will want to dominate you.”" 
Genesis3:17 And to Adam he said, “Because you obeyed your wife instead of me, and you ate from the forbidden tree when I had commanded you not to, the ground will be cursed because of you. You will eat of it through painful toil all the days of your life." 
Genesis3:18 It will sprout weeds and thorns, and you will eat the plants of the field." 
Genesis3:19 You will painfully toil and sweat to produce food to eat, until your body—taken from the ground—returns to the ground. For you are from dust, and to dust you will return.”" 
Genesis3:20 The man named his wife Eve— “Life-Giver,” because she would become the mother of every human being." 
Genesis3:21 Yahweh -God made garments from animal skins to clothe Adam and Eve. 
Genesis3:22 And Yahweh -God said, “The man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil. And now he might take in his hands fruit from the Tree of Life, and eat it, and live forever.”" 
Genesis3:23 Therefore, Yahweh -God expelled him from Eden’s paradise to till the ground from which he was taken." 
Genesis3:24 He drove them out of the garden, and placed fearsome angelic sentries east of the garden of Eden, with a turning fiery sword to guard the way to the Tree of Life." 
Genesis4:1 Now Adam had slept with his wife, and she conceived and bore a son named Cain. She said, “By the grace of Yahweh I have birthed a man!”" 
Genesis4:2 Then later, she gave birth to Cain’s brother Abel. Abel grew up to be a shepherd, and Cain became a farmer, working the ground." 
Genesis4:3 After some time passed, Cain presented an offering to Yahweh from the produce of the land." 
Genesis4:4 Abel also brought his offering, from among the finest of the firstborn of his flocks. Yahweh was very pleased with Abel and accepted his offering," 
Genesis4:5 but with Cain and his offering, Yahweh was not pleased, making Cain very furious and resentful." 
Genesis4:6 So Yahweh said to Cain, “Why are you so angry and bothered?" 
Genesis4:7 If you offer what is right, won’t you be accepted? But if you refuse to offer what is right, sin, the predator, is crouching in wait outside the door of your heart. It desires to have you, yet you must be its master.”" 
Genesis4:8 One day Cain said to his brother, “Let’s go out into the field.” When they arrived at the field, Cain rose and attacked and killed his brother Abel." 
Genesis4:9 Then Yahweh said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?” He answered, “How do I know? Am I my brother’s keeper?”" 
Genesis4:10 Yahweh said, “Listen—the voice of your brother’s blood is crying out to me from the ground! What have you done?" 
Genesis4:11 Now you are banished from the land, from the very ground that drank your brother’s blood from your hand!" 
Genesis4:12 When you try to cultivate the ground it will no longer produce crops for you; and you will be a fugitive, a homeless wanderer!”" 
Genesis4:13 Cain said to Yahweh, “My punishment is more than I can bear!" 
Genesis4:14 Look—you’ve thrown me off the land today, and now I must hide from your presence. As a fugitive and wanderer on the earth, anyone who meets me may choose to kill me!”" 
Genesis4:15 Yahweh responded, “Not so! If anyone kills you, I promise the seven-fold vengeance of Cain will be released upon him!” So, Yahweh put an identifying sign on Cain as a warning so that no one would dare kill him." 
Genesis4:16 Then Cain left the presence of Yahweh and journeyed to the Land of Wandering, east of Eden." 
Genesis4:17 Cain slept with his wife and she conceived and bore Enoch. Cain was building a city at the time, so he named it the Village of Enoch, after his son." 
Genesis4:18 Enoch’s son was Irad, and Irad’s son was Mehujael, and Mehujael’s son was Methushael, and Methushael’s son was Lamech." 
Genesis4:19 Lamech married two women, Dawn and Dusk." 
Genesis4:20 Dawn gave birth to Jabal, the first of those who lived in tents and raised livestock." 
Genesis4:21 Jabal’s brother was Jubal, the first of musicians who played instruments." 
Genesis4:22 Dusk gave birth to Tubalcain, the first of blacksmiths who forged all kinds of bronze and iron tools. His sister was Naamah." 
Genesis4:23 Lamech boasted to his wives: “Listen to me, Dawn and Dusk! Mark my words, O wives of Lamech! I have killed a man for wounding me, and a young man for bruising me." 
Genesis4:24 If killing Cain costs seven lives, for Lamech, it will cost seventy-seven!”" 
Genesis4:25 Adam slept with his wife again and she bore a son whom she named Seth, meaning “appointed,” for she declared, “God has appointed for me another son to replace Abel, because Cain killed him.”" 
Genesis4:26 After many years Seth had a son named Enosh. During his lifetime, people began to worship Yahweh and pray to him." 
Genesis5:1 When God created human beings he made them in the likeness of God and created them as male and female. After he had created them, he lovingly blessed them and named them “humanity.” So here is the family history of Adam and Eve:" 
Genesis5:2  
Genesis5:3 When Adam was one hundred and thirty, he fathered a child in his own likeness and in his own image, and he named him Seth." 
Genesis5:4 Adam lived an additional eight hundred years after Seth was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:5 The life span of Adam was nine hundred and thirty years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:6 When Seth was one hundred and five, he fathered Enosh." 
Genesis5:7 Seth lived an additional eight hundred and seven years after Enosh was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:8 The life span of Seth was nine hundred and twelve years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:9 When Enosh was ninety, he fathered Kenan." 
Genesis5:10 Enosh lived an additional eight hundred and fifteen years after Kenan was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:11 The life span of Enosh was nine hundred and five years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:12 When Kenan was seventy, he fathered Mahalalel." 
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived an additional eight hundred and forty years after Mahalalel was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:14 The life span of Kenan was nine hundred and ten years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel was sixty-five, he fathered Jared." 
Genesis5:16 Mahalalel lived an additional eight hundred and thirty years after Jared was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:17 The life span of Mahalalel was eight hundred and ninety-five years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:18 When Jared was one hundred and sixty-two, he fathered Enoch." 
Genesis5:19 Jared lived an additional eight hundred years after Enoch was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:20 The life span of Jared was nine hundred and sixty-two years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:21 When Enoch was sixty-five, he fathered Methuselah." 
Genesis5:22 Enoch walked with God for three hundred years after Methuselah was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:23 Enoch and God walked together as intimate friends; then God took him to himself, and he was seen no more. The life span of Enoch was three hundred and sixty-five years." 
Genesis5:24  
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah was one hundred and eighty-seven, he fathered Lamech." 
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived an additional seven hundred and eighty-two years after Lamech was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:27 The life span of Methuselah was nine hundred and sixty-nine years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:28 When Lamech was one hundred and eighty-two, he fathered a son" 
Genesis5:29 and named him Noah, saying, “He will relieve us from our hard work and painful toil, and from the ground that Yahweh cursed.”" 
Genesis5:30 Lamech lived an additional five hundred and ninety-five years after Noah was born and had many other sons and daughters. 
Genesis5:31 The life span of Lamech was seven hundred and seventy-seven years, and then he died." 
Genesis5:32 After Noah had lived five hundred years, he fathered Shem, Ham, and Japheth." 
Genesis6:1 When people began to populate the earth, they had many lovely daughters." 
Genesis6:2 Divine beings found them very appealing, so they took the women they wanted as their wives." 
Genesis6:3 Yahweh said, “My Spirit will not strive with humanity indefinitely, for they are mortal. Their life span will be shortened to only one hundred and twenty years.”" 
Genesis6:4 Back then, and later, there were giants on the earth, who were born as a result of the unholy union of heavenly beings with the human daughters. They were the mighty ones of old, warriors of renown." 
Genesis6:5 But Yahweh saw how extremely wicked humanity had become, for they imagined only evil, for all they thought about was doing evil all the time." 
Genesis6:6 And Yahweh was saddened that he had made humanity, and his heart was filled with pain." 
Genesis6:7 So Yahweh decided, “I will do away with my ruined creation —human beings that I created—people and animals, creeping things and flying birds, for it breaks my heart that I made them!”" 
Genesis6:8 But one man discovered grace before the face of Yahweh: Noah. 
Genesis6:9 This is the story of Noah. Noah was a godly man of integrity, without fault in his generation, and he lived close to God." 
Genesis6:10 Noah had three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth." 
Genesis6:11 Injustice and violence filled the world; the earth was ruined in the sight of God," 
Genesis6:12 for he saw how debased the world had become, for everyone was corrupt to the core." 
Genesis6:13 So God said to Noah, “I have decided that all living creatures must die, for their violence has filled the earth, and I will wipe them off the face of the earth!" 
Genesis6:14 Build a boat for yourself—an ark of sturdy timber. And this is how you are to design it: make separate compartments within it, and seal it inside and out with thick tar. The length is to be 450 feet, its width 75 feet, and its height 45 feet." 
Genesis6:15  
Genesis6:16 Cover it with a roof, but leave a gap of eighteen inches at the top for ventilation. Construct a door on the side of the ark, and design it with lower, middle, and upper decks." 
Genesis6:17 I am going to release a great flood that will destroy all life upon the earth, and every breathing thing under heaven will perish—everything on earth will die." 
Genesis6:18 But as for you, I will establish my covenant of friendship, and you will escape destruction by coming into the ark, you and your wife, your sons and their wives." 
Genesis6:19 Bring with you into the ark a pair of every animal, male and female; and they will also be spared." 
Genesis6:20 Different kinds of birds, animals large and small —two of every kind I will send to you to be kept alive." 
Genesis6:21 Also take with you every kind of food, both for you and them.”" 
Genesis6:22 So Noah completed all these preparations and did everything exactly as God had commanded him. 
Genesis7:1 The day came when Yahweh said to Noah, “Come into the ark, you and your entire household, for I have found you to be the only one righteous in my eyes in this generation." 
Genesis7:2 Take with you seven pairs of all the clean animals, both male and female, and one pair of all the unclean animals, both male and female." 
Genesis7:3 Take seven pairs of flying birds of all kinds, male and female, to make sure they survive on the earth." 
Genesis7:4 For in seven days I will send rain and flood the earth for forty days and forty nights. Every living thing that I made I will wipe off the face of the earth.” 
Genesis7:5 Noah obeyed all that Yahweh had commanded him. 
Genesis7:6 Noah was six hundred years old when the great flood came on the earth. 
Genesis7:7 Noah and his wife and his sons and their wives boarded the ark to escape the flood. 
Genesis7:8 Two of each animal, the male and female, clean and unclean, large and small, along with birds and crawling things, entered the ark with Noah as God had commanded." 
Genesis7:9  
Genesis7:10 Then seven days later, massive floodwaters covered the earth." 
Genesis7:11 It started in the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, in the second month on the seventeenth day. On that day, all the fountains of the subterranean deep cracked open and burst up through the ground. Heaven’s floodgates were opened," 
Genesis7:12 and heavy rains fell on the earth for forty days and forty nights. 
Genesis7:13 On that very day, Noah and his wife, their sons, Ham, Shem, and Japheth, and their wives, entered safely into the ark." 
Genesis7:14 Pairs of every species of animal entered with them—wild animals, domesticated animals, large and small, and every species of bird and winged creatures—every animal that has the breath of life came into the ark with Noah." 
Genesis7:15  
Genesis7:16 Both male and female went inside as God had commanded Noah; and Yahweh himself shut them in. 
Genesis7:17 For forty days, the flood engulfed the earth. The swelling floodwaters lifted up the ark until it rose high above the ground." 
Genesis7:18 The raging flood completely inundated the earth, but the ark floated safely, drifting upon the surface of the water," 
Genesis7:19 until the highest mountains were completely submerged beneath the rising waters. 
Genesis7:20 The waters rose over twenty-two feet above the highest mountains. 
Genesis7:21 Every living thing on the earth perished—domesticated animals, wild animals, birds, everything that moved on the earth, and all mankind perished." 
Genesis7:22 Everything perished—every animal on earth with the breath of life in its nostrils died. 
Genesis7:23 All life on earth was blotted out—all that he had made, animals large and small, wild and domesticated, birds and reptiles, including humanity, was wiped off the face of the earth. Only Noah was left behind, and those who were in the ark with him." 
Genesis7:24 And the waters covered the earth for 150 days. 
Genesis8:1 God’s heart was moved with compassion as he remembered Noah and all the animals, large and small, that were with him in the ark. And God caused a wind to sweep across the earth again and the waters subsided." 
Genesis8:2 He closed the subterranean fountains and the floodgates of heaven and held back the rain. 
Genesis8:3 After 150 days, the floodwaters gradually receded from the earth and the waters began to subside." 
Genesis8:4 And on the seventeenth day of the seventh month, the ark came to rest on the highest peak in Ararat." 
Genesis8:5 The waters continued to recede until the tenth month. And on the first day of the tenth month, all the mountaintops appeared." 
Genesis8:6 After forty more days Noah opened the window he had made in the ark 
Genesis8:7 and released a raven. It flew back and forth from the ark until the earth was dry. 
Genesis8:8 Then he sent out a dove to see if the waters had receded from the surface of the ground. 
Genesis8:9 But the dove found no place to rest, so it returned to Noah in the ark because the waters still covered the face of the earth. Noah put out his hand and grasped the dove and put it back into the ark." 
Genesis8:10 He waited another seven days and released the dove from the ark again. 
Genesis8:11 Before evening, the dove came back to him—and there in its beak was a freshly plucked olive leaf! So Noah realized that the waters had finally subsided from the earth." 
Genesis8:12 Then he waited another seven days and sent the dove out again, but this time it did not return to him in the ark." 
Genesis8:13 In Noah’s six hundred and first year, on the first day of the first month, the waters were dried up from the earth. Noah lifted the hatch, looked out, and saw the dry ground." 
Genesis8:14 On the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was dry." 
Genesis8:15 Then God said to Noah," 
Genesis8:16 “Come out of the ark, you and your wife, your sons, and their wives." 
Genesis8:17 Release all the animals with you and set them free—birds, animals large and small—every living thing. And they will multiply and abound and flourish on the earth and in the sky.”" 
Genesis8:18 So Noah and his family left the ark; 
Genesis8:19 and every animal large and small, every bird and crawling thing came out of the ark by families." 
Genesis8:20 Noah erected an altar dedicated to Yahweh. Then he selected ritually clean animals and birds of every species and offered them as burnt sacrifices on the altar. 
Genesis8:21 And when Yahweh smelled the sweet fragrance of Noah’s offerings, his heart was stirred, and he said, “Never again will I curse the earth because of people, even though the imagination of their hearts are evil from their childhood; nor will I ever again destroy every living creature as I have done. I promise this:" 
Genesis8:22 “As long as earth exists there will always be seasons of planting and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night.”" 
Genesis9:1 God lovingly blessed Noah and all his family and said to them, “Reproduce, be fruitful, and populate the earth." 
Genesis9:2 I will cause every living creature of earth, sky, and sea to fear and dread you from this time forward. They are now under your authority." 
Genesis9:3 You may now eat the meat of animals as well as green plants; I now give you everything for food for you. 
Genesis9:4 However, you must not eat meat with its lifeblood still in it, for its life is in the blood." 
Genesis9:5 If anyone takes another person’s life, I will demand an account—whether from man or beast, I will demand an account for taking a human life." 
Genesis9:6 “Whoever sheds human blood, by other humans he must have his own blood shed; for to kill a person is to kill one made in God’s own beautiful image." 
Genesis9:7 Now all of you, with my blessing, reproduce, be fruitful and repopulate the earth.”" 
Genesis9:8 Then God said to Noah and his family," 
Genesis9:9 “I establish my loving covenant with you, your descendants," 
Genesis9:10 and every living creature that is with you—animals large and small, birds, and every living thing that came out of the ark." 
Genesis9:11 I will maintain my loving covenant with you. I will never again completely destroy life on earth by means of a flood. Yes, never again will a flood destroy the whole earth!”" 
Genesis9:12 “Here is the sign for you and future generations that my loving covenant will endure between me and you and every animal that came with you out of the ark: 
Genesis9:13 I have placed my rainbow among the clouds, and it will be a sign of my loving covenant between me and the earth." 
Genesis9:14 Whenever I bring clouds over the earth, and the rainbow appears in the clouds," 
Genesis9:15 I will remember my covenant with you and with every living thing upon the earth. Never again will the waters become a flood to destroy life from the earth. 
Genesis9:16 When the rainbow is in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant I made with you and every living thing of every kind upon the earth.”" 
Genesis9:17 So God said to Noah, “The rainbow is my signature in the sky, my seal of love to confirm that I have kept my covenant between me and every living thing on earth.”" 
Genesis9:18 Noah’s sons came out of the ark with him: Shem, Ham (the father of Canaan), and Japheth." 
Genesis9:19 From these three sons of Noah the entire world was repopulated. 
Genesis9:20 Noah, a farmer, was the first to plant a vineyard." 
Genesis9:21 He drank so much of the wine he made that he got drunk and passed out naked inside his tent. 
Genesis9:22 And Ham, the father of Canaan, went into the tent and gazed on his shamefully exposed father. Then he went out and informed his brothers." 
Genesis9:23 So, Shem and Japheth took their father’s cloak and walked backwards with the cloak on their shoulders into the tent to cover up their naked father. Respectfully, they turned their faces away so as not to see their father lying there exposed." 
Genesis9:24 When Noah sobered up and realized what his youngest son had done to him," 
Genesis9:25 he uttered these words: “Cursed be your son, Canaan, and let him be the lowest of servants to his brothers.”" 
Genesis9:26 He also said, “Worthy of praise is Yahweh, the God of Shem! Let Canaan be Shem’s slave." 
Genesis9:27 May God enlarge Japheth’s family and increase his territory. May he share in the blessing of Shem, and his descendants make their homes among the tents of Shem, and may Canaan be his slave also!”" 
Genesis9:28 After the flood, Noah lived another three hundred and fifty years." 
Genesis9:29 Noah’s entire lifetime was nine hundred and fifty years, and then he died." 
Genesis10:1 This is the story of the descendants of Noah’s three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth. After the great flood, they fathered many children." 
Genesis10:2 The sons of Japheth were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras." 
Genesis10:3 The sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah." 
Genesis10:4 The sons of Javan were Elishah, Tarshish, Cyprus, and Rhodes." 
Genesis10:5 All of Japheth’s descendants lived in their respective tribes and regions, each group speaking its own language. From there, the people spread to distant shores and faraway lands." 
Genesis10:6 The sons of Ham were Ethiopia, Egypt, Libya, and Canaan." 
Genesis10:7 The sons of Cush were Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah were Sheba and Dedan." 
Genesis10:8 Cush also had a son named Nimrod, who became known as the first mighty conqueror." 
Genesis10:9 He was a mighty despot before Yahweh. There was a saying, “Like Nimrod, a mighty despot before Yahweh.”" 
Genesis10:10 The centers of his kingdom, Babel, Erech, and Akkad, were in the land of Shinar." 
Genesis10:11 From there, he went into Assyria and built the cities of Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah," 
Genesis10:12 and Resen (which is between the great city of Calah and Nineveh). 
Genesis10:13 Egypt was the father of the Ludites, Anamites, Lehabites, Naphtuhites," 
Genesis10:14 Pathrusites, Casluhites, and Caphtorites (the ancestors of the Philistines)." 
Genesis10:15 Canaan was the father of Sidon (his firstborn), the Hittites," 
Genesis10:16 the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites," 
Genesis10:17 the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites," 
Genesis10:18 the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. The families of the Canaanites eventually scattered across" 
Genesis10:19 the Canaanite territory extending from Sidon toward Gerar to Gaza including Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha." 
Genesis10:20 These were the descendants of Ham by families, lands, languages, and nations." 
Genesis10:21 Shem, the older brother of Japheth, also had sons and was the ancestor of all the sons of Eber, who were also known as “Hebrews.”" 
Genesis10:22 The sons of Shem were Elam, Assyria, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram." 
Genesis10:23 The sons of Aram were Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash." 
Genesis10:24 Arphaxad was the father of Shelah, the father of Eber." 
Genesis10:25 Eber was the father of two sons: Peleg, (or “Division,” for during his lifetime the earth was divided), and his brother Joktan, the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah," 
Genesis10:26  
Genesis10:27 Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah," 
Genesis10:28 Obal, Abimael, Sheba," 
Genesis10:29 Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All the descendants of Joktan" 
Genesis10:29 Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All the descendants of Joktan" 
Genesis10:30 lived in the territory that extends all the way from Mesha toward the eastern hills of Sephar. 
Genesis10:31 These were the families of Shem according to their genealogies, by their families, lands, languages, and nations." 
Genesis10:32 After the flood, Noah’s descendants formed nations as the people spread throughout the earth." 
Genesis11:1 Now everyone at that time spoke a single language with one vocabulary. 
Genesis11:2 As people migrated eastward, they found a large plain in the land of Shinar and settled there." 
Genesis11:3 They said to one another, “Come, let’s unite together and make bricks of clay and burn them until they become hard.” So, they piled up the bricks they made to serve as stones and collected tar for mortar." 
Genesis11:4 Then they said, “Come, let’s begin work to build ourselves a city with a lofty tower that rises into the heavens. We’ll make a name for ourselves, a monument to us, instead of being scattered all over the earth.”" 
Genesis11:5 But when Yahweh came down to see the city and the tower which mortals had started building," 
Genesis11:6 he said, “If they have begun this as one people sharing a common language, then nothing they plan to do will be impossible for them." 
Genesis11:7 Come, let us go down and confuse their language and prevent them from understanding each other.”" 
Genesis11:8 So Yahweh scattered them over the entire earth, and they stopped building their city." 
Genesis11:9 That is why the city was called Babel—because it was there that Yahweh confused the language of the whole world and from there the people were scattered over the face of the earth. 
Genesis11:10 These are the descendants of Shem. Two years after the flood, at the age of one hundred, Shem had a son named Arphaxad." 
Genesis11:11 And after Arphaxad was born, Shem lived another five hundred years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:12 When Arphaxad was thirty-five, he had a son named Shelah." 
Genesis11:13 And after Shelah was born Arphaxad lived another four hundred and three years and had other sons and daughters. 
Genesis11:14 When Shelah was thirty, he had a son named Eber." 
Genesis11:15 And after Eber was born, Shelah lived another four hundred and three years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:16 When Eber was thirty-four, he had a son named Peleg." 
Genesis11:17 And after Peleg was born, Eber lived another four hundred and thirty years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:18 When Peleg was thirty, he had a son named Reu." 
Genesis11:19 And after Reu was born, Peleg lived another two hundred and nine years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:20 When Reu was thirty-two, he had a son named Serug." 
Genesis11:21 And after Serug was born, Reu lived another two hundred and seven years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:22 When Serug was thirty, he had a son named Nahor." 
Genesis11:23 And after Nahor was born, Serug lived another two hundred years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:24 When Nahor was twenty-nine, he had a son named Terah." 
Genesis11:25 And after Terah was born, Nahor lived another one hundred and nineteen years and had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:26 When Terah was seventy, he had sons named Abram, Nahor, and Haran." 
Genesis11:27 Here are the descendants of Terah. Terah was the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran, and Haran was the father of Lot." 
Genesis11:28 Haran preceded his father, Terah, in death in the land of his birth, in the Chaldean city of Ur." 
Genesis11:29 The brothers, Abram and Nahor, were both married. Abram’s wife was Sarai. Nahor married the daughter of his deceased brother Haran; her name was Milcah, and her sister was Iscah." 
Genesis11:30 Now Sarai was barren and childless. 
Genesis11:31 Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot, the son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram’s wife, and they all departed together from the Chaldean city of Ur to go into the land of Canaan. But when they journeyed as far as Haran, they settled there." 
Genesis11:32 Terah lived two hundred and five years and died in Haran. 
Genesis12:1 Now Yahweh said to Abram, “Leave it all behind — your native land, your people, your father’s household, and go to the land that I will show you." 
Genesis12:2 Follow me, and I will make you into a great nation. I will exceedingly bless and prosper you, and I will make you famous, so that you will be a tremendous source of blessing for others." 
Genesis12:3 I will bless all who bless you and curse all who curse you. And through you all the families of the earth will be blessed.” 
Genesis12:4 So Abram obeyed Yahweh and left; and Lot went with him. Now Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran after his father died. 
Genesis12:5 He took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, and all the possessions and people he had acquired in Haran; and they departed for the land of Canaan. When they arrived in the land of Canaan," 
Genesis12:6 Abram passed through the land and stopped at the sacred site of Shechem, famous for the great oak tree of Moreh. At that time, the Canaanites were also in the land." 
Genesis12:7 Then Yahweh appeared before Abram and said, “This is the land I will personally deliver to your seed.” So Abram erected an altar there to Yahweh, who had appeared before him." 
Genesis12:8 From there, he journeyed on toward the hill country east of Bethel and pitched his tent with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. And there he built another altar to Yahweh where he prayed and worshiped Yahweh." 
Genesis12:9 Then Abram journeyed from there by stages through the southern desert region. 
Genesis12:10 At that time, a severe famine struck the land of Canaan, forcing Abram to travel down to Egypt and live there as a foreigner." 
Genesis12:11 When he drew near to Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, “Look, I’m worried because I know that you are a beautiful, gorgeous woman." 
Genesis12:12 When the Egyptian men take one look at you, they will say, ‘She is his wife.’ Then they will kill me in order to have you." 
Genesis12:13 Just tell them you are my sister so that they will treat me well for your sake and spare my life.” 
Genesis12:14 When Abram entered Egypt, everyone noticed Sarai’s stunning beauty." 
Genesis12:15 When Pharaoh’s dignitaries spotted her, they went to Pharaoh and raved about her beauty. Then they took Sarai into the palace and made her part of Pharaoh’s harem." 
Genesis12:16 Because she pleased Pharaoh, Abram got along very well in Egypt and received royal treatment: he was given sheep, cattle, male and female donkeys, camels, and male and female slaves." 
Genesis12:17 But Yahweh struck Pharaoh and his household with terrible diseases because he had taken Abram’s wife, Sarai." 
Genesis12:18 So Pharaoh sent for Abram and said, “How could you do this to me? Why didn’t you tell me she was your wife?" 
Genesis12:19 And why did you lie to me by saying, ‘She’s my sister,’ so that I took her as my wife? Now, here’s your wife back; take her and begone!”" 
Genesis12:20 Then Pharaoh gave strict orders to his men to escort them out of Egypt along with everything they had. 
Genesis13:1 Abram took his wife and all that he owned, and left Egypt. They returned to the southern desert region, and Lot went with them." 
Genesis13:2 Now Abram had become very rich in livestock, silver, and gold." 
Genesis13:3 And he journeyed on from place to place as far as the hill country region of Bethel. He returned to the place between Bethel and Ai where he had pitched his tent at the beginning. 
Genesis13:4 This was the place he had first built an altar to pray and worship Yahweh. 
Genesis13:5 Now Lot, who accompanied Abram, was also rich and had accumulated flocks, herds, family, and servants." 
Genesis13:6 Arguments erupted between Abram’s herdsmen and Lot’s herdsmen because the land could not support both living together, for their animals and possessions were too numerous. At that time, Canaanites and Perizzites were also in the land." 
Genesis13:7  
Genesis13:8 So Abram said to Lot, “Let’s not quarrel with each other, or between our herdsmen, since we are relatives." 
Genesis13:9 Look at the vast land that is in front of you! Let’s settle in different regions. If you choose the land on the left, then I’ll go right, and if you want land on the right, then I’ll go left.”" 
Genesis13:10 Lot lifted his eyes and carefully surveyed the land around him all the way to Zoar. He noticed that the Jordan Valley was fertile and well-watered (this was before Yahweh had destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah). It looked beautiful, like the garden of Yahweh, or like Egypt." 
Genesis13:11 So Lot chose to settle in the Jordan Valley. He departed toward the east, and the family split up and separated from each other." 
Genesis13:12 Abram settled in the land of Canaan while Lot settled in the cities of the lowlands, at a place near Sodom." 
Genesis13:13 Now, the people of Sodom were extremely wicked and rebelled against Yahweh." 
Genesis13:14 After Lot separated from him, Yahweh spoke to Abram, “Lift up your eyes and look around you to the north, the south, the east, and the west." 
Genesis13:15 As far as you can see in every direction is the land that I will give to you forever—to you and your seed. 
Genesis13:16 I will multiply them until they are as numerous as the specks of dust on the earth. If anyone could count the dust of the earth, then your offspring could also be counted." 
Genesis13:17 Now, get up and walk through the land—its length and its breadth. All the land you walk upon will be my gift to you!”" 
Genesis13:18 Abram moved his camp and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron, and there he built another altar to Yahweh." 
Genesis14:1 During the reign of Amraphel king of Babylon, he allied with three other kings: Arioch king of Ellasar, Kedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goyim." 
Genesis14:2 These four kings went to war against five kings: Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, which is Zoar." 
Genesis14:3 Kedorlaomer had oppressed these five kings for twelve years until they rebelled against him in the thirteenth year. They all became regional allies in the valley of Siddim, which is now the Dead Sea." 
Genesis14:4  
Genesis14:5 In the fourteenth year, Kedorlaomer went to war with his allied kings. They defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, the Emites in Shavah-kiriathaim," 
Genesis14:6 and the Horites in the hill country of Seir, as far as El Paran near the desert." 
Genesis14:7 Then they turned back and attacked En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh) and subdued all the country of the Amalekites and the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar." 
Genesis14:8 Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar), marched out in battle formation in the valley of Siddim." 
Genesis14:9 There they faced off against Kedorlaomer, king of Elam; Tidal, king of Goyim; Amraphel, king of Babylon; and Arioch, king of Ellasar—four kings against five." 
Genesis14:10 Now the valley of Siddim was full of tar pits, and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah and their armies retreated from the battle and fled, some of them fell into the tar pits, and the rest fled to the hill country." 
Genesis14:11 The four victorious kings captured all the possessions and all the food of Sodom and Gomorrah and left. 
Genesis14:12 They captured Lot, Abram’s nephew who had been living in Sodom, and took him and all his possessions." 
Genesis14:13 One who escaped came to Abram the Hebrew and told him what had happened to Lot. Abram was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite. Mamre had two brothers, Eshcol and Aner, who were allied by treaty with Abram." 
Genesis14:14 When Abram heard that his nephew Lot had been taken captive by the four kings, he mobilized all the men in his camp, 318 in all who had been born and trained in his own household, and he pursued the invaders as far north as Dan." 
Genesis14:15 Then, during the night, Abram strategically divided his forces and defeated them. His forces attacked them and routed them as far as Hobah, north of Damascus." 
Genesis14:16 He recovered all the stolen possessions and brought back his nephew Lot, together with the women and all the prisoners." 
Genesis14:17 After Abram returned from defeating Kedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet Abram at the valley of the Shaveh (known as the King’s Valley)." 
Genesis14:18 And Melchizedek, who was both a priest of the Most High God and the king of Salem, brought out to Abram bread and wine." 
Genesis14:19 He spoke over him a special blessing, saying, “Blessed is Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth." 
Genesis14:20 And blessed be God Most High, whose power delivered your enemies into your hands!” Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of all he possessed." 
Genesis14:21 Then the king of Sodom said to Abram, “Just give me the people you rescued; keep all the spoils for yourself.”" 
Genesis14:22 But Abram said to the king of Sodom, “I raised my hand to Yahweh, God Most High, and I pledged a solemn oath to the Possessor of heaven and earth" 
Genesis14:23 that I would keep nothing for myself that belongs to you, not even a thread of a garment or sandal strap. That way, you will never be able to say, ‘I was the one that made Abram rich.’" 
Genesis14:24 I will take nothing except what my young men have eaten, and the share of those who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre. Let them take their share.”" 
Genesis15:1 Afterwards, the word of Yahweh came to Abram in a vision and said, “Abram, don’t yield to fear, for I am your Faithful Shield and your Abundant Reward.”" 
Genesis15:2 But Abram replied, “Lord Yahweh, what good is your reward if I remain childless? I’m about to die without a son, and my servant, Eliezer of Damascus, will inherit all my wealth." 
Genesis15:3 A servant in my household will end up with everything because you have not given me any children.” 
Genesis15:4 Immediately, the word of Yahweh came to him: “ No! Eliezer will not be your heir. I will give you a son from your own body to be your heir.”" 
Genesis15:5 Then Yahweh brought him outside his tent and said, “Gaze into the night sky. Go ahead and try to count the stars.” He continued, “Your seed will be as numerous as the stars!”" 
Genesis15:6 And Abram trusted every word Yahweh had spoken! And because of his faith, Yahweh credited it to him as righteousness." 
Genesis15:7 Then he said to him, “I am Yahweh, who brought you out of the Babylonian city of Ur, to give you all this land to possess.”" 
Genesis15:8 But Abram said, “Lord Yahweh, how can I be sure that I can possess this land for myself?”" 
Genesis15:9 Yahweh said to him, “Bring me a heifer, a female goat, and a ram, each three years old, also a turtledove and a young pigeon.”" 
Genesis15:10 So, Abram brought the animals to him and killed them. He cut them in two (except the birds) and laid each half opposite the other in two rows." 
Genesis15:11 Vultures swooped down upon the carcasses, but Abram stood there and drove the vultures away." 
Genesis15:12 As the heavy veil of night fell, Abram went into a deep state of sleep, and suddenly a great dreadful darkness surrounded him and he was filled with fear." 
Genesis15:13 Then Yahweh said to Abram, “Know this: your descendants will live as strangers in a foreign country. They will be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years." 
Genesis15:14 Afterward I will punish that nation for enslaving them, and your descendants will come out of slavery with untold wealth." 
Genesis15:15 You, however, will go to your ancestors in peace and live a full life." 
Genesis15:16 And after the fourth generation, your descendants will return here; for then the sin of the Amorites will be ripe for judgment.”" 
Genesis15:17 When the sun had set, and it was very dark, there suddenly appeared a smoking firepot and a blazing torch that passed between the split carcasses." 
Genesis15:18 On that day, Yahweh entered into covenant with Abram: “I have given this land to your descendants, from the Egyptian border to the great river Euphrates," 
Genesis15:19 the entire land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites," 
Genesis15:20 the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites," 
Genesis15:21 the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.”" 
Genesis16:1 Now Sarai had borne no children for Abram. She had an Egyptian slave girl named Hagar," 
Genesis16:2 so Sarai said to Abram, “Please listen. Since Yahweh has kept me childless, go sleep with my maidservant. Perhaps through her I can build you a family.” Abram listened and did what Sarai asked." 
Genesis16:3 Abram had already lived ten years in the land of Canaan when his wife Sarai took her Egyptian slave girl Hagar and gave her to Abram to be his second wife. 
Genesis16:4 He slept with Hagar, and she conceived. Once Hagar realized she was pregnant, she belittled Sarai and despised her." 
Genesis16:5 So Sarai went to Abram and said, “ It’s totally your fault that Hagar despises me—and you’re not standing up for me! I gave my slave girl to your embrace, and when she found out she was pregnant, she despised me. May Yahweh judge between us who is in the right!”" 
Genesis16:6 Abram responded, “She’s your slave girl under your authority, so do with her whatever you think best.” Then Sarai cruelly mistreated Hagar, who then ran away from her." 
Genesis16:7 The angel of Yahweh encountered Hagar by a spring in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur." 
Genesis16:8 He asked her, “Hagar, servant of Sarai, where have you come from and what are you doing here?” She replied, “I’m running away from Sarai, my mistress.”" 
Genesis16:9 The angel of Yahweh told her, “You have to go back to your mistress and humbly submit to her.”" 
Genesis16:10 The angel added, “I will greatly multiply your descendants until no one can count them.”" 
Genesis16:11 Yahweh ’s angel continued, “You are now pregnant, and soon you will give birth to a son. You will name him Ishmael, for Yahweh has heard your cries of distress." 
Genesis16:12 Your son will have a wild nature that no one can tame. He will be hostile toward everyone, and everyone will be hostile toward him; and he will live at odds with all his kinsmen.”" 
Genesis16:13 After her encounter with Yahweh, Hagar called him by a special name, “You are the God of My Seeing,” for she said, “Oh my, did I just see God and live to talk about it?”" 
Genesis16:14 That is why the well is called Spring of the Living One Who Watches Over Me. The well is still there, between Kadesh and Bered." 
Genesis16:15 Hagar returned and bore Abram a son, whom Abram named Ishmael." 
Genesis16:16 Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar gave birth to Ishmael. 
Genesis17:1 When Abram was ninety-nine years old, Yahweh appeared to him again and said, “I am the God who is more than enough. Live your life in my presence and be blameless." 
Genesis17:2 I will confirm my covenant between me and you, and I will greatly multiply your descendants.”" 
Genesis17:3 Then Abram fell on his face in awe before God, and God said to him," 
Genesis17:4 “I establish my covenant with you: “You will become a father of many nations. 
Genesis17:5 You will no longer be named Abram because I am changing your name to Abraham, for I have made you a father of many nations." 
Genesis17:6 I will make you abundantly fruitful, more than you expect. I will make nations out of you, and kings will trace their lineage back to you." 
Genesis17:7 Yes, I will establish my eternal covenant of love between me and you, and it will extend to your descendants throughout their generations. I will be your children’s God, just as I am your God." 
Genesis17:8 I will give to you and your seed the land to which you have migrated. The entire land of Canaan will be yours and your descendants’ as an everlasting possession. And I will be their God forever!” 
Genesis17:9 God explained to Abraham, “Your part of the covenant is to obey its terms, you and your descendants throughout the ages." 
Genesis17:10 So here is the sign of the covenant that you are to keep, so that it will endure between you and me and your descendants: circumcise every male among you." 
Genesis17:11 You must undergo cutting off the flesh of your foreskin as a special sign of the covenant between me and you. 
Genesis17:12 Throughout your generations, each male child must be circumcised eight days after his birth. This includes those not of your descendants—those born in your household and foreign-born servants whom you have purchased;" 
Genesis17:13 they must be circumcised, homeborn and purchased alike. In this way, you will carry in your flesh the sign of my everlasting covenant." 
Genesis17:14 Any uncircumcised male who does not have the foreskin of his flesh cut off will be cut off from his people, for he has broken my covenant.”" 
Genesis17:15 God also said to Abraham: “Concerning your wife Sarai, you are not to call her Sarai anymore, but Sarah, ‘My Princess,’ will be her name." 
Genesis17:16 I will wonderfully bless her, and I will certainly give you a son through her. Yes, I will bless her greatly, and she will become a mother of nations; kings of nations will arise among her children!”" 
Genesis17:17 Then Abraham laughed so hard he fell to the ground, saying to himself, “How in the world can a hundred-year-old man become a father? How can my wife Sarah get pregnant at ninety?”" 
Genesis17:18 And then he spoke out loud: “O, that Ishmael might prosper with your blessing!”" 
Genesis17:19 God said, “Listen to me. I promise that you and Sarah will have a son, and you will call him Isaac. I will confirm my everlasting covenant of love with him and his seed." 
Genesis17:20 And regarding Ishmael, I have heard your cry and I will indeed bless him. I will cause him to have many, many descendants. He will father twelve rulers, and I will make him into a great nation." 
Genesis17:21 But my everlasting covenant relationship is with Isaac, who will be born to you and Sarah about this time next year.”" 
Genesis17:22 When he had finished speaking with Abraham, God ascended up to heaven." 
Genesis17:23 So without delay Abraham obeyed God and took his son Ishmael and every male in his household (whether born in his house or foreign-born servants) and circumcised them that very same day. 
Genesis17:24 Abraham was already ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised," 
Genesis17:25 and his son Ishmael was thirteen. 
Genesis17:26 They were both circumcised on the same day," 
Genesis17:27 along with all the men of his household (whether born in his house or foreign-born servants). 
Genesis18:1 Yahweh appeared once again to Abraham while he lived by the oak grove of Mamre. During the hottest part of the day, as Abraham sat at his tent door," 
Genesis18:2 he looked up and suddenly saw three men standing nearby. As soon as he saw them, he ran from his tent to welcome them. He bowed down to the ground" 
Genesis18:3 and said, “My Lord, if I have found favor in your sight, don’t pass me by. Stay for a while with your servant." 
Genesis18:4 I’ll have some water brought to you all so that you can wash your feet. Rest here a while under the tree. 
Genesis18:5 Since you’ve stopped by your servant’s home and honored me with your presence, I’ll have food prepared for you so that you can be refreshed; then you can go on your way.” “Very well,” they responded, “go ahead and do as you have said.”" 
Genesis18:6 Abraham hurried back into the tent and said to Sarah, “Quick, we have guests! Get three measures of fine flour, knead it, and bake some bread.”" 
Genesis18:7 Then Abraham ran to the herd, selected a tender choice calf, and told his servant, “Hurry—prepare this calf for my guests!”" 
Genesis18:8 Then he brought the meal they had prepared—roasted meat, bread, curds, and milk—and set it before his guests. Abraham stood by them under the tree while they ate." 
Genesis18:9 They asked him, “Where is your wife Sarah?” He answered, “Over there—in the tent.”" 
Genesis18:10 Then one spoke up and said, “I will return about this time next year, when your wife Sarah will certainly have a son.” Sarah overheard it, for she was at the tent door not far behind him." 
Genesis18:11 Now, both Abraham and Sarah were already very old, and Sarah was past the age of childbearing." 
Genesis18:12 Sarah laughed to herself with disbelief, saying, “A woman my age—have a baby? After I’m worn out will I now enjoy marital bliss and conceive —and with my aged husband?”" 
Genesis18:13 Yahweh knew her thoughts and asked Abraham, “Why is Sarah laughing, saying, ‘How can a woman my age have a baby?’" 
Genesis18:14 Do you think there is anything too marvelous for Yahweh? I will appear to you at the appointed time next year and Sarah will have a son!” 
Genesis18:15 Sarah was afraid, so she denied it, saying, “I wasn’t laughing.” But he said, “Yes, you were!”" 
Genesis18:16 Afterward, the three men departed and walked toward Sodom, and Abraham went off with them to see them on their way." 
Genesis18:17 As they walked, Yahweh said, “Should I really hide from Abraham what I intend to do?" 
Genesis18:18 After all, he will become a great and powerful nation, and every nation on earth will seek bliss like his." 
Genesis18:19 It is true; I have singled him out as my own, so that he will lead his family and household to follow my ways and live by what is right and just. I will fulfill all the promises that I have spoken to him.”" 
Genesis18:20 Yahweh explained to Abraham, “The outcry for justice against Sodom and Gomorrah is so great and their sin so blatant" 
Genesis18:21 that I must go down and see if their wicked actions are as great as the outrage that has come to me, and if not, then I will know.”" 
Genesis18:22 As Yahweh ’s two companions went on toward Sodom, Abraham remained there, as Yahweh paused before Abraham." 
Genesis18:23 So Abraham came forward to present his case before Yahweh, and said, “Are you really going to sweep away the righteous while you judge the wicked?" 
Genesis18:24 What if you find fifty righteous people in Sodom? Isn’t your mercy great enough to forgive? Why judge the entire city at the cost of the fifty righteous who live there? 
Genesis18:25 That’s not who you are—one who would slay the righteous with the wicked, treating them both the same way! Wouldn’t the Merciful Judge of all the earth always do what is right?”" 
Genesis18:26 And Yahweh said, “Alright. If I find fifty righteous in Sodom, I will spare the whole city for their sake.”" 
Genesis18:27 Abraham spoke up again and said, “I am just a man formed from earth’s dust and ashes but allow me to be so bold as to ask you, my Lord." 
Genesis18:28 What if there are only five lacking, and you only find forty-five righteous in Sodom? It’s not who you are to destroy the entire city for lack of five righteous people.” And Yahweh said, “Alright. If I find forty-five righteous in Sodom, I will spare the whole city.”" 
Genesis18:29 “But what if there are only forty?” Abraham asked further. Yahweh answered, “Alright. If I only find forty, I will spare the city.”" 
Genesis18:30 Abraham paused —then he said, “Lord, please don’t be offended with me, and let me speak . . . What if there are only thirty?” Yahweh answered, “Alright. If I find only thirty, I will spare the city.”" 
Genesis18:31 Abraham ventured even further, asking, “Allow me to dare speak this way to you, my Lord. But what if you find there only twenty righteous?” Yahweh answered, “Alright. For the sake of the twenty I will not destroy the city.”" 
Genesis18:32 Then Abraham took a deep breath and asked, “Once more, please don’t let my Lord be angry with me if I make but one more request. What if you find only ten righteous?” And Yahweh answered, “Alright. I will extend my mercy and not destroy the city for the sake of ten righteous.”" 
Genesis18:33 Yahweh finished speaking with Abraham. He immediately went on his way, and Abraham returned home." 
Genesis19:1 That evening, the two angels came to Sodom while Lot was sitting at the city’s gateway. When Lot saw them, he got up to meet them and bowed with his face to the ground." 
Genesis19:2 He said, “Please, my lords, come to your servant’s house to spend the night and wash your feet; then you can rise early and go on your way.” “No,” they answered. “We will be fine to spend the night in the town square.”" 
Genesis19:3 But Lot was so insistent they finally agreed to go to his house. Lot had unleavened bread baked for them and prepared a feast, and they ate." 
Genesis19:4 But before they retired for the night, the men of the city, men young and old, from every part of the city of Sodom, to the last man, surrounded the house." 
Genesis19:5 They shouted out to Lot, “Where are the men who came to your house tonight? Bring them out to us so that we can have sex with them!”" 
Genesis19:6 Lot went outside, shutting the door behind him," 
Genesis19:7 and said to them, “No! My brothers, I beg you, don’t sink to such depravity!" 
Genesis19:8 Look, I have two virgin daughters; I’ll bring them out to you, and you can do with them as you please. Only don’t do anything to these men, for they are guests in my house.”" 
Genesis19:9 “Get out of our way,” they replied. “This guy comes as a foreigner to live among us, and now he dares to judge us! We’ll inflict more harm on you than on them!” Just then they lunged at Lot and tried to break down the door," 
Genesis19:10 but the two angels reached out and pulled Lot safely back into the house and bolted the door. 
Genesis19:11 They struck the men outside the house, young and old, with one blazing flash of light so that they could not find the door!" 
Genesis19:12 Then the visitors said to Lot, “Who else lives here? Do you have any other family here—sons or daughters, sons-in-law or daughters-in-law who live in Sodom? Get them all out of the city," 
Genesis19:13 because we are about to destroy this place. A tremendous outcry against the people has come before Yahweh, and he has sent us here to destroy it!”" 
Genesis19:14 So Lot went out to find the two men who were pledged to marry his daughters and told them, “Hurry, leave the city, for Yahweh is about to destroy it!” But they thought he was only joking and paid him no attention." 
Genesis19:15 At dawn, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Go! Take your wife and your two daughters and leave or you will be consumed in the judgment about to come to the city!”" 
Genesis19:16 But Lot hesitated, so the angels grabbed his hands and the hands of his wife and daughters and brought them outside the city, because Yahweh was merciful to them." 
Genesis19:17 Once they were safely outside the city, the angels said to them, “Run for your lives! Don’t stop anywhere in the plain until you’ve reached the mountains. And don’t even look back, or you’ll die!”" 
Genesis19:18 Lot replied, “Oh no, my lords." 
Genesis19:19 You’ve been so gracious to your servant, and you’ve been so kind to save our lives, but I can’t make it to the mountains. It’s so far from here; disaster will overtake me, and I’ll die!" 
Genesis19:20 Look, over there is a village close enough to run to, and it’s a small one. Let me escape there instead. You can see that it’s such a small village. Let my life be spared!”" 
Genesis19:21 “All right,” he replied. “I will grant this request too. I will not destroy that village." 
Genesis19:22 Now, you must hurry. Run to that village, for I can’t do anything until you are there.” (That is why the village was called Zoar.)" 
Genesis19:23 By mid-morning, Lot arrived at the small village of Zoar," 
Genesis19:24 And Yahweh ’s fire from heaven fell upon Sodom and Gomorrah. 
Genesis19:25 He completely destroyed the cities of the plain, and all their inhabitants and whatever grew in the valley." 
Genesis19:26 But Lot’s wife turned and gazed longingly on the city and turned into a pillar of salt. 
Genesis19:27 That morning, Abraham hurried back to the place where he had stood before Yahweh." 
Genesis19:28 Looking down toward all the land of the plain, he saw columns of smoke billowing up from Sodom and Gomorrah —like the smoke of a furnace!" 
Genesis19:29 So before God destroyed the cities of the plain where Lot had settled, he remembered his affection for Abraham and spared Lot from all the destruction." 
Genesis19:30 Afraid to remain in Zoar, Lot left there and settled in the hill country and lived in a cave with his two daughters." 
Genesis19:31 One day, his firstborn suggested to the younger, “Our father is getting old, and there isn’t a man anywhere who could impregnate us in the normal way." 
Genesis19:32 Come, let’s get our father drunk with wine and have sex with him. That way we can at least have children through our father.”" 
Genesis19:33 That night, they got their father drunk with wine, and the firstborn went in and slept with him. Lot was so drunk he didn’t have a clue about what had happened." 
Genesis19:34 The next day, the firstborn said to the younger, “I slept with my father last night. Tonight, it’s your turn. We’ll get him drunk with wine, and you can sleep with him and we can preserve our family line through our father.”" 
Genesis19:35 So they got their father drunk the second time; and the younger went in and slept with him. He was once again so drunk he had no clue what had happened. 
Genesis19:36 As a result, both Lot’s daughters became pregnant by their father." 
Genesis19:37 The older daughter had a son named Moab, who is the ancestor of the Moabites of today." 
Genesis19:38 The younger also had a son named Ben-Ammi, the ancestor of the Ammonites of today." 
Genesis20:1 Abraham journeyed from the oaks of Mamre to the southern region of Canaan and settled between Kadesh and Shur, to live as a temporary resident in Gerar." 
Genesis20:2 Now Abraham had said about his wife Sarah, “She’s my sister.” So Abimelech, king of Gerar, sent for her and took her into his harem." 
Genesis20:3 One night, God appeared to Abimelech in a dream and said to him, “You’re as good as dead, for you have taken into your harem a married woman!”" 
Genesis20:4 Now Abimelech had not touched Sarah, so he said to the Lord, “Would you really destroy people who are innocent?" 
Genesis20:5 Didn’t the man tell me, ‘She is my sister’? And didn’t she agree and say, ‘Yes, he’s my brother’? I’ve done this with clean hands and a clear conscience.”" 
Genesis20:6 Then God answered him in the dream, “Yes, I know you did this with a clear conscience. Furthermore, it was I who kept you from sinning and kept you from touching her." 
Genesis20:7 Now return the man’s wife, and because he is a prophet, he will intercede on your behalf that you may live. But if you don’t give her back—you will certainly die, you and your entire household.”" 
Genesis20:8 So Abimelech rose early in the morning and gathered all his servants. When he told them about his dream, they were terrified!" 
Genesis20:9 Abimelech summoned Abraham and asked him, “What is this trick you have played on us? I did nothing wrong to you. Why would you bring such great guilt upon me and my kingdom? You have done to me what no one should ever do to another.”" 
Genesis20:10 Then Abimelech asked, “What is your purpose in doing this?”" 
Genesis20:11 Abraham answered, “Because I thought, ‘There’s no one here that fears God. They will kill me to get to my wife.’" 
Genesis20:12 Besides, she really is my half sister. She’s my father’s daughter, but not my mother’s, so I married her." 
Genesis20:13 When God sent me out to wander from my father’s house, I said to her, ‘Here is how you can show your love for me. Everywhere we go, you must say about me, “He’s my brother.” ’ ”" 
Genesis20:14 Then Abimelech gave Abraham’s wife Sarah back to him. In addition, he gave him sheep, cattle, and both male and female servants." 
Genesis20:15 Abimelech told him, “Look, my land is now before you; feel free to settle wherever you please.”" 
Genesis20:16 Then he turned to Sarah and said, “I am giving your brother a thousand pieces of silver as compensation to settle any claim against me, to exonerate you in the eyes of all who are with you, and to clear your reputation.”" 
Genesis20:17 Then Abraham prayed to God; and God healed Abimelech! He also healed Abimelech’s wife and female servants of their infertility and they began to have babies again. 
Genesis20:18 For Yahweh had shut all the wombs of the women of Abimelech’s household because he took Sarah, Abraham’s wife." 
Genesis21:1 Yahweh visited Sarah, just as he said he would, and fulfilled his promise to her." 
Genesis21:2 And Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the exact time God had promised them." 
Genesis21:3 Abraham named his son Isaac, the miracle son, whom Sarah bore him." 
Genesis21:4 When Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, as God had commanded him." 
Genesis21:5 Abraham was one hundred years old when his son Isaac was born. 
Genesis21:6 Sarah said, “God has brought me laughter, and everyone who hears about this will laugh with me.”" 
Genesis21:7 And she added, “Who would ever have told Abraham that Sarah would one day nurse children! Even though Abraham is an old man, look—I have given him a son!”" 
Genesis21:8 Isaac grew and was weaned; and on the day Sarah weaned him, Abraham prepared a great feast." 
Genesis21:9 But Sarah noticed the son of Hagar, the Egyptian, was mocking her son Isaac." 
Genesis21:10 So she said to Abraham, “Get rid of this slave woman and her son. Banish them, for the son of that slave woman must not become a coheir with my son Isaac!”" 
Genesis21:11 Abraham was very upset over Sarah’s demand, for Ishmael was his son too." 
Genesis21:12 God spoke to Abraham, “Don’t be distressed over the slave woman and her son. Listen to every word Sarah says to you, and do it, for it will be through Isaac your promise of descendants will be fulfilled." 
Genesis21:13 Rest assured, I will make the son of your slave woman into a nation too, because he is your son.”" 
Genesis21:14 Abraham rose up early the next morning, bundled up some food and a skin of water, and strapped them to Hagar’s shoulders. Then he gave her his son and sent them away. So, Hagar and her son Ishmael departed and wandered off into the wilderness of Beersheba." 
Genesis21:15 When the water was gone, she grew desperate, so she left her son under a bush." 
Genesis21:16 Then she walked about the distance of a bowshot and sat down, for she thought, “I can’t bear to watch my son die.” As she sat nearby, she broke into tears and sobbed uncontrollably." 
Genesis21:17 And God heard the voice of the boy. The angel of God called out to Hagar from the heavenly realm and said, “What’s the matter, Hagar? Don’t be afraid, for God has heard the voice of your son crying as he lies there." 
Genesis21:18 Get up! Help the boy up and hold him by the hand, for I will make him into a great nation.”" 
Genesis21:19 Then God opened her eyes to reveal a well of water. She went over to the well and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a long, cool drink." 
Genesis21:20 God was with Ishmael as he grew up in the wilderness of Paran. He became an expert archer," 
Genesis21:21 and his mother, Hagar, arranged a marriage for him with an Egyptian woman." 
Genesis21:22 At that time, King Abimelech and Phicol, his army commander, came to Abraham and said, “ It is obvious that God is with you and blesses everything you do." 
Genesis21:23 Now swear to me here before God that you will not deceive me, my children, or my descendants, but as I have shown you loyal friendship, so you will treat me—and the land in which you live as a foreigner—with loyal friendship.”" 
Genesis21:24 “I give you my oath,” Abraham answered." 
Genesis21:25 But Abraham voiced a complaint about a well of water which Abimelech’s servants had seized by force. 
Genesis21:26 Abimelech responded, “This is the first time I’ve heard about this; you never told me before now. I don’t know who has done this.”" 
Genesis21:27 So Abraham took sheep and cattle, gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant." 
Genesis21:28 Then Abraham set apart from his flock seven ewe lambs," 
Genesis21:29 and Abimelech asked him, “What are you doing with these seven ewe lambs you have set apart?”" 
Genesis21:30 He replied, “ I am giving them to you, and by accepting these seven ewe lambs, you acknowledge the proof that I dug this well and it belongs to me.”" 
Genesis21:31 That place was called Beersheba, because it was there that both men swore an oath to one another." 
Genesis21:32 After completing their pact at Beersheba, King Abimelech and Phicol, his army commander, departed and returned to Philistine country." 
Genesis21:33 Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and there he worshiped Yahweh, the everlasting God." 
Genesis21:34 And Abraham lived many years in peace as a foreigner in Philistine country. 
Genesis22:1 Some time later, God tested Abraham. He said, “Abraham!” “Yes, I’m here,” Abraham answered." 
Genesis22:2 God said, “Please take your son, your only son, Isaac, whom I know you dearly love, and go to the land of Moriah. Offer him up to me as a burnt offering on one of the mountains which I will show you.”" 
Genesis22:3 Early the next morning, Abraham cut the wood for the burnt offering, loaded it on his donkey, and set out for the distant place God had shown him. He took with him two of his servants, and his son Isaac." 
Genesis22:4 On the third day, Abraham looked up and saw the place in the distance." 
Genesis22:5 “Stay here with the donkey,” Abraham told the young men. “Isaac and I will go up and worship; then we will return to you.”" 
Genesis22:6 So Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and placed it on Isaac’s back. Abraham carried the knife and the fire, and the two of them walked up the mountain together." 
Genesis22:7 “Father?” Isaac broke the silence. “Yes, my son,” Abraham replied. “We have the wood and the fire,” Isaac said, “but where is the lamb for the burnt offering?”" 
Genesis22:8 Abraham answered, “My son, God himself will provide the lamb for an offering.” So they went on together." 
Genesis22:9 When they arrived at the place on Mount Moriah that God had shown him, Abraham built an altar and stacked the wood on it. He tied up his son Isaac and laid him on top of the wood on the altar." 
Genesis22:10 Then Abraham took the knife in his hand to plunge it into his son," 
Genesis22:11 but the angel of Yahweh called to him from heaven, saying, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Yes, I’m here,” he answered." 
Genesis22:12 “Do not lay a hand on the boy or harm him,” he said, “for now, I know you are fully dedicated to me, since you did not withhold your son, your beloved son, from me.”" 
Genesis22:13 As Abraham looked up, his eyes fell upon a ram caught by its horns in a nearby thicket. Abraham took the ram and sacrificed it on the altar as a burnt offering in Isaac’s place." 
Genesis22:14 So Abraham named that place Yahweh Appears. Even to this day, it is said, “On Yahweh ’s Mountain there is vision.”" 
Genesis22:15 Yahweh ’s angel spoke a second time from heaven: 
Genesis22:16 “ ‘I solemnly promise you, by the glory of my own name,’ decrees Yahweh, ‘because you have obeyed my voice and did not withhold from me your son—your beloved son — I will greatly bless you! I will make sure your seed becomes as numerous as the stars of heaven and as the sand of the seashore. Your offspring will take possession of the city gates of their enemies." 
Genesis22:17  
Genesis22:18 Because you have obeyed me, the entire world will be blessed through your seed.’ ”" 
Genesis22:19 So Abraham and Isaac returned to the waiting servants, and they departed for Beersheba, where Abraham had settled." 
Genesis22:20 Some time later Abraham heard the news, “ You’re an uncle! Milcah and your brother Nahor have eight sons: Uz, their firstborn, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram, Chesed, Hazo, Phildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel the father of Rebekah.”" 
Genesis22:21  
Genesis22:22  
Genesis22:23  
Genesis22:24 Moreover, Nahor had another wife, Reumah, who also had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah." 
Genesis23:1 Sarah lived to be one hundred and twenty-seven. 
Genesis23:2 She died in the land of Canaan at Kiriath Arba, now known as Hebron. There Abraham mourned and wept for her." 
Genesis23:3 After his time of mourning was over, Abraham got up from where her body lay, and went to speak with the Hittites. He said to them," 
Genesis23:4 “I live as an alien and stranger among you. Sell me some of your property so I can use it as a burial site for my wife.” 
Genesis23:5 “Hear us, my lord,” the Hittites answered, “you are a mighty prince of God among us. You may bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us would withhold our burial ground from you.”" 
Genesis23:6  
Genesis23:7 Then Abraham bowed respectfully before the Hittites, the people of the land" 
Genesis23:8 and he said to them, “If you are willing to allow my wife a proper burial, then please hear me out. On my behalf please persuade your countryman, Ephron the son of Zohar," 
Genesis23:9 to sell me the cave he owns at Machpelah on the edge of his land. Let him sell it to me publicly and at the full price, so that I may own it for a burial site.”" 
Genesis23:10 Now Ephron the Hittite was sitting there among his countrymen. He spoke up so all the Hittites who sat at the gate as elders of the city could hear him, and said," 
Genesis23:11 “No, my lord, listen. I will give you not only the cave but also the entire field! In the presence of my people, I will give it to you to bury your dead.”" 
Genesis23:12 Then Abraham bowed low before the people of the land," 
Genesis23:13 and he said to Ephron in the hearing of all the people, “No, please listen, kind sir. I will pay whatever the field is worth. Take my money so that I can bury my dead there.”" 
Genesis23:14 “If you insist, my lord,” answered Ephron. “But what is a piece of land worth only four hundred silver shekels to men of our standing? Go ahead and bury your dead.”" 
Genesis23:15  
Genesis23:16 So Abraham agreed to the amount, weighed out the four hundred silver shekels according to the merchant’s standard of weight, and in the presence of the Hittites he gave the money to Ephron."
