Genesis23:17 So Ephron’s field of Machpelah, east of Mamre, along with the cave and all the trees within its borders, passed" 
Genesis23:18 to Abraham as his legal possession. The complete transaction was carried out in the presence of the Hittites—those who sat as elders at the city gates. 
Genesis23:19 After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave there in the field of Machpelah near Mamre (now Hebron) in the land of Canaan." 
Genesis23:20 Both the field and the cave passed from the Hittites to Abraham’s possession to be his family burial ground. 
Genesis24:1 Now, Yahweh had wonderfully blessed Abraham in every way, and he became a very old man, well advanced in years." 
Genesis24:2 One day, Abraham called for his trusted head servant, who was in charge of all that he had, and said “Please, put your hand here under my thigh," 
Genesis24:3 and I will make you swear by Yahweh, the God of heaven and earth, that you will not acquire a wife for my son among the daughters of the Canaanites among whom I am living." 
Genesis24:4 Promise me you will go instead to my relatives in my native land and find a wife among them for my son Isaac.” 
Genesis24:5 The servant asked him, “Suppose the woman is not willing to come back with me to this land. Should I then take your son back to your native land?”" 
Genesis24:6 “Absolutely not,” Abraham answered, “make sure that you do not take my son back there." 
Genesis24:7 For Yahweh, the God of heaven, took me from my father’s house and from the land of my birth. He spoke to me and solemnly promised that he would give this land to my descendants. I know he will send his angel before you so that you can find a wife for my son from there." 
Genesis24:8 And if for any reason the woman is unwilling to come back with you, then you are released from this oath that you swear to me. But no matter what, do not take my son back there!”" 
Genesis24:9 So, the servant put his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham and swore an oath to carry out his wishes." 
Genesis24:10 So the servant took ten of his master’s camels, loaded them with all sorts of gifts, some of the best things his master owned, and journeyed toward the distant land of Mesopotamia until he got to the village where Abraham’s brother Nahor had lived." 
Genesis24:11 He had his camels kneel by a well outside the village. It was evening, the time when the women came out to draw water." 
Genesis24:12 He prayed: “ Yahweh, God of my master Abraham, let my journey here be a success and show your gracious love to my master Abraham." 
Genesis24:13 I am standing here by this well, and the young women of the village are coming out to draw water. Give me a sign." 
Genesis24:14 I will say to one of the girls, ‘Please, lower your jar and give me a drink.’ And if she is the right one, the girl whom you have chosen to be a wife for your servant Isaac, then let her say to me, ‘Drink, and I will also draw water for your camels.’ By this sign I will know that you have shown your gracious love to my master.”" 
Genesis24:15 Suddenly, before he had finished praying, there was Rebekah approaching the well with her jar on her shoulder. She was the daughter of Bethuel and the granddaughter of Milcah and Abraham’s brother Nahor." 
Genesis24:16 The young woman was strikingly beautiful, unmarried, and still a virgin. She walked down to the well, filled her jar, and came back up." 
Genesis24:17 Abraham’s servant hurried over to meet her and said, “Please, lower your jar and give me a little drink.”" 
Genesis24:18 She responded, “Drink, my lord.” She then quickly lowered her jar to give him a drink." 
Genesis24:19 Then, after she had finished giving him a drink, she added, “I will also draw water for your camels until they have finished drinking.”" 
Genesis24:20 She quickly emptied her jar into the watering trough and ran back to the well to draw more water, until she had watered all the camels." 
Genesis24:21 Meanwhile, the servant stared at her, silently pondering whether Yahweh had indeed made his journey successful." 
Genesis24:22 After the camels had finished drinking, he took a costly gold nose ring weighing a half shekel and two solid gold bracelets weighing ten shekels, and gave them to Rebekah." 
Genesis24:23 Then he asked her, “Tell me, who is your father? Is there room in your father’s house for me and my men to stay tonight?”" 
Genesis24:24 She replied, “My father’s name is Bethuel, and my grandparents are Milcah and Nahor.”" 
Genesis24:25 She went on, “There is room for you to spend the night at our home, and plenty of straw and food for the camels.”" 
Genesis24:26 Upon hearing this, the servant bowed down and worshiped Yahweh," 
Genesis24:27 saying, “Praised be Yahweh, the God of Abraham, for you have faithfully kept your promise to my master and displayed your wonderful kindness and love. Yahweh, you led me straight to the very place of my master’s relatives!”" 
Genesis24:28 Then the girl quickly ran to inform her mother and family about all that had happened. 
Genesis24:29 Now Rebekah had an older brother named Laban, and when he heard everything the man had told his sister and saw her gold nose ring and the costly bracelets dangling on her wrists, Laban ran out to meet the man waiting at the well—and there he was standing beside his camels." 
Genesis24:30  
Genesis24:31 Then Laban said to the man, “Friend, Yahweh has wonderfully blessed you; please, come to my home. Why are you standing out here when I have prepared the house for you and a place for your camels?”" 
Genesis24:32 So the man came into the house, and his camels were unloaded and given straw and feed. The servants brought water to wash the man’s feet and the feet of the men with him." 
Genesis24:33 But when they set food before him, he said, “I won’t eat until I tell you why I’m here.” “Please, tell us,” Laban said." 
Genesis24:34 “I am Abraham’s servant,” he began." 
Genesis24:35 “ Yahweh has wonderfully blessed my master, and he has become extremely wealthy. Yahweh has given him flocks and herds, camels and donkeys, a fortune in silver and gold, and many male and female servants." 
Genesis24:36 And my master’s wife, Sarah, has miraculously borne him a son in her old age, and my master’s son is the sole heir of his fortune." 
Genesis24:37 My master has put me under an oath saying, ‘Do not get a wife for my son from among the daughters of the Canaanites among whom I am living." 
Genesis24:38 Instead, go to my relatives in my native land and find a wife among them for my son.’" 
Genesis24:39 In reply I said to my master, ‘Suppose the woman refuses to return with me.’" 
Genesis24:40 He answered me, ‘ Yahweh, in whose presence I have walked all these years, will send his angel with you and will make your mission successful. You will find a wife for my son from among my relatives, my father’s family." 
Genesis24:41 If you go to my relatives, and they will not give her to you, then and only then will you be released from this oath.’" 
Genesis24:42 So, when I arrived at the well today, I prayed, ‘O  Yahweh, God of my master Abraham, make my mission a success." 
Genesis24:43 I’m here standing at this well. May the right girl for my master’s son come out to draw water. And when she does, I will say to her “Please give me a little drink from your jar.”" 
Genesis24:44 And if she replies, saying, “Drink, and I will also draw water for your camels,” then I might know that she is the young woman Yahweh has chosen for my master’s son.’" 
Genesis24:45 “Before I had even finished my heartfelt prayer, there was Rebekah coming out with her water jar on her shoulder! After she went down to the well and filled her jar, I said to her, ‘Please give me a drink.’" 
Genesis24:46 She quickly lowered her jar and said, ‘Drink, and I will also draw water for your camels.’ So, I drank, and she also watered the camels." 
Genesis24:47 Then I asked her, ‘Tell me, who is your father?’ She said, ‘I’m the daughter of Bethuel, son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore to him.’ When I learned she was of my master’s family, I put a gold ring in her nose and gold bracelets on her wrists," 
Genesis24:48 and I bowed before Yahweh and worshiped him. I praised Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, who led me perfectly on the right path to find the very daughter of my master’s brother to marry his son." 
Genesis24:49 Now, if you will show faithful love to my master and do what is right, then give me permission to take Rebekah back to his land. If not, tell me, and I will go on my way.”" 
Genesis24:50 After hearing this, Laban and Bethuel said, “This was all planned by Yahweh! If this is his plan, what can we say?" 
Genesis24:51 Here stands our Rebekah before you. You may take her and go and let her marry your master’s son and fulfill Yahweh ’s plan for her.” 
Genesis24:52 When Abraham’s servant heard their words, he bowed his face down to the ground and worshiped Yahweh." 
Genesis24:53 Then he brought expensive jewelry of silver and gold and exquisitely embroidered garments, and gave them all to Rebekah, and he gave costly gifts to her brother and her mother." 
Genesis24:54 Afterward, the servant and his men ate and drank, and they spent the night in Laban’s home. First thing in the morning, he said to them, “Allow me now to return to my master.”" 
Genesis24:55 Her brother and mother replied, “Please let Rebekah remain with us a while—for ten days or so, then she may go with you.”" 
Genesis24:56 He responded, “Please, Yahweh has made my mission successful. Send me off with her to my master, don’t make me wait.”" 
Genesis24:57 They answered him, “We will call for Rebekah and see what she wants to do.”" 
Genesis24:58 So they called for Rebekah and asked her, “Will you go with this man?” “I will gladly go with him,” she answered." 
Genesis24:59 So they sent their sister Rebekah away with her servant girls to go with Abraham’s servant and his men. 
Genesis24:60 As she departed, they blessed her with these words: “Our dear sister, may you become thousands of ten thousands! May your descendants gain possession of the city gates of their foes!”" 
Genesis24:61 Then Rebekah and her servant girls mounted the camels and followed the servant and his men. 
Genesis24:62 Meanwhile, Isaac, who was living in the southern desert of Canaan, had just come back to his camp from the Well of the Living One Who Watches Over Me." 
Genesis24:63 Isaac went out in the evening into the field to meditate. He looked up, and saw camels coming in the distance." 
Genesis24:64 As Rebekah got closer, she raised her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she nearly fell off her camel!" 
Genesis24:65 She whispered to the servant, “Who is that man walking in the field toward us?” “Why, he’s the one about whom I told you, that’s my young master!” the servant said. So Rebekah quickly took her veil and covered her face." 
Genesis24:66 Then the servant began to explain to Isaac in detail all the amazing things that had happened. 
Genesis24:67 Isaac fell in love with Rebekah. He took her to be his wife, and they were married, and he brought her into his mother Sarah’s tent. In this way, Isaac was greatly comforted after his mother’s death." 
Genesis25:1 Now Abraham had taken another wife, whose name was Keturah." 
Genesis25:2 She and Abraham had sons named Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah." 
Genesis25:3 Jokshan was the father of Sheba and Dedan. 
Genesis25:4 Dedan’s sons were the Ashurites, the Letushites, and the Leummites. Midian’s sons were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the descendants of Keturah." 
Genesis25:5 Abraham gave all that he possessed to Isaac," 
Genesis25:6 but to his sons by his concubines he gave gifts while he was still living. He sent them all away eastward, separating them from his son Isaac." 
Genesis25:7 Abraham lived a total of one hundred and seventy-five years. 
Genesis25:8 Abraham took his final breath, dying at a good old age. After having lived a full, content life, he joined his ancestors." 
Genesis25:9 His sons, Isaac and Ishmael, buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field which had belonged to Ephron, the son of Zohar the Hittite, east of Mamre." 
Genesis25:10 They buried him next to his wife Sarah, in the same field that Abraham purchased from the Hittites." 
Genesis25:11 After Abraham had passed, God greatly blessed his son Isaac, and Isaac settled near the well named the Well of the Living One Who Watches Over Me." 
Genesis25:12 This is the account of the descendants of Abraham’s son Ishmael, whom Sarah’s servant, Hagar the Egyptian, bore to Abraham." 
Genesis25:13 The names of Ishmael’s sons in their birth order are: Nebaioth the firstborn; and Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam," 
Genesis25:14 Mishma, Dumah, Massa," 
Genesis25:15 Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah." 
Genesis25:16 These twelve sons of Ishmael became princes of twelve tribes that were named after them, listed by the places they settled and camped." 
Genesis25:17 They occupied the land from Havilah to Shur, which is east of Egypt, in the direction of Assyria. And Ishmael lived in hostility toward all of his people. At the age of one hundred and thirty-seven, Ishmael breathed his last and died and was joined to his ancestors." 
Genesis25:18  
Genesis25:19 This is how the story of Isaac begins. He was the beloved son of Abraham and the successor of Abraham’s blessing. 
Genesis25:20 When he was forty, he married Rebekah. She was the daughter of Bethuel and the sister of Laban. Both her father and brother were Arameans from Paddan-Aram." 
Genesis25:21 Now, Rebekah was unable to have children, but Isaac pleaded with Yahweh on behalf of his wife because she was barren—and she did get pregnant, for Yahweh responded to Isaac’s prayer." 
Genesis25:22 During her pregnancy, Rebekah could feel twins thrashing and struggling with each other inside her womb. So she went to inquire of Yahweh, saying, “Why do I have to live with this?”" 
Genesis25:23 And Yahweh answered her, saying, “The two sons in your womb will become two nations, and the two peoples within you will become rivals. One people will become stronger than the other, and the older will serve the younger.”" 
Genesis25:24 And when the time came for Rebekah to give birth, sure enough, she had twins!" 
Genesis25:25 The first one came out reddish and covered with hair like a hairy garment; so they named him Esau. 
Genesis25:26 And his brother came out with his hand grasping Esau’s heel; so, they named him Jacob. Isaac was sixty when the twins were born." 
Genesis25:27 When the boys grew up, Esau became a rugged outdoorsman and a hardy hunter, but Jacob was more contemplative, content to stay close to home." 
Genesis25:28 Isaac loved Esau because he was fond of eating wild game, but Rebekah dearly loved Jacob." 
Genesis25:29 One day, when Jacob was cooking a stew, Esau returned from hunting, and he was famished." 
Genesis25:30 Smelling the aroma of food, Esau said to Jacob, “I’m starving! Let me eat some of that red stuff you’re cooking.” (This is why he is also called Edom.)" 
Genesis25:31 “Yes, but first you must trade me your birthright,” Jacob replied." 
Genesis25:32 “Can’t you see I’m dying of hunger?” Esau said. “What good is the birthright if I’m dead?” 
Genesis25:33 But Jacob insisted, “First, swear to me that you’ll give it to me.” So, Esau swore an oath and surrendered his birthright to Jacob." 
Genesis25:34 Then Jacob gave Esau some lentil stew and bread. When Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and walked away. Esau cared nothing about his own birthright." 
Genesis26:1 Now, another famine struck the land, like the one in Abraham’s time. Isaac traveled to Gerar where Abimelech was the Philistine king." 
Genesis26:2 Yahweh appeared before Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt. Stay in the land that I will reveal to you." 
Genesis26:3 Live there as a foreigner, and my presence will be with you. I promise to bless you, for I will give all these lands to you and your descendants. I will fulfill the oath I swore to your father Abraham." 
Genesis26:4 I will make your descendants as numerous as the stars of heaven, and I will one day give them all these lands. I will bless all the nations of the earth through your offspring" 
Genesis26:5 because Abraham was faithful to me. He listened to my voice and yielded his heart to follow my direction. He kept my commandments, my instructions, and my teachings.”" 
Genesis26:6 So Isaac settled in Gerar. 
Genesis26:7 The men of the land noticed Rebekah’s beauty and asked Isaac about her. “Oh, she’s just my sister,” he replied. Rebekah was indeed very beautiful, and he was afraid to tell them, “She’s my wife,” for he thought that the men of the land would kill him and take his wife." 
Genesis26:8 One day, after Isaac had lived there awhile, the Philistine king Abimelech looked out his window and saw Isaac affectionately caressing his wife Rebekah." 
Genesis26:9 So Abimelech summoned Isaac and said, “So, this woman is your wife! Why did you say that she was your sister?” “Because I thought the men of the land would kill me and take her,” Isaac said." 
Genesis26:10 “Don’t you realize what you’ve done to us?” Abimelech asked. “One of our men might have slept with your wife, and you would have brought guilt and punishment upon us!”" 
Genesis26:11 So Abimelech issued a stern warning to all the people, saying, “Whoever lays a hand on this man or his wife shall be put to death!”" 
Genesis26:12 Isaac planted crops in that land, and in the same year reaped a hundred-fold harvest, for Yahweh greatly blessed him!" 
Genesis26:13 Isaac grew richer and richer until he was extremely wealthy. 
Genesis26:14 He acquired so many flocks and herds and so many servants that the Philistines grew jealous," 
Genesis26:15 so the Philistines stopped up all of Isaac’s wells and filled them with dirt—the same wells his father’s servants had dug in the time of Abraham. 
Genesis26:16 Eventually Abimelech’s jealousy got the better of him, and he said to Isaac, “You must leave us! You have become too rich and powerful for us!”" 
Genesis26:17 So Isaac departed from Abimelech’s domain and camped near the Wadi of Gerar, where he settled." 
Genesis26:18 Isaac reopened and restored the wells his father Abraham had originally dug—the wells the Philistines had stopped up after Abraham’s death. And Isaac gave these wells the same names that his father had given them. 
Genesis26:19 One day, when Isaac’s servants dug in the valley, they uncovered a spring-fed well." 
Genesis26:20 But the herdsmen of Gerar picked a fight with Isaac’s herdsmen, saying, “That’s our well!” So Isaac named the well Argument because of the sharp contention." 
Genesis26:21 They dug a second well, and it also caused a quarrel, so Isaac named that well Hostility." 
Genesis26:22 Then he pulled up stakes from there and dug a third well, but this one was dug without any quarreling, so Isaac named it Spacious, saying, “Finally, Yahweh has made ample room for us and we will become prosperous in the land!”" 
Genesis26:23 From there, Isaac moved his camp to Beersheba." 
Genesis26:24 That very night, Yahweh appeared before him and said: “I am the God of Abraham your father. You will never need to fear a thing for I am with you and I will greatly bless you. Your children will flourish and succeed because of the promises I gave my servant Abraham.”" 
Genesis26:25 So Isaac built an altar, prayed, and worshiped Yahweh there. He pitched his tent there, and his servants started digging another well." 
Genesis26:26 Then King Abimelech came to Isaac from Gerar with his adviser Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol. 
Genesis26:27 Isaac asked them, “Why have you come to me now, since you hated me and sent me away?”" 
Genesis26:28 They answered, “We have witnessed firsthand how powerfully Yahweh ’s favor has been with you, so we say, let there be a peace treaty among us. Let us make a covenant" 
Genesis26:29 to do each other no harm. Swear to us that you will not trouble us, just as we have not troubled you and have always treated you well and sent you away in peace. Now look at how Yahweh has blessed you!”" 
Genesis26:30 So Isaac prepared a wonderful feast for them, and they all ate and drank together." 
Genesis26:31 Then the next morning, they got up early and exchanged oaths. Isaac said goodbye to them, and they left Isaac in peace." 
Genesis26:32 Later that same day, Isaac’s servants came with wonderful news about the well they had dug, saying, “We’ve just found water!”" 
Genesis26:33 So he named it Oath. This is why the city where they dug the well is called Beersheba to this day. 
Genesis26:34 When Esau was forty, he married two Hittite women, Judith the daughter of Beeri and Basemath the daughter of Elon." 
Genesis26:35 They made life miserable for Isaac and Rebekah. 
Genesis27:1 When Isaac was very old and blind, he called for his oldest son, Esau, and asked him, “My son?” “ I am here, father,” Esau answered." 
Genesis27:2 “As you can see, I am now an old man,” Isaac said. “And I may die any day now." 
Genesis27:3 So please take your hunting gear—your bow and arrows—and go out into the field and hunt some wild game for me. 
Genesis27:4 Then prepare me a savory meal, the food I love, and bring it to me. After I eat it, I will bless you from my innermost being before I die.”" 
Genesis27:5 Now, Rebekah was eavesdropping on their conversation. So, when Esau left for the field to hunt for game to cook for Isaac," 
Genesis27:6 she found Jacob and said to him, “I just overheard your father say to your brother Esau," 
Genesis27:7 ‘Bring me some wild game and prepare a savory meal for me. Afterward I will bless you in the presence of Yahweh before I die.’ 
Genesis27:8 Listen carefully, my son, and do everything I tell you." 
Genesis27:9 Go out to the flock and bring me two of the best young goats. I’ll cook them for your father and prepare a delicious meal, the way he loves it." 
Genesis27:10 Then, you take it to him to eat and your father will bless you before he dies.”" 
Genesis27:11 Jacob objected, “But my brother Esau is covered with hair, and I’m smooth skinned." 
Genesis27:12 If my father feels my hairless skin, he’ll know I’m not Esau. He’ll think I’m a trickster, and I’ll end up bringing a curse upon myself rather than a blessing!”" 
Genesis27:13 “My son,” his mother said, “let any curse against you fall on me alone! Just do what I say and go and get the goats for me.”" 
Genesis27:14 So he went and got the goats and brought them to his mother. She prepared a delicious meal, just the way his father loved it." 
Genesis27:15 Then Rebekah picked out the best clothes of her older son Esau and put them on her younger son Jacob. 
Genesis27:16 She covered Jacob’s hands and the soft part of his neck with goatskins. 
Genesis27:17 Then she handed Jacob the tasty dish and the bread that she had prepared 
Genesis27:18 and he took them to Isaac. “Father?” Jacob said. Isaac replied, “Which one of my sons are you?”" 
Genesis27:19 Jacob answered, “It’s I—Esau—your firstborn. I have done as you asked. Please, sit up. Eat some of this delicious game you love so that you may give me your innermost blessing.”" 
Genesis27:20 Isaac asked his son, “How in the world did you find game so quickly, my son?” “ Yahweh, your God, caused it to come right to me,” he replied." 
Genesis27:21 Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Please, please, come closer that I may touch you, my son. I need to really know for certain that you are Esau.”" 
Genesis27:22 So Jacob inched closer to his father Isaac, who felt his skin and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands are Esau’s.”" 
Genesis27:23 Because his hands were hairy like his brother Esau’s, Isaac was tricked and did not recognize that it was really Jacob. As he was about to give him his blessing," 
Genesis27:24 Isaac asked him again, “Are you really my son Esau?” Jacob answered, “I am.”" 
Genesis27:25 “Then bring the food to me,” Isaac said, “and let me eat my son’s game. Then I will give you my blessing.” So, Jacob gave his father the food and he ate it. He brought him wine and he drank it." 
Genesis27:26 Then Isaac said to him, “My son, come near and kiss me.”" 
Genesis27:27 So he came near and kissed him. Isaac recognized the smell of his son’s clothes and blessed him, saying, “Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a lush field that Yahweh has blessed!" 
Genesis27:28 May God give you heaven’s dew, the fatness of earth, and an abundance of grain and new wine!" 
Genesis27:29 Let peoples serve you and nations bow down to you! May you be master over your brothers and may your mother’s sons bow down to you! Those who curse you will be cursed and those who bless you will be blessed!” 
Genesis27:30 No sooner had Jacob left from receiving the blessing of his father Isaac, than his brother Esau arrived home from hunting." 
Genesis27:31 He too prepared a savory meal for his father and took it to him. He said to Isaac, “Sit up my father; eat some of your son’s wild game so that you can give me your innermost blessing.”" 
Genesis27:32 His father Isaac asked him, “Who are you?” “I’m Esau, your firstborn son,” he answered." 
Genesis27:33 When Isaac realized what had happened, he began to tremble and shake violently. He asked, “Who was it then that hunted wild game and brought it to me? I’ve already eaten it all before you came, and I gave him the blessing—yes, and he will be blessed indeed!”" 
Genesis27:34 When Esau heard his father’s words, he burst into bitter weeping and uncontrollable sobbing. He said to his father, “ Bless me! Bless me too, father!”" 
Genesis27:35 Isaac answered, “Your brother was here and deceived me. He has taken away your blessing.”" 
Genesis27:36 Esau exclaimed, “Jacob, that heel grabber —the name fits him perfectly! Now he has tricked me twice! He stole my birthright, and now he’s robbed me of my blessing!” Then he asked, “ Father, haven’t you reserved a blessing for me?”" 
Genesis27:37 Isaac replied, “ You don’t understand. My blessing will empower him to be master over you. I have already given him all his brothers and relatives as servants. My blessing will richly provide him with grain and new wine. What more is left for me to do for you, my son?”" 
Genesis27:38 Esau pleaded with his father, “Is that the only blessing you have to give? Bless me too, my father!” Esau could not hold back his tears and he wept loudly." 
Genesis27:39 Then Isaac his father spoke these words: “You will live far from earth’s bounty and far from heaven’s dew on high. 
Genesis27:40 You will live by the sword and serve your brother; but when you grow restless, you will break free from his control.”" 
Genesis27:41 Esau hated Jacob because he stole his blessing. He said to himself, “In a short time my father will be gone. After the time for mourning his death has passed, I will kill my brother Jacob!”" 
Genesis27:42 But when Rebekah found out about her older son Esau’s plan, she quickly sent for her younger son Jacob, and said to him, “Listen, your brother Esau is planning to exact revenge by killing you." 
Genesis27:43 My son, you must do what I say. Run away at once to my brother Laban in Haran." 
Genesis27:44 You can live with him for a while until your brother calms down. 
Genesis27:45 After your brother’s anger has died down and he has forgotten what you’ve done to him, I will send a messenger to bring you back from there. Why should I lose both my sons in one day?”" 
Genesis27:46 Later, Rebekah said to Isaac, “I’m so disgusted with our Hittite daughters-in-law. I’d rather die than see Jacob marry one of these local girls—these Hittite women!”" 
Genesis28:1 Before Jacob left, Isaac called for him, blessed him, and gave him these instructions: “I forbid you to marry any Canaanite woman." 
Genesis28:2 Leave at once for the land of your grandfather Bethuel in Paddan-Aram and find one of the young women there to marry, one of your uncle Laban’s daughters." 
Genesis28:3 May the God who is always more than enough bless you abundantly. May he make you fruitful and multiply your descendants until you become many tribes! 
Genesis28:4 May he impart to you and your seed the blessing of Abraham so that you may possess the land where you now live as a foreigner—the land God gave to Abraham.” 
Genesis28:5 Then Isaac sent Jacob on his way to Paddan-Aram, the land of his grandfather Bethuel the Aramean, to the home of Laban, Bethuel’s son and the brother of Rebekah, the mother of Jacob and Esau." 
Genesis28:6 Esau had learned that Isaac had blessed Jacob and had sent him away to Paddan-Aram to find a wife there, and that while blessing him, Isaac had instructed him, “Don’t marry a Canaanite woman.”" 
Genesis28:7 Furthermore, Esau found out that Jacob had obeyed his parents and fled to Paddan-Aram." 
Genesis28:8 Esau, knowing that his father Isaac disapproved of Canaanite women," 
Genesis28:9 went to Ishmael’s clan and chose a woman named Mahalath to be one of his wives. She was the daughter of Ishmael, Abraham’s son, and sister of Nebaioth." 
Genesis28:10 Jacob left Beersheba and journeyed toward Haran. 
Genesis28:11 He encountered a certain place at sunset and camped there for the night. He took a stone from there, made it his pillow, and lay down to sleep." 
Genesis28:12 He had a dream of a stairway securely fixed on the earth and reaching into heaven. And there were messengers of God ascending and descending on the stairway. 
Genesis28:13 And Yahweh stood beside him and said to him, “I am Yahweh, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac. You are lying on the very ground that I will give to you and your descendants." 
Genesis28:14 They will become as numerous as the specks of dust on the earth, and they will extend their territory in all directions. Through you and your descendants, I will bless the whole world." 
Genesis28:15 Never forget—I am always with you and will protect you wherever you go. And one day I will bring you back to this land, for I will neither leave you nor fail to fulfill every word I have promised you.”" 
Genesis28:16 When Jacob awoke from his dream, he was overcome with awe and said, “ Yahweh is here! He is in this place and I didn’t realize it!”" 
Genesis28:17 Terrified and overwhelmed, he said, “How awesome is this place! I have stumbled right into the House of God! This place is a portal, the very gate of heaven!”" 
Genesis28:18 Early in the morning, Jacob took the stone he had under his head, set it up as a pillar and anointed it by pouring oil over the top of it." 
Genesis28:19 He named that place Bethel; though the city was once called Luz. 
Genesis28:20 Then Jacob committed himself to God, saying, “If you will always be with me and protect me on this long journey, and if you give me bread to eat and clothing to wear," 
Genesis28:21 and if I return safely to my father’s house, then Yahweh, you will be my God!" 
Genesis28:22 See! I have set up this sacred stone pillar and it will be your house, God. I promise to set aside a tenth of all that you give me as my gift to you.”" 
Genesis29:1 Jacob resumed his journey and entered the land east of Canaan. 
Genesis29:2 One day, Jacob came to a well out in the open country where the shepherds watered their animals. Three flocks of sheep and goats were lying near the well, which had a large, heavy stone covering the opening." 
Genesis29:3 Whenever all the flocks gathered there, the shepherds would roll away the stone from the mouth of the well, water their sheep, and then roll the stone back over the top of the well." 
Genesis29:4 Jacob approached them and said, “Good day, friends; where are you from?” “We’re from Haran,” they answered." 
Genesis29:5 “Do you happen to know Laban, a grandson of Nahor?” Jacob said. “We do,” they replied." 
Genesis29:6 Jacob asked, “How’s he doing?” “He’s doing well,” they answered. “As a matter of fact, here comes his daughter Rachel right now with her flock.”" 
Genesis29:7 Jacob said to them, “Look, it’s not time for the animals to gather together here; it’s now the hottest hour of the day. Let’s go ahead and water the sheep, then you can go and pasture them.”" 
Genesis29:8 They replied, “First, we have to wait until all the flocks are gathered. After that, the stone needs to be rolled away from the mouth of the well, then we’ll water the animals.”" 
Genesis29:9 While they were still speaking, Rachel, the shepherdess, drew near to the well with her father’s sheep." 
Genesis29:10 As soon as Jacob took one good look at Rachel, the beautiful daughter of his uncle Laban, he quickly went over to the mouth of the well and single-handedly rolled away the stone and watered all the flock of his uncle Laban!" 
Genesis29:11 Immediately, he walked up to Rachel and kissed her! Unable to hold back his tears, Jacob wept aloud." 
Genesis29:12 After he composed himself, he explained to Rachel, “I’m your father’s nephew, your aunt Rebekah’s son.” Upon hearing this, Rachel ran to tell her father." 
Genesis29:13 When Laban heard the news that his nephew Jacob had arrived at the well, he ran to greet him. Laban hugged and kissed Jacob and welcomed him into his home. After Jacob told him the story of all that happened," 
Genesis29:14 Laban said to him, “Certainly, you are my own flesh and blood!” Jacob stayed with him for an entire month and worked for him." 
Genesis29:15 Afterward, Laban said to Jacob, “Just because you’re my relative doesn’t mean I expect you to work for nothing. Tell me, what do you want your wages to be?”" 
Genesis29:16 (Now, Laban had two daughters; the older was Leah, and the younger was Rachel." 
Genesis29:17 Rachel had a lovely figure and was gorgeous, but Leah’s eyes were weak.)" 
Genesis29:18 Jacob had fallen in love with Rachel, so he answered Laban, “I will serve you for seven years for the hand of your younger daughter, Rachel!”" 
Genesis29:19 Laban replied, “I’d rather give her to you than to some other man. Stay and work for me.”" 
Genesis29:20 So Jacob served Laban for seven years in exchange for Rachel, but because he loved her so deeply, the seven years seemed like only a few days." 
Genesis29:21 After the seven years, Jacob said to Laban, “My time is fulfilled; give me your daughter so that I may marry her and sleep with her.”" 
Genesis29:22 So Laban prepared a wedding feast and invited all the people of the surrounding area. 
Genesis29:23 That night, Laban tricked Jacob by bringing his older daughter Leah to Jacob’s tent, and he slept with her on his wedding night!" 
Genesis29:24 (Laban assigned Zilpah to be Leah’s servant.) 
Genesis29:25 When Jacob woke up the next morning, he was shocked to find Leah lying next to him! So he confronted Laban and said, “What have you done to me? Didn’t I serve you these seven years for Rachel? Why have you tricked me?”" 
Genesis29:26 Laban answered, “It’s not our custom here to give the younger daughter in marriage before the older daughter is married." 
Genesis29:27 Wait until this bridal week of festivities is over, then I’ll give you Rachel, but you must serve me for another seven years.”" 
Genesis29:28 Jacob complied with Laban’s request. After he completed the prescribed week of Leah’s wedding feast, Laban gave his daughter Rachel to be his wife, and he slept with her. Rachel was his true love, not Leah. (Laban assigned Bilhah as his daughter Rachel’s servant.) And Jacob remained there serving Laban for another seven years." 
Genesis29:29  
Genesis29:30  
Genesis29:31 When Yahweh saw that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb, but Rachel remained childless." 
Genesis29:32 Leah conceived, gave birth to a son, and named him Reuben, saying, “Because Yahweh looked upon me with compassion in my misery, surely, my husband will love me now!”" 
Genesis29:33 She conceived again, gave birth to a son, and named him Simeon, saying, “ Yahweh has heard that I am despised, and in his mercy, he has given me this son also.”" 
Genesis29:34 Leah conceived the third time, gave birth to a son, and named him Levi, saying, “This time my husband will be joined to me, because now I’ve given him three sons!”" 
Genesis29:35 Once again, Leah conceived and gave birth to a son. She named him Judah, saying, “This time I will praise the Lord!” Then she stopped bearing children for a while." 
Genesis30:1 When Rachel saw that she could not give Jacob children, her jealousy toward her sister simmered. So, she said to Jacob, “Give me sons, or I’ll die!”" 
Genesis30:2 Jacob became furious with Rachel and said, “Am I God? He’s the one keeping you from bearing children!”" 
Genesis30:3 She replied, “Here’s my servant Bilhah. Sleep with her. She can be my surrogate; then I can have children through her and build a family.”" 
Genesis30:4 So Rachel gave her servant Bilhah to Jacob as another wife, and Jacob slept with her." 
Genesis30:5 And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son," 
Genesis30:6 and Rachel named him Dan, saying, “God has vindicated me. He heard my voice and gave me a son.”" 
Genesis30:7 Then her servant Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son. 
Genesis30:8 Rachel named him Naphtali, saying, “I have wrestled mightily with my sister, and I won!”" 
Genesis30:9 Meanwhile, when Leah saw that she had ceased bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as another wife." 
Genesis30:10 Zilpah bore Jacob a son, and Leah named him Gad, saying, “What good fortune!”" 
Genesis30:11  
Genesis30:12 Zilpah bore Jacob a second son," 
Genesis30:13 and Leah named him Asher, saying, “Oh happy day! All the women will say, ‘She’s happy now!’ ”" 
Genesis30:14 One day, during wheat harvest, Reuben found some mandrake plants in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Rachel said to Leah, “Please give me some of your son’s mandrakes.”" 
Genesis30:15 Leah replied, “You already took away the affection of my husband, so now you’re going to take my son’s mandrakes, too?” Rachel said, “All right then, I’ll let him sleep with you tonight in exchange for some of your son’s mandrakes.”" 
Genesis30:16 That evening, when Jacob was coming home from the field, Leah went out to meet him and said, “You must sleep with me tonight, for I’ve paid for your services with my son’s mandrakes.” So Jacob slept with Leah that night." 
Genesis30:17 God listened compassionately to Leah’s cry, she became pregnant, and bore Jacob a fifth son," 
Genesis30:18 whom she named Issachar, saying, “God rewarded me for giving my maidservant to my husband.”" 
Genesis30:19 Once again, Leah conceived and bore Jacob a sixth son," 
Genesis30:20 whom she named Zebulun, saying, “God has given me good gifts for my husband! Now he will accept me, for I’ve given him six sons.”" 
Genesis30:21 Lastly, Leah gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah." 
Genesis30:22 God listened to Rachel’s heart-cry, and had compassion on her, and made her fertile." 
Genesis30:23 She conceived, and bore a son, and named him Joseph, saying, “God has taken away my disgrace. May Yahweh add to me another son.”" 
Genesis30:24  
Genesis30:25 After Rachel gave birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me to go back home to my homeland." 
Genesis30:26 You know how hard I’ve worked for you these many years to finish paying for my two wives. Give them to me along with my children, and I’ll be on my way.”" 
Genesis30:27 Laban countered, “If you please, I have learned by divine inquiry that I have become prosperous because of you and the blessing of Yahweh that’s on your life." 
Genesis30:28 Just name your price, and I’ll give it to you.”" 
Genesis30:29 Jacob replied, “You know how hard I’ve worked for you and how your livestock has increased under my care." 
Genesis30:30 The little you had before I came has multiplied greatly, for Yahweh has blessed you wonderfully because I am here. But now, I need to provide for my own family, too.”" 
Genesis30:31 So Laban asked, “What should I give you?” “Nothing,” Jacob replied, “You don’t need to give me a thing. If you will do but one thing for me, I will continue to care for your flocks:" 
Genesis30:32 Just let me pass through all your flocks today and take out every speckled and spotted sheep or goat, and every black lamb. That’s all the payment I ask." 
Genesis30:33 And in the future, when you review my wages, the integrity of my dealings with you will be obvious. If you find any animal among mine that is not speckled, spotted, or black, then you will know that I stole it.”" 
Genesis30:34 “Agreed!” Laban said. “We’ll do what you’ve suggested.” 
Genesis30:35 But that same day, Laban secretly removed all the male and female goats that were speckled or spotted (all that had white on them) and all the black lambs and left them under the care of his sons." 
Genesis30:36 He set a distance of a three-day journey between himself and Jacob, while Jacob continued to tend the rest of Laban’s flocks." 
Genesis30:37 Jacob, however, cut green branches of poplar, almond, and plane trees and peeled back part of their bark, to expose the white inner wood of the branches." 
Genesis30:38 Then he set the partially peeled branches inside the water troughs where the goats would see them when they came to drink. 
Genesis30:39 For they mated when they came to the water troughs, and as they lowered their heads to drink, they saw the stripped branches in front of their eyes. Miraculously they gave birth to streaked, speckled, and spotted young." 
Genesis30:40 But with the mating ewes, on the other hand, he made them face the streaked or completely black animals in Laban’s flock. By doing this, he produced his own special flocks, which he didn’t allow to mingle with Laban’s." 
Genesis30:41 Moreover, every time the stronger females were in heat, Jacob laid the partially peeled branches in the water troughs in front of the flock, so that they would mate among the branches." 
Genesis30:42 But he didn’t place the branches in front of the scrawny goats when they mated, leaving the feeble animals for Laban and the stronger for himself." 
Genesis30:43 In this way, Jacob quickly grew very wealthy and owned large flocks, a great number of camels and donkeys, and many male and female servants." 
Genesis31:1 Now Jacob heard that the sons of Laban were complaining, “Everything Jacob owns he has taken from our father! He gained all his wealth from what our father owned.”" 
Genesis31:2 And Jacob saw that Laban no longer viewed him favorably as he once did. 
Genesis31:3 Then Yahweh said to Jacob, “Return to the land of your ancestors where you were born; and remember, I will be with you.”" 
Genesis31:4 So Jacob sent a message for Leah and Rachel to meet him in the field where his flocks were grazing. 
Genesis31:5 When they arrived, he said to them, “I can see that your father’s attitude toward me has changed, but the God of my father has been with me." 
Genesis31:6 You both know I have worked for your father as hard as I could," 
Genesis31:7 although he has cheated me and reduced my wages over and over. Yet God has not allowed him to harm me. 
Genesis31:8 If he said, ‘The speckled ones will be your wages,’ then all the flock bore speckled. If he said, ‘The striped ones will be your wages,’ then all the flock bore striped." 
Genesis31:9 Because of these miracles, God has taken away your father’s livestock and given them to me!”" 
Genesis31:10 Jacob continued, “Once during the breeding season, I had a dream. I saw that the male goats who were mating were all speckled, streaked, or spotted." 
Genesis31:11 In the dream, the angel of God called me by my name, ‘Jacob.’ ” “ ‘I am here,’ I answered." 
Genesis31:12 “Then he said, ‘Observe and note that all the male goats that are mating are speckled, streaked, or spotted, for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you." 
Genesis31:13 I am the God who appeared to you at Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and made a vow to me. Now leave this land at once and return to the land of your birth.’ ”" 
Genesis31:14 Then Rachel and Leah answered him, “Our father doesn’t want us to inherit a portion from his estate." 
Genesis31:15 Hasn’t he treated us as outsiders and not as members of his family? Not only did he sell us like property, but he has also spent our purchase price!" 
Genesis31:16 Our father’s wealth that God has given you was legally ours and our children to begin with! So, go ahead and do whatever God has told you.”" 
Genesis31:17 Jacob immediately put his wives and children on camels 
Genesis31:18 and took with him all the livestock and everything he had amassed in Paddan-Aram. He set out to return to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan. 
Genesis31:19 One day, when Laban had gone to his fields to shear his sheep, Rachel stole her father’s household idols." 
Genesis31:20 Jacob had outwitted Laban the Aramean by secretly departing without telling him. 
Genesis31:21 He fled with all that he had, and after crossing the Euphrates, he headed for the hill country of Gilead." 
Genesis31:22 And it wasn’t until three days later that Laban discovered Jacob had left. 
Genesis31:23 Laban, along with some of his relatives, took off in pursuit and chased after Jacob for seven days. He had almost caught up with him in the hill country of Gilead," 
Genesis31:24 when God appeared to Laban the Aramean in a dream and warned him, “Be careful that you neither harm nor threaten Jacob.”" 
Genesis31:25 Now Jacob had set up his camp with his flocks on a hill, and Laban had him in his sights. Laban and his companions set up their camp nearby." 
Genesis31:26 Laban approached Jacob and said, “ Nephew, what have you done? You’ve deceived me and carried away my daughters like captives on a battlefield." 
Genesis31:27 Why did you trick me and run away in secret without telling me? I would have sent you off joyously, celebrating with singing and dancing to the tambourine and stringed instruments." 
Genesis31:28 And why did you not even let me kiss my daughters and grandchildren goodbye? What you have done is foolish! 
Genesis31:29 I could harm you, but the God of your father spoke to me in a dream last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you neither harm nor threaten Jacob.’" 
Genesis31:30 Now I realize you ran away because you desperately long to return to your father’s house, but why did you steal my gods?”" 
Genesis31:31 “ I left in a hurry because I was afraid,” Jacob answered, “and I thought you would take your daughters from me by force." 
Genesis31:32 Whoever has taken your gods will be put to death! So here, in the presence of our relatives, if you can find among our possessions anything that is yours, take it.” Now Jacob had no clue that Rachel had stolen the gods." 
Genesis31:33 Laban went immediately into Jacob’s tent to search. Then he went into the tents of Zilpah and Bilhah but found nothing. After searching Leah’s tent, he went into Rachel’s." 
Genesis31:34 Now Rachel had taken the family gods and put them in her camel’s cushion and sat on them. 
Genesis31:35 She said to her father, “Please my father, don’t be angry if I don’t rise before you, for I’m having my period.” When he rummaged through her tent, he did not find them." 
Genesis31:36 Then Jacob became angry and complained to Laban, “What have I done wrong? What sin have I committed that you would hotly pursue me as if you were chasing a criminal?" 
Genesis31:37 You have rummaged through all my things, and did you find anything of your own property? If you did, set them here in front of your relatives and mine. Let them decide between the two of us.”" 
Genesis31:38 Jacob continued, “For the last twenty years I have served you, and the whole time your sheep and goats did not miscarry, nor did I feast on any of your rams." 
Genesis31:39 If one from your flock was mauled by a wild beast, I didn’t bring it to you; I absorbed the loss myself. And you always made me pay for any missing animal, whether snatched by day or by night." 
Genesis31:40 Many times, scorching heat consumed me by day and hard frost by night; I endured sleepless nights." 
Genesis31:41 For these twenty years that I’ve lived among you, I slaved away fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flocks. And besides all that, you’ve reduced my wages over and over." 
Genesis31:42 If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Awesome One of Isaac, had not been on my side, you certainly would have sent me away empty-handed! But God in his mercy took notice of how much I’ve suffered and how hard I’ve worked—and that’s why he rebuked you last night in your dream!”" 
Genesis31:43 Laban replied to Jacob, “These women are my daughters, these children are my grandchildren; and the flocks are mine. In fact, everything you see belongs to me. But from today I will not be able to do anything more for my daughters and grandchildren." 
Genesis31:44 Come now, let’s form a covenant between you and me. Let it endure as a witness between us.”" 
Genesis31:45 So Jacob took a stone, set it up as a pillar," 
Genesis31:46 and in the presence of his relatives, he said to them, “Everyone gather stones, and place them here in a pile.” And they did so, and afterward, they ate together next to the heap of stones." 
Genesis31:47 Laban and Jacob named the place in both languages, Aramaic and Hebrew, calling it Witness Heap." 
Genesis31:48 And Laban said, “Today, this heap of stones will be a witness between you and me of our enduring friendship.” He called it Witness Heap" 
Genesis31:49 and Watchpost, for Laban declared, “May Yahweh keep his eyes on us when we are absent from each other." 
Genesis31:50 If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women besides my daughters, remember that even though no one else is with us, God is watching us." 
Genesis31:51 Here is the heap of stones and here is the memorial pillar that stands between us. 
Genesis31:52 This heap and this pillar stand as witnesses that I will never pass beyond them to harm you and that you will never pass beyond them to harm me. 
Genesis31:53 Now may the God of Abraham and the god of Nahor judge between us!” Jacob made his vow by the Awesome One of his father Isaac. 
Genesis31:54 Then Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and called everyone together for a meal, and they remained there all night on the mountain." 
Genesis31:55 Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and grandchildren, blessed them, and then he returned home." 
Genesis32:1 As Jacob continued toward Canaan, the angels of God came to meet him!" 
Genesis32:2 When he saw them, he exclaimed, “This is God’s military camp!” So he named that place Two Camps of Angels." 
Genesis32:3 Jacob sent messengers ahead to his brother Esau who was living in the land of Seir, the country of Edom." 
Genesis32:4 He instructed them, “Give this message from me to my master Esau, ‘I am your servant. I have lived with our uncle Laban all these years" 
Genesis32:5 and have acquired sheep, cattle, donkeys, and both male and female servants. I send this message to you, my master, in the hope of finding favor in your eyes.’ ”" 
Genesis32:6 When the messengers returned to Jacob, they informed him, “We gave your brother Esau your message, and he himself is coming here to meet you. In fact, he’s on his way now with four hundred men!”" 
Genesis32:7 Gripped with fear to the point of panic, Jacob split all the people who were with him into two camps, and also the flocks, herds, and camels." 
Genesis32:8 He said to himself, “If Esau attacks the first camp and destroys them, at least the other camp will escape.”" 
Genesis32:9 Then Jacob prayed, “ Yahweh, God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, you said to me, ‘Return to your country and to your family, and I will make you prosper.’" 
Genesis32:10 I am so unworthy of all the loving-kindness and faithfulness that you have showered upon me, your servant. When I crossed this river Jordan years ago, all I had to my name was a staff in my hand, and now I have increased to become two camps!" 
Genesis32:11 Save me, I pray, from the hand of my brother Esau, for I’m afraid he will come and kill all of us, including the women and children." 
Genesis32:12 You said to me, ‘I will certainly prosper you and make your offspring as innumerable as the sand of the sea.’ ”" 
Genesis32:13 So he spent the night there. From what he had with him, Jacob sent a gift to his brother Esau:" 
Genesis32:14 two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams," 
Genesis32:15 thirty female camels and their calves, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys." 
Genesis32:16 He placed them in the care of his servants, each herd by itself. He told them, “Go on ahead of me, and put some space between each herd.”" 
Genesis32:17 He gave these instructions to the one in the lead, “When my brother Esau meets you, and asks, ‘Who is your master? Where are you going? Who owns these herds you are driving?’" 
Genesis32:18 then you are to say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob. He’s sent them as a gift to you, my lord Esau, and he is coming behind us.’ ”" 
Genesis32:19 He likewise instructed the leaders of the second and the third herds and all those following them, “You must say the same thing when you meet Esau." 
Genesis32:20 And be sure to say, ‘Your servant Jacob is coming behind us,’ ” because he reasoned, “If I can appease Esau with these gifts before I have to meet him face-to-face, he may accept me.”" 
Genesis32:21 So he sent the gifts on ahead of him, while he spent that night in the camp." 
Genesis32:22 During the night, Jacob arose, woke up his two wives, his two maidservants, and eleven sons, and had them cross the ford of the Jabbok River." 
Genesis32:23 He sent them across along with everything he had," 
Genesis32:24 and Jacob was left all alone. Suddenly, out of nowhere, a man appeared and wrestled with him until daybreak." 
Genesis32:25 When the man saw that he was not winning the match, he struck Jacob’s hip and knocked it out of joint, leaving it wrenched as he continued to wrestle with him." 
Genesis32:26 Eventually, the man said to him, “Let me go, for the day is breaking.” But Jacob refused. “No! Not until you bless me!”" 
Genesis32:27 “What is your name?” asked the man. “Jacob,” he replied." 
Genesis32:28 “Not anymore,” the man said to him. “Your new name is Israel, for you have struggled both with God and with people and have overcome.”" 
Genesis32:29 Jacob said, “Please, tell me your name.” “Why ask my name?” the man replied, then he spoke a blessing over Jacob." 
Genesis32:30 So Jacob named the place Penuel, saying, “I have seen God face-to-face, yet my life has been spared!”" 
Genesis32:31 The sun rose upon him as he crossed the Jabbok River from Penuel, limping because of his hip." 
Genesis32:32 To this day, the Israelites do not eat the thigh muscle attached to the hip socket, because the man struck Jacob’s hip socket at the thigh muscle." 
Genesis33:1 When Jacob looked up, he saw Esau approaching with four hundred men. So he quickly divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two maidservants." 
Genesis33:2 He lined up the maidservants and their children in front, then Leah and her children, then Rachel and Joseph last of all." 
Genesis33:3 Then Jacob went ahead of them to face Esau. As he approached his brother, he bowed down to the ground seven times before reaching him." 
Genesis33:4 But Esau ran to Jacob and hugged him! He threw his arms around Jacob’s neck, he kissed him, and they wept in each other’s arms." 
Genesis33:5 When Esau looked up and saw the women and the children, he said to Jacob, “Who are all these coming behind you?” Jacob replied, “These are the children, your niece and nephews, whom God has so graciously given your servant.”" 
Genesis33:6 Then the maidservants came forward with their children and bowed low before Esau. 
Genesis33:7 Leah likewise came forward with her children and bowed down. Finally, Rachel and Joseph came forward and they bowed down to the ground before Esau." 
Genesis33:8 Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send all these animals to me?” “I was hoping to find favor with you, my lord,” Jacob replied." 
Genesis33:9 “But my brother,” Esau laughed, “I have plenty. Keep what you have for yourself.”" 
Genesis33:10 Jacob replied, “No, please. If I have found favor in your eyes, please take the gifts. For truly, seeing your face after all these years, it’s like looking upon the face of God! Since you have received me so warmly," 
Genesis33:11 please accept the blessing I have brought to you, for God has poured his grace over me, and I have everything I want!” So with these words Jacob urged him, and Esau accepted the gifts." 
Genesis33:12 Then Esau said, “Let’s start out on our journey, and I’ll walk alongside you.”" 
Genesis33:13 But Jacob replied, “My lord, you can see how tired the children are, and all our flocks and herds are nursing their young. If we drive them too hard for even a day, they will die." 
Genesis33:14 Please, my lord, go ahead of your servant. Let me move along slowly with my flocks and herds, to give them a chance to graze. I must think of my children, too, so go ahead of me, my lord, until I catch up with you in Seir.”" 
Genesis33:15 Esau said, “Well, at least let me leave you with some of my men.” “You’re so kind,” Jacob replied, “but there’s no need to do so.”" 
Genesis33:16 So that very day Esau and his men left to return to Seir," 
Genesis33:17 but Jacob went the opposite direction to Succoth instead, and built himself a house there. He also built shelters for his livestock, and that is why Jacob named the place Succoth." 
Genesis33:18 Jacob’s journey home from Paddan-Aram finally brought him safe and sound to the Canaanite city of Shechem, where he camped just outside of the city." 
Genesis33:19 He purchased the field where he pitched his tent from the clan of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for one hundred pieces of silver." 
Genesis33:20 There he set up an altar and named it To the True God, the God of Israel." 
Genesis34:1 One day, Jacob and Leah’s daughter, Dinah, went out to visit the neighborhood girls." 
Genesis34:2 Now Hamor, the tribal chief of the Hivites, had a son named Shechem, who was the prince. When he saw how attractive Dinah was, he seized her and sexually assaulted her." 
Genesis34:3 His heart was drawn to Dinah, Jacob’s daughter, so he sought to win her affection and spoke tenderly to her." 
Genesis34:4 Shechem went to his father Hamor and said, “Get me this girl to be my wife.”" 
Genesis34:5 Now Jacob heard that Shechem had violated his daughter Dinah; but his sons were in the fields with his livestock, so he waited to act until his sons returned home." 
Genesis34:6 Shechem’s father Hamor brought his son to speak with Jacob about marriage arrangements. 
Genesis34:7 During their conversation, Jacob’s sons came in from the field, and they heard the report of what Shechem had done to their sister Dinah. They were shocked and furious over the shameful act Shechem committed against Israel —a criminal act of sexual assault that never should have happened." 
Genesis34:8 But Hamor tried to defuse the situation, saying, “Listen, my son Shechem is in love with your daughter. Please, give her to him in marriage." 
Genesis34:9 Let’s allow our people to intermarry. Let your daughters marry our sons, and we will let our daughters marry your sons." 
Genesis34:10 You may settle here among us, live anywhere you wish, move about freely, and acquire property.”" 
Genesis34:11 Then Shechem spoke up and said to Dinah’s family, “Please, let me find favor in your eyes. Whatever you require of me, I’ll pay." 
Genesis34:12 Set the bride-price as high as you wish, no matter how expensive it is; I’ll pay whatever you ask. Only give me the young woman to be my wife.”" 
Genesis34:13 The sons of Jacob concocted a devious plan. Because of the rape of their sister Dinah, they answered Shechem and Hamor deceitfully," 
Genesis34:14 saying, “Our culture will not allow us to consent to our sister Dinah marrying someone who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us." 
Genesis34:15 We would only approve of the marriage on one condition: you must circumcise every male among you and become as we are. 
Genesis34:16 Then we will allow our daughters to marry your sons and your daughters to marry our sons. And we will live among you and become one people. 
Genesis34:17 But if you refuse to be circumcised, then we will take our sister and be gone.”" 
Genesis34:18 The offer pleased Hamor and his son Shechem. 
Genesis34:19 And the young man lost no time in doing what they asked, because Shechem really wanted to marry Jacob’s daughter! Now Shechem was the most honored in all his family," 
Genesis34:20 so Hamor and his son went to the gate of the city and spoke to the men, saying," 
Genesis34:21 “These people are our friends. Let them settle in our land and make themselves at home; there’s plenty of room in the country for them. And, let’s agree to exchange our daughters in marriage to them and to unite us as one people." 
Genesis34:22 They only have one condition in order to live among us and to unite with us as one people: every male among us must be circumcised as they are circumcised. 
Genesis34:23 Won’t all of their livestock and property become ours? Let us agree to their request, so they will live among us.”" 
Genesis34:24 So all the townsmen agreed with Hamor and his son Shechem, and every male of the city was circumcised." 
Genesis34:25 Three days later, while all the men were still suffering in pain, two of Jacob’s sons, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, took their swords and attacked the unsuspecting city. They slaughtered every male of the city." 
Genesis34:26 They even killed Hamor and his son Shechem, and took Dinah from Shechem’s house, and left." 
Genesis34:27 Later, Jacob’s other sons came upon the dead bodies, and they looted the city as retribution for the violation of their sister." 
Genesis34:28 They seized the flocks, herds, and donkeys, and all that was in the city and in the field." 
Genesis34:29 They plundered all their wealth and their household goods and took their wives and children as captives. 
Genesis34:30 When Jacob found out, he said to Simeon and Levi, “ What have you done? You have ruined my name, and now the Canaanites and Perizzites and all the people of this land will hate me! My men are so few that if they unite against me and attack, I and all my household will be destroyed!”" 
Genesis34:31 But they answered him, “Why should they treat our sister like a whore?”" 
Genesis35:1 God said to Jacob, “Arise, go at once to Bethel, and settle there. Build an altar there to God, who appeared to you when you were fleeing from your brother Esau.”" 
Genesis35:2 So Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, “Get rid of every foreign god you have, purify yourselves, and change your clothes." 
Genesis35:3 Then come with me; let us go up to Bethel. I will build an altar there to God who answered my prayer when I was in distress and whose presence has been with me wherever I have gone.” 
Genesis35:4 Then they surrendered all the foreign gods they had as well as their earrings. Jacob buried them under the oak tree near Shechem. 
Genesis35:5 As they made their way to Bethel, a tremendous fear of God fell upon all the cities around them, and no one dared pursue them." 
Genesis35:6 Jacob and all the people who were with him arrived in the land of Canaan at Luz, now known as Bethel." 
Genesis35:7 He built an altar there and named it El-Bethel, because it was the place that God had unveiled himself when Jacob was running from his brother." 
Genesis35:8 During that time, Rebekah’s nurse Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree near Bethel, and they named the place Weeping Oak." 
Genesis35:9 After Jacob returned from Paddan-Aram, God appeared to him once again and blessed him," 
Genesis35:10 saying, “Your name was once Jacob, but no longer. Your new name is Israel!” God named him Israel" 
Genesis35:11 and said to him, “I am the God who is more than enough. Go and have many children, and they will multiply. A nation and a gathering of many nations will come from you; and you will be the ancestor of kings." 
Genesis35:12 I will give you the land which I gave to Abraham and to Isaac, and after you are gone, to your descendants.”" 
Genesis35:13 Then God ascended into heaven from the place where he had spoken to him. 
Genesis35:14 Jacob set up a stone pillar to memorialize the place where he had met with God. He poured over it a drink offering and anointed the pillar with oil. 
Genesis35:15 Jacob called the place where God had spoken with him Bethel. 
Genesis35:16 From Bethel, they journeyed on, and as they were approaching Ephrath, Rachel went into very hard and painful labor." 
Genesis35:17 As she was having great difficulty in giving birth, the midwife said to her, “Don’t be afraid, for you’re having another son!”" 
Genesis35:18 With her dying breath, Rachel said, “His name is Son of My Sorrow,” but his father called him Son of My Right Hand." 
Genesis35:19 Rachel died and was buried on the road to Ephrath (now Bethlehem). 
Genesis35:20 Jacob set up a pillar to mark her burial site, and it is known as The Marker of Rachel’s Tomb to this day." 
Genesis35:21 Israel journeyed on and pitched his tent beyond Migdal Eder. 
Genesis35:22 While Israel was living in that land, Reuben went and slept with Bilhah, his father’s concubine, and Israel found out. Jacob had twelve sons." 
Genesis35:23 Leah’s sons were Reuben (Jacob’s firstborn), Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun." 
Genesis35:24 Rachel’s sons were Joseph and Benjamin. 
Genesis35:25 The sons of Rachel’s maidservant Bilhah were Dan and Naphtali. 
Genesis35:26 The sons of Leah’s maidservant Zilpah were Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Jacob born to him in Paddan-Aram. 
Genesis35:27 Jacob came home to his father Isaac in Mamre, near Kiriath Arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had lived as foreigners." 
Genesis35:28 Isaac was one hundred and eighty when he breathed his last and died. 
Genesis35:29 He died an old man and had lived a full life when he joined his ancestors. And his sons Esau and Jacob buried him there. 
Genesis36:1 Here are the descendants of Esau, also known as Edom:" 
Genesis36:2 Esau chose three wives from among Canaanite women: Adah (daughter of Elon the Hittite), Oholibamah (daughter of Anah son of Zibeon the Hivite), and Basemath (Ishmael’s daughter, sister of Nebaioth)." 
Genesis36:3  
Genesis36:4 Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau; Basemath bore Reuel; 
Genesis36:5 and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are the sons who were born to Esau in the land of Canaan." 
Genesis36:6 Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and his entire household, and the cattle, livestock, and possessions he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and migrated to the land of Seir some distance from his brother Jacob." 
Genesis36:7 Since the land where Jacob was living could not support their combined possessions and livestock," 
Genesis36:8 Esau (also known as Edom), settled in the hill country of Seir." 
Genesis36:9 This is the account of Esau, ancestor of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir:" 
Genesis36:10 Esau’s wife Adah bore him one son Eliphaz, and Eliphaz had five sons: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz. Eliphaz also had a son with his concubine Timna, named Amelek. Esau’s wife Basemath bore him one son Reuel, and Reuel had four sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah." 
Genesis36:11  
Genesis36:12  
Genesis36:13  
Genesis36:14 Esau’s wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon, bore Esau three sons: Jeush, Jalam, and Korah." 
Genesis36:15 These are the tribes descended from Esau’s firstborn son Eliphaz whom Adah bore, and who lived in Edom: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz, Korah, Gatam, and Amalek." 
Genesis36:16  
Genesis36:17 These are the tribes descended from Esau and Basemath’s son Reuel, who lived in Edom: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah." 
Genesis36:18 These are the tribes descended from Esau’s wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah: Jeush, Jalam, and Korah." 
Genesis36:19 This concludes the list of the descendants of Esau (also known as Edom) and their respective tribes. 
Genesis36:20 These are the tribes descended from Seir the Horite, of the original inhabitants of Edom: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. All of these were the descendants of Seir." 
Genesis36:21  
Genesis36:22 Lotan’s sons were Hori and Heman, and Lotan’s sister was Timna." 
Genesis36:23 Shobal’s sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam." 
Genesis36:24 Zibeon’s sons were Aiah and Anah. This is the Anah who discovered the springs in the wilderness while he was grazing the donkeys of his father Zibeon. 
Genesis36:25 Anah’s son was Dishon, and Anah’s daughter was Oholibamah." 
Genesis36:26 Dishon’s sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Keran." 
Genesis36:27 Ezer’s sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan." 
Genesis36:28 Dishan’s sons were Uz and Aran. 
Genesis36:29 This concludes the list of the Horite tribes in the land of Seir: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan." 
Genesis36:30  
Genesis36:31 Long before the Israelites had kings, there were eight kings who reigned in Edom," 
Genesis36:32 beginning with Bela son of Beor from the city of Dinhabah. 
Genesis36:33 When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from the city of Bozrah succeeded." 
Genesis36:33 When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from the city of Bozrah succeeded." 
Genesis36:34 When Jobab died, Husham from the land of Teman succeeded him." 
Genesis36:34 When Jobab died, Husham from the land of Teman succeeded him." 
Genesis36:35 When Husham died, Hadad from the city of Avith, who was the son of Bedad, succeeded him. Hadad defeated the Midianites in the land of Moab." 
Genesis36:36 When Hadad died, Samlah of Masrekah succeeded him." 
Genesis36:37 When Samlah died, Shaul of Rehoboth-on-the-River succeeded him." 
Genesis36:38 When Shaul died, Baal-Hanan son of Achbor succeeded him." 
Genesis36:39 When Baal-Hanan son of Achbor died, Hadar from the city of Pau succeeded him. (His wife was Mehetabel the daughter of Matred, Me-Zahab’s granddaughter.)" 
Genesis36:40 These are the tribes of Esau according to their clans and regions: Timna, Alvah, Jetheth, Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon, Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar," 
Genesis36:41  
Genesis36:42  
Genesis36:43 Magdiel, and Iram. This concludes the list of the Edomite tribes who settled and possessed land in different regions of their country. All the Edomites descended from Esau." 
Genesis37:1 This is the story of the family of Jacob, who had settled in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived as an immigrant. Jacob’s son Joseph was seventeen, and he served his older half brothers, the sons of his father’s wives Bilhah and Zilpah, helping them watch over the flocks. One day Joseph went to his father with a bad report about their behavior." 
Genesis37:2  
Genesis37:3 Now Israel’s love for Joseph surpassed that for his other sons because he was born to him in his old age. So Israel had made him a richly ornamented robe. 
Genesis37:4 When Joseph’s brothers saw that their father loved him more than he loved them, they hated him and would not speak a kind word to him." 
Genesis37:5 One night Joseph had a dream, and when he shared it with his brothers, they hated him even more!" 
Genesis37:6 “Listen to this dream I had,” he told them." 
Genesis37:7 “There we were, binding sheaves of grain in the field. Suddenly, my sheaf rose up and stood upright. Then your sheaves gathered around mine and bowed down to mine!”" 
Genesis37:8 His brothers asked him, “Oh, so you think you’re going to be our king? Do you actually think you’re destined to rule over us?” So, the dream that he told them about made them hate him even more." 
Genesis37:9 Then another night he had a dream, and he shared it with his brothers, saying, “Listen, I had another dream. This time, the sun and moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”" 
Genesis37:10 When his father and brothers heard it, his father scolded him, “What kind of dream is that? Do you really think that I, and your mother, and your brothers are going to come and bow to the ground before you?”" 
Genesis37:11 So his brothers grew more jealous of him, but his father kept pondering Joseph’s dream." 
Genesis37:12 One day, when his brothers had gone to Shechem to care for their father’s flock," 
Genesis37:13 Israel called for Joseph and said to him, “Your brothers are grazing the flocks near Shechem. I want you to go join them.” “ Yes, Father, I’ll go.” Joseph replied." 
Genesis37:14 Jacob added, “Go find out how your brothers are doing with the flocks and bring word back to me.” So, his father sent him off from the valley of Hebron. When Joseph arrived at Shechem" 
Genesis37:15 and began to roam the countryside looking for his brothers, a man approached him and asked, “What are you looking for?”" 
Genesis37:16 “I’m looking for my brothers, who are taking care of their flock,” he answered. “Please tell me, do you know where they are?”" 
Genesis37:17 The man replied, “They’ve left here already. I overheard them mention that they were going to Dothan.” So, Joseph took off to catch up with his brothers and found them at Dothan." 
Genesis37:18 As he was still a long distance away, the brothers recognized him by his robe, and by the time he reached them, they had plotted together to kill him." 
Genesis37:19 They said to each other, “Here comes this dream expert." 
Genesis37:20 Let’s kill him and throw his body into one of these dry wells. We can say that a wild animal ate him. Then we’ll see how his dreams turn out!” 
Genesis37:21 When Reuben heard of this, he tried to save Joseph’s life. “Don’t take his life,” he said." 
Genesis37:22 “No bloodshed! Let’s throw him into this pit in the middle of nowhere, but don’t hurt him.” Reuben said these things because he planned to return later to rescue Joseph and take him back to his father." 
Genesis37:23 When Joseph finally caught up with his brothers, they seized him, stripped him of his ornamented robe, his beautiful full-length robe," 
Genesis37:24 and threw him into the dry, empty pit." 
Genesis37:25 Afterward, the brothers sat down to eat their food. When they looked up, they saw a caravan of Ishmaelite merchants coming from Gilead on their way to Egypt. They had many camels loaded with myrrh, spices, and perfumes." 
Genesis37:26 Judah spoke up and said to his brothers, “What will we gain by murdering our brother and covering up his blood?" 
Genesis37:27 I have an idea! Let’s sell Joseph to these Ishmaelites and not lay a hand on him, for he is our brother, our own flesh and blood.” His brothers agreed." 
Genesis37:28 And when the Midianites (also known as Ishmaelites) came by, Joseph’s brothers lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and the merchants took Joseph far away to Egypt." 
Genesis37:29 Later, Reuben went to the pit, and saw that Joseph was gone. He was overcome with grief and tore his clothes." 
Genesis37:30 He went to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do now?”" 
Genesis37:31 Then they took Joseph’s colorful robe, killed a goat, and dipped the robe in its blood." 
Genesis37:32 They took the blood-stained robe back to their father and said, “We found this. Look it over—doesn’t it belong to your son?”" 
Genesis37:33 Jacob recognized it instantly and cried out, “It’s my son’s robe! Some wild animal must have killed him. My son Joseph has been torn to pieces!”" 
Genesis37:34 Overcome with grief, Jacob tore his clothes, put on sackcloth, and mourned for his son a long time." 
Genesis37:35 All his sons and daughters came and tried to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. He told them, “No, I will mourn for him the rest of my life, until I join my son in the realm of the dead.” Joseph’s father wept and wept for his son." 
Genesis37:36 Meanwhile, the Midianites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, one of Pharaoh’s officials, the captain of the guard." 
Genesis38:1 Around that time, Judah left his brothers at Hebron and went to Adullam to stay with a man named Hirah." 
Genesis38:2 There he met and married a Canaanite girl, the daughter of Shua. He slept with her and" 
Genesis38:3 she became pregnant and gave birth to a son, and Judah named him Er." 
Genesis38:4 She conceived again and gave birth to another son and named him Onan. 
Genesis38:5 While they were staying in Chezib, she gave birth to a third son and named him Shelah." 
Genesis38:6 When their sons were grown, Judah arranged for Er, his oldest son, to marry a girl named Tamar." 
Genesis38:7 But Er had become so wicked in the sight of Yahweh that Yahweh ended his life. 
Genesis38:8 Then Judah said to Er’s brother Onan, “Your duty is to join yourself to her. Go perform your duty as a brother-in-law and provide an heir for your brother.”" 
Genesis38:9 Onan, however, did not want to produce a child that would not be his own rightful heir, so whenever he and Tamar had intercourse, he purposely spilled his semen onto the ground to keep her from getting pregnant and having a child that would belong to his dead brother." 
Genesis38:10 But what he did was wicked in Yahweh ’s sight, so he took Onan’s life also." 
Genesis38:11 Then Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar, “Live as a widow in your father’s house until my youngest son Shelah grows up.” So Tamar went home to her parents, but Judah worried that Shelah would end up dead like his brothers." 
Genesis38:12 After some time passed, Judah’s wife, the daughter of Shua, died. When his time of mourning was over, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite went to Timnah to enjoy the festivities at the sheep-shearing." 
Genesis38:13 Meanwhile, Tamar had learned that Shelah had grown up, but Judah had still not given him to her to father a child for her deceased husband. So, when Tamar found out her father-in-law was coming to Timnah for the sheep-shearing, she removed her widow’s clothes and covered herself with a veil to disguise herself. And she sat waiting at the crossroads where Judah would have to pass by." 
Genesis38:14  
Genesis38:15 When Judah saw her, she was wearing a veil over her face, so he thought she was a prostitute. He had no clue that she was his own daughter-in-law. So he approached her and said, “Come sleep with me.” “What will you give me if I do?” she answered." 
Genesis38:16  
Genesis38:17 He responded, “I will send you a young goat from the flock.” “What guarantee can you give me that you will really send it?” she asked." 
Genesis38:18 “Well, what pledge do you want?” Judah asked. Tamar answered, “Give me your necklace with your personal signet and the staff you’re holding.” So, he gave them to her, and he went and slept with her. When she got up and went home, she removed her veil and put her widow’s garment back on. Later, she discovered she was pregnant." 
Genesis38:19  
Genesis38:20 Soon afterward, Judah sent the young goat by his friend Hirah the Adullamite to recover his items from the prostitute, but she was nowhere to be found." 
Genesis38:21 So he asked the townspeople, “Where is the temple prostitute that sits by the crossroads?” They answered, “No prostitute has been there.”" 
Genesis38:22 So he returned to Judah and informed him, “I couldn’t find her, and besides that, the townspeople said that no prostitute has been there.”" 
Genesis38:23 Judah replied, “Let her keep my pledge. If we try to go get it now, we’ll become a laughingstock. After all, I did send you with the payment I promised her, and you couldn’t find her.”" 
Genesis38:24 About three months later, Judah was told, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has been promiscuous, and now she is pregnant!” Indignant, Judah said, “Bring her out, and let her be burned!”"
