Psalms57:11 Lord God, be exalted as you soar throughout the heavens. May your shining glory be shown in the skies! Let it be seen high above all the earth!"

You only give “justice” in exchange for a bribe. For the right price you let others get away with murder. 
Psalms58:2  
Psalms58:3 Wicked wanderers even from the womb—that’s who you are! You lie with your words, and your teaching is poison." 
Psalms58:4  
Psalms58:5 Like cobras closing their ears to the most expert of the charmers, you strike out against all who are near." 
Psalms58:6 O God, break their fangs; shatter the teeth of these ravenous lions!" 
Psalms58:7 Let them disappear like water falling on thirsty ground. Let all their weapons be useless.

Psalms58:11 Then everyone will say, “There is a God who judges the judges” and “There is a great reward in loving God!”"

Psalms59:2 Save me from these murdering men, these bloodthirsty killers." 
Psalms59:3 See how they set an ambush for my life. They’re fierce men ready to launch their attack against me. O Lord, I’m innocent; protect me!" 
Psalms59:4 I’ve done nothing to deserve this, yet they are already plotting together to kill me. Arise, Lord, see what they’re scheming, and come and meet with me." 
Psalms59:5 Awaken, O God of Israel! Commander of Angel Armies, arise to punish these treacherous people who oppose you! Don’t go soft on these hard-core killers!"

Psalms59:7 boiling over with rage, shouting out their curses, convinced that they’ll never get caught." 
Psalms59:8 But you, Lord, break out laughing at their plans, amused by their arrogance, scoffing at their sinful ways." 
Psalms59:9 My strength is found when I wait upon you. Watch over me, God, for you are my mountain fortress; you set me on high!" 
Psalms59:10 The God of passionate love will meet with me. My God will empower me to rise in triumph over my foes. 
Psalms59:11 Don’t kill them; stagger them all with a vivid display of power and scatter them with your armies of angels, O mighty God, our protector! Use your awesome power to make them wanderers and vagabonds and then bring them down." 
Psalms59:12 They are nothing but proud, cursing liars. They sin in every word they speak, boasting in their blasphemies!" 
Psalms59:13 May your wrath be kindled to destroy them; finish them off! Make an end of them and their deeds until they are no more! Let them all know and learn that God is the ruler over Jacob, the God-King over all the earth!" 
Psalms59:14 Here they come again— prowling, growling like a pack of stray dogs in the city." 
Psalms59:15 Drifting, devouring, and coming in for the kill, they refuse to sleep until they’ve eaten their fill." 
Psalms59:16 But as for me, your strength shall be my song of joy. At each and every sunrise, my lyrics of your love will fill the air! For you have been my glory-fortress, a stronghold in my day of distress." 
Psalms59:17 O my strength, I sing with joy your praises. O my stronghold, I sing with joy your song! O my Savior, I sing with joy the lyrics of your faithful love for me!" 
Psalms60:1 God, it seems like you walked off and left us! Why have you turned against us? You have been angry with us. O Lord, we plead, come back and help us as a father." 
Psalms60:2 The earth quivers and quakes before you, splitting open and breaking apart. Now come and heal it, for it is shaken to its depths." 
Psalms60:3 You have taught us hard lessons and made us drink the wine of bewilderment. 
Psalms60:4 You have given miraculous signs to those who love you. As we follow you, we fly the flag of truth, and all who love the truth will rally to it." 
Psalms60:5 Come to your beloved ones and gently draw us out. For Lord, you save those whom you love. Come with your might and strength!" 
Psalms60:6 Then I heard the Lord speak in his holy splendor. From his sanctuary I heard the Lord promise: “In my triumph I will be the one to measure out the portion of my inheritance to my people, and I will secure the land as I promised you. Shechem, Succoth, Gilead, Manasseh, they are all still mine!” he says. “Judah will continue to produce kings and lawgivers, and Ephraim will produce great warriors." 
Psalms60:7  
Psalms60:8 Moab will become my lowly servant. Edom will likewise serve my purposes. I will lift up a shout of victory over the land of Philistia! 
Psalms60:9 But who will bring my triumph into the strong city? Who will lead me into Edom’s fortresses?” 
Psalms60:10 Have you really rejected us, refusing to fight our battles?"

Psalms60:12 With God’s help we will fight like heroes, and he will trample down our every foe!" 
Psalms61:1 O God, hear my prayer. Listen to my heart’s cry." 
Psalms61:2 For no matter where I am, even when I’m far from home, I will cry out to you for a father’s help. When I’m feeble and overwhelmed by life, guide me into your glory, where I am safe and sheltered." 
Psalms61:3 Lord, you are a paradise of protection to me. You lift me high above the fray. None of my foes can touch me when I’m held firmly in your wraparound presence!" 
Psalms61:4 Keep me in this glory. Let me live continually under your splendor-shadow, hiding my life in you forever." 
Psalms61:5 You have heard my sweet resolutions to love and serve you, for I am your beloved. And you have given me an inheritance of rich treasures, which you give to all your devoted lovers." 
Psalms61:6 You treat me like a king, giving me a full and abundant life, years and years of reigning, like many generations rolled into one." 
Psalms61:7 I will live enthroned with you forever! Guard me, God, with your unending, unfailing love. Let me live my days walking in grace and truth before you." 
Psalms61:8 And my praises will fill the heavens forever, fulfilling my vow to make every day a love-gift to you!" 
Psalms62:1 I stand silently to listen for the one I love, waiting as long as it takes for the Lord to rescue me. For God alone has become my Savior." 
Psalms62:2 He alone is my safe place; his wraparound presence always protects me. For he is my champion defender; there’s no risk of failure with God. So why would I let worry paralyze me, even when troubles multiply around me?" 
Psalms62:3 But look at these who want me dead, shouting their vicious threats at me! The moment they discover my weakness, they all begin plotting to take me down." 
Psalms62:4 Liars, hypocrites, with nothing good to say— all their energies are spent on moving me from this exalted place." 
Psalms62:5 I am standing in absolute stillness, silent before the one I love, waiting as long as it takes for him to rescue me. Only God is my Savior, and he will not fail me." 
Psalms62:6 For he alone is my safe place. His wraparound presence always protects me as my champion defender. There’s no risk of failure with God! So why would I let worry paralyze me, even when troubles multiply around me?" 
Psalms62:7 God’s glory is all around me! His wraparound presence is all I need, for the Lord is my Savior, my hero, and my life-giving strength." 
Psalms62:8 Trust only in God every moment! Tell him all your troubles and pour out your heart-longings to him. Believe me when I tell you—he will help you! 
Psalms62:9 Before God all the people of the earth, high or low, are like smoke that disappears, like a vapor that quickly vanishes away. Compared to God they’re nothing but vanity, nothing at all!" 
Psalms62:10 The wealth of the world is nothing to God. So if your wealth increases, don’t be boastful or put your trust in your money. And don’t you think for a moment that you can get away with stealing by overcharging others just to get more for yourself!" 
Psalms62:11 God said to me once and for all, “All the strength and power you need flows from me!” And again I heard it clearly said," 
Psalms62:12 “All the love you need is found in me!” And it’s true that you repay people for what they do. 
Psalms63:1 O God of my life, I’m lovesick for you in this weary wilderness. I thirst with the deepest longings to love you more, with cravings in my heart that can’t be described. Such yearning grips my soul for you, my God!" 
Psalms63:2 I’m energized every time I enter your heavenly sanctuary to seek more of your power and drink in more of your glory. 
Psalms63:3 For your tender mercies mean more to me than life itself. How I love and praise you, God!" 
Psalms63:4 Daily I will worship you passionately and with all my heart. My arms will wave to you like banners of praise. 
Psalms63:5 I overflow with praise when I come before you, for the anointing of your presence satisfies me like nothing else. You are such a rich banquet of pleasure to my soul." 
Psalms63:6 I lie awake each night thinking of you and reflecting on how you help me like a father. I sing through the night under your splendor-shadow, offering up to you my songs of delight and joy!" 
Psalms63:7  
Psalms63:8 With passion I pursue and cling to you. Because I feel your grip on my life, I keep my soul close to your heart." 
Psalms63:9 Those who plot to destroy me shall descend into the darkness of hell. 
Psalms63:10 They will be consumed by their own evil and become nothing more than dust under our feet. 
Psalms63:11 These liars will be silenced forever! But with the anointing of a king I will dance and rejoice along with all his devoted lovers who trust in him. 
Psalms64:1 Lord, can’t you hear my cry, my bitter complaint? Keep me safe from this band of criminals and from the conspiracy of these wicked men. They gather in their secret counsel to destroy me." 
Psalms64:2  
Psalms64:3 Can’t you hear their slander, their lies? Their words are like poison-tipped arrows shot from the shadows. They are unafraid and have no fear of consequences." 
Psalms64:4  
Psalms64:5 They persist with their evil plans and plot together to hide their traps. They boast, “No one can see us or stop us!”" 
Psalms64:6 They search out opportunities to pervert justice as they plan the “perfect crime.” How unsearchable is their endless evil! They try desperately to hide the deep darkness of their hearts. 
Psalms64:7 But all the while God has his own fire-tipped arrows! Suddenly, without warning, they will be pierced and struck down." 
Psalms64:8 Staggering backward, they will be destroyed by the very ones they spoke against. All who see this will view them with scorn." 
Psalms64:9 Then all will stand awestruck over what God has done, seeing how he vindicated the victims of these crimes." 
Psalms64:10 The lovers of God will be glad, rejoicing in the Lord. They will be found in his glorious wraparound presence, singing songs of praise to God!" 
Psalms65:1 O God in Zion, to you even silence is praise! You who answers prayer, all of humanity comes before you with their requests." 
Psalms65:2  
Psalms65:3 Though we are overcome by our many sins, your sacrifice covers over them all." 
Psalms65:4 How blessed is the one you choose to live near you in your courts. The beauty of your house, your holy temple, satisfies us." 
Psalms65:5 You answer our prayers with amazing wonders and with awe-inspiring displays of power. You are the righteous God who helps us like a father. Everyone everywhere looks to you, for you are the confidence of all the earth, even to the farthest islands of the sea." 
Psalms65:6 What jaw-dropping, astounding power is yours! You are the mountain maker who sets them all in place." 
Psalms65:7 You muzzle the roar of the mighty seas and the rage of mobs with their noisy riots. 
Psalms65:8 O God, to the farthest corners of the planet people will stand in awe, startled and stunned by your signs and wonders. Sunrise brilliance and sunset beauty both take turns singing their songs of joy to you." 
Psalms65:9 Your visitations of glory bless the earth; the rivers of God overflow and enrich it. You paint the wheat fields golden as you provide rich harvests. 
Psalms65:10 Every field is watered with the abundance of rain— showers soaking the earth and softening its clods, causing seeds to sprout throughout the land." 
Psalms65:11 You crown the earth with the fruits of your goodness. Wherever you go, the tracks of your chariot wheels drip with oil." 
Psalms65:12 Luxuriant green pastures boast of your bounty as you make every hillside blossom with joy. 
Psalms65:13 The grazing meadows are covered with flocks, and the fertile valleys are clothed with grain, each one dancing and shouting for joy, creation’s celebration! They’re all singing their songs of praise to you!" 
Psalms66:1 Everyone everywhere, lift up your joyful shout to God!" 
Psalms66:2 Sing your songs tuned to his glory! Tell the world how wonderful he is. 
Psalms66:3 For he’s the awe-inspiring God, great and glorious in power! We’ve never seen anything like him! Mighty in miracles, you cause your enemies to tremble. No wonder they all surrender and bow before you!" 
Psalms66:4 All the earth will bow down to worship; all the earth will sing your glories forever! 
Psalms66:5 Everyone will say, “Come and see the incredible things God has done; it will take your breath away! He multiplies miracles for his people!”" 
Psalms66:6 He made a highway going right through the Red Sea as the Hebrews passed through on dry ground, exploding with joyous excitement over the miracles of God." 
Psalms66:7 In his great and mighty power he rules forever, watching over every movement of every nation. So beware, rebel lands; he knows how to humble you!" 
Psalms66:8 Praise God, all you peoples. Praise him everywhere and let everyone know you love him!" 
Psalms66:9 There’s no doubt about it: God holds our lives safely in his hands. He’s the one who keeps us faithfully following him. 
Psalms66:10 O Lord, we have passed through your fire; like precious metal made pure, you’ve proved us, perfected us, and made us holy." 
Psalms66:11 You’ve captured us, ensnared us in your net. Then, like prisoners, you placed chains around our necks." 
Psalms66:12 You’ve allowed our enemies to prevail against us. We’ve passed through fire and flood, yet in the end you always bring us out better than we were before, saturated with your goodness." 
Psalms66:13 I come before your presence with my sacrifice. I’ll give you all that I’ve promised, everything I have." 
Psalms66:14 When I was overcome in my anguish, I promised to give you my sacrifice. Here it is! All that I said I would offer you is yours." 
Psalms66:15 I’ll throw it all—the best I have to bring—into the fire as the fragrance of my sacrifice ascends unto you. 
Psalms66:16 All you lovers of God who want to please him, come and listen, and I’ll tell you what he did for me." 
Psalms66:17 I cried aloud to him with all my heart, and he answered me! Now my mouth overflows with the highest praise." 
Psalms66:18 Yet if I had closed my eyes to my sin, the Lord God would have closed his ears to my prayer." 
Psalms66:19 But praises rise to God, for he paid attention to my prayer and answered my cry to him!" 
Psalms66:20 I will forever praise this God who didn’t close his heart when I prayed and never said no when I asked him for help. He never once refused to show me his tender love. 
Psalms67:1 God, keep us near your mercy-fountain and bless us! And when you look down on us, may your face beam with joy!" 
Psalms67:2 Send us out all over the world so that everyone everywhere will discover your ways and know who you are and see your power to save. 
Psalms67:3 Let all the nations burst forth with praise; let everyone everywhere love and enjoy you! 
Psalms67:4 Then how glad the nations will be when you are their King. They will sing, they will shout, for you give true justice to the people. Yes! You, Lord, are the shepherd of the nations!" 
Psalms67:5 No wonder the peoples praise you! Let all the people praise you more! 
Psalms67:6 The harvest of the earth is here! God, the very God we worship, keeps us satisfied at his banquet of blessings." 
Psalms67:7 And the blessings keep coming! All the ends of the earth will give him the honor he deserves and be in awe of him! 
Psalms68:1 God! Arise with awesome power, and every one of your enemies will scatter in fear!" 
Psalms68:2 Chase them away—all these God-haters. Blow them away as a puff of smoke. Melt them away like wax in the fire. One good look at you and the wicked vanish. 
Psalms68:3 But let all the righteous be glad! Yes, let them all rejoice in your presence and be carried away with gladness. Let them laugh and be radiant with joy!" 
Psalms68:4 Let them sing their celebration-songs for the coming of the cloud rider whose name is Yah! 
Psalms68:5 To the fatherless he is a father. To the widow he is a champion friend. The lonely he makes part of a family. The prisoners he leads into prosperity until they sing for joy. This is our Holy God in his Holy Place! But for the rebels there is heartache and despair. 
Psalms68:6  
Psalms68:7 O Lord, it was you who marched in front of your people, leading them through the wasteland." 
Psalms68:8 The earth shook beneath your feet; the heavens filled with clouds before the presence of the God of Sinai. The sacred mountain shook at the sight of the face of Israel’s God. 
Psalms68:9 You, O God, sent the reviving rain upon your weary inheritance, showers of blessing to refresh it." 
Psalms68:10 So there your people settled. And in your kindness you provided the poor with abundance. 
Psalms68:11 God Almighty declares the word of the gospel with power, and the warring women of Zion deliver its message:" 
Psalms68:12 “The conquering legions have themselves been conquered. Look at them flee!” Now Zion’s women are left to gather the spoils. 
Psalms68:13 When you sleep between sharpened stakes, I see you sparkling like silver and glistening like gold, covered by the beautiful wings of a dove!" 
Psalms68:14 When the Almighty found a king for himself, it became white as snow in his shade." 
Psalms68:15 O huge, magnificent mountain, you are the mighty kingdom of God! All the other peaks, though impressive and imposing, look with envy on you, Mount Zion! For Zion is the mountain where God has chosen to live forever." 
Psalms68:16  
Psalms68:17 Look! The mighty chariots of God! Ten thousands upon ten thousands, more than anyone could ever number. God is at the front, leading them all from Mount Sinai into his sanctuary with the radiance of holiness upon him." 
Psalms68:18 He ascends into the heavenly heights, taking his many captured ones with him, leading them in triumphal procession. And gifts were given to men, even the once rebellious, so that they may dwell with Yah." 
Psalms68:19 What a glorious God! He gives us salvation over and over, then daily he carries our burdens!" 
Psalms68:20 Our God is a mighty God who saves us over and over! For the Lord, Yahweh, rescues us from the ways of death many times." 
Psalms68:21 But he will crush every enemy, shattering their strength. He will make heads roll for they refuse to repent of their stubborn, sinful ways." 
Psalms68:22 I hear the Lord God saying to all the enemies of his people, “You’d better come out of your hiding places, all of you who are doing your best to stay far away from me. Don’t you know there’s no place to hide?" 
Psalms68:23 For my people will be the conquerors; they will soon have you under their feet. They will crush you until there is nothing left!” 
Psalms68:24 O God, my King, your triumphal processions keep moving onward in holiness; you’re moving onward toward the Holy Place!" 
Psalms68:25 Leaders in front, then musicians, with young maidens in between, striking their tambourines." 
Psalms68:26 And they sing, “Let all God’s princely people rejoice! Let all the congregations bring their blessing to God, saying, ‘The Lord of the fountain! The Lord of the fountain of life! The Lord of the fountain of Israel!’ ”" 
Psalms68:27 Astonishingly, it’s the favored youth leading the way: princes of praise in their royal robes and exalted princes are among them, along with princes who have wrestled with God." 
Psalms68:28 Display your strength, God, and we’ll be strong! For your miracles have made us who we are. Lord, do it again and parade from your temple your mighty power. By your command even kings will bring gifts to you." 
Psalms68:29  
Psalms68:30 God, rebuke the beast-life that hides within us! Rebuke those who claim to be “strong ones,” who lurk within the congregation and abuse the people out of their love for money. God scatters the people who are spoiling for a fight." 
Psalms68:31 Africa will send her noble envoys to you, O God. They will come running, stretching out their hands in love to you." 
Psalms68:32 Let all the nations of the earth sing songs of praise to Almighty God! Go ahead, all you nations—sing your praise to the Lord!" 
Psalms68:33 Make music for the one who strides the ancient skies. Listen to his thunderous voice of might split open the heavens. 
Psalms68:34 Give it up for God, for he alone has all the strength and power! Proclaim his majesty! For his glory shines down on Israel. His mighty strength soars in the clouds of glory." 
Psalms68:35 God, we are consumed with awe, trembling before you as your glory streams from your Holy Place. The God of power shares his mighty strength with Israel and with all his people. God, we give our highest praise to you!" 
Psalms69:1 God, my God, come and save me! These floods of trouble have risen higher and higher. The water is up to my neck! I’m sinking into the mud with no place to stand, and I’m about to drown in this storm." 
Psalms69:2  
Psalms69:3 I’m weary, exhausted with weeping. My throat is dry, my voice is gone, my eyes are swollen with sorrow, and I’m waiting for you, God, to come through for me." 
Psalms69:4 I can’t even count all those who hate me for no reason. Many influential men want me silenced, yet I’ve done nothing against them. Must I restore what I never took away?" 
Psalms69:5 God, my life is an open book to you. You know every sin I’ve ever done. For nothing within me is hidden from your sight!" 
Psalms69:6 Lord Yahweh of Angel Armies, keep me from ever being a stumbling block to others, to those who love you. Lord God of Israel, don’t let what happens to me be the source of confusion to those who are passionate for you." 
Psalms69:7 Because of my love for you, Lord, I have been mocked, cursed, and disgraced." 
Psalms69:8 Even my own brothers, those of my family, act as though they don’t want anything to do with me." 
Psalms69:9 My love for you has my heart on fire! My passion for your house consumes me! Nothing will turn me away, even though I endure all the insults of those who insult you." 
Psalms69:10 When they see me seeking for more of you with weeping and fasting, they all just scoff and scorn at my passion." 
Psalms69:11 When I humble myself with sorrow over my sin, it gives them a reason to mock me even more." 
Psalms69:12 The leaders, the influential ones—how they scorn my passion for you! I’ve become the talk of the town, the theme of drunkards’ songs." 
Psalms69:13 But I keep calling out to you, Yahweh! I know you will bend down to listen to me, for now is the season of favor. Because of your faithful love for me, your answer to my prayer will be my sure salvation." 
Psalms69:14 Pull me out of this mess! Don’t let me sink! Rescue me from those who hate me and from all this trouble I’m in! 
Psalms69:15 Don’t let this flood drown me. Save me from these deep waters or I’ll go down to the pit of destruction. 
Psalms69:16 Oh, Lord God, answer my prayers! I need to see your tender kindness, your grace, your compassion, and your constant love. Just let me see your face, and turn your heart toward me. Come running quickly to your servant. In this deep distress, come and answer my prayer." 
Psalms69:17  
Psalms69:18 Come closer as a friend and redeem me. Set me free so my enemies cannot say that you are powerless. 
Psalms69:19 See how they dishonor me in shame and disgrace? You know, Lord, what I’m going through, and you see it all." 
Psalms69:20 I’m heartsick and heartbroken by it all. Their contempt has crushed my soul. I looked for sympathy and compassion but found only empty stares. 
Psalms69:21 I was hungry and they gave me bitter food. I was thirsty and they offered me vinegar. 
Psalms69:22 Let their “feasts” turn to ashes. Let their “peace and security” become their downfall. 
Psalms69:23 Make them blind as bats, groping in the dark. Let them be feeble, trembling continually." 
Psalms69:24 Pour out your fury on them all! Consume them with the fire of your anger! Burn down the walled palace where they live! Leave them homeless and desolate! 
Psalms69:25  
Psalms69:26 For they come against the one you yourself have struck, and they scorn the pain of those you’ve pierced." 
Psalms69:27 Pile on them the guilt of their sins. Don’t let them ever go free. 
Psalms69:28 Leave them out of your list of the living! Blot them out of your Book of Life! Never name them as your own! 
Psalms69:29 I am burdened and broken by this pain. When your miracle rescue comes to me, it will lift me to the highest place." 
Psalms69:30 Then my song will be a burst of praise to you. My glory-shouts will make your fame even more glorious to all who hear my praises! 
Psalms69:31 For I know, Yahweh, that my praises mean more to you than all my gifts and sacrifices." 
Psalms69:32 All who seek you will see God do this for them, and they’ll overflow with gladness. Let this revive your hearts, all you lovers of God!" 
Psalms69:33 For Yahweh does listen to the poor and needy and will not abandon his prisoners of love. 
Psalms69:34 Let all the universe praise him! The high heavens and everyone on earth, praise him! Let the oceans deep, with everything in them, keep it up!" 
Psalms69:35 God will come to save his Zion-people. God will build up his cities of Judah, for there his people will live in peace." 
Psalms69:36 All their children will inherit the land, and the lovers of his name will live there safe and secure." 
Psalms70:1 Please, Lord! Come quickly and rescue me! God, show me your favor and restore me." 
Psalms70:2 Let all who seek my life be humiliated and confused. God, send them sprawling, all who wish me evil; they just want me dead." 
Psalms70:3 Scoff at every scoffer and cause them all to be utter failures! Let them be ashamed and horrified over their complete defeat. 
Psalms70:4 But let all who passionately seek you erupt with excitement and joy over what you’ve done! Let all your devoted lovers, who continually rejoice in the Savior, say aloud, “How great and glorious is our God!”" 
Psalms70:5 Lord, in my place of weakness and need, won’t you turn your heart toward me and hurry to help me? For you are my Savior, and I’m always in your thoughts. So don’t delay to deliver me now, for you are my God." 
Psalms71:1 Lord, you are my secure shelter. Don’t ever let me down!" 
Psalms71:2 Let your justice be my breakthrough. Bend low to my whispered cry and save me from all my enemies! 
Psalms71:3 You’re the only place of protection for me. I keep coming back to hide myself in you, for you are like a mountain-cliff fortress where I’m kept safe." 
Psalms71:4 Let me escape from these cruel and wicked men, and save me from the hands of the evil one." 
Psalms71:5 For you are my only hope, Lord! I’ve hung on to you, trusting in you all my life." 
Psalms71:6 It was you who supported me from the day I was born, loving me, helping me through my life’s journey. You’ve made me into a miracle; no wonder I trust you and praise you forever! Many marvel at my success, but I know it is all because of you, my mighty protector!" 
Psalms71:7  
Psalms71:8 I’m overflowing with your praise for all you’ve done, and your splendor thrills me all day long." 
Psalms71:9 Now that I’m old, don’t set me aside. Don’t let go of me when my strength is spent." 
Psalms71:10 For all my enemies whisper behind my back. They’re waiting for me to fall so they can finish me off. They’re convinced you’ve left me and that you’ll never come to my rescue. They’re saying, “Let’s get him now! He has no savior!”" 
Psalms71:11  
Psalms71:12 O God, stay close to me! Don’t just watch from a distance! Hurry to help me, my God!" 
Psalms71:13 Cover these accusers of mine with shame and failure! Destroy them all, for they only want to kill me!" 
Psalms71:14 No matter what, I’ll trust in you to help me. Nothing will stop me from praising you to magnify your glory!" 
Psalms71:15 I couldn’t begin to count the times you’ve been there for me. With the skill of a poet I’ll never run out of things to say about how you faithfully kept me from danger. 
Psalms71:16 I will come forth in your mighty strength, O my Lord God. I’ll tell everyone that you alone are the perfect one." 
Psalms71:17 From my childhood you’ve been my teacher, and I’m still telling everyone of your miracle-wonders!" 
Psalms71:18 God, now that I’m old and gray, don’t walk away. Give me grace to demonstrate to the next generation all your mighty miracles and your excitement, to show them your magnificent power!" 
Psalms71:19 For your glorious righteousness reaches up to the high heavens. No one could ever be compared to you! Who is your equal, O God of marvels and wonders?" 
Psalms71:20 Even though you’ve let us sink down with trials and troubles, I know you will revive us again, lifting us up from the dust of death." 
Psalms71:21 Give us even more greatness than before. Turn and comfort us once again. 
Psalms71:22 My loving God, the harp in my heart will praise you. Your faithful heart toward us will be the theme of my song. Melodies and music will rise to you, the Holy One of Israel." 
Psalms71:23 I will shout and sing your praises for all you are to me— Savior, lover of my soul!" 
Psalms71:24 I’ll never stop telling others how perfect you are, while all those who seek my harm slink away ashamed and defeated!" 
Psalms72:1 O God, make the king a godly judge like you and give the king’s son the gift of justice too." 
Psalms72:2 Help him to give true justice to your people, honorably and equally to all." 
Psalms72:3 Then the mountains of influence will be fruitful, and from your righteousness prosperity and peace will flow to all the people." 
Psalms72:4 May the poor and humble have an advocate with the king. May he consider the children of the poor and crush the cruel oppressor. 
Psalms72:5 The sun and moon will stop shining before your devoted lovers will stop worshiping; for ages upon ages the people will love and adore you! 
Psalms72:6 Your favor will fall like rain upon our surrendered lives, like showers reviving the earth." 
Psalms72:7 In the days of his reign the righteous will spring forth with the abundance of peace and prosperity forevermore. 
Psalms72:8 May he subdue and take dominion from sea to sea; may he rule from the river to the rim. 
Psalms72:9 Desert nomads are bowing at his feet; every enemy is falling facedown, biting the dust!" 
Psalms72:10 Distant kings will surrender and come with their gifts from every continent and coastland; they will offer their tribute to you. 
Psalms72:11 O King of kings, they will all bow before you. O King of kings, every nation will one day serve you." 
Psalms72:12 He will care for the needy and neglected when they cry to him for help. The humble and helpless will know his kindness, for with a father’s compassion he will save their souls." 
Psalms72:13  
Psalms72:14 They will be rescued from tyranny and torture, for their lifeblood is precious in his eyes." 
Psalms72:15 Long live this King! May the wealth of the world be laid before him. May there be ceaseless praise and prayer to him. May all the blessing be brought to him. 
Psalms72:16 Bless us with a bountiful harvest, with golden grain swaying on the mountain fields! May the cities be full of praising people, fruitful and filled—" 
Psalms72:17 so that his name may be honored forever! May the fame of his name spring forth! May it shine on, like the sunshine! In him all will be blessed to bless others, and may all the people bless the One who blessed them." 
Psalms72:18 Praise Yahweh forever, the God of Israel! He is the one and only God of wonders, surpassing every expectation." 
Psalms72:19 The blazing glory of his name will be praised forever! May all the earth overflow with his glory! Faithful is our King! Amen! 
Psalms72:20 This concludes the poetry sung by David, Jesse’s son." 
Psalms73:1 No one can deny it—God is really good to Israel and to all those with pure hearts. But I nearly missed seeing it for myself. 
Psalms73:2 Here’s my story: I narrowly missed losing it all. 
Psalms73:3 I was stumbling over what I saw the wicked doing. For when I saw the boasters with such wealth and prosperity, I became jealous over their smug security." 
Psalms73:4 Indulging in whatever they wanted, going where they wanted, doing what they wanted, and with no care in the world, no pain, no problems—they seemed to have it made. They lived as though life would never end." 
Psalms73:5  
Psalms73:6 They didn’t even try to hide their pride and opulence. Cruelty and violence are parts of their lifestyle. 
Psalms73:7 Pampered and pompous, vice oozes from their souls; they overflow with vanity." 
Psalms73:8 They’re such snobs—looking down their noses. They even scoff at God! They are nothing but bullies threatening God’s people. 
Psalms73:9 They are loudmouths with no fear of God, pretending to know it all— windbags full of hot air, impressing only themselves." 
Psalms73:10 Yet the people keep coming back to listen to more of their nonsense. 
Psalms73:11 They tell their cohorts, “God will never know. See, he has no clue of what we’re doing.”" 
Psalms73:12 These are the wicked ones I’m talking about! They never have to lift a finger, living a life of ease while their riches multiply." 
Psalms73:13 Have I been foolish to play by the rules and keep my life pure? 
Psalms73:14 Here I am suffering under your discipline day after day. I feel like I’m being punished all day long. 
Psalms73:15 If I had given in to my pain and spoken of what I was really feeling, it would have sounded like unfaithfulness to the next generation." 
Psalms73:16 When I tried to understand it all, I just couldn’t. It was too puzzling—too much of a riddle to me." 
Psalms73:17 But then one day I was brought into the sanctuaries of God, and in the light of glory, my distorted perspective vanished. Then I understood that the destiny of the wicked was near!" 
Psalms73:18 They’re the ones who are on the slippery path, and God will suddenly let them slide off into destruction to be consumed with terrors forever!" 
Psalms73:19 It will be an instant end to all their life of ease; a blink of the eye and they’re swept away by sudden calamity! They’re all nothing more than momentary monarchs— 
Psalms73:20 soon to disappear like a dream when one awakes. When the rooster crows, Lord God, you’ll despise their life of fantasies." 
Psalms73:21 When I saw all of this, what turmoil filled my heart, piercing my opinions with your truth." 
Psalms73:22 I was so stupid. I was senseless and ignorant, acting like a brute beast before you, Lord." 
Psalms73:23 Yet, in spite of all this, I still belong to you; you hold me by my right hand." 
Psalms73:24 You lead me with your secret wisdom. And following you brings me into your brightness and glory! 
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but you? You’re all I want! No one on earth means as much to me as you. 
Psalms73:26 Lord, so many times I fail; I fall into disgrace. But when I trust in you, I have a strong and glorious presence protecting and anointing me. Forever you’re all I need!" 
Psalms73:27 Those who abandon the worship of God will perish. The false and unfaithful will be silenced, never heard from again." 
Psalms73:28 But I’ll keep coming closer and closer to you, Lord Yahweh, for your name is good to me. I’ll keep telling the world of your awesome works, my faithful and glorious God!" 
Psalms74:1 Are you really going to leave us, God? Would you turn your back on us, rejecting your people? We are yours, your very own. Will your anger smolder against us forever?" 
Psalms74:2 Don’t forget that we are your beloved ones. Wrap us back into your heart again, for you chose us. You brought us out of our slavery and bondage and made us your favored ones, your Zion-people, your home on earth." 
Psalms74:3 Turn your steps toward this devastation. Come running to bring your restoring grace to these ruins, to what the enemy has done to devastate your Holy Place." 
Psalms74:4 They have come into the very midst of your dwelling place, roaring like beasts, setting up their banners to flaunt their conquest." 
Psalms74:5 Now everything is in shambles! They’ve totally destroyed it. Like a forest chopped down to the ground, there’s nothing left." 
Psalms74:6 All of the beauty of the craftsmanship of the inner place has been ruined, smashed, broken, and shattered." 
Psalms74:7 They’ve burned it all to the ground. They’ve violated your sanctuary, the very dwelling place of your glory and your name." 
Psalms74:8 They boasted, “Let’s completely crush them! Let’s wipe out every trace of this God. Let’s burn up every sacred place where they worship this God.”" 
Psalms74:9 We don’t see any miraculous signs anymore. There’s no longer a prophet among us who can tell us how long this devastation will continue. 
Psalms74:10 God, how much longer will you let this go on and allow these barbarians to blaspheme your name? Will you stand back and watch them get away with this forever?" 
Psalms74:11 Why don’t you do something? You have the power to break in, so why would you hide your great power from us? Don’t hold back! Unleash your might and give them a final blow." 
Psalms74:12 You have always been, and always will be, my King. You are the mighty conqueror, working wonders all over the world." 
Psalms74:13 It was you who split the sea in two by your glorious strength. You smashed the power of Tannin, the sea monster." 
Psalms74:14 You crushed the might of Leviathan, the great dragon, then you took the crumbs and fed them to the sharks." 
Psalms74:15 With your glory you opened up springs and fountains, then you spoke, and the ever-flowing springs of Jordan dried up so we could cross over." 
Psalms74:16 You own the day and the night. Sunlight and starlight call you Creator. 
Psalms74:17 The four corners of the earth were formed by your hands, and every changing season owes its beauty to you." 
Psalms74:18 O  Yahweh, don’t ever forget how these arrogant enemies, like fools, have mocked your name." 
Psalms74:19 Lord, aren’t we your beloved dove that praises you? Protect us from these wild beasts who want to harm us. Don’t leave us as lambs among wolves! You can’t abandon us after all we’ve been through!" 
Psalms74:20 Remember your promises to us, for darkness covers the land, giving the violent ones a hiding place." 
Psalms74:21 Don’t let these insults continue. Can’t you see that we are your downtrodden and oppressed people? Make the poor and needy into a choir of praise to you! 
Psalms74:22 Don’t ignore these ignorant words, this continual mocking. Rise up, God; it’s time to defend yourself from all this." 
Psalms74:23 Never forget what your adversaries are saying. For their rage and uproar rise continually against you. It’s time to stand up to them! 
Psalms75:1 God, our hearts spill over with praise to you! We overflow with thanks, for your name is the “Near One.” All we want to talk about is your wonderful works! And we hear your reply:" 
Psalms75:2 “When the time is ripe I will arise, and I will judge the world with perfect righteousness." 
Psalms75:3 Though I have set the earth firmly on its pillars, I will shake it until it totters, and everyone’s hearts will tremble.”" 
Psalms75:4 God warns the proud, “Stop your arrogant boasting!” And he warns the wicked, “Don’t think for a moment you can resist me!" 
Psalms75:5 Why would you speak with such stubborn pride? Don’t you dare raise your fist against me!” 
Psalms75:6 This I know: the favor that brings promotion and power doesn’t come from anywhere on earth, for no one exalts a person but God, the true judge of all. He alone determines where favor rests. He anoints one for greatness and brings another down to his knees." 
Psalms75:7  
Psalms75:8 A foaming cup filled with judgment mixed with fury is in the hands of the Lord Yahweh, full to the brim and ready to run over. He filled it up for the wicked, and they will drink it down to the very last drop!" 
Psalms75:9 But I will proclaim the victory of the God of Jacob. My melodies of praise will make him known. 
Psalms75:10 My praises will break the powers of wickedness, while the righteous will be promoted and become powerful!" 
Psalms76:1 God is well known in the land of Judah. He is famous throughout Israel," 
Psalms76:2 making his home in Jerusalem, living here on Mount Zion." 
Psalms76:3 That’s where he smashes every weapon of war that comes against him. That’s where he uses the broken arrows as kindling for his mighty bonfire. 
Psalms76:4 God, you are so resplendent and radiant! Your majesty shines from your everlasting mountain. Nothing could be compared to you in glory!" 
Psalms76:5 Even the mightiest of men have been paralyzed by your presence. They were so stunned and lifeless, not even the strongest one could lift a hand." 
Psalms76:6 When Jacob’s God roared his rebuke, soldiers and their steeds all fell to the ground, stunned and lying still." 
Psalms76:7 No wonder you are greatly feared! You are the awe-inspiring God! For who could ever stand before your face when your fierce anger burns and live to tell about it. 
Psalms76:8 As the earth itself holds its breath in awe before you, judgment is decreed from heaven." 
Psalms76:9 You arise to punish evil and defend the gentle upon the earth. 
Psalms76:10 You have power to transform man’s futile anger into praise. The fury of your enemies only causes your fame to increase. 
Psalms76:11 So you’d better keep every promise you’ve ever made to the Awesome One, Yahweh! Let all people bring their extravagant gifts to him alone." 
Psalms76:12 He is famous for breaking the spirit of the powers that be. And the kings of the earth will know him as the Fearsome One! 
Psalms77:1 I poured out my complaint to you, God. I lifted up my voice, shouting out for your help." 
Psalms77:2 When I was in deep distress, in my day of trouble, I reached out for you with hands stretched out to heaven. Over and over I kept looking for you, God, but your comforting grace was nowhere to be found." 
Psalms77:3 As I thought of you I moaned, “God, where are you?” I’m overwhelmed with despair as I wait for your help to arrive." 
Psalms77:4 I can’t get a wink of sleep until you come and comfort me. Now I’m too burdened to even pray! 
Psalms77:5 My mind wandered, thinking of days gone by— the years long since passed." 
Psalms77:6 Then I remembered the worship songs I used to sing in the night seasons, and my heart began to fill again with thoughts of you. So my spirit went out once more in search of you." 
Psalms77:7 Would you really walk off and leave me forever, my Lord God? Won’t you show me your kind favor, delighting in me again?" 
Psalms77:8 Has your well of sweet mercy dried up? Will your promises never come true? 
Psalms77:9 Have you somehow forgotten to show me love? Are you so angry that you’ve closed your heart of compassion toward me? 
Psalms77:10 Lord, what wounds me most is that it’s somehow my fault that you’ve changed your heart toward me and I no longer see the years of the Mighty One or your right hand of power." 
Psalms77:11 Yet I could never forget all your miracles, my God, as I remember all your wonders of old." 
Psalms77:12 I ponder all you’ve done, Lord, musing on all your miracles." 
Psalms77:13 It’s here in your presence, in your sanctuary, where I learn more of your ways, for holiness is revealed in everything you do. Lord, you’re the one and only, the great and glorious God!" 
Psalms77:14 Your display of wonders, miracles, and power makes the nations acknowledge you." 
Psalms77:15 By your glory-bursts you’ve rescued us over and over. Just ask the sons of Jacob or the sons of Joseph, and they will tell you! And all of us, your beloved ones, know that it’s true!" 
Psalms77:16 When the many waters of the Red Sea took one look at you, they were afraid and ran away to hide— trembling to its depths!" 
Psalms77:17 Storm clouds filled with water high in the skies; cloudbursts and thunderclaps announced your approach. Lightning-flashes lit up the landscape. 
Psalms77:18 Rolling whirlwinds exploded with sonic booms of thunder, rumbling as the skies shouted out your story with light and sound and wind. Everything on earth shook and trembled as you drew near." 
Psalms77:19 Your steps formed a highway through the seas with footprints on a pathway no one even knew was there. 
Psalms77:20 You led your people forward by your loving hand, blessed by the leadership of Moses and Aaron." 
Psalms78:1 Beloved ones, listen to this instruction. Open your heart to the revelation of this mystery that I share with you." 
Psalms78:2 A parable and a proverb are hidden in what I say— an intriguing riddle from the past. 
Psalms78:3 We’ve heard true stories from our fathers about our rich heritage. We will continue to tell our children and not hide from the rising generation the great marvels of our God— his miracles and power that have brought us all this far. 
Psalms78:4  
Psalms78:5 The story of Israel is a lesson in God’s ways. He established decrees for Jacob and established the law in Israel, and he commanded our forefathers to teach them to their children." 
Psalms78:6 For perpetuity God’s ways will be passed down from one generation to the next, even to those not yet born." 
Psalms78:7 In this way, every generation will set its hope in God and not forget his wonderful works but keep his commandments." 
Psalms78:8 By following his ways they will break the past bondage of their fickle fathers, who were a stubborn, rebellious generation and whose spirits strayed from the eternal God. They refused to love him with all their hearts." 
Psalms78:9 Take, for example, the sons of Ephraim. Though they were all equipped warriors, each with weapons, when the battle began they retreated and ran away in fear." 
Psalms78:10 They didn’t really believe the promises of God; they refused to trust him and move forward in faith. 
Psalms78:11 They forgot his wonderful works and the miracles of the past," 
Psalms78:12 even their exodus from Egypt, the epic miracle of his might. They forgot the glories of his power at the place of passing over." 
Psalms78:13 God split the sea wide open, and the waters stood at attention on either side as the people passed on through!" 
Psalms78:14 By day the moving glory-cloud led them forward. And all through the night the fire-cloud stood as a sentry of light. 
Psalms78:15 In the days of desert dryness, he split open the mighty rock, and the waters flowed like a river before their very eyes. He gave them all they wanted to drink from his living springs." 
Psalms78:16  
Psalms78:17 Yet they kept their rebellion alive against God Most High, and their sins against God continued to be counted." 
Psalms78:18 In their hearts they tested God just to get what they wanted, asking for the food their hearts craved." 
Psalms78:19 Like spoiled children they grumbled against God, demanding he prove his love by saying, “Can’t God provide for us in this barren wilderness? Will he give us food, or will he only give us water? Where’s our meal?”" 
Psalms78:20  
Psalms78:21 Then God heard all their complaining and was furious! His anger flared up against his people. 
Psalms78:22 For they turned away from faith and walked away in fear; they failed to trust in his power to save them when he was near. 
Psalms78:23 Still he spoke on their behalf, and the skies opened up; the windows of heaven poured out food, the mercy bread-manna. The grain of grace fell from the clouds." 
Psalms78:24  
Psalms78:25 Humans ate angels’ food—the meal of the mighty ones. His grace gave them more than enough! 
Psalms78:26 The heavenly winds of miracle power blew in their favor, and food rained down upon them; succulent quail quieted their hunger as they ate all they wanted." 
Psalms78:27  
Psalms78:28 Food fell from the skies, thick as clouds; their provision floated down right in front of their eyes!" 
Psalms78:29 He gave them all they desired, and they ate to their fill." 
Psalms78:30 But before they had even finished, even with their food still in their mouths, God’s fiery anger arose against them, killing the finest of their mighty men." 
Psalms78:31  
Psalms78:32 Yet in spite of all this, they kept right on sinning. Even when they saw God’s marvels, they refused to believe God could care for them." 
Psalms78:33 So God cut their lives short with sudden disaster, with nothing to show for their lives but fear and failure." 
Psalms78:34 When he cared for them they ignored him, but when he began to kill them, ending their lives in a moment, they came running back to God, pleading for mercy." 
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God, the Mighty One, was their strong protector, the Hero-God who would come to their rescue." 
Psalms78:36 But their repentance lasted only as long as they were in danger; they lied through their teeth to the true God of the Covenant. So quickly they wandered away from his promises, following God with their words and not their hearts! Their worship was only flattery." 
Psalms78:37  
Psalms78:38 But amazingly, God—so full of compassion—still forgave them. He covered over their sins with his love, refusing to destroy them all. Over and over he held back his anger, restraining wrath to show them mercy." 
Psalms78:39 He knew that they were made from mere dust— frail, fragile, and short-lived, here today and gone tomorrow." 
Psalms78:40 How many times they rebelled in their desert days! How they grieved him with their grumblings. 
Psalms78:41 Again and again they limited God, preventing him from blessing them. Continually they turned back from him and provoked the Holy One of Israel!" 
Psalms78:42 They forgot his great love, how he took them by his hand, and with redemption’s kiss he delivered them from their enemies." 
Psalms78:43 They disregarded all the epic signs and marvels they saw when they escaped from Egypt’s bondage. They forgot the judgment of the plagues that set them free. 
Psalms78:44 God turned their rivers into blood, leaving the people thirsty." 
Psalms78:45 He sent them vast swarms of filthy flies that sucked their blood. He sent hordes of frogs, ruining their lives." 
Psalms78:46 Grasshoppers consumed all their crops. 
Psalms78:47 Every garden and every orchard was flattened with blasts of hailstones, their fruit trees ruined by a killing frost." 
Psalms78:48 Even their cattle fell prey, pounded by the falling hail; their livestock were struck with bolts of lightning." 
Psalms78:49 Finally, he unleashed upon them the fierceness of his anger. Such fury! He sent them sorrow and devastating trouble by his mighty band of destroying angels; messengers of death were dispatched against them." 
Psalms78:50 He lifted his mercy and let loose his fearful anger and did not spare their lives. He released the judgment-plagues to rage through their land. God struck down in death all the firstborn sons of Egypt— the pride and joy of each family. 
Psalms78:51  
Psalms78:52 Then, like a shepherd leading his sheep, God led his people out of tyranny, guiding them through the wilderness like a flock." 
Psalms78:53 Safely and carefully God led them out, with nothing to fear. But their enemies he led into the sea. He took care of them there once and for all!" 
Psalms78:54 Eventually God brought his people to the Holy Land, to a land of hills that he had prepared for them." 
Psalms78:55 He drove out and scattered all the peoples occupying the land, staking out an inheritance, a portion for each of Israel’s tribes." 
Psalms78:56 Yet for all of this, they still rebelled and refused to follow his ways, provoking to anger the God Most High." 
Psalms78:57 Like traitors turning back, they forsook him. They were even worse than their fathers! They became treacherous deceivers, crooked and corrupt, and worshiped false gods in the high places, bringing low the name of God with every idol they erected. No wonder he was filled with jealousy and furious with anger!" 
Psalms78:58  
Psalms78:59 Enraged with anger, God turned his wrath on them, and he rejected his people with disgust." 
Psalms78:60 God walked away from them and left his dwelling place at Shiloh, abandoning the place where he had lived among them," 
Psalms78:61 allowing his emblem of strength, his glory-ark, to be captured. Enemies stole the very source of Israel’s power." 
Psalms78:62 God vented his rage, allowing his people to be butchered when they went out to battle, for his anger was intense against his very own." 
Psalms78:63 Their young men fell on the battlefield and never came back. Their daughters never heard their wedding songs, since there was no one left to marry!" 
Psalms78:64 Their priests were slaughtered and their widows were killed before they had time to weep. 
Psalms78:65 Then all at once the Almighty awakened as though he had been asleep. Like a mighty man he arose, roaring into action!" 
Psalms78:66 He blasted into battle, driving back every foe, defeating them and disgracing them for time and eternity." 
Psalms78:67 He rejected Joseph’s family, the tribe of Ephraim." 
Psalms78:68 He chose instead the tribe of Judah and Mount Zion, which he loves." 
Psalms78:69 There he built his towering temple, strong and enduring as the earth itself." 
Psalms78:70 God also chose his beloved one, David. He promoted him from caring for sheep and made him his prophetic servant." 
Psalms78:71 God prepared David and took this gentle shepherd-king and presented him before the people as the one who would love and care for them with integrity, a pure heart, and the anointing to lead Israel, his holy inheritance." 
Psalms78:72  
Psalms79:1 God, won’t you do something? Barbarians have invaded your inheritance. Your temple of holiness has been violated, and Jerusalem has been left in ruins." 
Psalms79:2 The corpses of your loving people are lying in the open— food for the beasts and the birds. 
Psalms79:3 The shed blood of your servants has soaked the city, with no one left to bury the dead." 
Psalms79:4 Now the nearby nations heap their scorn upon us, scoffing, mocking us incessantly." 
Psalms79:5 How much longer, O  Yahweh, must we endure this? Does your anger have no end? Will your jealousy burn like a raging fire?" 
Psalms79:6 If you’re going to pour out your anger, pour it out on all these nations around us, not on us! They’re the ones who do not love you like we do!" 
Psalms79:7 See how they’ve attacked us, consuming the land, leaving it desolate." 
Psalms79:8 Please, God, don’t hold the sins of our fathers against us. Don’t make us pay for their sins. Hurry to our side, and let your tenderhearted mercy meet us in our need, for we are devastated beyond belief." 
Psalms79:9 Our hero, come and rescue us! O God of the breakthrough, for the glory of your name, come and help us! Forgive and restore us; heal us and cover us in your love." 
Psalms79:10 Why should all the nations sneer at us, saying, “Where is this God of yours?” Now is the time, Lord. Show your people and all the world that you will avenge this slaughter and bloodshed once and for all!" 
Psalms79:11 Listen, Lord! Hear the sighing of all the prisoners of war, all those doomed to die. Demonstrate your glory-power, and come and rescue your condemned children!" 
Psalms79:12 Lord God, take what these mocking masses have done to us and pay it all back to them seven times over." 
Psalms79:13 Then we, your devoted lovers, will forever thank you, praising your name from generation to generation!" 
Psalms80:1 God-Enthroned, be revealed in splendor as you ride upon the cherubim! How perfectly you lead us, a people set free. Loving shepherd of Israel—listen to our hearts’ cry! Shine forth from your throne of dazzling light." 
Psalms80:2 In the sight of Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh, stir up your mighty power in full display before our eyes. Break through and reveal yourself by coming to our rescue." 
Psalms80:3 Revive us, O God! Let your beaming face shine upon us with the sunrise rays of glory; then nothing will be able to stop us." 
Psalms80:4 O God, the mighty Commander of Angel Armies, how much longer will you smolder in anger? How much longer will you be disgusted with your people even when they pray?" 
Psalms80:5 You have fed us with sorrow and grief and made us drink our tears by the bowlful. 
Psalms80:6 You’ve made us a thorn in the side of all the neighboring lands, and now they just laugh at us with their mocking scorn." 
Psalms80:7 Come back, come back, O God, and restore us! You are the Commander of Angel Armies. Let your beaming face shine upon us with the sunrise rays of glory, and then nothing will be able to stop us!" 
Psalms80:8 Remember how you transplanted us here like a tender vine from Egypt. You cleared the land for your vineyard, evicting the nations from your land and planting us here. The roots of your vineyard went deep into the soil and filled the land with fruit." 
Psalms80:9  
Psalms80:10 Because of your favor on your vineyard, blessing extended to every mountain of influence. Through this flourishing vineyard mighty ones were raised up. The nations were blessed by your fruitful vineyard of Israel, all the way from the Mediterranean Sea to the Euphrates." 
Psalms80:11  
Psalms80:12 So Lord, why have you broken down your fence of favor around us? Trespassers can steal the fruit from off our vines, and now every wild beast comes breaking through our wall to ravage us. You’ve left us without protection!" 
Psalms80:13  
Psalms80:14 Come back, come back, O God to restore us! You are the Commander of Angel Armies. Look down from heaven and see our crisis. Come down and care for your lovely vineyard once again." 
Psalms80:15 Nurture our root and our fruit with your loving care. Raise up the Branch-Man, the Son whom you’ve made strong." 
Psalms80:16 Enemies chopped down our vine and set it on fire; now show them your anger and let them perish by your frown. 
Psalms80:17 Strengthen this Branch-Man, the Son of your love, the Son of Man who dwells at your right hand." 
Psalms80:18 Then we will never turn back from you. Revive us again, that we may trust in you." 
Psalms80:19 O God, the mighty Commander of Angel Armies, come back and rescue us! Let your beaming face shine upon us with the sunrise rays of glory. Then nothing will ever stop us again!" 
Psalms81:1 Lord, just singing about you makes me strong! So I’ll keep shouting for joy to Jacob’s God, my champion." 
Psalms81:2 Let the celebration begin! I will sing with drum accompaniment and with the sweet sound of the harp and guitar strumming. 
Psalms81:3 Go ahead! Blow the jubilee trumpet to begin the feast! Blow it before every joyous celebration and festival. 
Psalms81:4 For God has given us these seasons of joy, days that the God of Jacob decreed for us to celebrate and rejoice." 
Psalms81:5 He has given these feasts to remind us of his triumph over Egypt, when he went out to wage war against them. I heard the message in an unknown tongue as he said to me," 
Psalms81:6 “I have removed your backbreaking burdens and have freed your hands from the hard labor and toil. 
Psalms81:7 You called out to me in your time of trouble, and I rescued you. I came down from the realm of the secret place of thunder, where mysteries hide. I came down to save you. I tested your hearts at the place where there was no water to drink, the place of your bitter argument with me.”" 
Psalms81:8 “Listen to me, my dear people. For I’m warning you, and you’d better listen well! For I hold something against you." 
Psalms81:9 Don’t ever be guilty of worshiping any other god but me. 
Psalms81:10 I am your only God, the living God. Wasn’t I the one who broke the strongholds over you and raised you up out of bondage? Open your mouth with a mighty decree; I will fulfill it now, you’ll see! The words that you speak, so shall it be!" 
Psalms81:11 But my people still wouldn’t listen; my princely people would not yield to me. 
Psalms81:12 So I lifted my grace from off of their lives, and I surrendered them to the stubbornness of their hearts. For they were living according to their own selfish fantasies." 
Psalms81:13 O that my people would once and for all listen to me and walk faithfully in my footsteps, following my ways." 
Psalms81:14 Then and only then will I conquer your every foe and tell every one of them, ‘You must go!’" 
Psalms81:15 Those who hate my ways will cringe before me and their punishment will be eternal. 
Psalms81:16 But I will feed you with my spiritual bread. You will feast and be satisfied with me, feeding on my revelation-truth like honey dripping from the cliffs of the high place.”" 
Psalms82:1 All rise! For God now comes to judge as he convenes heaven’s courtroom. He judges every judge and rules in the midst of the gods, saying," 
Psalms82:2 “How long will you judges refuse to listen to the voice of true justice and continue to corrupt what is right by judging in favor of the wrong?” 
Psalms82:3 “Defend the defenseless, the fatherless and the forgotten, the disenfranchised and the destitute." 
Psalms82:4 Your duty is to deliver the poor and the powerless; liberate them from the grasp of the wicked. 
Psalms82:5 But you continue in your darkness and ignorance while the foundations of society are shaken to the core! 
Psalms82:6 Didn’t I commission you as judges, saying, ‘You are all like gods, since you judge on my behalf. You are all like sons of the Most High, my representatives.’" 
Psalms82:7 Nevertheless, in death you are nothing but mere men! You will be laid in the ground like any prince and you will die.”" 
Psalms82:8 All rise! For God now takes his place as judge of all the earth. Don’t you know that everything and everyone belongs to him? The nations will be sifted in his hands! 
Psalms83:1 God, you have to do something! Don’t be silent and just sit idly by." 
Psalms83:2 Can’t you see what they’re doing? All your enemies are stirred up in an uproar! They despise you, Lord. In their defiant arrogance they rise up to host their secret council against your people. They conspire together to come and harm your cherished ones—your hidden ones." 
Psalms83:3  
Psalms83:4 Our enemies keep saying, “Now is the time to wipe Israel off the map. We’ll destroy even the memory of her existence!”" 
Psalms83:5 They’ve made their pact, consulting and conspiring, aligning together in their covenant against God." 
Psalms83:6 All the sons of Ishmael, the desert sheiks and the nomadic tribes, Amalekites, Canaanites, Moabites, and all the nations that surround us, Philistines, Phoenicians, Gadarenes, and Samaritans; allied together they’re ready to attack!" 
Psalms83:7  
Psalms83:8  
Psalms83:9 Do to them all what you did to the Midianites who were defeated by Gideon. Or what you did to Sisera and Jabin when Deborah and Barak defeated them by the Kishon River. 
Psalms83:10 Do to your enemies what you did at Endor, whose rotting corpses fertilized the land." 
Psalms83:11 Repeat history, God! Make all their “noble ones” die like Oreb, Zebah, and Zalmunna, who said in their pride, “We will seize God’s people along with all their pleasant lands!”" 
Psalms83:12  
Psalms83:13 Blow them away, God, like straw in the wind, like a tumbleweed in the wilderness!" 
Psalms83:14 Burn them up like a raging fire roaring down the mountainside; consume them all until only charred sticks remain! 
Psalms83:15 Chase them away like before a mighty storm and terrifying tempest. 
Psalms83:16 O Lord, disgrace them until their faces fill with shame, and make them acknowledge the glory of your name." 
Psalms83:17 Make them utter failures in everything they do until they perish in total disgrace and humiliation," 
Psalms83:18 so they will know that you, and you alone, are Yahweh, the only Most High God exalted over all the earth!" 
Psalms84:1 God of Heaven’s Armies, you find so much beauty in your people! They’re like lovely sanctuaries of your presence." 
Psalms84:2 Deep within me are these lovesick longings, desires and daydreams of living in union with you. When I’m near you, my heart and my soul will sing and worship with my joyful songs of you, my true source and spring of life!" 
Psalms84:3 O Lord of Heaven’s Armies, my King and my God, even the sparrows and swallows are welcome to build a nest among your altars to raise their young." 
Psalms84:4 What pleasure fills those who live every day in your temple, enjoying you as they worship in your presence!" 
Psalms84:5 How enriched are they who find their strength in the Lord; within their hearts are the highways of holiness! 
Psalms84:6 Even when their paths wind through the dark valley of tears, they dig deep to find a pleasant pool where others find only pain. He gives to them a brook of blessing filled from the rain of an outpouring." 
Psalms84:7 They grow stronger and stronger with every step forward, and the God of all gods will appear before them in Zion." 
Psalms84:8 Hear my cry, O God of Heaven’s Armies! God of Jacob, listen to my loving prayer." 
Psalms84:9 God, your wraparound presence is our defense. In your kindness look upon the faces of your anointed ones." 
Psalms84:10 For just one day of intimacy with you is like a thousand days of joy rolled into one! I’d rather stand at the threshold in front of the Gate Beautiful, ready to go in and worship my God, than to live my life without you in the most beautiful palace of the wicked." 
Psalms84:11 For the Lord God is brighter than the brilliance of a sunrise! Wrapping himself around me like a shield, he is so generous with his gifts of grace and glory. Those who walk along his paths with integrity will never lack one thing they need, for he provides it all!" 
Psalms84:12 O Lord of Heaven’s Armies, what euphoria fills those who forever trust in you!" 
Psalms85:1 Lord, your love has poured out so many amazing blessings on our land! You’ve restored Jacob’s destiny from captivity." 
Psalms85:2 You’ve forgiven our many sins and covered every one of them in your love. 
Psalms85:3 So now it’s obvious that your blazing anger has ended and the furious fire of wrath has been extinguished by your mercy. 
Psalms85:4 So bring us back to loving you, God our Savior. Restore our hearts so that we’ll never again feel your anger rise against us." 
Psalms85:5 Will you forever hold a grudge? Will your anger endure for all time? 
Psalms85:6 Revive us again, O God! I know you will! Give us a fresh start! Then all your people will taste your joy and gladness." 
Psalms85:7 Pour out even more of your love on us! Reveal more of your kindness and restore us back to you! 
Psalms85:8 Now I’ll listen carefully for your voice and wait to hear whatever you say. Let me hear your promise of peace— the message every one of your godly lovers longs to hear. Don’t let us in our ignorance turn back from following you. 
Psalms85:9 For I know your power and presence shines on all your devoted lovers. Your glory always hovers over all who bow low before you. 
Psalms85:10 Your mercy and your truth have married each other. Your righteousness and peace have kissed. 
Psalms85:11 Flowers of your faithfulness are blooming on the earth. Righteousness shines down from the sky. 
Psalms85:12 Yes, the Lord keeps raining down blessing after blessing, and prosperity will drench the land with a bountiful harvest." 
Psalms85:13 For deliverance goes before him, preparing a path for his steps." 
Psalms86:1 Lord, bend down to listen to my prayer. I am in deep trouble. I’m broken and humbled, and I desperately need your help." 
Psalms86:2 Guard my life, for I’m your faithful friend, your loyal servant for life. I turn to you in faith, my God, my hero; come and rescue me!" 
Psalms86:3 Lord God, hear my constant cry for help; show me your favor and bring me to your fountain of grace!" 
Psalms86:4 Restore joy to your loving servant once again, for all I am is yours, O God." 
Psalms86:5 Lord, you are so good to me, so kind in every way and ready to forgive, for your grace-fountain keeps overflowing, drenching all your devoted lovers who pray to you." 
Psalms86:6 God, won’t you pay attention to this urgent cry? Lord, bend down to listen to my prayer." 
Psalms86:7 Whenever trouble strikes, I will keep crying out to you, for I know your help is on the way." 
Psalms86:8 God, there’s no one like you; there’s no other god as famous as you. You outshine all others, and your miracles make it easy to know you." 
Psalms86:9 Lord Almighty, you are the one who created all the nations. Look at them—they’re all on their way! Yes, the day will come when they all will worship you and put your glory on display." 
Psalms86:10 You are the one and only God. What miracles! What wonders! What greatness belongs to you! 
Psalms86:11 Teach me more about you, how you work and how you move, so that I can walk onward in your truth until everything within me brings honor to your name." 
Psalms86:12 With all my heart and passion I will thank you, my God! I will give glory to your name, always and forever!" 
Psalms86:13 You love me so much, and you have placed your greatness upon me. You rescued me from the deepest place of darkness, and you have delivered me from a certain death." 
Psalms86:14 God, look at how these arrogant ones have defied me. Like a vicious band of violent men, they have tried to kill me. They wouldn’t worry for a moment that they were sinning against you!" 
Psalms86:15 But Lord, your nurturing love is tender and gentle. You are slow to get angry yet so swift to show your faithful love. You are full of abounding grace and truth." 
Psalms86:16 Bring me to your grace-fountain so that your strength becomes mine. Be my hero and come rescue your servant once again! 
Psalms86:17 Send me a miraculous sign to show me how much you love me, so that those who hate me will see it and be ashamed. Don’t they know that you, Lord, are my comforter, the one who comes to help me?" 
Psalms87:1 High upon his hills of holiness stands God’s city. 
Psalms87:2 How God loves the gates of Zion, his favorite place on earth." 
Psalms87:3 So many glorious things have been proclaimed over Zion, God’s holy city!" 
Psalms87:4 For the Lord says, “Here are the nations who will acknowledge me as God: Egypt, Iraq, Palestine, and the Mediterranean people, even distant Ethiopia. They will all boast, ‘I was born in Zion!’ ”" 
Psalms87:5 But over Zion it will be said, “The mighty Man was born there, and he will establish it.” For the God Most High will truly bless Jerusalem." 
Psalms87:6 And when he counts her citizens, recording them in his registry, he will write by their names: “This one was born again here!”" 
Psalms87:7 And the princes of God’s feasts will sing and dance, singing, “Every fountain of delight springs up from your life within me!”" 
Psalms88:1 Yahweh is the God who continually saves me. I weep before you night and day. 
Psalms88:2 Please bend down and listen to my sobbing, for my life is riddled with troubles and death is just around the corner!" 
Psalms88:3 Everyone sees my life ebbing out. They consider me a hopeless case and see me as a dead man. 
Psalms88:4 They’ve all left me here to die, helpless, like one who is doomed for death." 
Psalms88:5 They’re convinced you’ve forsaken me, certain that you’ve forgotten me completely— abandoned, pierced, with nothing to look forward to but death." 
Psalms88:6 They have discarded me and thrown me down into the deepest darkness as into a bottomless pit. 
Psalms88:7 I feel your wrath, and it’s a heavy weight upon me, drowning me beneath a sea of sorrow." 
Psalms88:8 Why did you turn all my friends against me? You’ve made me like a cursed man in their eyes. No one wants to be with me now. You’ve caught me in a trap with no way out. 
Psalms88:9 Every day I beg for your help. Can’t you see my tears? My eyes are swollen with weeping. My arms are wide, longing for mercy, but you’re nowhere to be found." 
Psalms88:10 How can those who are cut off from your care even know that you are there? Do departed spirits rise up to praise you? 
Psalms88:11 Who can give thanks for your love in the graveyard? Who preaches your faithfulness in the place of destruction? 
Psalms88:12 Does death’s darkness declare your miracles? How can anyone who’s in the grave, where all is forgotten, remember how you keep your promises?" 
Psalms88:13 Lord, you know my prayer before I even whisper it. At each and every sunrise you will continue to hear my cry until you answer." 
Psalms88:14 O Lord, why have you thrown my life away? Will you keep turning the other way every time I call out to you?" 
Psalms88:15 I’ve had to live in poverty and trouble all my life. Now I’m humiliated, broken, and helpless before your terrors and I can’t take it anymore." 
Psalms88:16 I’m overwhelmed by your burning anger. I’ve taken the worst you could give me, and I’m speechless before you." 
Psalms88:17 I’m drowning beneath the waves of this sorrow, cut off with no one to help." 
Psalms88:18 All my loved ones and friends keep far away from me, leaving me all alone with only darkness as my friend." 
Psalms89:1 This forever-song I sing of the gentle love of God! Young and old alike will hear about your faithful, steadfast love—never failing!" 
Psalms89:2 Here’s my chorus: “Your mercy grows through the ages. Your faithfulness is firm, rising up to the skies.”" 
Psalms89:3 I heard the Lord say, “My covenant has been made, and I’m committed forever to my chosen one, David." 
Psalms89:4 I have made my oath that there will be sons of David forever, sons that are kings through every generation.”" 
Psalms89:5 Can you hear it? Heaven is filled with your praises, O Lord! All the holy ones are praising you for your miracles. The sons of God are all praising you for your mighty wonders. We could search the skies forever and never find one like you. All the mighty angels could not be compared to you." 
Psalms89:6  
Psalms89:7 You are a God who is greatly to be feared as you preside over the council of holy ones. You are surrounded by trembling ones who are overwhelmed with fear and dread, stunned as they stand in awe of you!" 
Psalms89:8 So awesome are you, O  Yahweh, Lord God of Angel Armies! Where could we find anyone as glorious as you? Your faithfulness shines all around you!" 
Psalms89:9 You rule over oceans and the swelling seas. When their stormy waves rise, you speak, and they lie still." 
Psalms89:10 You crushed the strongholds of Egypt, and all your enemies were scattered at the mighty display of your glory-power." 
Psalms89:11 All the heavens and everything on earth belong to you, for you are the Creator of all that is seen and unseen." 
Psalms89:12 The four corners of the earth were put in place by you. You made the majestic mountains that are still shouting their praises to your name. 
Psalms89:13 Breathtaking and awesome is your power! Astounding and unbelievable is your might and strength when it goes on display! 
Psalms89:14 Your glorious throne rests on a foundation of righteousness and just verdicts. Grace and truth are the attendants who go before you. 
Psalms89:15 O Lord, how blessed are the people who know the triumphant shout, for they walk in the radiance of your presence." 
Psalms89:16 We can do nothing but leap for joy all day long, for we know who you are and what you do, and you’ve exalted us on high." 
Psalms89:17 The glory of your splendor is our strength, and your marvelous favor makes us even stronger, lifting us even higher!" 
Psalms89:18 You are our King, the holiest one of all; your wraparound presence is our protection." 
Psalms89:19 You spoke to your prophets in visions, saying, “I have found a mighty hero for my people. I have chosen David as my loving servant and exalted him. I have anointed him as king with the oil of my holiness." 
Psalms89:20  
Psalms89:21 I will be strength to him, and I will give him my grace to sustain him no matter what comes." 
Psalms89:22 None of his enemies will get the best of him, nor will the wicked one overpower him." 
Psalms89:23 For I will crush his every adversary and do away with all who hate him. 
Psalms89:24 Because I love him and treasure him, my faithfulness will always protect him. I will place my great favor upon him, and I will cause his power and fame to increase." 
Psalms89:25 I will set his hand over the sea and his right hand over the rivers. 
Psalms89:26 And he will come before me, saying, ‘You truly are my Father, my only God, and my strong deliverer!’" 
Psalms89:27 I am setting him apart, favoring him as my firstborn son. I will make him the most high king in all the earth!" 
Psalms89:28 I will love him forever and always show him kindness. My covenant with him will never be broken. 
Psalms89:29 For I have decreed that he will always have an heir— a dynasty that will release the days of heaven on earth. 
Psalms89:30 But if his children turn from me and forsake my words, refusing to walk in my truth, renouncing and violating my laws, then I will surely punish them for their sins with my stern discipline until they regret it." 
Psalms89:31  
Psalms89:32  
Psalms89:33 But I will never, no never, lift my faithful love from off their lives. My kindness will prevail and I will never disown them." 
Psalms89:34 How could I revoke my covenant of love that I promised David? For I have given him my word, my holy, irrevocable word. How could I lie to my loving servant David?" 
Psalms89:35  
Psalms89:36 Sons of David will continue to reign on his throne, and their kingdom will endure as long as the sun is in the sky. This covenant will be an unbreakable promise that I have established for all time.”" 
Psalms89:37  
Psalms89:38 Why have you rejected me, the one you anointed? Why would you cast me away? Why would you lose your temper with me?" 
Psalms89:39 You have torn up the contract you made with me, your servant. You have stripped away my crown and thrown it to the ground." 
Psalms89:40 You have torn down all my walls of defense and have made my every hiding place into ruins. 
Psalms89:41 All the passersby attack and rob me while my neighbors mock! 
Psalms89:42 Instead of fighting for me, you take the side of my enemies, even giving them strength to subdue me, and then watched them celebrate their victory!" 
Psalms89:43 You are no longer helping me in battle. You’ve forsaken me to the swords of those who would strike me down. 
Psalms89:44 You’ve made my regal splendor to decrease and allowed my rule to be overthrown. 
Psalms89:45 Because of you, I’ve become old before my time, and I’m publicly disgraced!" 
Psalms89:46 How long will you hide your love from me? Have you left me for good? How long will your anger continue to burn against me? 
Psalms89:47 Remember, Lord, I am nothing but dust, here today and so soon blown away. Is this all you’ve created us for? For nothing but this?" 
Psalms89:48 Which one of us will live forever? We are all mortal, terminal, for we will all one day die. Which one of us would ever escape our appointment with death and dodge our own funeral?" 
Psalms89:49 So God, where is all this love and kindness you promised us? What happened to your covenant with David?" 
Psalms89:50 Have you forgotten how your own servants are being slandered? Lord God, it seems like I’m carrying in my heart all the pain and abuse of many people." 
Psalms89:51 They have relentlessly insulted and persecuted us, your anointed ones." 
Psalms89:52 Nevertheless, blessed be our God forever and ever. Amen! Faithful is our King!"
