Psalms119:133 Prepare before me a path filled with your promises, and don’t allow even one sin to have dominion over me." 
Psalms119:134 Rescue me from the oppression of ungodly men so that I can keep all your precepts. 
Psalms119:135 Smile on me, your loving servant. Instruct me in what is right in your eyes." 
Psalms119:136 When I witness the rebellious breaking your laws, it makes me weep uncontrollably!" 
Psalms119:137 Lord, your judgments reveal your righteousness, and your verdicts are always fair." 
Psalms119:138 The motive behind your every word is pure, and your teachings are remarkably faithful and true." 
Psalms119:139 I’ve been consumed with a furious passion to do what’s right, all because of the way my enemies disrespect your laws." 
Psalms119:140 All your promises glow with fire; that’s why I’m a lover of your Word. 
Psalms119:141 Even though I’m considered insignificant and despised by the world, I’ll never abandon your ways." 
Psalms119:142 Your righteousness has no end; it is everlasting, and your rules are perfectly fair." 
Psalms119:143 Even though my troubles overwhelm me with anguish, I still delight and cherish every message you speak to me." 
Psalms119:144 Give me more revelation so that I can live for you, for nothing is more pure and eternal than your truth." 
Psalms119:145 Answer my passionate prayer, O Lord, and I’ll obey everything you say." 
Psalms119:146 Save me, God, and I’ll follow your every instruction." 
Psalms119:147 Before the day dawns, I’ll be crying out for help and wrapping your words into my life." 
Psalms119:148 I lie awake every night pondering your promises to me. 
Psalms119:149 Lord, listen to my heart’s cry, for I know your love is real for me; breathe life into me again by the revelation of your justice." 
Psalms119:150 Here they come—these lawless rebels are coming near, but they are all so far away from your laws." 
Psalms119:151 God, you are near me always, so close to me; every one of your commands reveals truth." 
Psalms119:152 I’ve known all along how true and unchanging is every word you speak, established forever!" 
Psalms119:153 Look upon all my misery and come be my hero to rescue me, for I will never forget what you’ve revealed to me." 
Psalms119:154 Take my side and defend me in these sufferings; redeem me and revive me, just like you promised you would." 
Psalms119:155 The wicked are so far from salvation, for they could not care less about your message of truth." 
Psalms119:156 Your tender mercies are what I need, O God; give me back my life again through the revelation of your judgments." 
Psalms119:157 I have so many enemies who persecute me, yet I won’t swerve from following your ways." 
Psalms119:158 I grieve when I see how the faithless ones live, for they just walk away from your promises." 
Psalms119:159 Lord, see how much I truly love your instructions. So in your tender kindness, breathe life into me again." 
Psalms119:160 The sum total of all your words adds up to absolute truth, and every one of your righteous decrees is everlasting." 
Psalms119:161 The powerful elite have persecuted me without a cause, but my heart trembles in awe because of your miracle-words." 
Psalms119:162 Your promises are the source of my bubbling joy; the revelation of your Word thrills me like one who has discovered hidden treasure. 
Psalms119:163 I despise every lie and hate every falsehood, for I am passionate about keeping your precepts." 
Psalms119:164 I stop to praise you seven times a day, all because your ways are perfect!" 
Psalms119:165 There is such a great peace and well-being that comes to the lovers of your Word, and they will never be offended." 
Psalms119:166 Lord, I’m longing for more of your salvation, for I want to do what pleases you." 
Psalms119:167 My love for your ways is indescribable; in my innermost being I want to follow them perfectly! 
Psalms119:168 I will keep your instructions and follow your counsel; all my ways are an open book before you. 
Psalms119:169 Lord, listen to my prayer. It’s like a sacrifice I bring to you; I must have more revelation of your Word!" 
Psalms119:170 Take my words to heart when I ask you, Lord; rescue me, just like you promised!" 
Psalms119:171 I offer you my joyous praise for all that you’ve taught me. 
Psalms119:172 Your wonderful words will become my song of worship, for everything you’ve commanded is perfect and true." 
Psalms119:173 Place your hands of strength and favor upon me, for I’ve made my choice to follow your ways." 
Psalms119:174 I wait for your deliverance, O Lord, for your words thrill me like nothing else!" 
Psalms119:175 Invigorate my life so that I can praise you even more, and may your truth be my strength!" 
Psalms119:176 I’ll never forget what you’ve taught me, Lord, but when I wander off and lose my way, come after me, for I am your beloved!" 
Psalms120:1 I was desperate for you to help me in my struggles, and you did!" 
Psalms120:2 So come and deliver me now from this treachery and false accusation. 
Psalms120:3 O lying deceivers, don’t you know what is your fate?" 
Psalms120:4 You will be pierced through with condemnation and consumed with burning coals of fire! 
Psalms120:5 Why am I doomed to live as an alien, scattered among these cruel savages? Am I destined to dwell in the darkened tents of desert nomads?" 
Psalms120:6 For too long I’ve had to live among those who hate peace. 
Psalms120:7 I speak words of peace while they speak words of war, but they refuse to listen."

Psalms121:2  
Psalms121:3 He will guard and guide me, never letting me stumble or fall. God is my keeper; he will never forget nor ignore me." 
Psalms121:4 He will never slumber nor sleep; he is the Guardian-God for his people, Israel." 
Psalms121:5 Yahweh himself will watch over you; he’s always at your side to shelter you safely in his presence. 
Psalms121:6 He’s protecting you from all danger both day and night. 
Psalms121:7 He will keep you from every form of evil or calamity as he continuously watches over you. 
Psalms121:8 You will be guarded by God himself. You will be safe when you leave your home, and safely you will return. He will protect you now, and he’ll protect you forevermore!"

Psalms127:4 Children born to a young couple will one day rise to protect and provide for their parents.

Psalms137:4 But how could we sing the song of the Lord in this foreign wilderness?

Psalms138:2 I bow down before your divine presence and bring you my deepest worship as I experience your tender love and your living truth. For your Word and the fame of your name have been magnified above all else! 
Psalms138:3 At the very moment I called out to you, you answered me! You strengthened me deep within my soul and breathed fresh courage into me." 
Psalms138:4 One day all the kings of the earth will rise to give you thanks when they hear the living words that I have heard you speak. 
Psalms138:5 They too will sing of your wonderful ways, for your ineffable glory is great!" 
Psalms138:6 For though you are lofty and exalted, you stoop to embrace the lowly. Yet you keep your distance from those filled with pride." 
Psalms138:7 By your mighty power I can walk through any devastation, and you will keep me alive, reviving me. Your power set me free from the hatred of my enemies." 
Psalms138:8 You keep every promise you’ve ever made to me! Since your love for me is constant and endless, I ask you, Lord, to finish every good thing that you’ve begun in me!" 
Psalms139:1 Lord, you know everything there is to know about me." 
Psalms139:2 You perceive every movement of my heart and soul, and you understand my every thought before it even enters my mind." 
Psalms139:3 You are so intimately aware of me, Lord. You read my heart like an open book and you know all the words I’m about to speak before I even start a sentence! You know every step I will take before my journey even begins." 
Psalms139:4  
Psalms139:5 You’ve gone into my future to prepare the way, and in kindness you follow behind me to spare me from the harm of my past. You have laid your hand on me!" 
Psalms139:6 This is just too wonderful, deep, and incomprehensible! Your understanding of me brings me wonder and strength." 
Psalms139:7 Where could I go from your Spirit? Where could I run and hide from your face? 
Psalms139:8 If I go up to heaven, you’re there! If I go down to the realm of the dead, you’re there too!" 
Psalms139:9 If I fly with wings into the shining dawn, you’re there! If I fly into the radiant sunset, you’re there waiting!" 
Psalms139:10 Wherever I go, your hand will guide me; your strength will empower me." 
Psalms139:11 It’s impossible to disappear from you or to ask the darkness to hide me, for your presence is everywhere, bringing light into my night." 
Psalms139:12 There is no such thing as darkness with you. The night, to you, is as bright as the day; there’s no difference between the two." 
Psalms139:13 You formed my innermost being, shaping my delicate inside and my intricate outside, and wove them all together in my mother’s womb." 
Psalms139:14 I thank you, God, for making me so mysteriously complex! Everything you do is marvelously breathtaking. It simply amazes me to think about it! How thoroughly you know me, Lord!" 
Psalms139:15 You even formed every bone in my body when you created me in the secret place; carefully, skillfully you shaped me from nothing to something." 
Psalms139:16 You saw who you created me to be before I became me! Before I’d ever seen the light of day, the number of days you planned for me were already recorded in your book." 
Psalms139:17 Every single moment you are thinking of me! How precious and wonderful to consider that you cherish me constantly in your every thought! O God, your desires toward me are more than the grains of sand on every shore! When I awake each morning, you’re still with me." 
Psalms139:18  
Psalms139:19 O God, come and slay these bloodthirsty, murderous men! For I cry out, “Depart from me, you wicked ones!”" 
Psalms139:20 See how they blaspheme your sacred name and lift up themselves against you, but all in vain!" 
Psalms139:21 Lord, can’t you see how I despise those who despise you? For I grieve when I see them rise up against you." 
Psalms139:22 I have nothing but complete hatred and disgust for them. Your enemies shall be my enemies! 
Psalms139:23 God, I invite your searching gaze into my heart. Examine me through and through; find out everything that may be hidden within me. Put me to the test and sift through all my anxious cares." 
Psalms139:24 See if there is any path of pain I’m walking on, and lead me back to your glorious, everlasting way— the path that brings me back to you." 
Psalms140:1 Lord, protect me from this evil one! Rescue me from these violent schemes!" 
Psalms140:2 He concocts his secret strategy to divide and harm others, stirring up trouble one against another." 
Psalms140:3 They are known for their sharp rhetoric of poisonous, hateful words." 
Psalms140:4 Keep me safe, Lord, out of reach from these wicked and violent men, and guard me, God, for they have plotted an evil scheme to ruin me and bring me down." 
Psalms140:5 They are proud and insolent; they’ve set an ambush for me in secret. They are determined to snare me in their net like captured prey. 
Psalms140:6 O Lord, you are my God and my saving strength! My Hero-God, you wrap yourself around me to protect me. For I’m surrounded by your presence in my day of battle. Lord Yahweh, hear my cry. May my voice move your heart to show me mercy." 
Psalms140:7  
Psalms140:8 Don’t let the wicked triumph over me, but bring down their every strategy to subdue me or they will become even more arrogant!" 
Psalms140:9 Those who surround me are nothing but proud troublemakers. May they drink the poison of their own poisonous words. 
Psalms140:10 May their slanderous lives never prosper! Let evil itself hunt them down and pursue them relentlessly until they are thrown into fiery pits from which they will never get out! Let burning coals of hellfire fall upon their heads! 
Psalms140:11  
Psalms140:12 For I know, Lord, that you will be the hero of all those they persecute, and you will secure justice for the poor." 
Psalms140:13 Your godly ones will thank you no matter what happens. For they choose and cherish your presence above everything else! 
Psalms141:1 Please, Lord, come close and come quickly to help me! Listen to my prayer as I call out to you." 
Psalms141:2 Let my prayer be as the evening sacrifice that burns like fragrant incense, rising as my offering to you as I lift up my hands in surrendered worship!" 
Psalms141:3 God, give me grace to guard my lips from speaking what is wrong." 
Psalms141:4 Guide me away from temptation and doing evil. Save me from sinful habits and from keeping company with those who are experts in evil. Help me not to share in their sin in any way! 
Psalms141:5 When one of your godly ones corrects me or one of your faithful ones rebukes me, I will accept it like an honor I cannot refuse. It will be as healing medicine that I swallow without an offended heart. Even if they are mistaken, I will continue to pray." 
Psalms141:6 When the leaders and judges are condemned, falling upon the rocks of justice, then they’ll know my words to them were true!" 
Psalms141:7 Like an earthquake splits open the earth, so the world of hell will open its mouth to swallow their scattered bones." 
Psalms141:8 But you are my Lord and my God; I only have eyes for you! I hide myself in you, so don’t leave me defenseless." 
Psalms141:9 Protect me! Keep me from the traps of wickedness they set for me. 
Psalms141:10 Let them all stumble into their own traps while I escape without a scratch! 
Psalms142:1 God, I’m crying out to you! I lift up my voice boldly to beg for your mercy." 
Psalms142:2 I spill out my heart to you and tell you all my troubles. 
Psalms142:3 For when I was desperate, overwhelmed, and about to give up, you were the only one there to help. You gave me a way of escape from the hidden traps of my enemies." 
Psalms142:4 I look to my left and right to see if there is anyone who will help, but there’s no one who takes notice of me. I have no hope of escape, and no one cares whether I live or die." 
Psalms142:5 So I cried out to you, Lord, my only hiding place. You’re all I have, my only hope in this life, my last chance for help." 
Psalms142:6 Please listen to my heart’s cry, for I am low and in desperate need of you! Rescue me from all those who persecute me, for I am no match for them." 
Psalms142:7 Bring me out of this dungeon so I can declare your praise! And all the righteous will celebrate all the wonderful things you’ve done for me! 
Psalms143:1 Lord, you must hear my prayer, for you are faithful to your promises. Answer my cry, O righteous God!" 
Psalms143:2 Don’t bring me into your courtroom for judgment, for there is no one who is righteous before you." 
Psalms143:3 My enemies have chased and caught me and crushed my life into dust. Now I’m living in the darkness of death’s shadow. 
Psalms143:4 My inner being is in depression and my heart is heavy, dazed with despair." 
Psalms143:5 I remember the glorious miracles of days gone by, and I often think of all the wonders of old." 
Psalms143:6 Now I’m reaching out to you, thirsting for you like the dry, cracked ground thirsts for rain." 
Psalms143:7 Lord, come quickly and answer me, for my depression deepens and I’m about to give up. Don’t leave me now or I’ll die!" 
Psalms143:8 Let the dawning day bring me revelation of your tender, unfailing love. Give me light for my path and teach me, for I trust in you." 
Psalms143:9 Save me from all my enemies, for I hide myself in you." 
Psalms143:10 I just want to obey all you ask of me. So teach me, Lord, for you are my God. Your gracious Spirit is all I need, so lead me on good paths that are pleasing to you, my one and only God!" 
Psalms143:11 Lord, if you rescue me, it will bring you more glory, for you are true to your promises. Bring me out of these troubles!"

Psalms144:1 There is only one strong, safe, and secure place for me; it’s in God alone who gives me strength for the battle." 
Psalms144:2 He’s my shelter of love and my fortress of faith, who wraps himself around me as a secure shield. I hide myself in this one who subdues enemies before me." 
Psalms144:3 Lord, what is it about us that you would even notice us? Why do you even bother with us?" 
Psalms144:4 For man is nothing but a faint whisper, a mere breath. We spend our days like nothing more than a passing shadow." 
Psalms144:5 Step down from heaven, Lord, and come down! Make the mountains melt at your touch." 
Psalms144:6 Loose your fiery lightning flashes and scatter your enemies. Overthrow them with your terrifying judgments. 
Psalms144:7 Reach down from your heavens and rescue me from this hell and deliver me from these dark powers. 
Psalms144:8 They speak nothing but lies; their words are pure deceit. Nothing they say can ever be trusted. 
Psalms144:9 My God, I will sing you a brand-new song! The harp inside my heart will make music to you!" 
Psalms144:10 I will sing of you, the one who gives victory to kings— the one who rescues David, your loving servant, from the fatal sword." 
Psalms144:11 Deliver me and save me from these dark powers who speak nothing but lies. Their words are pure deceit, and you can’t trust anything they say." 
Psalms144:12 Deliver us! Then our homes will be happy. Our sons will grow up as strong, sturdy men and our daughters with graceful beauty, royally fashioned as for a palace." 
Psalms144:13 Our barns will be filled to the brim, overflowing with the fruits of our harvest. Our fields will be full of sheep and cattle, too many to count, and our livestock will not miscarry their young. Our enemies will not invade our land, and there’ll be no breach in our walls." 
Psalms144:14  
Psalms144:15 What bliss we experience when these blessings fall! The people who love and serve our God will be happy indeed! 
Psalms145:1 My heart explodes with praise to you! Now and forever my heart bows in worship to you, my King and my God!" 
Psalms145:2 Every day I will lift up my praise to your name with praises that will last throughout eternity. 
Psalms145:3 Lord, you are great and worthy of the highest praise! For there is no end to the discovery of the greatness that surrounds you." 
Psalms145:4 Generation after generation will declare more of your greatness and declare more of your glory. 
Psalms145:5 Your magnificent splendor and the miracles of your majesty are my constant meditation. 
Psalms145:6 Your awe-inspiring acts of power have everyone talking! I’m telling people everywhere about your excellent greatness! 
Psalms145:7 Our hearts bubble over as we celebrate the fame of your marvelous beauty, bringing bliss to our hearts. We shout with ecstatic joy over your breakthrough for us." 
Psalms145:8 You’re kind and tenderhearted to those who don’t deserve it and very patient with people who fail you. Your love is like a flooding river overflowing its banks with kindness. 
Psalms145:9 God, everyone sees your goodness, for your tender love is blended into everything you do." 
Psalms145:10 Everything you have made will praise you, fulfilling its purpose. And all your godly ones will be found bowing before you." 
Psalms145:11 They will tell the world of the lavish splendor of your kingdom and preach about your limitless power. 
Psalms145:12 They will demonstrate for all to see your miracles of might and reveal the glorious majesty of your kingdom. 
Psalms145:13 You are the Lord who reigns over your never-ending kingdom through all the ages of time and eternity! You are faithful to fulfill every promise you’ve made. You manifest yourself as kindness in all you do. 
Psalms145:14 Weak and feeble ones you will sustain. Those bent over with burdens of shame you will lift up. 
Psalms145:15 You have captured our attention and the eyes of all look to you. You give what they hunger for at just the right time. 
Psalms145:16 When you open your generous hand, it’s full of blessings, satisfying the longings of every living thing." 
Psalms145:17 You are fair and righteous in everything you do, and your love is wrapped into all your works." 
Psalms145:18 You draw near to those who call out to you, listening closely, especially when their hearts are true." 
Psalms145:19 Every godly one receives even more than what they ask for. For you hear what their hearts really long for, and you bring them your saving strength." 
Psalms145:20 God, you watch carefully over all your devoted lovers like a bodyguard, but you will destroy the ungodly." 
Psalms145:21 I will praise you, Lord! Let everyone everywhere join me in praising the beautiful Lord of holiness from now through eternity!"

Psalms146:2 I will spend my life praising you and singing high praises to you, my God, every day of my life!"

Psalms146:4  
Psalms146:5 But those who hope in the Lord will be happy and pleased! Our help comes from the God of Jacob! 
Psalms146:6 You keep all your promises. You are the Creator of heaven’s glory, earth’s grandeur, and the ocean’s greatness." 
Psalms146:7 The oppressed get justice with you. The hungry are satisfied with you. Prisoners find their freedom with you. 
Psalms146:8 You open the eyes of the blind, and you fully restore those bent over with shame. You love those who love and honor you."

Psalms147:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! How beautiful it is when we sing our praises to the beautiful God, for praise makes you lovely before him and brings him great delight!" 
Psalms147:2 Yahweh builds up Jerusalem; he gathers up the outcasts and brings them home. 
Psalms147:3 He heals the wounds of every shattered heart. 
Psalms147:4 He sets his stars in place, calling them all by their names." 
Psalms147:5 How great is our God! There’s absolutely nothing his power cannot accomplish, and he has infinite understanding of everything." 
Psalms147:6 Yahweh supports and strengthens the humble, but the ungodly will be brought down to the dust." 
Psalms147:7 Sing out with songs of thanksgiving to the Lord! Let’s sing our praises with melodies overflowing! 
Psalms147:8 He fills the sky with clouds, sending showers to water the earth so that the grass springs up on the mountain fields and the earth produces food for man." 
Psalms147:9 All the birds and beasts who cry with hunger to him are fed from his hands. 
Psalms147:10 His people don’t find security in strong horses, for horsepower is nothing to him. Manpower is even less impressive!" 
Psalms147:11 Yahweh shows favor to those who fear him, those who wait for his tender embrace." 
Psalms147:12 Jerusalem, praise the Lord! Zion, worship your God!" 
Psalms147:13 For he has strengthened the authority of your gates. He even blesses you with more children. 
Psalms147:14 He’s the one who brings peace to your borders, feeding you the most excellent of fare." 
Psalms147:15 He sends out his orders throughout the world; his words run as swift messengers, bringing them to pass." 
Psalms147:16 He blankets the earth with glistening snow, painting the landscape with frost." 
Psalms147:17 Sleet and hail fall from the sky, causing waters to freeze before winter’s icy blast." 
Psalms147:18 Then he speaks his word and it all melts away; as the warm spring winds blow, the streams begin to flow." 
Psalms147:19 In the same way, he speaks his word to Jacob, and to Israel he brings his life-giving instruction." 
Psalms147:20 He has dealt with Israel differently than with any other people, for they have received his laws. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!"

Proverbs1:1 Here are kingdom revelations, words to live by, and words of wisdom given to empower you to reign in life, written as proverbs by Israel’s King Solomon, David’s son." 
Proverbs1:2 Within these sayings will be found the revelation of wisdom and the impartation of spiritual understanding. Use them as keys to unlock the treasures of true knowledge. 
Proverbs1:3 Those who cling to these words will receive discipline to demonstrate wisdom in every relationship and to choose what is right and just and fair. 
Proverbs1:4 These proverbs will give you great skill to teach the immature and make them wise, to give youth the understanding of their design and destiny." 
Proverbs1:5 For the wise, these proverbs will make you even wiser, and for those with discernment, you will be able to acquire brilliant strategies for leadership." 
Proverbs1:6 These kingdom revelations will break open your understanding to unveil the deeper meaning of parables, poetic riddles, and epigrams, and to unravel the words and enigmas of the wise." 
Proverbs1:7 We cross the threshold of true knowledge when we live in obedient devotion to God. Stubborn know-it-alls will never stop to do this, for they scorn true wisdom and knowledge." 
Proverbs1:8 Pay close attention, my child, to your father’s wise words and never forget your mother’s instructions." 
Proverbs1:9 For their insight will bring you success, adorning you with grace-filled thoughts and giving you reins to guide your decisions." 
Proverbs1:10 When peer pressure compels you to go with the crowd and sinners invite you to join in, you must simply say, “No!”" 
Proverbs1:11 When the gang says— “We’re going to steal and kill and get away with it. 
Proverbs1:12 We’ll take down the rich and rob them. We’ll swallow them up alive and take what we want from whomever we want. 
Proverbs1:13 Then we’ll take their treasures and fill our homes with loot. 
Proverbs1:14 So come on and join us. Take your chance with us. We’ll divide up all we get; we’ll each end up with big bags of cash!”— 
Proverbs1:15 my son, refuse to go with them and stay far away from them." 
Proverbs1:16 For crime is their way of life and bloodshed their specialty. 
Proverbs1:17 To be aware of their snare is the best way of escape. 
Proverbs1:18 They’ll resort to murder to steal their victim’s assets, but eventually it will be their own lives that are ambushed." 
Proverbs1:19 In their ungodly disrespect for God they bring destruction on their own lives. 
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom’s praises are sung in the streets and celebrated far and wide. 
Proverbs1:21 Yet wisdom’s song is not always heard in the halls of higher learning. But in the hustle and bustle of everyday life its lyrics can always be heard above the din of the crowd. You will hear wisdom’s warning as she preaches courageously to those who stop to listen: 
Proverbs1:22 “Foolish ones, how much longer will you cling to your deception? How much longer will you mock wisdom, you cynical scorners who fight the facts?" 
Proverbs1:23 Come back to your senses and be restored to reality. Don’t even think about refusing my rebuke! Don’t you know that I’m ready to pour out my spirit of wisdom upon you and bring to you the revelation of my words that will make your heart wise? 
Proverbs1:24 I’ve called to you over and over; still you refuse to come to me. I’ve pleaded with you again and again, yet you’ve turned a deaf ear to my voice." 
Proverbs1:25 Because you have laughed at my counsel and have insisted on continuing in your stubbornness," 
Proverbs1:26 I will laugh when your calamity comes and will turn away from you at the time of your disaster. Make a joke of my advice, will you? Then I’ll make a joke out of you!" 
Proverbs1:27 When the storm clouds of terror gather over your head, when dread and distress consume you and your catastrophe comes like a hurricane," 
Proverbs1:28 you will cry out to me, but I won’t answer. Then it will be too late to expect my help. When desperation drives you to search for me, I will be nowhere to be found." 
Proverbs1:29 Because you have turned up your nose at me and closed your eyes to the facts and refused to worship me in awe — 
Proverbs1:30 because you scoffed at my wise counsel and laughed at my correction— 
Proverbs1:31 now you will eat the bitter fruit of your own ways. You’ve made your own bed; now lie in it! So how do you like that? 
Proverbs1:32 Like an idiot you’ve turned away from me and chosen destruction instead. Your self-satisfied smugness will kill you. 
Proverbs1:33 But the one who always listens to me will live undisturbed in a heavenly peace. Free from fear, confident and courageous, that one will rest unafraid and sheltered from the storms of life.”" 
Proverbs2:1 My child, will you treasure my wisdom? Then, and only then, will you acquire it. And only if you accept my advice and hide it within will you succeed." 
Proverbs2:2 So train your heart to listen when I speak and open your spirit wide to expand your discernment— then pass it on to your sons and daughters. 
Proverbs2:3 Yes, cry out for comprehension and intercede for insight." 
Proverbs2:4 For if you keep seeking it like a man would seek for sterling silver, searching in hidden places for cherished treasure," 
Proverbs2:5 then you will discover the fear of the Lord and find the true knowledge of God. 
Proverbs2:6 Wisdom is a gift from a generous God, and every word he speaks is full of revelation and becomes a fountain of understanding within you." 
Proverbs2:7 For the Lord has a hidden storehouse of wisdom made accessible to his godly ones. He becomes your personal bodyguard as you follow his ways, protecting and guarding you as you choose what is right." 
Proverbs2:8  
Proverbs2:9 Then you will discover all that is just, proper, and fair, and be empowered to make the right decisions as you walk into your destiny." 
Proverbs2:10 When wisdom wins your heart and revelation breaks in, true pleasure enters your soul." 
Proverbs2:11 If you choose to follow good counsel, divine design will watch over you and understanding will protect you from making poor choices." 
Proverbs2:12 It will rescue you from evil in disguise and from those who speak duplicities. 
Proverbs2:13 For they have left the paths of righteousness and walk in the ways of darkness. 
Proverbs2:14 They take pleasure when evil prospers and thoroughly enjoy a lifestyle of sin. 
Proverbs2:15 But they’re walking on a path to nowhere, wandering away into deeper deception." 
Proverbs2:16 Only wisdom can save you from the flattery of the promiscuous woman— she’s such a smooth-talking seductress! 
Proverbs2:17 She left her husband and has forgotten her wedding vows. 
Proverbs2:18 You’ll find her house on the road to hell," 
Proverbs2:19 and all the men who go through her doors will never come back to the place they were— they will find nothing but desolation and despair. 
Proverbs2:20 Follow those who follow wisdom and stay on the right path. 
Proverbs2:21 For all my godly lovers will enjoy life to the fullest and will inherit their destinies. 
Proverbs2:22 But the treacherous ones who love darkness will lose not only all they could have had, but even their own souls!" 
Proverbs3:1 My child, if you truly want a long and satisfying life, never forget the things that I’ve taught you. Follow closely every truth that I’ve given you. Then you will have a full, rewarding life." 
Proverbs3:2  
Proverbs3:3 Hold on to loyal love and don’t let go, and be faithful to all that you’ve been taught. Let your life be shaped by integrity, with truth written upon your heart." 
Proverbs3:4 That’s how you will find favor and understanding with both God and men— you will gain the reputation of living life well. 
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the Lord completely, and do not rely on your own opinions. With all your heart rely on him to guide you, and he will lead you in every decision you make." 
Proverbs3:6 Become intimate with him in whatever you do, and he will lead you wherever you go." 
Proverbs3:7 Don’t think for a moment that you know it all, for wisdom comes when you adore him with undivided devotion and avoid everything that’s wrong." 
Proverbs3:8 Then you will find the healing refreshment your body and spirit long for. 
Proverbs3:9 Glorify God with all your wealth, honoring him with your firstfruits, with every increase that comes to you." 
Proverbs3:10 Then every dimension of your life will overflow with blessings from an uncontainable source of inner joy! 
Proverbs3:11 My child, when the Lord God speaks to you, never take his words lightly, and never be upset when he corrects you." 
Proverbs3:12 For the Father’s discipline comes only from his passionate love and pleasure for you. Even when it seems like his correction is harsh, it’s still better than any father on earth gives to his child." 
Proverbs3:13 Blessings pour over the ones who find wisdom, for they have obtained living-understanding." 
Proverbs3:14 As wisdom increases, a great treasure is imparted, greater than many bars of refined gold." 
Proverbs3:15 It is a more valuable commodity than gold and gemstones, for there is nothing you desire that could compare to her." 
Proverbs3:16 Wisdom extends to you long life in one hand and wealth and promotion in the other. Out of her mouth flows righteousness, and her words release both law and mercy." 
Proverbs3:17 The ways of wisdom are sweet, always drawing you into the place of wholeness." 
Proverbs3:18 Seeking for her brings the discovery of untold blessings, for she is the healing tree of life to those who taste her fruits." 
Proverbs3:19 The Lord laid the earth’s foundations with wisdom’s blueprints. By his living-understanding all the universe came into being. 
Proverbs3:20 By his divine revelation he broke open the hidden fountains of the deep, bringing secret springs to the surface as the mist of the night dripped down from heaven." 
Proverbs3:21 My child, never drift off course from these two goals for your life: to walk in wisdom and to discover your purpose. Don’t ever forget how they empower you." 
Proverbs3:22 For they strengthen you inside and out and inspire you to do what’s right; you will be energized and refreshed by the healing they bring. 
Proverbs3:23 They give you living hope to guide you, and not one of life’s tests will cause you to stumble." 
Proverbs3:24 You will sleep like a baby, safe and sound— your rest will be sweet and secure." 
Proverbs3:25 You will not be subject to terror, for it will not terrify you. Nor will the disrespectful be able to push you aside," 
Proverbs3:26 because God is your confidence in times of crisis, keeping your heart at rest in every situation." 
Proverbs3:27 Why would you withhold payment on your debt when you have the ability to pay? Just do it! 
Proverbs3:28 When your friend comes to ask you for a favor, why would you say, “Perhaps tomorrow,” when you have the money right there in your pocket? Help him today!" 
Proverbs3:29 Why would you hold a grudge in your heart toward your neighbor who lives right next door? 
Proverbs3:30 And why would you quarrel with those who have done nothing wrong to you? Is that a chip on your shoulder? 
Proverbs3:31 Don’t act like those bullies or learn their ways. 
Proverbs3:32 Every violent thug is despised by the Lord, but every tender lover finds friendship with God and will hear his intimate secrets." 
Proverbs3:33 The wicked walk under God’s constant curse, but the righteous walk under a stream of his blessing, for they seek to do what is right." 
Proverbs3:34 If you walk with the mockers you will learn to mock, but God’s grace and favor flow to the meek." 
Proverbs3:35 Stubborn fools fill their lives with disgrace, but glory and honor rest upon the wise." 
Proverbs4:1 Listen to my correction, my sons, for I speak to you as your father. Let discernment enter your heart and you will grow wise with the understanding I impart." 
Proverbs4:2 My revelation-truth is a gift to you, so remain faithful to my instruction." 
Proverbs4:3 For I, too, was once the delight of my father and cherished by my mother—their beloved child." 
Proverbs4:4 Then my father taught me, saying, “Never forget my words. If you do everything that I teach you, you will reign in life.”" 
Proverbs4:5 So make wisdom your quest— search for the revelation of life’s meaning. Don’t let what I say go in one ear and out the other. 
Proverbs4:6 Stick with wisdom and she will stick to you, protecting you throughout your days. She will rescue all those who passionately listen to her voice." 
Proverbs4:7 Wisdom is the most valuable commodity—so buy it! Revelation-knowledge is what you need—so invest in it! 
Proverbs4:8 Wisdom will exalt you when you exalt her truth. She will lead you to honor and favor when you live your life by her insights. 
Proverbs4:9 You will be adorned with beauty and grace, and wisdom’s glory will wrap itself around you, making you victorious in the race." 
Proverbs4:10 My son, if you will take the time to stop and listen to me and embrace what I say, you will live a long and happy life full of understanding in every way." 
Proverbs4:11 I have taken you by the hand in wisdom’s ways, pointing you to the path of integrity." 
Proverbs4:12 Your progress will have no limits when you come along with me, and you will never stumble as you walk along the way." 
Proverbs4:13 So receive my correction no matter how hard it is to swallow, for wisdom will snap you back into place— her words will be invigorating life to you." 
Proverbs4:14 Do not detour into darkness or even set foot on that path. 
Proverbs4:15 Stay away from it; don’t even go there! 
Proverbs4:16 For troublemakers are restless if they are not involved in evil. They are not satisfied until they have brought someone harm. 
Proverbs4:17 They feed on darkness and drink until they’re drunk on the wine of wickedness. 
Proverbs4:18 But the lovers of God walk on the highway of light, and their way shines brighter and brighter until the perfect day." 
Proverbs4:19 But the wicked walk in thick darkness, like those who travel in fog, and yet don’t have a clue why they keep stumbling!" 
Proverbs4:20 Listen carefully, my dear child, to everything that I teach you, and pay attention to all that I have to say." 
Proverbs4:21 Fill your thoughts with my words until they penetrate deep into your spirit. 
Proverbs4:22 Then, as you unwrap my words, they will impart true life and radiant health into the very core of your being." 
Proverbs4:23 So above all, guard the affections of your heart, for they affect all that you are. Pay attention to the welfare of your innermost being, for from there flows the wellspring of life." 
Proverbs4:24 Avoid dishonest speech and pretentious words. Be free from using perverse words no matter what! 
Proverbs4:25 Set your gaze on the path before you. With fixed purpose, looking straight ahead, ignore life’s distractions." 
Proverbs4:26 Watch where you’re going! Stick to the path of truth, and the road will be safe and smooth before you." 
Proverbs4:27 Don’t allow yourself to be sidetracked for even a moment or take the detour that leads to darkness. 
Proverbs5:1 Listen to me, my son, for I know what I’m talking about. Listen carefully to my advice" 
Proverbs5:2 so that wisdom and discernment will enter your heart, and then the words you speak will express what you’ve learned." 
Proverbs5:3 Remember this: The lips of a seductress seem sweet like honey, and her smooth words are like music in your ears." 
Proverbs5:4 But I promise you this: In the end all you’ll be left with is a bitter conscience. For the sting of your sin will pierce your soul like a sword. 
Proverbs5:5 She will ruin your life, drag you down to death, and lead you straight to hell." 
Proverbs5:6 She has prevented many from considering the paths of life. Yes, she will take you with her where you don’t want to go, sliding down a slippery road and not even realizing where the two of you will end up!" 
Proverbs5:7 Listen to me, young men, and don’t forget this one thing I’m telling you— run away from her as fast as you can!" 
Proverbs5:8 Don’t even go near the door of her house unless you want to fall into her seduction. 
Proverbs5:9 In disgrace you will relinquish your honor to another, and all your remaining years will be squandered— given over to the cruel one." 
Proverbs5:10 Why would you let strangers take away your strength while the labors of your house go to someone else? 
Proverbs5:11 For when you grow old you will groan in anguish and shame as sexually transmitted diseases consume your body. 
Proverbs5:12 And then finally you’ll admit that you were wrong and say, “If only I had listened to wisdom’s voice and not stubbornly demanded my own way, because my heart hated to be told what to do!" 
Proverbs5:13 Why didn’t I take seriously the warning of my wise counselors? Why was I so stupid to think that I could get away with it? 
Proverbs5:14 Now I’m totally disgraced and my life is ruined! I’m paying the price— for the people of the congregation are now my judges.” 
Proverbs5:15 My son, share your love with your wife alone. Drink from her well of pleasure and from no other." 
Proverbs5:16 Why would you have sex with a stranger or with anyone other than her? 
Proverbs5:17 Reserve this pleasure for you and her alone and do not share it with another. 
Proverbs5:18 Your sex life will be blessed as you take joy and pleasure in the wife of your youth. 
Proverbs5:19 Let her breasts be your satisfaction, and let her embrace intoxicate you at all times. Be continually delighted and ravished with her love!" 
Proverbs5:20 My son, why would you be exhilarated by an adulteress— by embracing a woman who is not yours?" 
Proverbs5:21 For God sees everything you do and his eyes are wide open as he observes every single habit you have. 
Proverbs5:22 Beware that your sins don’t overtake you and that the scars of your own conscience don’t become the ropes that tie you up. 
Proverbs5:23 Those who choose wickedness die for lack of self-control, for their foolish ways lead them astray, carrying them away as hostages— kidnapped captives robbed of destiny." 
Proverbs6:1 My son, if you cosign a loan for an acquaintance and guarantee his debt, you’ll be sorry that you ever did it!" 
Proverbs6:2 You’ll be trapped by your promise and legally bound by the agreement. So listen carefully to my advice: 
Proverbs6:3 Quickly get out of it if you possibly can! Swallow your pride, get over your embarrassment, and go tell your “friend” you want your name off that contract." 
Proverbs6:4 Don’t put it off, and don’t rest until you get it done." 
Proverbs6:5 Rescue yourself from future pain and be free from it once and for all. You’ll be so relieved that you did! 
Proverbs6:6 When you’re feeling lazy, come and learn a lesson from this tale of the tiny ant. Yes, all you lazybones, come learn from the example of the ant and enter into wisdom." 
Proverbs6:7 The ants have no chief, no boss, no manager— no one has to tell them what to do." 
Proverbs6:8 You’ll see them working and toiling all summer long, stockpiling their food in preparation for winter." 
Proverbs6:9 So wake up, sleepyhead. How long will you lie there? When will you wake up and get out of bed?" 
Proverbs6:10 If you keep nodding off and thinking, “I’ll do it later,” or say to yourself, “I’ll just sit back awhile and take it easy,” just watch how the future unfolds!" 
Proverbs6:11 By making excuses you’ll learn what it means to go without. Poverty will pounce on you like a bandit and move in as your roommate for life. 
Proverbs6:12 Here’s another life lesson to learn from observing wayward and wicked men. You can tell they are lawless. They’re constant liars, proud deceivers, full of clever ploys and convincing plots." 
Proverbs6:13  
Proverbs6:14 Their twisted thoughts are perverse, and they are always scheming to stir up trouble, and sowing strife with every step they take." 
Proverbs6:15 But when calamity comes knocking on their door, suddenly and without warning they’re undone— broken to bits, shattered, with no hope of healing." 
Proverbs6:16 There are six evils God truly hates and a seventh that is an abomination to him: 
Proverbs6:17 Putting others down while considering yourself superior, spreading lies and rumors, spilling the blood of the innocent," 
Proverbs6:18 plotting evil in your heart toward another, gloating over doing what’s plainly wrong," 
Proverbs6:19 spouting lies in false testimony, and stirring up strife between friends. These are entirely despicable to God!" 
Proverbs6:20 My son, obey your father’s godly instruction and follow your mother’s life-giving teaching." 
Proverbs6:21 Fill your heart with their advice and let your life be shaped by what they’ve taught you. 
Proverbs6:22 Their wisdom will guide you wherever you go and keep you from bringing harm to yourself. Their instruction will whisper to you at every sunrise and direct you through a brand-new day. 
Proverbs6:23 For truth is a bright beam of light shining into every area of your life, instructing and correcting you to discover the ways to godly living." 
Proverbs6:24 Truth will protect you from immorality and from the promiscuity of another man’s wife. Your heart won’t be enticed by her flatteries or lust over her beauty— nor will her suggestive ways conquer you. 
Proverbs6:25  
Proverbs6:26 Prostitutes reduce a man to poverty, and the adulteress steals your soul— she may even cost you your life!" 
Proverbs6:27 For how can a man light his pants on fire and not be burned? 
Proverbs6:28 Can he walk over hot coals of fire and not blister his feet? 
Proverbs6:29 What makes you think that you can sleep with another man’s wife and not get caught? Do you really think you’ll get away with it? Don’t you know it will ruin your life? 
Proverbs6:30 You can almost excuse a thief if he steals to feed his own family. 
Proverbs6:31 But if he’s caught, he still has to pay back what he stole sevenfold; his punishment and fine will cost him greatly." 
Proverbs6:32 Don’t be so stupid as to think you can get away with your adultery. It will destroy your life, and you’ll pay the price for the rest of your days." 
Proverbs6:33 You’ll discover what humiliation, shame, and disgrace are all about, for no one will ever let you forget what you’ve done." 
Proverbs6:34 A husband’s jealousy makes a man furious; he won’t spare you when he comes to take revenge. 
Proverbs6:35 Try all you want to talk your way out of it— offer him a bribe and see if you can manipulate him with your money. Nothing will turn him aside when he comes to you with vengeance in his eyes! 
Proverbs7:1 Stick close to my instruction, my son, and follow all my advice." 
Proverbs7:2 If you do what I say you will live well. Guard your life with my revelation-truth, for my teaching is as precious as your eyesight." 
Proverbs7:3 Treasure my instructions, and cherish them within your heart." 
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom, “I love you,” and to understanding, “You’re my sweetheart.”" 
Proverbs7:5 “May the two of you protect me, and may we never be apart!” For they will keep you from the adulteress, with her smooth words meant to seduce your heart." 
Proverbs7:6 Looking out the window of my house one day 
Proverbs7:7 I noticed among the mindless crowd a simple, naïve young man who was about to go astray." 
Proverbs7:8 There he was, walking down the street. Then he turned the corner, going on his way hurrying to the house of the harlot— the woman he had planned to meet." 
Proverbs7:9 There he was in the twilight as darkness fell, convinced no one was watching as he entered the black shadows of hell." 
Proverbs7:10 That’s when their rendezvous began. A woman of the night appeared, dressed to kill the strength of any man. She was decked out as a harlot, pursuing her amorous plan." 
Proverbs7:11 Her voice was seductive, rebellious, and boisterous as she wandered far from what’s right." 
Proverbs7:12 Her type can be found soliciting on street corners on just about any night. 
Proverbs7:13 She wrapped her arms around the senseless young man and held him tight— she enticed him with kisses that seemed so right. Then, with insolence, she whispered in his ear," 
Proverbs7:14 “Come with me. It’ll be all right. I’ve got everything we need for a feast. I’ll cook you a wonderful dinner. So here I am—I’m all yours! 
Proverbs7:15 You’re the very one I’ve looked for, the one I knew I wanted from the moment I saw you. That’s why I’ve come out here tonight, so I could meet a man just like you." 
Proverbs7:16 I’ve spread my canopy bed with coverings, lovely multicolored Egyptian linens ready for you to lie down on." 
Proverbs7:17 I’ve sprinkled the sheets with intoxicating perfume made from myrrh, aloes, and sweet cinnamon." 
Proverbs7:18 Come, let’s get comfortable and take pleasure in each other and make love all night!" 
Proverbs7:19 There’s no one home, for my husband’s away on business." 
Proverbs7:20 He left home loaded with money to spend, so don’t worry. He won’t be back until another month ends.”" 
Proverbs7:21 He was swayed by her sophistication, enticed by her longing embrace. She led him down the wayward path right into sin and disgrace. Quickly he went astray, with no clue where he was truly headed, taken like a dumb ox alongside the butcher. She was like a venomous snake coiled to strike, so she set her fangs into him!" 
Proverbs7:22  
Proverbs7:23 He’s like a man about to be executed with an arrow right through his heart— like a bird that flies into the net, unaware of what’s about to happen." 
Proverbs7:24 So listen to me, you young men. You’d better take my words seriously!" 
Proverbs7:25 Control your sexual urges and guard your hearts against lust. Don’t let your passions get out of hand and don’t lock your eyes onto a beautiful woman. Why would you want to even get close to temptation and seduction, to have an affair with her?" 
Proverbs7:26 She has pierced the souls of multitudes of men— many mighty ones have fallen and have been brought down by her. 
Proverbs7:27 If you’re looking for the road to hell, just go looking for her house!" 
Proverbs8:1 Can’t you hear the voice of Wisdom? From the top of the mountains of influence she speaks into the gateways of the glorious city. At the place where pathways merge, at the entrance of every portal, there she stands, ready to impart understanding, shouting aloud to all who enter, preaching her sermon to those who will listen." 
Proverbs8:2  
Proverbs8:3  
Proverbs8:4 “I’m calling to you, sons of Adam, yes, and to you daughters as well." 
Proverbs8:5 Listen to me and you will be prudent and wise. For even the foolish and feeble can receive an understanding heart that will change their inner being. 
Proverbs8:6 The meaning of my words will release within you revelation for you to reign in life. My lyrics will empower you to live by what is right. 
Proverbs8:7 For everything I say is unquestionably true, and I refuse to endure the lies of lawlessness— my words will never lead you astray." 
Proverbs8:8 All the declarations of my mouth can be trusted; they contain no twisted logic or perversion of the truth. 
Proverbs8:9 All my words are clear and straightforward to everyone who possesses spiritual understanding. If you have an open mind, you will receive revelation-knowledge." 
Proverbs8:10 My wise correction is more valuable than silver or gold. The finest gold is nothing compared to the revelation-knowledge I can impart.” 
Proverbs8:11 Wisdom is so priceless that it exceeds the value of any jewel. Nothing you could wish for can equal her. 
Proverbs8:12 “For I am Wisdom, and I am shrewd and intelligent. I have at my disposal living-understanding to devise a plan for your life." 
Proverbs8:13 Wisdom pours into you when you begin to hate every form of evil in your life, for that’s what worship and fearing God is all about. Then you will discover that your pompous pride and perverse speech are the very ways of wickedness that I hate!”" 
Proverbs8:14 “You will find true success when you find me, for I have insight into wise plans that are designed just for you. I hold in my hands living-understanding, courage, and strength." 
Proverbs8:15 I empower kings to reign and rulers to make laws that are just. 
Proverbs8:16 I empower princes to rise and take dominion, and generous ones to govern the earth." 
Proverbs8:17 I will show my love to those who passionately love me. For they will search and search continually until they find me. 
Proverbs8:18 Unending wealth and glory come to those who discover where I dwell. The riches of righteousness and a long, satisfying life will be given to them." 
Proverbs8:19 What I impart has greater worth than gold and treasure, and the increase I bring benefits more than a windfall of income." 
Proverbs8:20 I lead you into the ways of righteousness to discover the paths of true justice. 
Proverbs8:21 Those who love me gain great wealth and a glorious inheritance, and I will fill their lives with treasures.”" 
Proverbs8:22 “In the beginning I was there, for God possessed me even before he created the universe." 
Proverbs8:23 From eternity past I was set in place, before the world began. I was anointed from the beginning." 
Proverbs8:24 Before the oceans depths were poured out, and before there were any glorious fountains overflowing with water, I was there, dancing!" 
Proverbs8:25 Even before one mountain had been sculpted or one hill raised up, I was already there, dancing!" 
Proverbs8:26 When he created the earth, the fields, even the first atom of dust, I was already there." 
Proverbs8:27 When he hung the tapestry of the heavens and stretched out the horizon of the earth," 
Proverbs8:28 when the clouds and skies were set in place and the subterranean fountains began to flow strong, I was already there." 
Proverbs8:29 When he set in place the pillars of the earth and spoke the decrees of the seas, commanding the waves so that they wouldn’t overstep their boundaries," 
Proverbs8:30 I was there, close to the Creator’s side as his master artist. Daily he was filled with delight in me as I playfully rejoiced before him." 
Proverbs8:31 I laughed and played, so happy with what he had made, while finding my delight in the children of men.”" 
Proverbs8:32 “So listen, my sons and daughters, to everything I tell you, for nothing will bring you more joy than following my ways." 
Proverbs8:33 Listen to my counsel, for my instruction will enlighten you. You’ll be wise not to ignore it." 
Proverbs8:34 If you wait at wisdom’s doorway, longing to hear a word for every day, joy will break forth within you as you listen for what I’ll say." 
Proverbs8:35 For the fountain of life pours into you every time that you find me, and this is the secret of growing in the delight and the favor of the Lord." 
Proverbs8:36 But those who stumble and miss me will be sorry they did! For ignoring what I have to say will bring harm to your own soul. Those who hate me are simply flirting with death!” 
Proverbs9:1 Wisdom has built herself a palace upon seven pillars to keep it secure. 
Proverbs9:2 She has made ready a banquet feast and the sacrifice has been killed. She has mingled her wine, and the table’s all set." 
Proverbs9:3 She has sent out her maidens, crying out from the high place, inviting everyone to come and eat until they’re full." 
Proverbs9:4 “Whoever wants to know me and receive my wisdom," 
Proverbs9:5 come and dine at my table and drink of my wine. 
Proverbs9:6 Lay aside your simple thoughts and leave your paths behind. Agree with my ways, live in my truth, and you will find righteousness.”" 
Proverbs9:7 If you try to correct an arrogant cynic, expect an angry insult in return. And if you try to confront an evil man, don’t be surprised if all you get is a slap in the face!" 
Proverbs9:8 So don’t even bother to correct a mocker, for he’ll only hate you for it. But go ahead and correct the wise; they’ll love you even more." 
Proverbs9:9 Teach a wise man what is right and he’ll grow even wiser. Instruct the lovers of God and they’ll learn even more. 
Proverbs9:10 The starting point for acquiring wisdom is to be consumed with awe as you worship Yahweh. To receive the revelation of the Holy One, you must come to the one who has living-understanding." 
Proverbs9:11 Wisdom will extend your life, making every year more fruitful than the one before." 
Proverbs9:12 So it is to your advantage to be wise. But to ignore the counsel of wisdom is to invite trouble into your life. 
Proverbs9:13 There is a spirit named Foolish, who is boisterous and brash; she’s seductive and restless." 
Proverbs9:14 And there she sits at the gateway to the high places, on her throne overlooking the city." 
Proverbs9:15 She preaches to all who walk by her who are clueless as to what is happening: 
Proverbs9:16 “Come home with me.” She invites those who are easily led astray, saying," 
Proverbs9:17 “Illicit sex is the best sex of all. Our secret affair will be sweeter than all others.” 
Proverbs9:18 Little do they know when they answer her call that she dwells among the spirits of the dead, and all her guests soon become citizens of hell!" 
Proverbs10:1 The wisdom of Solomon: When wisdom comes to a son, joy comes to a father. When a son turns from wisdom, a mother grieves." 
Proverbs10:2 Gaining wealth through dishonesty is no gain at all. But honesty brings you a lasting happiness. 
Proverbs10:3 The Lord satisfies the longings of all his lovers, but he withholds from the wicked what their souls crave." 
Proverbs10:4 Slackers will know what it means to be poor, while the hard worker becomes wealthy." 
Proverbs10:5 Know the importance of the season you’re in and a wise son you will be. But what a waste when an incompetent son sleeps through his day of opportunity! 
Proverbs10:6 The lover of God is enriched beyond belief, but the evil man only curses his luck." 
Proverbs10:7 The reputation of the righteous becomes a sweet memorial to him, while the wicked life only leaves a rotten stench." 
Proverbs10:8 The heart of the wise will easily accept instruction. But those who do all the talking are too busy to listen and learn. They’ll just keep stumbling ahead into the mess they created. 
Proverbs10:9 The one who walks in integrity will experience a fearless confidence in life, but the one who is devious will eventually be exposed." 
Proverbs10:10 The troublemaker always has a clever plan and won’t look you in the eye, but the one who speaks correction honestly can be trusted to make peace." 
Proverbs10:11 The teachings of the lovers of God are like living truth flowing from the fountain of life, but the words of the wicked hide an ulterior motive." 
Proverbs10:12 Hatred keeps old quarrels alive, but love draws a veil over every insult and finds a way to make sin disappear." 
Proverbs10:13 Words of wisdom flow from the one with true discernment. But to the heartless, words of wisdom become like rods beating their backside." 
Proverbs10:14 Wise men don’t divulge all that they know, but chattering fools blurt out words that bring them to the brink of ruin." 
Proverbs10:15 A rich man’s wealth becomes like a citadel of strength, but the poverty of the poor leaves their security in shambles." 
Proverbs10:16 The lovers of God earn their wages for a life of righteousness, but the wages of the wicked are squandered on a life of sin." 
Proverbs10:17 If you readily receive correction, you are walking on the path to life. But if you reject rebuke, you’re guaranteed to go astray." 
Proverbs10:18 The one who hides his hatred while pretending to be your friend is nothing but a liar. But the one who slanders you behind your back proves that he’s a fool, never to be trusted." 
Proverbs10:19 If you keep talking, it won’t be long before you’re saying something really wrong. Prove you’re wise from the very start— just bite your tongue and be strong!" 
Proverbs10:20 The teachings of the godly ones are like pure silver, bringing words of redemption to others, but the heart of the wicked is corrupt." 
Proverbs10:21 The lovers of God feed many with their teachings, but the foolish ones starve themselves for lack of an understanding heart." 
Proverbs10:22 True enrichment comes from the blessing of the Lord, with rest and contentment in knowing that it all comes from him." 
Proverbs10:23 The fool finds fun in planning to do wrong, but the wise delight in having discernment." 
Proverbs10:24 The lawless are haunted by their fears and what they dread will come upon them, but the longings of the lovers of God will all be fulfilled." 
Proverbs10:25 The wicked are blown away by every stormy wind. But when a catastrophe comes, the lovers of God have a secure anchor." 
Proverbs10:26 To trust a lazy person to get a job done will be as irritating as smoke in your eyes— as enjoyable as a toothache! 
Proverbs10:27 Living in the worship and awe of God will bring you many years of contented living. So how could the wicked ever expect to have a long, happy life?" 
Proverbs10:28 Lovers of God have a joyful feast of gladness, but the ungodly see their hopes vanish right before their eyes." 
Proverbs10:29 The beautiful ways of God are a safe resting place for those who have integrity. But to those who work wickedness the ways of God spell doom. 
Proverbs10:30 God’s lover can never be greatly shaken. But the wicked will never inherit the covenant blessings. 
Proverbs10:31 The teachings of the righteous are loaded with wisdom, but the words of the evil ones are crooked and perverse." 
Proverbs10:32 Words that bring delight pour from the lips of the godly, but the words of the wicked are duplicitous." 
Proverbs11:1 Dishonest business practice is something that Yahweh truly hates. But it pleases him when we apply the right standards of measurement. 
Proverbs11:2 When you act with presumption, convinced that you’re right, don’t be surprised if you fall flat on your face! But humility leads to wisdom." 
Proverbs11:3 Integrity will lead you to success, but treachery will destroy your dreams." 
Proverbs11:4 When judgment day comes, all the wealth of the world won’t help you one bit. So be rich in righteousness, for that’s the only thing that can save you in death." 
Proverbs11:5 Those with good character walk on a smooth path, with no detour or deviation. But the wicked keep falling because of their own wickedness." 
Proverbs11:6 Integrity will keep a good man from falling. But the unbeliever is trapped, held captive to his sinful desires." 
Proverbs11:7 When an evil man dies, all hope is lost, for his misplaced confidence goes in the coffin, buried along with him." 
Proverbs11:8 The righteous are snatched away from trouble, and the wicked show up in their place." 
Proverbs11:9 The teachings of hypocrites can destroy you, but revelation-knowledge will rescue the righteous." 
Proverbs11:10 The blessing that rests on the righteous releases strength and favor to the entire city, but shouts of joy will be heard when the wicked one dies." 
Proverbs11:11 The blessing of favor resting upon the righteous influences a city to lift it higher, but wicked leaders tear it apart by their words." 
Proverbs11:12 To quarrel with a neighbor is senseless. Bite your tongue; be wise and keep quiet! 
Proverbs11:13 You can’t trust gossipers with a secret; they’ll just go blab it all. Put your confidence instead in a trusted friend, for he will be faithful to keep it in confidence." 
Proverbs11:14 People lose their way without wise leadership, but a nation succeeds and stands in victory when it has many good counselors to guide it." 
Proverbs11:15 The evil man will do harm when confronted by a righteous man, because he hates those who await good news." 
Proverbs11:16 A gracious, generous woman will be honored with a splendid reputation, but the woman who hates the truth lives with disgrace and is surrounded by men who are cutthroats, only greedy for money." 
Proverbs11:17 A man of kindness attracts favor, while a cruel man attracts nothing but trouble." 
Proverbs11:18 Evil people may get a short-term gain, but to sow seeds of righteousness will bring a true and lasting reward." 
Proverbs11:19 A son of righteousness experiences the abundant life, but the one who pursues evil hurries to his own death." 
Proverbs11:20 The Lord can’t stand the stubborn heart bent toward evil, but he treasures those whose ways are pure." 
Proverbs11:21 Assault your neighbor and you will certainly be punished, but God will rescue the children of the godly." 
Proverbs11:22 A beautiful woman who abandons good morals is like a fine gold ring dangling from a pig’s snout. 
Proverbs11:23 True lovers of God are filled with longings for what is pleasing and good, but the wicked can only expect doom." 
Proverbs11:24 Generosity brings prosperity, but withholding from charity brings poverty." 
Proverbs11:25 Those who live to bless others will have blessings heaped upon them, and the one who pours out his life to pour out blessings will be saturated with favor." 
Proverbs11:26 People will curse the businessman with no ethics, but the one with a social conscience receives praise from all." 
Proverbs11:27 Living your life seeking what is good for others brings untold favor, but those who wish evil for others will find it coming back on them." 
Proverbs11:28 Keep trusting in your riches and down you’ll go! But the lovers of God rise up like flowers in the spring. 
Proverbs11:29 The fool who brings trouble to his own family will be cut out of the will, and the family servant will do better than he." 
Proverbs11:30 But a life lived loving God bears lasting fruit, for the one who is truly wise wins souls." 
Proverbs11:31 If the righteous are barely saved, what’s in store for all the wicked?" 
Proverbs12:1 To learn the truth you must long to be teachable, or you can despise correction and remain ignorant." 
Proverbs12:2 If your heart is right, favor flows from the Lord, but a devious heart invites his condemnation." 
Proverbs12:3 You can’t expect success by doing what’s wrong. But the lives of his lovers are deeply rooted and firmly planted. 
Proverbs12:4 The integrity and strength of a virtuous wife transforms her husband into an honored king. But the wife who disgraces her husband weakens the strength of his identity. 
Proverbs12:5 The lovers of God are filled with good ideas that are noble and pure, but the schemes of the sinner are crammed with nothing but lies." 
Proverbs12:6 The wicked use their words to ambush and accuse, but the lovers of God speak to defend and protect." 
Proverbs12:7 The wicked are taken out, gone for good, but the godly families shall live on." 
Proverbs12:8 Everyone admires a man of principles, but the one with a corrupt heart is despised." 
Proverbs12:9 Just be who you are and work hard for a living, for that’s better than pretending to be important and starving to death." 
Proverbs12:10 A good man takes care of the needs of his pets, while even the kindest acts of a wicked man are still cruel." 
Proverbs12:11 Work hard at your job and you’ll have what you need. Following a get-rich-quick scheme is nothing but a fantasy. 
Proverbs12:12 The cravings of the wicked are only for what is evil, but righteousness is the core motivation for the lovers of God, and it keeps them content and flourishing." 
Proverbs12:13 The wicked will get trapped by their words of gossip, slander, and lies. But for the righteous, honesty is its own defense." 
Proverbs12:14 For there is great satisfaction in speaking the truth, and hard work brings blessings back to you." 
Proverbs12:15 A fool is in love with his own opinion, but wisdom means being teachable." 
Proverbs12:16 If you shrug off an insult and refuse to take offense, you demonstrate discretion indeed. But the fool has a short fuse and will immediately let you know when he’s offended." 
Proverbs12:17 Truthfulness marks the righteous, but the habitual liar can never be trusted." 
Proverbs12:18 Reckless words are like the thrusts of a sword, cutting remarks meant to stab and to hurt. But the words of the wise soothe and heal." 
Proverbs12:19 Truthful words will stand the test of time, but one day every lie will be seen for what it is." 
Proverbs12:20 Deception fills the hearts of those who plot harm, but those who plan for peace are filled with joy." 
Proverbs12:21 Calamity is not allowed to overwhelm the righteous, but there’s nothing but trouble waiting for the wicked." 
Proverbs12:22 Live in the truth and keep your promises, and the Lord will keep delighting in you, but he detests a liar." 
Proverbs12:23 Those who possess wisdom don’t feel the need to impress others with what they know, but foolish ones make sure their ignorance is on display." 
Proverbs12:24 If you want to reign in life, don’t sit on your hands. Instead, work hard at doing what’s right, for the slacker will end up working to make someone else succeed." 
Proverbs12:25 Anxious fear brings depression, but a life-giving word of encouragement can do wonders to restore joy to the heart." 
Proverbs12:26 Lovers of God give good advice to their friends, but the counsel of the wicked will lead them astray." 
Proverbs12:27 A passive person won’t even complete a project, but a passionate person makes good use of his time, wealth, and energy." 
Proverbs12:28 Abundant life is discovered by walking in righteousness, but holding on to your anger leads to death." 
Proverbs13:1 A wise son or daughter desires a father’s discipline, but the know-it-all never listens to correction." 
Proverbs13:2 The words of the wise are kind and easy to swallow, but the unbeliever just wants to pick a fight and argue." 
Proverbs13:3 Guard your words and you’ll guard your life, but if you don’t control your tongue, it will ruin everything." 
Proverbs13:4 The slacker wants it all and ends up with nothing, but the hard worker ends up with all that he longed for." 
Proverbs13:5 Lovers of God hate what is phony and false, but the wicked are full of shame and behave shamefully." 
Proverbs13:6 Righteousness is like a shield of protection, guarding those who keep their integrity, but sin is the downfall of the wicked." 
Proverbs13:7 One pretends to be rich but is poor. Another pretends to be poor but is quite rich. 
Proverbs13:8 The self-assurance of the rich is their money, but people don’t kidnap and extort the poor!" 
Proverbs13:9 The virtues of God’s lovers shine brightly in the darkness, but the flickering lamp of the ungodly will be extinguished." 
Proverbs13:10 Wisdom opens your heart to receive wise counsel, but pride closes your ears to advice and gives birth only to quarrels and strife." 
Proverbs13:11 Wealth quickly gained is quickly wasted — easy come, easy go! But if you gradually gain wealth, you will watch it grow." 
Proverbs13:12 When hope’s dream seems to drag on and on, the delay can be depressing. But when at last your dream comes true, life’s sweetness will satisfy your soul." 
Proverbs13:13 Despise the word, will you? Then you’ll pay the price and it won’t be pretty! But the one who honors the Father’s holy instructions will be rewarded." 
Proverbs13:14 When the lovers of God teach you truth, a fountain of life opens up within you, and their wise instruction will deliver you from the ways of death." 
Proverbs13:15 Everyone admires a wise, sensible person, but the treacherous walk on the path of ruin." 
Proverbs13:16 Everything a wise and shrewd man does comes from a source of revelation-knowledge, but the behavior of a fool puts foolishness on parade!" 
Proverbs13:17 An undependable messenger causes a lot of trouble, but the trustworthy and wise messengers release healing wherever they go." 
Proverbs13:18 Poverty and disgrace come to the one who refuses to hear criticism. But the one who is easy to correct is on the path of honor. 
Proverbs13:19 When God fulfills your longings, sweetness fills your soul. But the wicked refuse to turn from darkness to see their desires come to pass." 
Proverbs13:20 If you want to grow in wisdom, spend time with the wise. Walk with the wicked and you’ll eventually become just like them." 
Proverbs13:21 Calamity chases the sin-chaser, but prosperity pursues the God-lover." 
Proverbs13:22 The benevolent man leaves an inheritance that endures to his children’s children, but the wealth of the wicked is treasured up for the righteous." 
Proverbs13:23 The lovers of God will live a long life and get to enjoy their wealth, but the ungodly will suddenly perish." 
Proverbs13:24 If you withhold correction and punishment from your children, you demonstrate a lack of true love. So prove your love and be prompt to punish them." 
Proverbs13:25 The lovers of God will have more than enough, but the wicked will always lack what they crave." 
Proverbs14:1 Every wise woman encourages and builds up her family, but a foolish woman over time will tear it down by her own actions." 
Proverbs14:2 Lovers of truth follow the right path because of their wonderment and worship of God. But the devious display their disdain for him. 
Proverbs14:3 The words of a proud fool will all come back to haunt him. But the words of the wise will become a shield of protection around them. 
Proverbs14:4 The only clean stable is an empty stable. So if you want the work of an ox and to enjoy an abundant harvest, you’ll have a mess or two to clean up!" 
Proverbs14:5 An honest witness will never lie, but a deceitful witness lies with every breath." 
Proverbs14:6 The intellectually arrogant seek for wisdom, but they never seem to discover what they claim they’re looking for. For revelation-knowledge flows to the one who hungers for understanding." 
Proverbs14:7 The words of the wise are like weapons of knowledge. If you need wise counsel, stay away from the fool." 
Proverbs14:8 For the wisdom of the wise will keep life on the right track, while the fool only deceives himself and refuses to face reality." 
Proverbs14:9 Fools mock the need for repentance, while the favor of God rests upon all his lovers." 
Proverbs14:10 Don’t expect anyone else to fully understand both the bitterness and the joys of all you experience in your life. 
Proverbs14:11 The household of the wicked is soon torn apart, while the family of the righteous flourishes." 
Proverbs14:12 You can rationalize it all you want and justify the path of error you have chosen, but you’ll find out in the end that you took the road to destruction." 
Proverbs14:13 Superficial laughter can hide a heavy heart, but when the laughter ends, the pain resurfaces." 
Proverbs14:14 Those who turn from the truth get what they deserve, but a good person receives a sweet reward." 
Proverbs14:15 A gullible person will believe anything, but a sensible person will confirm the facts." 
Proverbs14:16 A wise person is careful in all things and turns quickly from evil, while the impetuous fool moves ahead with overconfidence." 
Proverbs14:17 An impulsive person has a short fuse and can ruin everything, but the wise show self-control." 
Proverbs14:18 The naïve demonstrate a lack of wisdom, but the lovers of wisdom are crowned with revelation-knowledge." 
Proverbs14:19 Evil ones will pay tribute to good people and eventually come to be servants of the godly. 
Proverbs14:20 The poor are disliked even by their neighbors, but everyone wants to get close to the wealthy." 
Proverbs14:21 It’s a sin to despise one who is less fortunate than you, but when you are kind to the poor, you will prosper and be blessed." 
Proverbs14:22 Haven’t you noticed how evil schemers always wander astray? But kindness and truth come to those who make plans to be pure in all their ways. 
Proverbs14:23 If you work hard at what you do, great abundance will come to you. But merely talking about getting rich while living to only pursue your pleasures brings you face-to-face with poverty." 
Proverbs14:24 The true net worth of the wise is the wealth that wisdom imparts. But the way of life for the fool is his foolishness. 
Proverbs14:25 Speak the truth and you’ll save souls, but in the spreading of lies treachery thrives." 
Proverbs14:26 Confidence and strength flood the hearts of the lovers of God who live in awe of him, and their devotion provides their children with a place of shelter and security." 
Proverbs14:27 To worship God in wonder and awe opens a fountain of life within you, empowering you to escape death’s domain." 
Proverbs14:28 A king glories in the number of his loyal followers, but a dwindling population spells ruin for any leader." 
Proverbs14:29 When your heart overflows with understanding you’ll be very slow to get angry. But if you have a quick temper, your impatience will be quickly seen by all." 
Proverbs14:30 A tender, tranquil heart will make you healthy, but jealousy can make you sick." 
Proverbs14:31 Insult your Creator, will you? That’s exactly what you do every time you oppress the powerless! Showing kindness to the poor is equal to honoring your maker." 
Proverbs14:32 The wicked are crushed by every calamity, but the righteous find a strong hope in the time of death." 
Proverbs14:33 Wisdom soothes the heart of the one with living-understanding, but the heart of the fool just stockpiles stupidity." 
Proverbs14:34 A nation is exalted by the righteousness of its people, but sin heaps disgrace upon the land." 
Proverbs14:35 A wise and faithful servant receives promotion from the king, but the one who acts disgracefully gets to taste the anger of the king." 
Proverbs15:1 Respond gently when you are confronted and you’ll defuse the rage of another. Responding with sharp, cutting words will only make it worse. Don’t you know that being angry can ruin the testimony of even the wisest of men?" 
Proverbs15:2 When wisdom speaks, understanding becomes attractive. But the words of the fool make their ignorance look laughable." 
Proverbs15:3 The eyes of the Lord are everywhere and he takes note of everything that happens. He watches over his lovers, and he also sees the wickedness of the wicked." 
Proverbs15:4 When you speak healing words, you offer others fruit from the tree of life. But unhealthy, negative words do nothing but crush their hopes." 
Proverbs15:5 You’re stupid to mock the instruction of a father, but welcoming correction will make you brilliant." 
Proverbs15:6 There is prosperity in the house of the righteous, but the house of the wicked is filled with trouble, no matter how much money they have." 
Proverbs15:7 When wisdom speaks, revelation-knowledge is released, but finding true wisdom in the word of a fool is futile." 
Proverbs15:8 It is despicable to the Lord when people use the worship of the Almighty as a cloak for their sin, but every prayer of the righteous is pleasing to his heart." 
Proverbs15:9 The Lord detests the lifestyle of the wicked, but he loves those who pursue purity." 
Proverbs15:10 Severe punishment awaits the one who turns away from the truth, and those who rebel against correction will die." 
Proverbs15:11 Even hell itself holds no secrets from the Lord God, for before his eyes, all is exposed— and so much more the heart of every human being." 
Proverbs15:12 The know-it-all never esteems the one who tries to correct him. He refuses to seek good advice from the wise. 
Proverbs15:13 A cheerful heart puts a smile on your face, but a broken heart leads to depression." 
Proverbs15:14 Lovers of God hunger after truth, but those without understanding feast on foolishness and don’t even realize it." 
Proverbs15:15 Everything seems to go wrong when you feel weak and depressed. But when you choose to be cheerful, every day will bring you more and more joy and fullness." 
Proverbs15:16 It’s much better to live simply, surrounded in holy awe and worship of God, than to have great wealth with a home full of trouble." 
Proverbs15:17 It’s much better to have a meal of vegetables surrounded with love and grace than a steak where there is hate. 
Proverbs15:18 A touchy, hot-tempered man picks a fight, but the calm, patient man knows how to silence strife." 
Proverbs15:19 Nothing seems to work right for the lazy man, but life seems smooth and easy when your heart is virtuous." 
Proverbs15:20 When a son learns wisdom, a father’s heart is glad. But the man who shames his mother is a foolish son."
