Lamentations2:11 My eyes have no tears left, and my stomach is tied in knots. My heart is spilled out into the dirt over the destruction of the daughter of my people. Little toddlers and children collapse in the streets of the city." 
Lamentations2:12 They keep saying, “Mommy, I’m hungry,” as they faint like wounded men in the city streets. Their souls trickle away, and they breathe their last in the embrace of their mothers." 
Lamentations2:13 With what can I compare you? What can I say to you, beloved Jerusalem? To what can I liken you, in order to comfort you, fair Zion? For your wound is as deep as the sea. Who can heal you?" 
Lamentations2:14 The visions your prophets saw for you were false and deceptive whitewash. They neither exposed your guilt nor brought you back in repentance. The false visions they told you were nothing but delusions. 
Lamentations2:15 All who pass your way applaud in mockery. They wag their heads with contempt, saying over beloved Jerusalem, “Is this really the city called The Perfection of Beauty, The Joy of All the Earth?”" 
Lamentations2:16 Your enemies are a choir of scoffers and scolders. They hatefully sneer and glare at you. They gleefully exclaim, “We have destroyed her. This is the day we’ve been waiting for, and now we have lived to see it!”" 
Lamentations2:17 Yahweh has done everything he planned and has carried out his word. As he ordained long ago, he has destroyed without pity. He increased the power of your foes and caused your enemies to rejoice over you." 
Lamentations2:18 Beloved Zion, let your walls wail and your heart cry out to Yahweh. Let a waterfall of tears flow day and night. Give your eyes no rest; let tears keep streaming from your eyes." 
Lamentations2:19 Arise, cry aloud through the night as each watch begins. Pour your heart out like water before the presence of Yahweh. Raise your hands toward him; intercede for the lives of your children, who faint with hunger on every street corner." 
Lamentations2:20 “Look, Yahweh, and please consider. Whom have you ever treated this way? Should women eat their offspring, the little ones they have nursed? Should priests and prophets be slaughtered in the Lord’s sanctuary?" 
Lamentations2:21 “Both children and the elderly alike are lying dead in the dusty streets. My young men and maidens have both fallen by the sword. You have killed them on the day of your anger; you slaughtered them without mercy. 
Lamentations2:22 “You invited terror as if to a festival, to attack me from all sides. On the day of Yahweh ’s anger, none escaped and none survived. All whom I cherished and reared have been annihilated by the enemy.”" 
Lamentations3:1 I am the man acquainted with misery by the rod of his anger. 
Lamentations3:2 He has shepherded me into dark places with no light. 
Lamentations3:3 He laid a heavy hand on me, every day, over and over." 
Lamentations3:4 He has made my skin and my flesh waste away; he has broken my bones. 
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged and encircled me with a wall of hardship and bitterness. 
Lamentations3:6 He has made me sit in darkness like those long dead. 
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in with no way out and weighed me down with heavy chains. 
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry for help, he closes his ear to my prayer." 
Lamentations3:9 He has made my paths a maze and obstructed my ways. 
Lamentations3:10 He has become to me like a bear ready to pounce or a lion lying in wait. 
Lamentations3:11 He dragged me away, tore me to pieces, and then left me stunned and helpless." 
Lamentations3:12 He has drawn back his bowstring and used me as a target for his arrows. 
Lamentations3:13 He shot his arrows deep into my heart, sons from his quiver." 
Lamentations3:14 Everyone has made me an object of ridicule; they mock me all day long with their songs. 
Lamentations3:15 He has given me my fill of bitterness and made me drunk with wormwood. 
Lamentations3:16 He has ground my teeth with gravel and crushed me down to the dust. 
Lamentations3:17 You have snatched peace from my soul; I have entirely forgotten your goodness. 
Lamentations3:18 I thought to myself, “My endurance has evaporated; Yahweh has stamped out my hope for the future.”" 
Lamentations3:19 I remember wandering around in misery, drinking the poison of bitterness." 
Lamentations3:20 Whenever I ponder this, my soul fades away within me." 
Lamentations3:21 Yet there is one ray of hope when I remember this: 
Lamentations3:22 Yahweh ’s tender mercies have no end, and the kindness of his endless love is never exhausted." 
Lamentations3:23 New, fresh mercies greet me with every sunrise. So wonderfully great is your faithfulness!" 
Lamentations3:24 I tell my soul, “ Yahweh is my abundant portion. I need nothing more. So, I will put all my hope in him.”" 
Lamentations3:25 Yahweh is always good to all who trust in him, to the soul who searches for him." 
Lamentations3:26 It is always good to hope, quietly waiting for Yahweh ’s deliverance." 
Lamentations3:27 It is always good to learn patience and humility from a young age. 
Lamentations3:28 If your burden is heavy and hard to bear, endure it quietly." 
Lamentations3:29 Bow low in humble submission, for there may still be hope." 
Lamentations3:30 Offer a cheek to the one who would strike you, and learn to endure insults." 
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not reject you forever. 
Lamentations3:32 If he causes grief, he will show compassion from the cascading overflow of his endless love." 
Lamentations3:33 The Lord takes no delight in afflicting anyone with suffering. 
Lamentations3:34 To trample underfoot all the earth’s prisoners," 
Lamentations3:35 to violate human rights in the presence of the Most High," 
Lamentations3:36 to cheat someone of justice— these things the Lord does not approve. 
Lamentations3:37 Who can make anything happen unless the Lord is willing? 
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the decree of the Most High that both calamities and good things come? 
Lamentations3:39 Why, then, should anyone complain over the punishment of their sins?" 
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine our path, ponder our ways, and return to Yahweh ’s heart." 
Lamentations3:41 Let us pray, offering up our hearts in our hands to our God above." 
Lamentations3:42 “We have sinned and rebelled, and you have not yet pardoned us." 
Lamentations3:43 “You have blanketed us with your anger and then pursued us, slaughtering without pity." 
Lamentations3:44 You shrouded yourself in a cloud too thick for prayer to pierce. 
Lamentations3:45 You have reduced us to rubbish; we are the lowest scum among the nations. 
Lamentations3:46 “Our enemies are a choir of scoffers and scolders. 
Lamentations3:47 Panic and pitfall have been our portion; calamity and collapse have come upon us.” 
Lamentations3:48 My eyes stream with torrents of tears as I weep over the ruin of my beloved people. 
Lamentations3:49 My eyes will weep without stopping, with no relief in sight" 
Lamentations3:50 until Yahweh looks down from heaven and sees how much we suffer. 
Lamentations3:51 My heart is so grieved over Jerusalem, the daughter of my people." 
Lamentations3:52 My enemies, who hate me for no reason, have trapped me like a bird." 
Lamentations3:53 They buried me alive in a pit and covered it with stones. 
Lamentations3:54 Water rose over my head; I thought, “I’m going to die!”" 
Lamentations3:55 I called on your name, Yahweh, from the bottom of the pit." 
Lamentations3:56 You have heard me cry out to you: “Do not close your ears to my sighs and to my cries.” 
Lamentations3:57 You drew near when I called to you. You told me, “Do not be afraid!”" 
Lamentations3:58 Lord, like a lawyer you contended for my cause; you liberated my life, my Redeemer." 
Lamentations3:59 Yahweh, you have seen the wrong done to me; judge in my favor." 
Lamentations3:60 You have seen their malice and all their hateful plots against me. 
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their mockery, Yahweh, all their horrible schemes against me." 
Lamentations3:62 They murmur and whisper against me all day long. 
Lamentations3:63 See, from morning to night, I am the butt of their jokes." 
Lamentations3:64 Yahweh, repay them! Give them what their deeds deserve." 
Lamentations3:65 Give them dullness of heart as you place your curse upon them. 
Lamentations3:66 Pursue them with anger and destroy them from under your heavens! 
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has lost its luster! How the pure gold has lost its value! The sacred stones lie scattered at the corner of every street. 
Lamentations4:2 The precious children of Zion, once worth their weight in the finest gold, are now regarded as jars of clay made by a potter’s hands." 
Lamentations4:3 Even the vicious jackal is kind enough to nurse her young, but the daughter of my people is as cruel as the desert ostrich." 
Lamentations4:4 The nursing babies have such thirst that their tongues stick to the roofs of their mouths. Little children ask for bread, but no one gives them a crumb." 
Lamentations4:5 Those who used to feast on delicacies now lie dying in the streets. Those who were raised in luxury now huddle in garbage dumps. 
Lamentations4:6 The punishment of my beloved people exceeded the judgment of sinful Sodom, which was suddenly destroyed without an army attacking her." 
Lamentations4:7 Israel’s nobility was once brighter than snow, whiter than milk; their bodies glowed with health and glistened like jewels." 
Lamentations4:8 Now their faces are emaciated; they are not even recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones, as dry as a stick." 
Lamentations4:9 Better are those killed by the sword than those killed by famine. They slowly starve to death for lack of food. 
Lamentations4:10 The hands of caring women cooked their own children; they became their food when my beloved people were destroyed. 
Lamentations4:11 At the peak of Yahweh ’s wrath, he vented his fierce anger! He lit a fire in Zion and burned it down to the ground." 
Lamentations4:12 No king believed, nor anyone anywhere, that a hostile foe could ever break through the gates of Jerusalem." 
Lamentations4:13 Yet this happened because of the sins of her prophets and the crimes of her priests, who shed innocent blood in the heart of the city." 
Lamentations4:14 They wandered blindly through the city and were so defiled with blood that no one dared to even touch their clothes. 
Lamentations4:15 “Get away! Unclean!” people shouted at them. “Keep back! Keep back! Don’t touch!” So, they fled Jerusalem and wandered to the nations, but they were not welcome to stay there either." 
Lamentations4:16 The face of Yahweh scattered them; he will look on them no more. They had no respect for the priests, no honor for her leaders." 
Lamentations4:17 We strained our eyes continually and looked, watching for help that never came. From our watchtowers we waited for a nation to help, for a nation that could not save us." 
Lamentations4:18 Men stalked us so that we could not walk on our streets. Our end drew near, our time ran out, for our end had come." 
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than eagles in the sky; they hunted us on the mountains and lay in ambush for us in the wilderness. 
Lamentations4:20 They captured Yahweh ’s anointed leader, our king; the hope of our survival was caught in their traps. We thought that under his protective shadow we would live among the nations." 
Lamentations4:21 Go ahead, you people of Edom and Uz, laugh on and be glad like nothing will happen to you! Your day is coming to drink from this cup; you will get drunk and stagger naked." 
Lamentations4:22 Your punishment is over, fair daughter of Zion. Yahweh will never send you away into exile again. But people of Edom, he will punish your wickedness and expose your sins." 
Lamentations5:1 Yahweh, remember all that we’ve endured; look and see our disgrace." 
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has passed to strangers, our homes to foreigners." 
Lamentations5:3 We have no fathers; we are orphans, and our mothers are widows." 
Lamentations5:4 We have to pay for drinking water, and our firewood comes at a steep price." 
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are breathing down our necks; we are exhausted and allowed no rest. 
Lamentations5:6 We made a pact with Egypt and Assyria just to have enough food to eat. 
Lamentations5:7 Our ancestors sinned, and they are no more; and now we suffer their punishment." 
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is no one to rescue us from them. 
Lamentations5:9 We risk our lives to earn our bread because of armed gangs in the wilderness. 
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is scorched from the heat of famine. 
Lamentations5:11 Our enemies violated Zion’s women and young girls in the towns of Judah. 
Lamentations5:12 Our leaders have been taken and hanged; our elders have received no respect. 
Lamentations5:13 Young men are forced into menial labor, boys stagger under loads of wood." 
Lamentations5:14 The elders no longer give their advice; the young no longer make music. 
Lamentations5:15 Joy has vanished from our hearts; our dancing has turned to mourning. 
Lamentations5:16 Our joyful celebrations are over. We have sinned, and we are doomed!" 
Lamentations5:17 For this is why our hearts are sick and our eyes are veiled with tears," 
Lamentations5:18 because Mount Zion lies desolate with jackals roaming over it. 
Lamentations5:19 Yet you, Yahweh, reign forever! Your throne endures from age to age." 
Lamentations5:20 Have you forgotten us forever? Why have you abandoned us for so long? 
Lamentations5:21 Draw us back to yourself, Yahweh; let us come back to you! Restore us as we were before." 
Lamentations5:22 Have you utterly rejected us? Is there no limit to your anger? 
Ezekiel1:1 I am Ezekiel the son of Buzi, a priest. I was living among the Jewish exiles in Babylon by the River Chebar. One day when I was thirty, the heavens opened before my eyes, and I saw visions of God! It happened on the fifth day of the fourth month during the fifth year of King Jehoiachin’s exile. It was then that the word of Yahweh was powerfully revealed to me, and I felt the mighty hand of Yahweh come upon me!" 
Ezekiel1:2  
Ezekiel1:3  
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked into the spirit realm, I saw a fierce whirlwind and a massive cloud coming toward me from the north. Within the approaching cloud I saw raging flames of fire—flames enfolding themselves in flames! And outlining the glory cloud was a radiant light. And in the center of the flashing fire, I saw a substance of blinding brilliance with the appearance of electrum." 
Ezekiel1:5 And I also saw standing in the middle of the storm what seemed to be four creatures, each with the appearance of a man. They looked like this:" 
Ezekiel1:6 Each had four faces, and each had four wings." 
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight, but they had hooves like calves. Their hooves were bright and glistening like polished brass." 
Ezekiel1:8 All four of them had faces and wings, and each of the supernatural beings had a pair of human hands under its wings. There were hands on all four sides of the living creatures." 
Ezekiel1:9 They joined one another with their wings, forming a square. When they moved forward in whatever direction, they went together as a group, without turning their bodies." 
Ezekiel1:10 And all four beings had four faces: a human face in front, a lion’s face to the right, an ox’s face to the left, and an eagle’s face in the back." 
Ezekiel1:11 Their wings were divided into two pairs; one pair was extended upward so that each one touched the wingtips of the one next to it, and the other pair covered its body." 
Ezekiel1:12 And each one moved straight forward, moving where the Spirit directed them, and they did not turn as they moved." 
Ezekiel1:13 These living creatures looked like burning coals of fire, like blazing torches. Flames flashed back and forth between the creatures. The light was blinding, and lightning was flashing out of it." 
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures kept disappearing and reappearing like shooting stars. 
Ezekiel1:15 As I observed the four-faced creatures, I saw four shining wheels standing on the ground beside each of them." 
Ezekiel1:16 All four wheels looked entirely alike, glittering like sparkling jewels. Each appeared to have another wheel inside it." 
Ezekiel1:17 And the wheels were able to go in any direction the living creatures faced without turning to change direction. 
Ezekiel1:18 The circumference of the rims seemed enormous, breathtaking in size, and all four rims were full of eyes all the way around." 
Ezekiel1:19 Regardless of the direction the living creatures moved, the wheels moved with them, and when the living creatures levitated off the ground, the wheels levitated also." 
Ezekiel1:20 And the spirit in the living creatures was also in the wheels, and wherever the spirit went, the creatures went. Wherever the spirit wanted to go, the creatures went there, and the wheels also rose and went with them." 
Ezekiel1:21 So every time the creatures moved, or halted, or levitated, the wheels did also because the spirit of the living creatures was in the wheels." 
Ezekiel1:22 Suspended above the living creatures I saw something that looked like a platform spread out above them. The platform was fearsome, transparent, and shining like sparkling glass." 
Ezekiel1:23 And under the platform were the four living creatures with wings outstretched and touching one another and each with a pair of wings covering its body. 
Ezekiel1:24 When they moved, I heard the awesome sound of their wings. It was like the noise of roaring rapids, like the voice of Shaddai, and like the sound of an army advancing! And when the creatures stopped moving, they lowered their wings." 
Ezekiel1:25 Then, as they stood still, I heard someone speaking from above the platform over their heads." 
Ezekiel1:26 High on the platform over their heads, I saw something that looked like a beautiful throne made of sapphire. And high above on the throne was one who had the form of a man." 
Ezekiel1:27 I saw what appeared to be his waist upward, and it was bright and dazzling metal; and from what appeared to be his waist downward, I saw what looked like flames of fire, giving off a brilliant light all around." 
Ezekiel1:28 The radiance of the light encircling the throne had in it every color of the rainbow like on the day of a downpour. This was the appearance of the glory of Yahweh! And when I saw this, I fell on my face. Then, I heard the voice of someone speaking to me." 
Ezekiel2:1 He said to me, “Son of man, rise to your feet; I want to talk to you.”" 
Ezekiel2:2 The moment I heard these words, the Spirit flowed into me and set me upright on my feet." 
Ezekiel2:3 And I heard him speaking to me, saying, “Son of man, I am sending you to the rebellious sons of Israel who have refused allegiance to me. They and their ancestors have been in revolt against me up to the present day." 
Ezekiel2:4 I am sending you to a stubborn and hard-hearted people. Tell them, ‘Lord Yahweh says this.’" 
Ezekiel2:5 And whether they listen or not, this tribe of defiant rebels will know that a true prophet is among them." 
Ezekiel2:6 And you, son of man, do not be afraid of them or of anything they say, even though you may feel like you are stepping on thorns and sitting on scorpions. Do not be afraid of their angry words or intimidated by their glaring stares, for they are rebellion personified!" 
Ezekiel2:7 You are to speak my words to them whether they listen to you or rebelliously refuse. 
Ezekiel2:8 But you, son of man, listen to what I am saying to you: do not be like them, for they are a tribe of rebels. Now, open your mouth and eat what I am about to give you.”" 
Ezekiel2:9 Just then, I saw a hand holding a scroll reaching out to me." 
Ezekiel2:10 He unrolled it in front of me with writing on both sides. On it was written “Lament, Mourning, and Cries of Doom.”" 
Ezekiel3:1 He said to me, “Son of man, eat what you see in front of you; eat this scroll, then go and speak to the people of Israel.”" 
Ezekiel3:2 So I opened my mouth, and he fed me the scroll," 
Ezekiel3:3 saying, “Son of man, eat this scroll I am giving to you and fill your belly with it.” So I began to chew it, and it tasted as sweet as honey." 
Ezekiel3:4 Then he said, “Son of man, now go to the people of Israel and speak my words to them." 
Ezekiel3:5 I am not sending you to a nation that speaks a difficult foreign language but to the people of Israel— 
Ezekiel3:6 not to nations whose complex languages you could not understand and who speak words you cannot even pronounce. Surely, if I sent you to them, they would listen to you," 
Ezekiel3:7 but the people of Israel will refuse to listen to you because they refuse to listen to me. The whole family of Israel has a hard heart and is stubbornly defiant. 
Ezekiel3:8 But now, I will make you as stubborn and defiant as they are!" 
Ezekiel3:9 I will make your resolve as hard as the hardest stone, even harder than flint. So do not fear them or be afraid because of their angry stares, for they are a tribe of rebels.”" 
Ezekiel3:10 Then he said, “Son of man, listen carefully and take to heart everything I say to you," 
Ezekiel3:11 then go to the Jews in exile and say to them, ‘Listen to what Lord Yahweh says,’ whether they listen or not.”" 
Ezekiel3:12 Then the Spirit lifted me up, and behind me I heard a loud, thunderous voice: “Blessed be the glory of Yahweh in his sanctuary!”" 
Ezekiel3:13 I heard the sound of the living creatures as their wings kissed each other and the sound of the rumbling wheels beside them, like the sound of a great earthquake." 
Ezekiel3:14 The Spirit lifted me up and took me away. I went in the heat of my spirit, and the hand of Yahweh rested powerfully upon me." 
Ezekiel3:15 The Spirit took me to Tel Abib, the very place where the Jewish exiles were living beside the River Chebar. I remained there with them for a week, dumbfounded and overwhelmed by what I had seen and heard." 
Ezekiel3:16 Afterward, Yahweh spoke the following words to me:" 
Ezekiel3:17 “Son of man, I am appointing you as a watchman for the people of Israel. When you hear me speak to you, give them my warning." 
Ezekiel3:18 If I say to wicked people, ‘You are headed toward certain death,’ and you fail to give them warning by not speaking up to warn the evil ones against their evil courses so as to keep them alive, then the wicked ones will die for their sinful deeds. But I will hold you responsible for their deaths." 
Ezekiel3:19 But if you do warn the wicked and they keep sinning anyway, they’ll most certainly die for their sin, but you won’t die. You’ll have saved your life." 
Ezekiel3:20 And if righteous people turn away from righteous living and pursue a life of sin, I will set calamities before them, and they will die. Because you did not give them warning, they will die for their sinful deeds, and all the righteous deeds done earlier in life will not be remembered. But I will hold you responsible for their deaths." 
Ezekiel3:21 If, however, you do warn a righteous person not to sin and he responds to your warning, that person will live, and you will have also saved your life.”" 
Ezekiel3:22 While I was there, Yahweh seized me with his power, and he said to me, “Stand up, go out into the valley, and I will speak with you there.”" 
Ezekiel3:23 So I got up and went down into the valley and behold, the awesome glory of Yahweh manifested in front of me! It was like the vision of glory I had seen by the River Chebar, and I fell facedown." 
Ezekiel3:24 Then the Spirit flowed into me and set me on my feet. And I heard him say to me: “Go home and shut the door behind you. 
Ezekiel3:25 Behold! Son of man, you are about to be tied up with ropes and confined to your house and unable to mingle among the people." 
Ezekiel3:26 And I am going to make your tongue stick to the roof of your mouth; you will be mute and unable to speak to rebuke this rebellious people. 
Ezekiel3:27 But whenever I want to speak through you, I will give you back the power of speech. And then you will say to them, ‘This is what Lord Yahweh says: If you have a heart to hear, then hear! If you refuse to listen, then so be it!’—for they are a tribe of rebels.”" 
Ezekiel4:1 Yahweh said, “Son of man, take a clay tile and lay it in front of you. Sketch the city of Jerusalem on the tile." 
Ezekiel4:2 Build a wall around it and, to represent a siege, pile up a dirt ramp against the wall. Then prepare to attack it as if it were a real city. Surround it with armed soldiers and station battering rams on all sides." 
Ezekiel4:3 Then take an iron frying pan and place it on edge as though it were an iron wall between you and the city. Then direct your gaze toward the city, for it is under siege and you are besieging it. This is to be a sign, an object lesson for the people of Israel." 
Ezekiel4:4 “Then lie down on your left side and stay like that for 390 days. The people of Israel sinned for 390 years, and you will lie one day for each year they sinned. You will symbolically bear their guilt for as many days as you lie on that side and bear their punishment." 
Ezekiel4:5  
Ezekiel4:6 After that, lie down a second time but turn over on your right side and stay like that for forty days, one day for each year of the guilt of the people of Judah." 
Ezekiel4:7 Then direct your gaze on the model of the siege of Jerusalem, shake your fist at the city and prophesy against her." 
Ezekiel4:8 Behold, I am going to tie you up, and you will not be able to turn from one side to the other until the period of your seclusion is over." 
Ezekiel4:9 “Now take some wheat, barley, beans, lentils, and other grains; store them all together and make bread for yourself. This will be your food for the 390 days you are lying on your side." 
Ezekiel4:10 Of these grains, make a small loaf of bread at a set time each day." 
Ezekiel4:11 And you are to ration the water you drink to two cups at a set time each day. 
Ezekiel4:12 Eat the food as you would a barley cake. While everyone can see you, build a fire with dried human excrement, then bake the bread on the coals.”" 
Ezekiel4:13 And Yahweh said, “In this same way the Israelites will have to eat defiled food among the nations where I will disperse them.”" 
Ezekiel4:14 Then I replied, “Oh no! Lord Yahweh, I have never defiled myself, from childhood until now. I have never eaten meat from any animal that died a natural death or was killed by wild animals. Nothing unclean has ever entered my mouth.”" 
Ezekiel4:15 “Very well,” he said, “I will let you use dried cow manure instead of human excrement as fuel to bake your bread.”" 
Ezekiel4:16 Then he added, “Son of man, I am going to cut off Jerusalem’s food supply. In their horrible distress, the food they eat and the water they drink will be rationed." 
Ezekiel4:17 They will watch as it all runs out and look at each other in total despair. And they will slowly die of starvation because of their guilt.” 
Ezekiel5:1 Yahweh said, “Son of man, take a sharp sword; use it like a barber’s razor and shave your head and beard. Then take scales and divide the hair into three piles." 
Ezekiel5:2 When the days of your siege are over, burn up a third in the midst of the city of Jerusalem. Take another third and chop it up with the sword around the perimeter of the tile-city. Then scatter the last third to the wind. I will pursue those who escape the city with my sword." 
Ezekiel5:3 Also keep back a few hairs and wrap them up in the folds of your cloak. 
Ezekiel5:4 And of those, take just a few hairs and throw them on the fire and burn them. That fire will spread to all the people of Israel.”" 
Ezekiel5:5 Listen to what the Lord Yahweh says: “This represents Jerusalem. I have placed her in the center of the nations, encircled by foreign countries." 
Ezekiel5:6 Yet she has rebelled more wickedly against my ways and against my laws than the gentile nations that surround her. Indeed, she has deliberately rejected my commands and not kept my laws.”" 
Ezekiel5:7 Therefore, the Lord Yahweh says, “Because you acted more wickedly than the nations around you, since you do not keep my laws or respect my commands, and since you do not respect even the moral standards of the surrounding nations," 
Ezekiel5:8 therefore, behold—I, Lord Yahweh, declare that I am against you. And I will pass my judgment on you for all the nations to see." 
Ezekiel5:9 Because of all your loathsome practices, I am coming against you more severely than I have ever done before or will ever do again." 
Ezekiel5:10 As a result, those of you who are parents will eat their children, and children will eat their parents. I will punish you and scatter what remains of you to the winds." 
Ezekiel5:11 “For as surely as I live, I, Lord Yahweh, declare: because you have defiled my sanctuary with all your horrible and loathsome practices, I will withdraw from you and will not even glance on you with pity or spare you." 
Ezekiel5:12 Inside Jerusalem, a third of your people will die of plague or starvation. Outside the city, a third will fall by the sword. And a third I will scatter to the winds and pursue with my sword." 
Ezekiel5:13 Then my anger will come to an end and be fully spent against them, and then my justice will have been satisfied. When I have carried out my anger against them, they will know how jealous I am for them. Afterward, they will know that I, Yahweh, have spoken in my zeal." 
Ezekiel5:14 “Moreover, I will reduce you to a heap of ruins, an object of scorn to the surrounding nations, in the eyes of all who pass by." 
Ezekiel5:15 You will be mocked and insulted. Your downfall will be an appalling lesson to the surrounding nations. This will happen when I execute judgment on you in my furious anger and with my furious punishment. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel5:16 “I will release against them the deadly arrows of famine—arrows of destruction! Then I will make the famine even worse and cut off your food supply. 
Ezekiel5:17 I will send severe famine and wild animals against you to rob you of your children. Sickness and bloodshed will overtake you, and I will bring the sword down upon you. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel6:1 Yahweh said to me: 
Ezekiel6:2 “Son of man, turn toward the mountains of Israel and prophesy against them." 
Ezekiel6:3 Say to them, ‘Mountains of Israel, hear the words of Lord Yahweh! He prophesies to the mountains and hills, to the ravines and valleys: “ ‘I am wielding a sword against you and I will demolish your shrines." 
Ezekiel6:4 Your altars will be deserted, and your incense altars will be smashed. I will slay your people before your idols." 
Ezekiel6:5 I will lay the corpses of the Israelites before their idols, and I will scatter your bones all around your altars." 
Ezekiel6:6 Wherever you live, your cities will be ruined and your shrines deserted. All your altars will be destroyed and desecrated, and your idols shall be broken and banished. Your incense altars will be chopped down, and all your works wiped out." 
Ezekiel6:7 The slain will lie everywhere in your midst, and you will know that I am Yahweh!" 
Ezekiel6:8 “ ‘I will spare some of you who escape the sword when you are scattered among the nations. 
Ezekiel6:9 Those who escape will remember me among the nations where they are exiles. They will remember how my heart was crushed by their promiscuous ways when they turned their hearts away from me and by their wandering eyes that lusted after idols. They will loathe themselves for the evil things they did and all their detestable practices. 
Ezekiel6:10 And they will realize that it was no idle threat when I, Yahweh, declared that I would bring this severe punishment upon them.’ ”" 
Ezekiel6:11 Hear the words of Lord Yahweh: “Stomp your feet angrily and pound your fist, and say, ‘How awful!’ over all the evil abominations of the people of Israel. For they will fall by the sword, by food shortages, and by plague." 
Ezekiel6:12 “Those far away will die by the plague, those nearby shall fall by the sword, and those who survive will die of hunger. They will experience the fullness of my fury!" 
Ezekiel6:13 “And you will be convinced that I am Yahweh when their corpses lie scattered around their idols and around their altars, on every high hill, on every mountaintop, under every spreading tree, and under every leafy oak—every place where they have offered soothing incense to all their lifeless idols." 
Ezekiel6:14 I will stretch out my mighty arm against them and turn the land wherever they live into a complete wasteland from the desert to Riblah. And when this happens, they will be convinced that I am Yahweh!”" 
Ezekiel7:1 Yahweh spoke to me and said," 
Ezekiel7:2 “Son of man, tell the people of Israel what I, Lord Yahweh, say to the land of Israel: ‘Finished! The end has come over all the land." 
Ezekiel7:3 Now the end has come. I will unleash my anger on you and judge you for the way you have lived. I will judge you for all your loathsome practices. 
Ezekiel7:4 I will not show you pity nor spare you. Indeed, I will punish you for your conduct—the despicable evil you have done. Then you all will be convinced that I am Yahweh.’" 
Ezekiel7:5 “The Lord Yahweh says: ‘Look! An unheard-of disaster is coming. 
Ezekiel7:6 It’s all over for you! The end is near! Doom is awakened against you. Behold, the fateful day!" 
Ezekiel7:7 Now it is your turn to be punished. You who dwell in this land, doom is coming, for the day of Yahweh is near. The people will be shouting in panic, not shouting with joy at the shrines on the mountaintops." 
Ezekiel7:8 Very soon I will vent my fury on you and punish you until my anger is finished. I will judge you for the way you live and punish you for all your loathsome practices. 
Ezekiel7:9 I will show you no mercy nor spare you, but I will punish you for your conduct and for all your detestable deeds. Then you will know that I am Yahweh who has punished you.’ ”" 
Ezekiel7:10 “ ‘Now is the day of disaster. Behold, your doom has come! Brutality has blossomed, and pride is at its peak." 
Ezekiel7:11 Violence has grown up into a rod to punish their wickedness. None of these proud, evil people will survive. All their wealth and prestige will be swept away." 
Ezekiel7:12 The time of your doom is before you; the day of reckoning has come. Let not the buyer be happy nor the seller be sad, for wrath is upon the entire multitude." 
Ezekiel7:13 For the seller will not be able to return to what has been sold while they live. For the vision concerns them all. Each persists in sin, and no one will hold on to their lives." 
Ezekiel7:14 The trumpet sounds, everyone gets ready, but no one goes into battle, since my anger rests on everyone alike.’ ”" 
Ezekiel7:15 “ ‘Enemies are lurking outside the city; inside are disease and starvation. Anyone out in the countryside will die in battle; anyone in the city will die by starvation and plague. 
Ezekiel7:16 And those who escape will flee to the mountains, moaning like doves of the valleys, each one moaning for his sin." 
Ezekiel7:17 Every hand will grow weak. Gripped with fear, they wet themselves." 
Ezekiel7:18 Terror covers them as they put on sackcloth. Shame covers their faces as they shave their heads bald. 
Ezekiel7:19 They will throw their silver away in the streets and their gold like filthy menstrual rags. Their wealth will not be able to save them on the day of Yahweh ’s fury. For it will be worthless on that day to buy food to fill their stomachs since it was their wealth that led them into sin. 
Ezekiel7:20 Once they were so proud of their beautiful jewelry, out of which they made their disgusting, horrible idols. Now I have made their wealth and jewels repulsive to them." 
Ezekiel7:21 I will give your wealth to foreigners, as spoils of war to the evilest people on earth, and they will profane it." 
Ezekiel7:22 I will turn my face away from them while my hidden, treasured place is profaned and thugs force their way in and defile it." 
Ezekiel7:23 “ ‘Prepare plenty of chains to bind up the captives. For the country is full of bloodshed and murders, and the city is full of violence," 
Ezekiel7:24 so I will bring evil nations here to seize their homes. I will put an end to the pride of their elite, and their sanctuary will be profaned." 
Ezekiel7:25 Panic is on the way! They will look for peace but never find it. 
Ezekiel7:26 One disaster will follow another, with rumor upon rumor. They will seek a vision from the prophet but in vain. The priestly teaching of the divine law will be destroyed, along with the counsel of the elders." 
Ezekiel7:27 The king will go into mourning, the officials will be wrapped in despair, and the hands of the people will tremble in terror. I will punish them for the way they have lived and judge them the same way as they have judged others. Then they will be convinced that I am Yahweh!’ ”" 
Ezekiel8:1 In the sixth year of our exile, on the fifth day of the sixth month, the elders of Judah and I were sitting in my home. Suddenly, I felt the mighty power of the Lord Yahweh come over me." 
Ezekiel8:2 Behold, I saw a fiery human figure. His body looked like fire! Below the waist were flames of fire; above the waist he glistened as bright as glowing bronze." 
Ezekiel8:3 He reached out with what seemed like a hand and grabbed me by my hair. Then, in a great and wonderful vision from God, the Spirit lifted me up high in the air and transported me to Jerusalem. He took me to the entrance of the north gate of the inner court where the disgusting idol that provokes Yahweh ’s jealousy was standing." 
Ezekiel8:4 And all at once, the dazzling glory of the God of Israel appeared! It looked like what I had seen in the Euphrates Valley." 
Ezekiel8:5 Then God said, “Son of man, look now to the north.” So, I raised my eyes to the north, and standing there, north of the pathway entrance of the altar gate was this vile statue that infuriates God." 
Ezekiel8:6 And he said to me, “Son of man, do you see what they are doing? Look at all the repulsive, loathsome things that the people of Israel are practicing here! Their disgusting deeds drive me farther and farther from my sanctuary. And you will see even more shocking things than these.”" 
Ezekiel8:7 He next transported me to the entrance to the outer courtyard. I looked carefully and noticed there was a hole in the wall. 
Ezekiel8:8 He said, “Son of man, dig through the wall.” When I dug into the wall, I discovered a doorway." 
Ezekiel8:9 He said, “Now, go in and look at the shocking, despicable things they are doing inside.”" 
Ezekiel8:10 So I went through the door, and I noticed the walls were covered with drawings of every kind of snake, bugs, creeping things, repulsive animals, and all the disgusting idols of the people of Israel." 
Ezekiel8:11 Worshiping these animals were seventy leaders of Israel, and standing among them was Jaazaniah son of Shaphan. Each one had an incense burner in his hand, from which rose a fragrant cloud of incense." 
Ezekiel8:12 God said to me, “Son of man, have you seen what these leaders of Israel are doing in secret, each one worshiping in his own personal shrine? They say, ‘Oh, Yahweh cannot see us; he has left the land.’ ”" 
Ezekiel8:13 Then God said, “ This is not even the worst of the practices. You will see things more terrible than this.”" 
Ezekiel8:14 Next he took me to the entrance of the north gate of the temple of Yahweh where women were weeping over the death of the god Tammuz. 
Ezekiel8:15 He said, “Son of man, do you see their worship of Tammuz? You will see even more terrible things than that.”" 
Ezekiel8:16 Next he transported me to the north gate to the inner court of his temple. And there, at the entrance to Yahweh ’s sanctuary, between the porch and the altar, were about twenty-five men standing with their faces to the east worshiping the sun with their backs to Yahweh ’s sanctuary. They bowed down to the east, before the rising sun." 
Ezekiel8:17 God said to me, “Son of man, do you see this? It is bad enough that the people of Judah are doing these despicable things, but they also spread violence throughout the land and provoke my anger further! Look at how they insult me in every possible way they can." 
Ezekiel8:18 Therefore, with wrath I will deal with them. I will show neither pity nor mercy. They can cry to me as loudly as they want, but I will not listen to them.”" 
Ezekiel9:1 Then I heard the Lord shout loudly for me to hear, “Executioners, draw near! Each bring your weapon of destruction!”" 
Ezekiel9:2 Immediately six men advanced from the upper north gate, each one holding a deadly weapon. Also, I saw with them a man clothed in white linen, with a scribe’s case tied to his belt. They came and stood in front of the bronze altar." 
Ezekiel9:3 Then the dazzling glory of the God of Israel rose from above the cherubim where it had been resting and moved toward the threshold of the temple. Then he called to the man dressed in white linen with a scribe’s case at his side," 
Ezekiel9:4 and Yahweh said to him, “Go throughout Jerusalem, and place a mark on the foreheads of all who grieve and are greatly distressed over all the disgusting things done in the city.”" 
Ezekiel9:5 And I heard him say to the other six, “Follow the man in white linen through the city and strike down everyone with no mark on their forehead —old men, young men, girls, children, women—kill them all. Show no mercy, not even one glance of pity. But do not touch anyone with a mark on his forehead. And begin at my sanctuary.” So, they began with those men in front of the temple and with the elders." 
Ezekiel9:6  
Ezekiel9:7 He said to the executioners, “Go make my temple unfit for worship. Fill its courtyard with corpses. Now get to work.” They departed and began to strike the people throughout the city." 
Ezekiel9:8 While they were slaying, I was left alone in the temple. I threw myself facedown on the ground and cried out, “Oh, Lord Yahweh, are you really going to wipe out the remnant of Israel by venting your fury on Jerusalem?”" 
Ezekiel9:9 He answered me, “The guilt of the people of Israel and Judah is enormous. The land is full of bloodshed, and Jerusalem full of perverted justice. They say, ‘ Yahweh can’t see us! He has abandoned the nation.’" 
Ezekiel9:10 So, I no longer will show them any mercy nor give them one glance of pity. They hereby suffer the consequences of their own behavior.”" 
Ezekiel9:11 Just then, the man dressed in white linen with the scribe’s case came back and made his report: “ It is finished; I have carried out your orders.”" 
Ezekiel10:1 Then I saw a vision. On top of the dome over the heads of the cherubim, I saw something that looked like a sapphire throne." 
Ezekiel10:2 Yahweh said to the man dressed in white linen, “Go among the whirling wheels below the cherubim and take a handful of burning coals from between the living creatures and scatter them over the city.” While I was watching, he went in among the whirling wheels." 
Ezekiel10:3 The cherubim were on the south side of the temple as the man went in, and the billowing glory cloud filled the inner courtyard." 
Ezekiel10:4 The dazzling glory of Yahweh rose from between the cherubim and rested over the entrance of the temple. The temple was filled with the cloud, and the court was full of the radiance of the glory of Yahweh." 
Ezekiel10:5 The noise of the living creatures’ wings was as loud as the voice of God Almighty! The people could even hear it in the outer courtyard! 
Ezekiel10:6 Afterward, Yahweh commanded the man dressed in white linen to go among the wheels and take fire that was burning among the cherubim. So, the man went among them and stood beside one of the wheels." 
Ezekiel10:7 One of the living creatures then reached his hand into the fire between the cherubim and took some fire and placed it into the hands of the man dressed in white linen, who took it and left." 
Ezekiel10:8 It seemed that the living creatures had what looked like a human hand under their wings. 
Ezekiel10:9 I saw the four wheels by the living creatures, one wheel beside each living creature. And the wheels sparkled like precious gemstones." 
Ezekiel10:10 Each wheel appeared to be identical with another wheel inside it. 
Ezekiel10:11 As the wheels moved, they would go in any one of the four directions the living creatures faced, without having to turn around. They went in whatever direction their head faced without turning as they went." 
Ezekiel10:12 Their entire bodies, their backs, their hands, their wings—along with all four of their wheels—were covered with eyes all around." 
Ezekiel10:13 I heard a voice refer to the wheels as “whirling wheels.” 
Ezekiel10:14 Each living creature had four faces; the first was a cherub’s face, the second a human face, the third a lion’s face, and the fourth an eagle’s face." 
Ezekiel10:15 Then the cherubim rose to the sky. They were the same living creatures I had seen by the River Chebar. 
Ezekiel10:16 When the living creatures moved as a group in any direction, the wheels followed them, and when the living creatures levitated off the ground by spreading their wings, the wheels did not leave their place beside them." 
Ezekiel10:17 When the living creatures stopped, the wheels stopped; when the former rose, the wheels rose with them, since they shared the same living Spirit." 
Ezekiel10:18 Then the dazzling glory of Yahweh departed from the entrance of the temple and hovered over the living creatures. 
Ezekiel10:19 They spread their wings, and before my eyes, I saw them levitate from the ground, with the wheels spinning beside them. Then they paused at the entrance to the eastern gate of Yahweh ’s temple, with the dazzling glory of the God of Israel shining over them." 
Ezekiel10:20 These were the same living creatures I had seen beneath the throne of the God of Israel by the River Chebar; I knew that they were cherubim. 
Ezekiel10:21 Each had four faces, four wings, and what appeared to be human hands under their wings." 
Ezekiel10:22 Each one moved straight ahead. Their faces were identical to those I had seen by the River Chebar. 
Ezekiel11:1 God’s Spirit swept me off my feet and transported me to the eastern gate of the temple of Yahweh. And behold, there at the entrance to the gate facing east stood twenty-five men, including the two officials Jaazaniah son of Azzur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah." 
Ezekiel11:2 Then Yahweh said to me, “Son of man, these men are making evil plans to spread their wicked counsel throughout this city." 
Ezekiel11:3 They say, ‘Isn’t it time to start building houses again? Jerusalem is like an iron storage pot, and we are like meat, stored safely away.’" 
Ezekiel11:4 Therefore, prophesy against them. Prophesy, son of man!”" 
Ezekiel11:5 Then the Spirit of Yahweh fell on me, and said to me, “Tell the people: ‘I, Yahweh, say to the people of Israel, I know what you think, your every secret thought." 
Ezekiel11:6 You have murdered so many people in this city that its streets are filled with corpses. 
Ezekiel11:7 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says this: Yes, this city is the pot, and the corpses you have thrown into the streets, they are the meat! But I will drive you out of the city." 
Ezekiel11:8 Are you afraid of the sword? Then I will bring the sword against you, declares the Lord Yahweh." 
Ezekiel11:9 And I will bring you to justice and will drive you out of Jerusalem and hand you over to foreigners! 
Ezekiel11:10 I will judge you at the border of Israel and you will fall by the sword. Then you will know that I am Yahweh. 
Ezekiel11:11 For this city will not be your safe storage pot, nor will you be the meat inside. I will execute justice on you on the border of Israel." 
Ezekiel11:12 And then you will know that I am Yahweh, whose laws and commands you have disobeyed. Instead, you have embraced the customs of the neighboring nations.’ ”" 
Ezekiel11:13 While I was prophesying, Pelatiah son of Benaiah dropped dead. I threw myself facedown to the ground and cried out, “Oh no, Lord Yahweh, are you going to annihilate the remnant left in Israel?”" 
Ezekiel11:14 Yahweh spoke to me, saying:" 
Ezekiel11:15 “Son of man, the people living in Jerusalem are talking about you, your close relatives, and all the people of Israel. They are saying that the people in exile are away from Yahweh. ‘This country was given to us as our possession!’" 
Ezekiel11:16 Now give this message to your fellow exiles: ‘The Lord Yahweh says to you: Although I have sent you far away among the nations and I have scattered you to foreign countries, yet I have been your sanctuary for the time being wherever you have gone.’" 
Ezekiel11:17 And tell them, ‘The Lord Yahweh says this: I will gather you back from the peoples, and draw you together from the countries where you have been scattered and give you the land of Israel." 
Ezekiel11:18 When you return, you will purge the land of all its filthy idols and detestable practices." 
Ezekiel11:19 I will give you a new, undivided heart, and I will put a new spirit in you. I will remove your stubborn heart of stone and give you a tender heart that responds to me—a devoted heart." 
Ezekiel11:20 Therefore, you will keep my laws and faithfully obey my commands. Then you will truly be the people of my heart, and I will be the God you worship." 
Ezekiel11:21 But I will punish those whose hearts are devoted to their filthy idols and detestable practices. I will repay them for their conduct, declares Lord Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel11:22 Then the cherubim raised their wings to fly, and the wheels moved in unison with them; above them was the radiant glory of the God of Israel." 
Ezekiel11:23 And the glory of Yahweh went up from Jerusalem and stood above the mountain to the east of the city. 
Ezekiel11:24 Then, the Spirit snatched me away and transported me back among the exiles in Babylon. This was the vision given to me by the Spirit of God. Then the vision faded away." 
Ezekiel11:25 And I told the exiles everything that Yahweh had revealed to me. 
Ezekiel12:1 The word of Yahweh was revealed to me: 
Ezekiel12:2 “Son of man, you are living among a rebellious people. They have eyes, but they will not see; they have ears, but they will not hear because they are bent on rebellion." 
Ezekiel12:3 Therefore, son of man, pack a small bundle of your belongings and leave your home before nightfall. Let everyone see you go into exile. And go somewhere else, like you are a prisoner of war, while they watch you depart. Then perhaps they will understand that they are a rebellious people." 
Ezekiel12:4 While everyone is watching, pack your things like a refugee’s bundle, by daylight, and leave like an exile at sunset." 
Ezekiel12:5 Dig through the wall of your house and crawl out, carrying the bag with you. Make sure everyone is watching." 
Ezekiel12:6 Let them watch as you shoulder your pack and go out into the dusk. And cover your face so that you cannot see the land you are leaving since I have made you a walking parable—a sign to warn the people of Israel.” 
Ezekiel12:7 So, I did as I had been told. I packed my bag like an exile’s bundle, by daylight. And that evening, I dug a hole through the wall with my hands. Then while everyone watched, I shouldered my pack and went out into the dark." 
Ezekiel12:8 The next morning Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel12:9 “Son of man, did not the rebellious Israelites ask you, ‘What are you doing?’" 
Ezekiel12:10 Answer them with these words: ‘Lord Yahweh says to you that this prophetic oracle from God concerns the ruler in Jerusalem and all the people who live there.’ 
Ezekiel12:11 “Tell them, ‘I am a sign for you, for what I did is what is going to happen to them.’ They will be deported into exile." 
Ezekiel12:12 Their ruler will shoulder his pack, and they will dig a hole in the wall to let him out at night. He will cover his face so that he cannot see the land he is leaving." 
Ezekiel12:13 I will throw my net over him and trap him in it. I will take him to Babylon, to the land of the Chaldeans, where he will die without having seen it." 
Ezekiel12:14 And I will scatter in every direction all his attendants, officials, and his armies, and I will chase after them with the sword." 
Ezekiel12:15 When I scatter them among different nations and peoples, they will recognize that I am Yahweh." 
Ezekiel12:16 But I will let a few of them escape the sword, plague, and food shortages. I will let them live so that they can describe all their terrible actions to the people in the lands where they will go. And they will recognize that I am Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel12:17 Then Yahweh spoke to me, saying:" 
Ezekiel12:18 “Son of man, eat your food with quaking and trembling. Shudder with fear as you drink your water." 
Ezekiel12:19 Tell the people, ‘Lord Yahweh says this to the inhabitants of Jerusalem who remain in the land of Israel. They will tremble as they eat their food, and shake with fear when they drink their water because their enemies will strip the land bare on account of the violence of those who live there." 
Ezekiel12:20 When your populous cities have been destroyed and the countryside has been deserted, then you will recognize that I am Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel12:21 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel12:22 “Son of man, what do you understand of the proverb you say among yourselves in the land of Israel, ‘Time goes by, and these visions never happen’?" 
Ezekiel12:23 Very well, tell them, ‘Lord Yahweh says this: I will put an end to this proverb, and it will never be repeated in Israel again.’ Tell them instead: ‘The time has arrived for every vision to come true," 
Ezekiel12:24 for there will be no more false visions or deceptive prophecy among the people of Israel. 
Ezekiel12:25 I, Yahweh, will speak the word, and what I say will come true without delay. For what I speak, I will accomplish in your own lifetime, you tribe of rebels. For I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken!’ ”" 
Ezekiel12:26 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel12:27 “Son of man, behold, the people of Israel are saying that your visions and prophecies are about the distant future, not today. They say, ‘He is prophesying of times in the distant future.’" 
Ezekiel12:28 So tell them, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: There will be no more delay. My words will be fulfilled now! What I have prophesied will be done. Lord Yahweh has spoken!’ ”" 
Ezekiel13:1 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel13:2 “Son of man, prophesy against the prophets of Israel who claim to speak for me but are prophesying out of their own imagination. Say to them: ‘Listen, you lying prophets, to the words of Yahweh," 
Ezekiel13:3 for the Lord Yahweh has a message for you. “ ‘Disaster is in store for the wicked prophets who make up their own visions, but they have seen and heard nothing!" 
Ezekiel13:4 Israel, your prophets have been like scavengers prowling among the city ruins." 
Ezekiel13:5 They have not denounced the people’s sin or warned them of trouble coming so that Israel might stand up in battle on the day of Yahweh. 
Ezekiel13:6 Their visions are phony and their prophecies of what is coming are lies. They say, “ Yahweh says,” when I, Yahweh, did not send them with my message, and yet they actually expect me to fulfill their words!" 
Ezekiel13:7 Have you not seen false visions and prophesied false prophecies of the future? You declare: “This is what Yahweh says,” when I had not spoken." 
Ezekiel13:8 “ ‘Therefore, Lord Yahweh says to you: Because you have spoken your false prophecies and lying visions, behold, I am against you, says Lord Yahweh." 
Ezekiel13:9 I will punish the false prophets who see false visions and speak lies about what they see coming in the future. They will no longer belong to my council of elders, nor will their names be inscribed in the register of the people of Israel, nor will they ever return to the land of Israel. And then the people will know for sure that I am Lord Yahweh!" 
Ezekiel13:10 “ ‘For in truth, they have gravely misled my people, saying, “Everything is fine,” when devastation is at hand. When the false build a “wall of words ” they cover it with the whitewash of their lies." 
Ezekiel13:11 Prophesy to those who do so that their wall will fall! For I will send a stormy wind that will break loose with a deluge of rain and great hailstones. 
Ezekiel13:12 And down will come the wall! When it crumbles, will not the people say to you, “Where’s that whitewashed wall of yours now?”" 
Ezekiel13:13 “ ‘Therefore this is what I, Lord Yahweh, say to you: My anger will break forth as a windstorm and my wrath will break loose like a torrential rain, and I will send great hailstones to destroy your wall!" 
Ezekiel13:14 Yes, I will level this plastered wall to the ground and lay bare its flimsy foundation. And when it crumbles, you, too, will crumble and perish. And then you will realize that I am Yahweh!" 
Ezekiel13:15 “ ‘For then my wrath will be spent against the wall and upon those who have smeared it with plaster. And I will say to you, “The wall is gone, and so are those who smeared plaster over it." 
Ezekiel13:16 Those prophets of Israel who prophesied that Jerusalem would be fine, when devastation was near, will be no more!” For I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel13:17 “As for you, son of man, prophesy against the women of your people, who are witches masquerading as prophets. For they prophesy out of their own imagination. Prophesy against them" 
Ezekiel13:18 and say, ‘Hear what Lord Yahweh says to you: You women are doomed who sew magic bands on their wrists and make veils of varying sizes for their heads in order to ensnare the lives of people, young and old. Do you think you can get away with entrapping the lives of my people and still preserve your own lives?" 
Ezekiel13:19 You have dishonored my name among my people for a few handfuls of barley and a few pieces of bread. You put to death people who should not have died and kept alive people who should not have lived, because you lied to my people, and they listened to your lies!" 
Ezekiel13:20 “ ‘Therefore I, Lord Yahweh, say to you: I am against your wristbands with which you ensnare people’s souls. I will tear them from your arms! And I will let your victims go free, those whom you hunt down like birds." 
Ezekiel13:21 I will rip off your scarves and deliver my people from your hands. They will no longer be trapped by your power, and then you will know for sure that I am Yahweh." 
Ezekiel13:22 By your lies you have made righteous people greatly discouraged (although I have not discouraged them). And by your lies you have encouraged the wicked not to repent from their wicked ways and save their lives. 
Ezekiel13:23 Therefore you will no longer conjure up false visions or practice witchcraft. I will rescue my people from your deceptive power. And then you will know without a doubt that I am Yahweh.’ ” 
Ezekiel14:1 One day, some leaders of Israel came to me to consult about what God was saying. While they were sitting there with me," 
Ezekiel14:2 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel14:3 “Son of man, these men have set up idols deep within their hearts. And they have set idols before their eyes, so why should I let them ask me anything?" 
Ezekiel14:4 So tell them this, ‘Lord Yahweh says: If any Israelite comes to consult with the prophet despite a heart that worships idols and eyes looking on that which leads into wickedness, I, Yahweh, will answer that person directly. I will give the answer that is deserved because of the great extent of the idolatry." 
Ezekiel14:5 And in this way I hope to capture the hearts of the people of Israel who have all been estranged because of all their idols.’ 
Ezekiel14:6 “Therefore, prophesy to the people of Israel, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Repent! Come back to me and forsake your idols. Once and for all, turn your backs on all your detestable practices." 
Ezekiel14:7 If any Israelite or a foreigner living among you rejects me with idolatry of the heart and has intentionally gazed on that which leads into wickedness, yet goes to consult with a prophet, then, I, Yahweh, will answer that person myself." 
Ezekiel14:8 I will oppose that person! I will cut the idolator off from my people as an example and a warning to others. And then you will know that I am Yahweh. 
Ezekiel14:9 And if the prophet is tricked into speaking a prophetic word, it is I, Yahweh, who allowed this to happen, for I have decided to punish him with death." 
Ezekiel14:10 Both the prophet and the one who consults him will be equally punished for their guilt. 
Ezekiel14:11 I will do this so that they will never stray from me again or defile themselves with their rebellion. Then they will be my faithful people, and I will be their faithful God. For I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel14:12 Yahweh spoke to me, saying:" 
Ezekiel14:13 “Son of man, suppose all the people living in a certain country wander from me in pursuit of their sin, then I decide to come against them. I would punish them by destroying their food supply, inflicting famine on them, and causing people and animals to starve to death." 
Ezekiel14:14 Even if the three godly men, Noah, Daniel, and Job, all lived there, their righteous lives could only save themselves—no one else. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel14:14 Even if the three godly men, Noah, Daniel, and Job, all lived there, their righteous lives could only save themselves—no one else. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel14:14 Even if the three godly men, Noah, Daniel, and Job, all lived there, their righteous lives could only save themselves—no one else. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel14:15 “Or suppose I unleash wild animals on that country to roam through the land killing its people, making the land so dangerous that no one would dare pass through it." 
Ezekiel14:16 Even if these three godly men lived there, as surely as I, Lord Yahweh, am the living God, they would not be able to save even their own children. They alone would be spared, and the country would become a desert." 
Ezekiel14:17 “Or suppose I were to bring war upon that country, sending enemies with swords to devastate the land and wipe out both people and animals." 
Ezekiel14:18 Even if these three godly men lived there, as surely as I, Lord Yahweh, am the living God, they would not be able to save either son or daughter; they alone would be saved." 
Ezekiel14:19 “Or suppose I were to send a pandemic on that country and pour out my wrath upon the people with a bloody death and wipe out both people and animals. 
Ezekiel14:20 Even if Noah, Daniel, and Job lived there, as surely as I, Lord Yahweh, am the living God—their righteousness would only be able to save themselves and not even their own children.”" 
Ezekiel14:20 Even if Noah, Daniel, and Job lived there, as surely as I, Lord Yahweh, am the living God—their righteousness would only be able to save themselves and not even their own children.”" 
Ezekiel14:20 Even if Noah, Daniel, and Job lived there, as surely as I, Lord Yahweh, am the living God—their righteousness would only be able to save themselves and not even their own children.”" 
Ezekiel14:21 Lord Yahweh spoke to me, saying, “How much worse it will be for Jerusalem when I send my four dreadful scourges—sword, famine, wild animals, and pandemic—to destroy both people and animals." 
Ezekiel14:22 Even so, there will be a remnant left, a few who survive. Enemy soldiers will bring your wicked sons and daughters out of the city, and they will be brought here to Babylon. When you see them and realize how sinful are their ways and deeds, then you will not feel so sad when you understand that I, Yahweh, brought all these disasters upon Jerusalem." 
Ezekiel14:23 You will feel better when you see their evil way of life and actions, for then you will realize that I had a good reason to do what I did. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel15:1 Yahweh spoke to me, saying:" 
Ezekiel15:2 “Son of man, how is the wood of the grapevine different than that of other tree branches of the forest?" 
Ezekiel15:3 Can its wood be used for making anything? Can it be used as pegs to hang things on? 
Ezekiel15:4 Look, it is good only for building a fire. When fire burns off both ends and the middle is charred, is it useful for anything?" 
Ezekiel15:5 Even while it was intact, you could make nothing with it. When it’s burned and charred, it’s even more worthless.”" 
Ezekiel15:6 So, Lord Yahweh says: “Just like I made the wood of the vine only useful as firewood, so I will treat the people of Jerusalem." 
Ezekiel15:7 I will oppose them! They may have escaped one fire, but another fire will yet devour them. When I set my face against them, then you will know that I am Yahweh!" 
Ezekiel15:8 Because of their unfaithfulness and infidelity, I will reduce the land to a desert. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel16:1 Yahweh spoke again to me, saying," 
Ezekiel16:2 “Son of man, confront Jerusalem concerning all the despicable things they have done." 
Ezekiel16:3 Tell them, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Your ancestral origins were in the land where the Canaanites lived. Your father was an Amorite and your mother a Hittite." 
Ezekiel16:4 On the day you were born, there was no one there to cut your umbilical cord or wash you clean in water. There was no one there to rub you with salt and oil or wrap you in a baby blanket." 
Ezekiel16:5 No one did anything to help you. No one felt sorry for you or showed sympathy for you. Instead, you were dumped into an open field and forgotten. From the day you were born you were rejected and unloved.’ ”" 
Ezekiel16:6 “ ‘Then I passed by, and I saw you there squirming in your blood. And as you lay there covered with blood, I spoke over you: “Live!”" 
Ezekiel16:7 And I made you grow and flourish like the grass of the fields. You grew up and developed until you produced the most beautiful of features. You developed the breasts and hair of a young woman, but you were still quite naked and exposed." 
Ezekiel16:8 Then later, I passed by again and looked upon you. And behold, I saw that your time had come—you were now old enough for love. So, I spread the edge of my robe over you and covered your nakedness. I gave you my sacred oath. I, Lord Yahweh, made a divine covenant with you, saying, “You will be mine,” and I took you as my own and promised to care for you." 
Ezekiel16:9 Then I washed the blood off you and anointed you with fragrant oil. 
Ezekiel16:10 I dressed you in beautiful, embroidered gowns, fine leather shoes, a fine linen headband, and wrapped you in rich fabric." 
Ezekiel16:11 I adorned you with jewels, beautiful bracelets, and necklaces." 
Ezekiel16:12 I gave you a nose ring and earrings and placed a sparkling crown on your head. 
Ezekiel16:13 You were decked with gold and silver jewelry and dressed in luxurious linen and rich embroidered cloth. Your meals were feasts, made with the best bread, honey, and oil. You grew more and more beautiful, and you rose to be queen." 
Ezekiel16:14 And you became famous throughout the world because the splendor I lavished on you made your beauty perfect, declares Lord Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel16:15 “ ‘But you became confident in your own beauty and used your fame to act like a prostitute. You offered your sexual favors to each one who came along—and you became his! 
Ezekiel16:16 You took some of your fine clothes to decorate the tents on the high places where you worked as a prostitute. 
Ezekiel16:17 You also took your gold and silver jewelry, which I had given you, and made yourself male images and acted like a prostitute with them." 
Ezekiel16:18 You took your embroidered clothes and used these to dress them up, and you offered them my sacred oil and my sacred incense." 
Ezekiel16:19 I gave you food made with the best flour, olive oil, and honey. But you offered it as a fragrant incense to these idols. I, Lord Yahweh, declare: that is exactly what you did!" 
Ezekiel16:20 You even took the sons and daughters I had given you and sacrificed them as food for the idols. As if your prostitution was not bad enough," 
Ezekiel16:21 you slaughtered my children and burned them as offerings to idols! 
Ezekiel16:22 While you were doing the things I hate and behaving like a prostitute, you entirely forgot how you were when you were young. I found you naked and exposed, squirming in your own blood." 
Ezekiel16:23 “ ‘Now I, Lord Yahweh, say you are doomed! Not only did you do these evil things," 
Ezekiel16:24 in every city square you built an elevated shrine for the worship of idols. 
Ezekiel16:25 At the entry to every alley you put up your brothel and dragged your beauty through the mud. You spread your legs to all who passed by in countless acts of prostitution. 
Ezekiel16:26 You slept with your well-endowed neighbors, the Egyptians, provoking my anger with your lifestyle of a prostitute." 
Ezekiel16:27 So listen, I have taken action to punish you and remove your portion of my blessing. I have put you at the mercy of your enemies, the Philistine women, who blushed over your lewd behavior." 
Ezekiel16:28 Still unsatisfied, you prostituted yourself to the Assyrians and played the harlot with them. But even then, you were not satisfied." 
Ezekiel16:29 You committed further acts of fornication with the Babylonians, that nation of merchants, but that still did not satisfy you.’ ”" 
Ezekiel16:30 “ ‘I, Lord Yahweh, say: You make me extremely angry because of all these things that you have done. You have acted like a brazen prostitute who has no shame." 
Ezekiel16:31 You built an elevated shrine on every street to worship idols, put up your pavilion in every public square. But you are not like a common prostitute just out for the money." 
Ezekiel16:32 You are an adulterous wife who would rather have sex with strangers than her husband. 
Ezekiel16:33 Men will pay a prostitute, but you give presents to all your lovers! You bribe them to come from everywhere just to sleep with you." 
Ezekiel16:34 You are so different from other prostitutes. No one came to ask you for sex. The men you slept with did not pay you, but you paid them. Therefore, you are worse than a prostitute!’ ”" 
Ezekiel16:35 “ ‘Very well, Jerusalem, you whore! Hear the word of Yahweh!" 
Ezekiel16:36 Lord Yahweh says to you: You poured out your lust and are ready for sex. As a prostitute, you uncovered your genitals for your lovers and for all those disgusting idols. You killed your children to honor those false gods." 
Ezekiel16:37 “ ‘Behold, I will bring together all your lovers to whom you gave pleasure—all those you loved and all those you hated. Yes, I will bring them from everywhere, and I will strip you naked in front of them and let them see your genitals." 
Ezekiel16:38 “ ‘I will judge you with the same death sentence of an adulterer or murderer and bring upon you the fury of my burning rage. 
Ezekiel16:39 I will hand you over to your “lovers,” and they will destroy your elevated chamber and pull down your sacred shrines. They will strip you of your clothes, take away your lovely jewels, and leave you stark naked." 
Ezekiel16:40 Then they will gather an angry mob against you who will stone you to death and hack you to pieces with their swords. 
Ezekiel16:41 They will burn down your homes and execute justice on you while many other women look on. I will once and for all put an end to your wicked harlotry—no more paid lovers for you! 
Ezekiel16:42 “ ‘Once my fury is exhausted with you, I will calm down and let my anger die away. Then I will not be angry anymore." 
Ezekiel16:43 You have entirely forgotten how I treated you when you were young. And you have done nothing but provoke me, so now I, in turn, will cause you to suffer the consequences for your own behavior. And haven’t you committed such gross lewdness in addition to all your other shocking sins? I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel16:44 Yahweh says, “Behold, all who quote proverbs will surely apply this proverb to you, Jerusalem: Like mother, like daughter." 
Ezekiel16:45 Yes; you are a daughter exactly like your pagan mother, who hated her husband and her children. And you have become exactly like your sisters, who hated their husbands and their children. You and your sister cities had a Hittite mother and an Amorite father." 
Ezekiel16:46 Your big sister is Samaria with her daughter- villages to the north of you. Your little sister is Sodom with her daughter- villages to the south of you. 
Ezekiel16:47 Yet you were not satisfied to live as the people of Samaria and Sodom lived, copying all their wicked ways and imitating their loathsome practices. In very little time, you became more wicked than they were in all your ways." 
Ezekiel16:48 As surely as I, Lord Yahweh, live, your sister Sodom and her daughters never did the evil that you and your daughters have done." 
Ezekiel16:49 Behold, the guilt of your sister Sodom! She and her daughters were arrogant, gluttonous, and lived in carefree complacency. They never thought to help the poor and needy." 
Ezekiel16:50 They were proud, stubborn, and defiantly evil before me, and so I swept them away as you have seen." 
Ezekiel16:51 And yet Samaria never committed half the sins that you have. You have done more detestable things than they. When compared to all your gross practices, you have made your sisters seem innocent!" 
Ezekiel16:52 So now you will bear the shame of your deeds, which provides a measure of justification for your sisters, since your sins are viler than theirs. They now appear more upright than you are. So, suffer the utter disgrace and humiliation of having made your sisters appear more righteous than you!" 
Ezekiel16:53 “I will restore their fortunes; I will restore Sodom and her daughters. I will restore Samaria and her daughters, and then I will also restore your fortunes along with them." 
Ezekiel16:54 You will be totally ashamed of yourself and bear disgrace for all you have done in giving them comfort. 
Ezekiel16:55 When your sisters, Sodom and her daughter-villages and Samaria and her daughter-villages, are restored to what they were, then you, too, and your surrounding villages will be restored to what you were." 
Ezekiel16:56 Jerusalem, did you not gloat in your pride over your sister Sodom" 
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was uncovered? Now you are just like her! You have become the laughingstock of the Philistines, the Syrians, and all your neighbors who hate you and heap scorn on you." 
Ezekiel16:58 You have brought this on yourself with your lewdness and your disgusting deeds. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel16:59 “For I, Lord Yahweh say to you: I will treat you as you deserve, for you have broken your marriage covenant and despised your promises to me." 
Ezekiel16:60 But I will remember my covenant that I made with you in the days of your youth. I will renew that covenant with you, and it will last forever." 
Ezekiel16:61 When you get back your older and younger sisters, then you will remember your ways and feel ashamed. I will make them like daughters to you, but not on the basis of my new covenant with you." 
Ezekiel16:62 Yes, I will graciously renew my covenant with you, and then you will know me as Yahweh -God." 
Ezekiel16:63 You may remember your wretched past and feel deep remorse, yet you will be reduced to silence when you realize that I have forgiven you for everything you have done. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel17:1 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel17:2 “Son of man, propose this riddle to Israel, and speak this parable to the people." 
Ezekiel17:3 Tell them to guess the meaning of what I, Yahweh, have to say to them: “There once was this great eagle with huge wings, whose wingspan was very wide, with many beautiful colored feathers around its head. One day he came to the Lebanon Mountains, and taking a new branch of a cedar tree," 
Ezekiel17:4 he broke it off and carried it off to a land of traders. He planted it there in a city of merchants. 
Ezekiel17:5 Next, he took a young seedling from the land and planted it in a fertile field. He placed it like a willow tree by the side of a flowing stream." 
Ezekiel17:6 The young plant sprouted until it became a low spreading vine. Its branches grew up toward the great eagle, and its roots grew down deep into the soil. So, it became a vine, branching out and sprouting new shoots." 
Ezekiel17:7 But another great eagle appeared with huge wings and many feathers around its head. And soon the vine bent its roots toward him and stretched its branches toward this eagle from the place where it was planted, expecting this eagle to nourish it." 
Ezekiel17:8 The vine was planted in good soil, by the side of a generous stream so that the vine could branch out, bear fruit, and become a wonderful vine." 
Ezekiel17:9 So I, Lord Yahweh, ask you: Will the vine thrive? Will not the first eagle come and tear out its roots? Will it not strip off its grapes so that all its foliage withers? Then, even weak ones could easily uproot it!" 
Ezekiel17:10 Yes, it is planted—but will it thrive? Will it not shrivel up when the east wind blows? Will it not wither in the fertile soil where it had grown?”" 
Ezekiel17:11 Then Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel17:12 “Ask that tribe of rebels if they know the meaning of my parable. Tell them: ‘Listen, the king of Babylon swooped into Jerusalem. Then he carried away the king and top officials and took them off to Babylon." 
Ezekiel17:13 He took one from the royal family and made a treaty with him, forcing him to swear an oath of allegiance to him. He also carried off to Babylon the leading men of Israel" 
Ezekiel17:14 so that their kingdom would be humbled and not rise again and to ensure that the treaty would be kept. 
Ezekiel17:15 But he rebelled against Nebuchadnezzar and sent envoys to Egypt to procure himself horses and a large army. Will he get away with that? Will a man who has done this go unpunished? Will he break the treaty and still escape? 
Ezekiel17:16 As surely as I, Lord Yahweh, live, this king will die in Babylon! He will die in the country of the king who put him on the throne, because he despised his oath and broke the treaty he had made." 
Ezekiel17:17 Despite Pharaoh’s mighty army and numerous troops, he will not be able to save him in war. When the Babylonians build siege ramps and wooden towers to kill many in the city, the Egyptians will not lift a finger to help!" 
Ezekiel17:18 For he despised his oath by breaking the treaty to which he had pledged himself, and he will not go unpunished." 
Ezekiel17:19 “ ‘Therefore, I, Lord Yahweh, decree, as surely as I live, I swear it. It was my oath that he has despised and my treaty which he has broken. I will punish him for this!" 
Ezekiel17:20 I will throw my net over him and trap him in it. I will take him to Babylon and render judgment against him there for all the wicked things he did against me. 
Ezekiel17:21 All his elite troops will fall by the sword, and the survivors will be scattered in every direction. And then you will know that I, Yahweh, have decreed it.’ ”" 
Ezekiel17:22 Lord Yahweh says: “I will take the highest branch from a cedar tree. I will break off from the very top a tender shoot and plant it myself on a high and lofty mountain. 
Ezekiel17:23 I will plant it on the highest mountain in Israel, where it will put out branches and bear fruit and grow into a stately cedar tree. Every kind of bird will find a home in it. Yes, in the shade of its branches the birds will make their nests."
