Ezekiel17:24 And all the trees of the field will know that I, Yahweh, am the one who brings the tall tree low and raises the low tree high, who withers the green tree and makes the withered tree sprout again. I, Yahweh, have spoken and have done it!”" 
Ezekiel18:1 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel18:2 “Why do you keep repeating this proverb in the land of Israel: “The parents have eaten sour grapes, but the children are the ones with the sour taste in their mouths?" 
Ezekiel18:3 “As I, Lord Yahweh, live, you will have no reason to repeat this proverb in Israel." 
Ezekiel18:4 Listen, every human life belongs to me; both the parent and the child are mine. The one who sins is held responsible and will die.”" 
Ezekiel18:5 “Suppose a man is morally good, and his actions are righteous and honest." 
Ezekiel18:6 He does not take part in the heathen feasts on the mountains nor worship the false gods of the people of Israel. He does not commit adultery nor have sex with a woman during her period. 
Ezekiel18:7 He exploits and defrauds no one. He does not steal but generously feeds the hungry and clothes the needy. 
Ezekiel18:8 He does not lend at interest or take any profit. He refuses to do anything evil and gives honest judgment between one person and another. 
Ezekiel18:9 He keeps my laws and is honest in all that he does. A person like this is righteous, and he will certainly live. I, Lord Yahweh, have decreed it." 
Ezekiel18:10 “But suppose he has a son who is prone to violence and bloodshed," 
Ezekiel18:11 doing what his father never did. He takes part in the heathen feasts on the mountains, commits adultery," 
Ezekiel18:12 exploits the poor and needy, robs, and defrauds others. He worships the false gods and engages in despicable practices" 
Ezekiel18:13 and charges exorbitant interest for profit when lending money—such a person will by no means live. Because he committed all these appalling deeds, he will surely be put to death, and he alone will be responsible for his own death." 
Ezekiel18:14 “But suppose this evil man has a son who sees all the sins his father practiced, yet chooses not to follow his poor example" 
Ezekiel18:15 and refuses to take part in the heathen feasts on the mountains or worship the false gods of the people of Israel. He does not seduce another man’s wife," 
Ezekiel18:16 nor exploit nor defraud anyone. He does not steal but feeds the hungry and clothes the needy. 
Ezekiel18:17 He refuses to do anything evil and does not lend for profit or charge exorbitant interest. He faithfully keeps my laws and teachings—such a person will certainly live and not die for his father’s sins. 
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father, because he practiced fraud, cheated, and robbed others, doing what is wrong among his people, he will most certainly die in his guilt." 
Ezekiel18:19 “Yet you ask, ‘Why doesn’t the child bear his father’s guilt?’ If a person lives an honest and upright life and keeps my laws and faithfully follows my teachings, he will most certainly live." 
Ezekiel18:20 It is the one who sins who will die. A child is not to bear his father’s guilt nor a father his child’s guilt. Good people will be credited for their own righteousness and the wicked with his own wickedness.” 
Ezekiel18:21 “However, if the wicked man renounces all the sins he has committed, obeys my laws, and does what is fair and upright, he will most certainly live and will not die." 
Ezekiel18:22 I will not remember any of the sins he committed in the past. Because he has done what is right, he will most certainly live." 
Ezekiel18:23 I, Lord Yahweh, say: Do you think I enjoy seeing the wicked die? I would much rather see him renounce his sins and live!" 
Ezekiel18:24 “However, if the righteous man abandons his righteous ways and acts wickedly, and follows all the despicable practices of the wicked, should he be allowed to live? None of his righteous deeds will be remembered from then on, for he is guilty of being unfaithful to me, and he will most certainly die." 
Ezekiel18:25 “But you complain, ‘The Lord is being unfair. That’s not right.’ Now people of Israel, listen to me: Do you think my way of doing things is unjust? Aren’t you the ones who are acting unjustly?" 
Ezekiel18:26 When the righteous man abandons his righteous ways, does what is evil, and dies, he dies responsible for the wrong that he himself committed." 
Ezekiel18:27 Similarly, when the wicked man abandons his wicked ways to do what is good and right, he saves his own life." 
Ezekiel18:28 Having considered his ways and having chosen to renounce all his former sins, he will most certainly live and not die." 
Ezekiel18:29 And yet the people of Israel say, ‘What the Lord does is not just.’ Really? What I do is not just, people of Israel? Aren’t you the ones who are acting unjustly?" 
Ezekiel18:30 So from here on, you people of Israel, I, Lord Yahweh, say to you: I will judge each of you by what he does. Repent, turn away from all your sins, or your own wickedness will be your downfall." 
Ezekiel18:31 You must get rid of all the guilty sins you have committed and clothe yourselves with a new heart and a new spirit. People of Israel, choose life!" 
Ezekiel18:32 I find no pleasure watching anyone die, so repent and live! I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel19:1 “Now Ezekiel, sing this funeral song to mourn the rulers of Israel," 
Ezekiel19:2 saying, “ ‘What a lioness was your mother among lions! She lay down among the young lions and reared her cubs." 
Ezekiel19:3 One of the cubs she raised grew into a young and powerful man-eating lion who learned to hunt and devour his prey. 
Ezekiel19:4 The nations heard about him, and they trapped him in their pit. With hooks they dragged him off to Egypt." 
Ezekiel19:5 When she realized that he was not coming back, she despaired and her hopes were dashed. She chose another of her cubs to take his place and made a powerful lion of him too." 
Ezekiel19:6 He prowled among the pride and grew into a powerful man-eating lion, who learned to hunt and devour his prey." 
Ezekiel19:7 He demolished their strongholds and destroyed their cities. Whenever the lion released his roar, the land and all the people were terrified!" 
Ezekiel19:8 The nations marched out against him from all around. They threw their net over him, and he was trapped in their pit." 
Ezekiel19:9 With their hooks they captured him, led him with bands around his neck to the king of Babylon. They threw him into a dungeon so that his roar could never be heard again on the mountains of Israel." 
Ezekiel19:10 “ ‘Your mother was like a grapevine planted beside a stream, with luscious fruit and full of branches, because the water flowed so abundantly." 
Ezekiel19:11 She grew strong branches, and some grew very high, which became royal scepters. The vine grew higher and higher, up into the clouds. Everyone could see her towering height and the vast number of her branches." 
Ezekiel19:12 But disaster struck the vine. Angry, mighty hands pulled it up by its roots and threw it to the ground. The hot east wind dried it up, and her fruit shriveled and fell to the ground. Her strong stem was withered and was consumed by the fire." 
Ezekiel19:13 Now what remains of her has been transplanted to the desert, to a dry and thirsty land." 
Ezekiel19:14 Fire spread from her stem and devoured its fruit. There is not a strong branch left that could become a scepter for a king.’ ” This is a funeral song of lament, and it should be used as such." 
Ezekiel20:1 It was the seventh year, the fifth month, and the tenth day after our deportation to Babylon. Some of the elders of Israel came to request that I ask Yahweh for his message to them. As we sat together," 
Ezekiel20:2 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel20:3 “Son of man, give the elders of Israel this message: ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Are you really coming to consult with me? As surely as I live, I will tell you nothing. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel20:4 Then he said to me, “Judge them, Ezekiel! Bring a charge against them, son of man! Confront them over the disgusting practices of their ancestors!" 
Ezekiel20:5 Tell them what I, Lord Yahweh, say: ‘On the day when I chose Israel to be my people, I pledged my word to the tribes of Jacob, and I revealed myself to them in Egypt. I pledged my word to them and said: I am Yahweh your God." 
Ezekiel20:6 That day I promised that I would bring them out of Egypt to a land I had searched out especially for them—a land flowing with milk and honey, the most splendid land of all." 
Ezekiel20:7 And I said to them, “Every one of you, get rid of the things you love that I hate. Don’t make yourselves unfit for my presence by worshiping the idols of Egypt. For I am Yahweh your God.”" 
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against me and refused to listen to me. Not one of them rejected the detestable things their eyes feasted on, nor did they give up the idols of Egypt. So in my anger, I decided to pour out my wrath on them and vent my anger against them in the midst of Egypt.’" 
Ezekiel20:9 I acted to protect the honor of my name, so that I would not be profaned in the eyes of the surrounding nations who saw me reveal myself by bringing the Israelites out of Egypt.”" 
Ezekiel20:10 “So I brought them out of Egypt and led them into the desert. 
Ezekiel20:11 I gave them my laws and taught them my commands, so that they would follow them and live by them." 
Ezekiel20:12 And I also gave them my Sabbaths as a sign between me and them so that they might know that I, Yahweh, am the one who sets them apart as my own." 
Ezekiel20:13 But the Israelites rebelled against me in the wilderness and refused to keep my laws and rejected my commands (the one who obeys my laws will live by them), and they completely dishonored my Sabbaths. I was about to vent my fury on them in the desert and destroy them." 
Ezekiel20:14 But I chose to withhold my punishment for the sake of my holy name, that it not be profaned in the eyes of the nations who had seen my power in bringing the Israelites out of Egypt." 
Ezekiel20:15 So, I made a vow in the desert that I would not bring that generation into the most splendid land that I had given them, a land flowing with milk and honey." 
Ezekiel20:16 For they had scorned my commands, refused to keep my laws, dishonored my Sabbaths, and their hearts were attached to idols." 
Ezekiel20:17 In spite of this, I took pity on them, so I spared them from destruction and did not make an end of them in the desert." 
Ezekiel20:18 “I warned their children in the wilderness: ‘Do not follow the ways of your ancestors, do not practice their standard of justice, and do not defile yourselves with their foul idols." 
Ezekiel20:19 I am Yahweh your God. Keep my laws, obey my commands, and follow them." 
Ezekiel20:20 Keep my Sabbaths as holy days. Let them be a covenant -sign between me and you, to remind you that I am Yahweh your God.’" 
Ezekiel20:21 “However, like their parents, the next generation rebelled against me; they refused to keep my laws and did not respect or practice my standard of justice so that they would follow my laws and live by them. They also dishonored my Sabbaths. So, in my fury I decided to punish them and pour out my anger on them in the desert." 
Ezekiel20:22 But I restrained myself to protect the honor of my name so that I would not be profaned in the eyes of the surrounding nations who saw me reveal myself by bringing the Israelites out of Egypt. 
Ezekiel20:23 Moreover, I made a vow to them in the wilderness that I would scatter them throughout the nations and disperse them into foreign countries." 
Ezekiel20:24 I did this because they had broken my laws, refused to obey my commands, dishonored my Sabbaths, and their hearts chased after the idols of their ancestors." 
Ezekiel20:25 And for this reason I gave them laws and regulations that resulted in punishment, not life." 
Ezekiel20:26 And because they sacrificed their firstborn children in the fire, I declared them defiled and unfit to worship me. I did this to fill them with revulsion so that they would know that I am Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel20:27 “Therefore, son of man, speak to the people of Israel in exile and say to them, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Here is another instance of your ancestors blaspheming me by their infidelity." 
Ezekiel20:28 When I had brought them into the promised land, they discovered all sorts of high hills and all kinds of spreading trees. That is where they offered sacrifices and made offerings to false gods that provoked my anger. That is where they burned incense and poured out drink offerings to false gods." 
Ezekiel20:29 So I asked them: What is this high place where you go?’ ” (So its name is called High Place to this day.) 
Ezekiel20:30 “Say to the Israelites in exile, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Will you defile yourselves in the same way your ancestors did and act like a prostitute with your idol worship?" 
Ezekiel20:31 For when you offer your gifts and sacrifice your children as sacrifices by fire, you defile yourselves with all your idol worship to this very day. Do you think you can come and consult with me? As surely as I live, I will not answer you. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken!" 
Ezekiel20:32 “ ‘You are saying that you want to worship idols of wood and stone and be like the nations around you. But what you have in mind will never happen. 
Ezekiel20:33 As surely as I, Lord Yahweh, live, I warn you: I will continue to rule over you with a strong hand and with all my power and my wrath poured out!’ ”" 
Ezekiel20:34 “ ‘You have been scattered into many different nations and lands. But with a strong hand and my mighty power and outpoured wrath, I will bring you back." 
Ezekiel20:35 I will bring you into the desert of many nations, and there I will enter into judgment with you face-to-face!" 
Ezekiel20:36 Just as I entered into judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of Sinai, so I now judge you. I am Lord Yahweh, and I declare this to you." 
Ezekiel20:37 I will make you pass under the shepherd’s rod and take note of you, as a shepherd sorts his sheep. And I will bring you into the bond of the covenant." 
Ezekiel20:38 I will purge out the rebels from among you, those who sin against me. I will remove them from the land where they are exiled, but they will not enter the land of Israel. Then you will know that I am Yahweh." 
Ezekiel20:39 And now, you people of Israel, Lord Yahweh says: Go ahead, all of you, worship your foul idols, if that’s what you want to do. But later you will have to listen and obey me! Then you will no longer dishonor my holy name by worshiping your foul idols and bringing your offerings to them.’ ”" 
Ezekiel20:40 “ ‘Lord Yahweh says: On the high mountain of Israel set apart for me, all the people of Israel—everyone in the land—will worship me. I will be pleased with you and accept your sacrifices to me. And there I will require your best offerings, all your best gifts dedicated to me, and your holy sacrifices." 
Ezekiel20:41 I will take you out from among the nations and lands where you have been scattered and gather you together. Then I will be pleased with you just as I am pleased with the sweet aroma of burning sacrifices. And I will put my holiness on display through you before the nations. 
Ezekiel20:42 And when I bring you back to the soil of Israel, the land that I pledged my word to give to your ancestors, you will know that I am Yahweh." 
Ezekiel20:43 You will remember your ways and all the vile actions by which you have defiled yourselves, and you will be disgusted with yourselves for all the evil things you did." 
Ezekiel20:44 And when I deal with you, not as you deserve because of your wickedness, but in a way that brings honor to my name, then you, the people of Israel, will recognize that I am God. I, Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel20:45 Then Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel20:46 “Son of man, look toward the south and prophesy against the forests of the southern land." 
Ezekiel20:47 Prophesy to the southern forest, ‘Hear the word of Yahweh: Behold, I, Lord Yahweh, say to you that I am about to light a fire in you that will consume every green tree and every dry tree of your land! This blazing fire will not be extinguished as it spreads to the north and consumes everything in its path." 
Ezekiel20:48 Everyone will know that it was I, Yahweh, who started that fire, and no one will ever be able to put it out.’ ”" 
Ezekiel20:49 But I protested and said, “But Lord Yahweh, everyone is already complaining that I only speak in riddles.”" 
Ezekiel21:1 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel21:2 “Son of man, turn toward Jerusalem and denounce her place of worship. Prophesy against the land of Israel" 
Ezekiel21:3 and say to the people, ‘ Yahweh says to you: I am your enemy, and I am about to draw my sword and destroy the good and evil alike." 
Ezekiel21:4 I am going to use my drawn sword to cut off the upright and wicked alike, everyone alive from the south to the north." 
Ezekiel21:5 Then everyone will know that I, Yahweh, am the one who has unsheathed my sword, and I will not put it back again!’" 
Ezekiel21:6 “Son of man, groan with bitter grief before their eyes. Groan as though your heart is breaking with sorrow." 
Ezekiel21:7 And when they ask, ‘Why these groans?’ reply, ‘Because of what I’ve heard is about to happen. Every heart will melt with fear, and every hand grow limp. Everyone will faint with fright and wet themselves. Behold, it is coming now! It is here!’ I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel21:8 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel21:9 “Son of man, prophesy the message that I, the Lord, give you: “A sword, a sword of judgment has been sharpened and polished," 
Ezekiel21:10 sharpened for a great slaughter, polished to flash like lightning! Should we rejoice in the dynasty of my son, Judah? No! The sword despises every such stick." 
Ezekiel21:11 I handed over the sword for someone to polish so he can use it, sharp, shiny, and ready for the hand of the executioner." 
Ezekiel21:12 Shout and wail, son of man, for the sword will come against my people, and all the leaders of Israel. They are thrown to the sword like the rest of my people. So beat your breast in anguish," 
Ezekiel21:13 for surely, I am testing my people. What if the scepter of Judah, despised by the sword, does not survive? I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel21:14 “So prophesy, son of man, and strike hand to hand in anger! Let the sword strike twice, even three times. For the sword is prepared for those to be slain, a mighty sword to terrify, with a great slaughter from every side!" 
Ezekiel21:15 This sword will make them stumble and fall, and their hearts will melt with fear. I have posted the threatening sword at every gate to flash like lightning, polished for the slaughter." 
Ezekiel21:16 Slash to the right, and ready on the left, whichever way your blade turns!" 
Ezekiel21:17 I also will strike hand to hand in anger and satisfy my fury! I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel21:18 Then Yahweh said to me: 
Ezekiel21:19 “Son of man, mark out two roads for the king of Babylon to come with his sword; both should originate from the same land. Then put up a signpost at the place where the road branches off to the city." 
Ezekiel21:20 Mark out the routes for the sword to come to Rabbah-of-the-Ammonites, and to Judah, and its fortress of Jerusalem." 
Ezekiel21:21 For the king of Babylon is already at the place where the two roads begin. He is consulting his gods to decide which way to go by shuffling arrows and throwing them on the ground to see where they point. He asks his favorite idols for advice, and he examines the liver of a sacrificed animal." 
Ezekiel21:22 It tells him to take the road on the right leading to Jerusalem, so the king decides to go there. His plan is to ready the battering rams to be prepared when he gives the order for slaughter. His army will shout the battle cry and set up the battering rams against the gates. They will make siege ramps and construct siege towers." 
Ezekiel21:23 “The people of Jerusalem will believe that these omens are false, for they have made sworn treaties and think they are safe. But Nebuchadnezzar’s decision to attack will remind them of their sins against God, and as a result, they will be captured." 
Ezekiel21:24 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says, ‘You have uncovered your waywardness by parading all your evil deeds. You flaunt your sins in everything you do. Because of this, your enemies will capture you by force!" 
Ezekiel21:25 “ ‘As for you, wicked and unholy prince of Israel, Lord Yahweh has a message for you. Your doom is approaching. Your time is up, and I will put an end to your crimes. Take off your royal turban and remove your crown. Everything is about to be turned upside down. Lowly ones will be raised up, and those who seem important now will become nobodies." 
Ezekiel21:26  
Ezekiel21:27 A ruin, a ruin, I will make this city a ruin —until the one arrives, the rightful ruler. Then I will hand him the crown to rule.’ ”" 
Ezekiel21:28 “Son of man, prophesy and announce what I, Lord Yahweh, say to the mocking Ammonites: ‘A sword, a sword is drawn and ready for slaughter! It is a sharp, polished sword to flash like lightning and to destroy." 
Ezekiel21:29 The visions you had are false. When you consulted your gods about what would happen, their answers were lies. Your day of judgment has come! It’s high time for your punishment to reach its climax. The sword is going to cut off your heads!" 
Ezekiel21:30 “ ‘Put the sword back in its scabbard. I will punish you in the land where you were born, the land of your origin." 
Ezekiel21:31 I will vent my fury on you, blow the fire of my rage against you, and hand you over to brutal men whose skill is destruction." 
Ezekiel21:32 You will be fuel for the fire. Babylonian blood will flow in your homeland, and no one will remember anything about you. I, Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel22:1 Yahweh spoke to me again and said," 
Ezekiel22:2 “Son of man, are you ready to judge the city of murderers? You must confront her with all the disgusting things she has done!" 
Ezekiel22:3 Prophesy to the city what I, Lord Yahweh, say to them: ‘Jerusalem, you bring doom upon yourselves because you have shed the blood of so many. You are defiled by your man-made gods." 
Ezekiel22:4 You have become guilty by the blood you have shed and defiled by your hand-made gods. Your time is up! Your day of reckoning is on the way. Therefore, I have made you an object of scorn and a laughingstock to all the nations." 
Ezekiel22:5 Nations near and far will taunt you, O infamous city, full of turmoil." 
Ezekiel22:6 “ ‘Behold! The leaders of Israel within your walls, one and all, have selfishly abused their power to shed blood." 
Ezekiel22:7 They have despised their fathers and mothers, cheated the foreigners who live within your walls, and mistreated the widow and orphan." 
Ezekiel22:8 All that I call holy, you have treated with contempt. You have dishonored my Sabbaths." 
Ezekiel22:9 In your city are those who slander in order to have someone murdered. Living among you are those who eat food sacrificed to false gods in mountain shrines and commit obscene acts. 
Ezekiel22:10 Your men have sex with their father’s wives. They force themselves on women who are having their periods and are ceremonially unclean. 
Ezekiel22:11 In your city a man commits adultery with his neighbor’s wife, and another man seduces his daughter-in-law or sister, his own father’s daughter." 
Ezekiel22:12 Some of your people will murder for pay. Some extort their neighbors with threats of violence, charging them exorbitant interest for obscene profits, yet they have entirely forgotten about me! I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel22:13 “ ‘Now I will bring my fists down on your acts of robbery and murder in your midst! 
Ezekiel22:14 Do you think your courage will endure when I am through with you? Do you think your hands will remain steady on your day of reckoning? I, Yahweh, have spoken, and I will do it!" 
Ezekiel22:15 “ ‘I will scatter you among the nations and send you into exile to foreign countries. I will put an end to the uncleanness within you. 
Ezekiel22:16 Because of your own actions, you will be grossly defiled in the sight of the nations, and then you will understand and realize that I am Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel22:17 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel22:18 “Son of man, for me, the people of Israel have become worthless dross. They all have become like the useless byproduct of refined silver, bronze, tin, iron, lead, all mixed up together and left in the smelter; they are dross." 
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore, I, Lord Yahweh, say to the people of Jerusalem, ‘Since you have all become worthless dross, leftover metal, I will collect you inside Jerusalem." 
Ezekiel22:20 Just as the metalsmith collects silver, bronze, iron, lead, and tin in the furnace, and the fire is fanned underneath to melt them down, so I will collect you there and melt you down in my furious anger." 
Ezekiel22:21 I will put you all in the furnace and fan the fire of my rage to burn against you. I will melt you there in the middle of the furnace. 
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is refined in the smelter, so you will be melted down inside the city, and you will understand and recognize that I, Yahweh, have vented my fury against you.’ ”" 
Ezekiel22:23 Yahweh spoke to me again and said: 
Ezekiel22:24 “Son of man, say to Israel, ‘You are a land that has had no shower nor cleansing rain on the day of my anger." 
Ezekiel22:25 Your prophets plot conspiracies as lions stalk their prey. They have devoured the people, enriched themselves by seizing their wealth and jewels, and left behind many widows." 
Ezekiel22:26 Her priests have violated my law and have no respect for what is holy. They have made no distinction between what is holy and what is common. They have not taught people the difference between clean and unclean; they ignore my Sabbaths, and I have been defiled among them." 
Ezekiel22:27 Within the land are leaders who are like a pack of wolves who rip apart their prey, shed blood, and kill people to get money dishonestly." 
Ezekiel22:28 Her false prophets have plastered over these things with their empty visions and false prophecies, saying: “ Yahweh says this,” even though I, Yahweh, have not spoken." 
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have practiced extortion and robbery! They have oppressed the poor and needy and mistreated the foreigner among them who have no way of getting justice.’ ” 
Ezekiel22:30 “I searched for someone who would repair the wall, one who fills the gap, an intercessor to cry out for mercy, but I found no one. There was no one found who would keep my justice from destroying the nation." 
Ezekiel22:31 So I will pour out my fury on them, and like a fire I will destroy them. I will punish them for the evil they have done. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel23:1 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel23:2 “Son of man, there once were two women, daughters of the same mother." 
Ezekiel23:3 While they were still girls, they became prostitutes in Egypt. Men caressed their breasts; their virgin breasts were first fondled there." 
Ezekiel23:4 Oholah (Samaria) was the older and Oholibah (Jerusalem) her younger sister. I married them both, and they bore me sons and daughters." 
Ezekiel23:5 “Although Oholah was mine, she continued her prostitution. She lusted after her lovers, the Assyrians—warriors" 
Ezekiel23:6 wearing uniforms of purple, governors, and officials. All of them were young and handsome and skilled horsemen." 
Ezekiel23:7 She was a prostitute for all the most important men of Assyria. She made herself unfit for my presence by worshiping all the idols of every man she had sex with. 
Ezekiel23:8 When she was a girl in Egypt, men fondled her breasts and made love to her often, pouring their lust upon her repeatedly. She did not give up her prostitution." 
Ezekiel23:9 “So I handed her over to the power of her lovers, to the Assyrians whom she lusted after." 
Ezekiel23:10 They stripped her naked, seized her sons and daughters, and killed her with the sword. She became notorious among women because of her punishment for her sins." 
Ezekiel23:11 “Her sister Oholibah saw all this, but she was even more depraved and a wilder prostitute than her sister." 
Ezekiel23:12 She lusted after the Assyrians—warriors dressed to perfection, governors, and officials. All of them were young and handsome and skilled horsemen." 
Ezekiel23:13 Then I saw that she was completely immoral; both sisters were equally corrupt. 
Ezekiel23:14 But Oholibah sank ever deeper into unrestrained sexual immorality, worse than ever. She saw the wall carvings of the Babylonian men, painted bright red, the images of the elite men of Babylon (natives of Chaldea), and her lust was enflamed even more. The men looked magnificent, like officers going into battle on their horses! They were the high-ranking Babylonian chariot officers with beautiful sashes tied around their waists and elaborate turbans on their heads." 
Ezekiel23:15  
Ezekiel23:16 As soon as she saw them, she went wild with lust and sent messengers to them in Babylon to come and have sex with her." 
Ezekiel23:17 The Babylonians came to her, shared her love-bed, and defiled her with their lust. Once they had defiled her, she withdrew her affection and turned her back on them." 
Ezekiel23:18 She exposed her naked body to everyone and showed them openly that she was a prostitute. Therefore, I turned my back on her just as I had done with her sister." 
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she began sinking even deeper into her depraved sexual immorality, acting the way she did when she was a young prostitute in Egypt." 
Ezekiel23:20 She was driven by insatiable lust for Egyptian men, with genitals as large as donkeys’, ejaculating as forcefully as stallions." 
Ezekiel23:21 In that way, you were longing to repeat the debauchery of your youth, when men used to fondle your nipples in Egypt and played with your young breasts.”" 
Ezekiel23:22 “And so, Oholibah, Lord Yahweh says to you: ‘Behold, I will stir up all your former lovers against you, from whom you have withdrawn your affection. I will bring them against you from every side:" 
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans, men of Pekod, Shoa, and Koa, and all the Assyrians with them, young and handsome men, all governors, officials, and warriors—all of them skillful horsemen." 
Ezekiel23:24 They will attack you from the north and advance on you with chariots, wagons, and troops, setting themselves against you on all sides with shields and helmets. I will commit to them the right to judge you, and they will pass sentence on you as they see fit." 
Ezekiel23:25 I will direct my fiery indignation against you and let them treat you with fury. They will slice off your nose and ears, and what is left of your people will fall by the sword. They will capture your surviving sons and daughters and burn them alive." 
Ezekiel23:26 They will strip off your clothes and rob you of your fine jewels. 
Ezekiel23:27 “ ‘So I will put a stop to your debauchery and the habitual prostitution you began in Egypt. You will not view the Egyptians as your lovers anymore. You will never think of them again. 
Ezekiel23:28 For Lord Yahweh says: Now, I will hand you over to those you hate, those for whom you no longer feel affection." 
Ezekiel23:29 They will treat you with hatred, strip you of all that you have worked hard for, and leave you stark naked. Just as you were naked in your prostitution, so you will be naked when everyone discovers your debauchery and habitual prostitution!" 
Ezekiel23:30 You have brought all this on yourself because you have played the whore with the nations and have made yourself unfit for my presence by worshiping their idols. 
Ezekiel23:31 Since you have followed your sister’s footsteps, I will put her cup of punishment in your hands to drink." 
Ezekiel23:32 The Lord Yahweh says to you: “ ‘You will drink of the same cup as your sister, a large cup both deep and wide that contains much sorrow! Drink it all, for it will lead to mockery and insults!" 
Ezekiel23:33 You will be drunk with misery and sorrow. It is a cup of horror and devastation, the cup of your sister Samaria." 
Ezekiel23:34 You will drain it dry. Then, in despair, you will smash the cup in pieces, gnaw on the shattered shards, and cut off your own breasts, once fondled by others. For I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel23:35 “ ‘And so, Lord Yahweh says: Because you have forgotten me and have turned your back on me, you too will have to bear the consequences of your outrageous, depraved sexual immorality.’ ”" 
Ezekiel23:36 And Yahweh said to me, “Son of man, are you ready to render judgment upon Oholah and Oholibah and charge them with their detestable practices?" 
Ezekiel23:37 They have been wicked adulteresses. Their hands are dripping with blood, and they have committed gross adultery with their vile images. They have even committed murder by offering up their children, who are mine, as burnt sacrifices to feed their false gods!" 
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover, they have sinned against me by defiling my sanctuary and dishonoring my Sabbaths." 
Ezekiel23:39 The same day they sacrificed their children to their idols, they came into my sanctuary and made it unfit for worship. Yes, this is what they have done in my own house!" 
Ezekiel23:40 “Worse still, they sent messengers to summon men from far away to come have sex with them, and they came. To meet your lovers you bathed yourselves, painted your eyes, put on your provocative clothes with your finest jewelry." 
Ezekiel23:41 You reclined on your sumptuous couch with a table spread before you with my aromatic incense and oils. 
Ezekiel23:42 The noise of your party, a carefree crowd, echoed all around— a noisy crowd of drunk nomads! They put bracelets on the women’s arms and magnificent headpieces on their heads." 
Ezekiel23:43 I thought, ‘Isn’t that woman worn out with her adultery? Let them use her as a prostitute—she’s a professional.’" 
Ezekiel23:44 “Yet the men continued to have sex with her as one does with a prostitute, just as they slept with Oholah and Oholibah—those lewd women!" 
Ezekiel23:45 But there are righteous men who will declare them guilty of adultery and murder. For they are, indeed, adulteresses and their hands are stained with blood.”" 
Ezekiel23:46 Lord Yahweh says: “Gather a mob to deal with them, to rob and terrorize them!" 
Ezekiel23:47 Let the mob stone them and attack them with their swords. Let their sons and daughters be slaughtered and their houses set on fire. 
Ezekiel23:48 This is how I shall purge the land of lewdness so that all women will be warned not to commit prostitution as you have done. 
Ezekiel23:49 And you two sisters, you will be punished as your sins deserve. You will bear the price for your immorality and your vile idolatry. Then you will know that I am Lord Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel24:1 Yahweh spoke to me and said: “Son of man, write down today’s date: January 15, the ninth year of your captivity, for this is the very day the king of Babylon begins his attack on Jerusalem." 
Ezekiel24:2  
Ezekiel24:3 Pronounce this parable for these rebels: ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: “ ‘Put a cooking pot on the fire; put it on and fill it with water. 
Ezekiel24:4 Take one of the best of the flock, kill it, cut it in pieces, then put the best pieces in the pot — the thighs, the shoulders, and the choicest joints. Fill the pot with the most tender cuts, pile firewood underneath it and boil it until the bones are thoroughly cooked." 
Ezekiel24:5  
Ezekiel24:6 “ ‘For the Lord Yahweh says this: Disaster is in store for the bloody city, for you are like a scum-filled cooking pot that no amount of scrubbing can make clean! Empty the pot, piece by piece, in whatever order it comes." 
Ezekiel24:7 For your city is still full of bloodshed. Your people have poured the blood of your murdered victims on the bare rocks. You didn’t bother to pour it on the ground where at least the dust could cover it. 
Ezekiel24:8 To awaken my anger and exact vengeance, I have put the blood of those you murdered on a bare rock where it will not be hidden!" 
Ezekiel24:9 “ ‘I, Lord Yahweh, say to you: Doom is in store for the city of bloodshed. I, too, will pile up wood for the fire." 
Ezekiel24:10 Heap on the firewood! Light it! Cook the meat well until the broth boils away, until the bones are burned up!" 
Ezekiel24:11 Set the empty pot on the coals until it gets hot and the metal glows red-hot. The scum will become soft enough to scrub, and what is left will be burned off." 
Ezekiel24:12 But it still can’t be cleaned and its scum can’t be removed, so into the fire you go!" 
Ezekiel24:13 “ ‘ Jerusalem, the filth of your sexual immorality is infamous. Although I have tried to purify you, you were not cleansed of your filth, so now you will never be purged of your filth until I have emptied out my fury upon you." 
Ezekiel24:14 I, Yahweh, have decreed it. The time has come, and I will do it! I will not hold back or relent. I will show you no pity. I will judge you according to the way you have lived and acted. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel24:15 Yahweh spoke to me and said," 
Ezekiel24:16 “Son of man, behold, in the blink of an eye I am about to take from you your heart’s dearest treasure, the one you love the most. But you must not weep or wail or let your tears stream down your cheeks." 
Ezekiel24:17 Let no one hear your sobbing; don’t show that you are grieving. Wrap a turban around your head, put sandals on your feet, and dress normally. Don’t cover your face, and don’t eat the customary food of mourners.”" 
Ezekiel24:18 So, I preached to the people the next day as usual, and by evening, my wife had died. And I did as I had been instructed." 
Ezekiel24:19 The people then said to me, “What’s the meaning of this? Why are you acting this way?”" 
Ezekiel24:20 Then Yahweh spoke to me and I replied, “ Yahweh told me" 
Ezekiel24:21 to prophesy to the people of Israel the words of Lord Yahweh: ‘Behold! I am about to make my sanctuary ritually unclean. My temple has become your boast, the pride of your strength, the delight of your eyes, the joy of your hearts, and you believe it will keep you safe. But your children whom you left behind in Jerusalem will be killed by the sword." 
Ezekiel24:22 Then you will do as I have done; you will not cover your faces nor eat the customary food of mourners. 
Ezekiel24:23 You will keep turbans on your heads and your sandals on your feet. You will not mourn nor weep but will waste away for your sins and groan among yourselves. 
Ezekiel24:24 Thus Ezekiel is a sign for you. Everything he has done, you will do. And when this happens, you will certainly know that I am Lord Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel24:25 “And you, son of man, this is what will happen on the day that I deprive them of their temple -stronghold, their beautiful joy and glory, their heart’s desire, and their greatest treasure—I will also take away their sons and daughters." 
Ezekiel24:26 And on that day a survivor from Jerusalem will bring you the report. 
Ezekiel24:27 That same day, when he comes to you, your mouth will be opened, and you will be able to prophesy to the survivor and be silent no longer. And you will be a living symbol of hope for them, and they will see and acknowledge that I am Yahweh!”" 
Ezekiel25:1 Yahweh spoke to me and said," 
Ezekiel25:2 “Son of man, turn toward the land of the Ammonites and prophesy against them." 
Ezekiel25:3 Say to them, ‘Hear the words of Lord Yahweh. He says to you: You gloated when my temple became unfit for my presence. You celebrated when the land of Israel was left in ruins. And when the people of Judah were taken into captivity, you shouted, “Hooray!”" 
Ezekiel25:4 Therefore, behold! I am handing you over to the tribes of the eastern desert to come and take possession of you. They will take over your country and make their home in you. They will feast on the crops of your fields and drink the milk." 
Ezekiel25:5 I will turn your great city Rabbah into a camel yard. Yes, I will turn all the land of Ammon into sheepfolds. Then you will recognize that I am Yahweh." 
Ezekiel25:6 “ ‘Furthermore, Lord Yahweh says to you: Since you have clapped your hands and danced for joy, full of malicious glee at Israel’s fate," 
Ezekiel25:7 therefore behold! My mighty hand will be against you for this. I will hand you over to be looted by the nations, and I will obliterate you as a nation and you will perish as a country. I will reduce you to nothing, and then you will recognize that I am Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel25:8 Lord Yahweh says: “Moab and Seir have said that the people of Judah are no different from any other nation. 
Ezekiel25:9 Therefore, behold! I will let your enemies clear out the cities that defend the border of Moab—your fine cities, jewels of your land—Beth Jeshimoth, Baal-Meon, and Kiriathaim." 
Ezekiel25:10 To the same eastern desert tribes that invade Ammon, I will hand over Moab so that Ammon will be forgotten forever as a nation." 
Ezekiel25:11 I will carry out my punishment of Moab, and then they will know that I am Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel25:12 Lord Yahweh says: “The people of Edom took revenge on the people of Judah and sinned greatly in doing so. 
Ezekiel25:13 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says to you: ‘My hand will be against you, and I will slaughter every person and animal living among you. There will be no one left anywhere in the land, from Teman to Dedan, all the people will fall by the sword!" 
Ezekiel25:14 I will use my people Israel to take my vengeance on Edom. My people will treat Edom as my furious anger demands, and they will realize that this is my vengeance! I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel25:15 Lord Yahweh says, “The Philistines took revenge on the people of Judah, with deep hatred. They did their best to destroy them because of their long-standing hatred." 
Ezekiel25:16 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says to you: My hand will be against the Philistines; I will exterminate the Cherethite tribe and destroy the rest of the coastal peoples." 
Ezekiel25:17 I will execute frightful acts of vengeance and punish them in my wrath! And then they will realize that I, Yahweh, exacted my vengeance on them.”" 
Ezekiel26:1 On the first day of the month in the eleventh year of our captivity, Yahweh spoke to me and said:" 
Ezekiel26:2 “Son of man, the people of Tyre have been cheering the demise of Jerusalem, saying, ‘Hooray! She is fallen! The Gateway to the Nations is shattered! An opportunity has swung open for us. Now we will grow rich off her destruction!’" 
Ezekiel26:3 “Therefore, I, Lord Yahweh, say to Tyre, ‘Behold, I am against you, As the sea raises up its waves I will raise up many nations surging against you." 
Ezekiel26:4 They will destroy the walls of Tyre, and demolish her high towers. I will sweep away her rubble and reduce her to nothing but a bare rock." 
Ezekiel26:5 Tyre will be an island where fishermen dry out their nets. Your enemies will take away everything you have. I, Lord Yahweh, have decreed it." 
Ezekiel26:6 And as for her settlements on the mainland, they will all be slaughtered, and they will certainly learn that I am Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel26:7 “Lord Yahweh says to Tyre: “Look! I will bring Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, the world’s most powerful king, sweeping down from the north to destroy you with horses, chariots, cavalry, and an enormous army." 
Ezekiel26:8 His armies will massacre your settlements on the mainland. They will lay siege to you and construct towers to attack you. They will build siege-ramps against you and lay siege to you with interlocking shields over their heads. 
Ezekiel26:9 His soldiers will pummel your walls with battering rams and demolish your towers with iron bars. 
Ezekiel26:10 You’ll be covered with dust from their many horses and chariots. Your walls will shake at the rumble of their chariot wheels. His thundering horses will make your walls tremble as they storm through your gates and breach your walls. 
Ezekiel26:11 With galloping horses they will trample through every street and slaughter your people with the sword! They will throw your mighty pillars to the ground. 
Ezekiel26:12 Your enemies will take for themselves your valuable possessions and loot your merchandise. They will break down your walls and destroy your luxurious homes. And they will hurl the rubble and timber into the sea. 
Ezekiel26:13 I will silence the sound of your songs, and the music of your stringed instruments will not be heard again." 
Ezekiel26:14 I will reduce you to a bare rock where fishermen dry their nets, never to be rebuilt. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken, and I have decreed it.”" 
Ezekiel26:15 Lord Yahweh says to Tyre: “Surely, the islands will quake with fear at the sound of your fall. They will hear the groan of your wounded when your slaughter takes place." 
Ezekiel26:16 All the princes of the sea will vacate their thrones, laying aside their regal robes and embroidered cloaks. They will clothe themselves with trembling as they sit on the ground, stunned at your fate." 
Ezekiel26:17 This is the funeral song they will sing over you: “ ‘How you have vanished from the seas, O famous city, once a sea power with many people! You inspired great terror in all those who lived in her." 
Ezekiel26:18 Now, on the day of your fall, the islands and coasts are trembling with fear. They are paralyzed with great fear, because Tyre has vanished completely!’ ”" 
Ezekiel26:19 Lord Yahweh says: “When I make you a place of ruins like other deserted cities and I raise the deep to sweep over you and the ocean depths to cover you," 
Ezekiel26:20 then I will fling you down to the world of the dead to join the people of long ago. I will make you dwell deep in the underworld, in the abyss. And like the ruins of long ago, you will sink with them into oblivion. I will make sure that no one will ever live in you again, nor will you take your place in the land of the living." 
Ezekiel26:21 I will bring you to a dreadful, terrifying end, and you will be no more! People will look for you but never find you again. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel27:1 Yahweh spoke to me and said," 
Ezekiel27:2 “Son of man, sing a funeral song for Tyre." 
Ezekiel27:3 Say to Tyre, the city with ports on the Mediterranean Sea, merchant city for many peoples and many islands: “ ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Tyre, you have boasted: “I am the most beautiful city in the world!”" 
Ezekiel27:4 Your frontiers were on the high sea, and those who built you made you perfect in beauty." 
Ezekiel27:5 As if they were building a ship they used cypress from Mount Hermon for all your boards, and a tall cedar from Lebanon to make a mast to stand above you." 
Ezekiel27:6 They made your oars from oaks of Bashan, and made you a fine deck with pines they took from the coasts of Cyprus, and they decorated it with ivory." 
Ezekiel27:7 Your sails and banners were of colorful embroidered linen from Egypt. Your deck awnings were made from the finest purple and scarlet cloth from Cyprus. 
Ezekiel27:8 Your oarsmen were rulers from the cities of Sidon and Arvad, and your own seasoned men were your sailors." 
Ezekiel27:9 The elders and craftsmen of Byblos were those who caulked your seams. “ ‘Ships and sailors the world over came to trade with you. 
Ezekiel27:10 Your army was composed of warriors from Persia, Lud, and Libya. By hanging their weapons on your deck, they displayed your splendor." 
Ezekiel27:11 Soldiers from Arvad and Helech manned your walls all around. Brave warriors manned your watchtowers, hanging their shields all around your walls to make your beauty complete.’ ”" 
Ezekiel27:12 “ ‘The people of Tarshish traded with you because of your abundant resources of every kind. They imported your products and exported silver, iron, tin, and lead." 
Ezekiel27:13 The Greeks along with nations of Tubal and Meshech traded with you. They sold you slaves and metal utensils. 
Ezekiel27:14 The people of Beth Togarmah traded for your goods, your war horses and work horses and mules." 
Ezekiel27:15 The people of Rhodes traded with you; many coastal lands were your special customers and paid you in ivory tusks and ebony wood. 
Ezekiel27:16 Edom traded with you emeralds, purple cloth, embroidered work and fine linen, coral and rubies for your many fine manufactured goods." 
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel also paid for your goods with the finest millet, spices, honey, olive oil, and balm." 
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus traded with you, for quantities of your abundant resources of every kind, furnishing you with wine from Helbon and unbleached wool," 
Ezekiel27:19 casks of wine from Izalla, polished iron, cassia and calamus in exchange for your goods." 
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddle blankets. 
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the sheikhs of Kedar were your clients, selling sheep and goats to you." 
Ezekiel27:22 Businessmen of Sheba and Raamah traded with you the finest spices, precious stones, and gold." 
Ezekiel27:23 Haran, Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad were your trading partners." 
Ezekiel27:24 In your marketplace they sold you gorgeous embroidered clothing, purple cloaks, multi-colored carpets, and well-made cords for your merchandise.’ ”" 
Ezekiel27:25 “ ‘Heavily loaded Spanish ships carried your wares in the heart of the seas. 
Ezekiel27:26 Your oarsmen rowed you out to the open sea. Stormy east winds wrecked you and sent you to the bottom of the sea. 
Ezekiel27:27 On the day of your shipwreck, your riches, your goods, your cargo, your seamen, your sailors, your caulkers, your dealers, all the warriors you carry, and all the passengers who are aboard will sink into the heart of the sea!" 
Ezekiel27:28 When they hear the shouts of your drowning sailors, the people on shore will tremble with fear." 
Ezekiel27:29 All the oarsmen will abandon ship; the seamen and sailors will come ashore. 
Ezekiel27:30 They will wail aloud over you and weep bitterly. They will throw dust on their heads in mourning and lie in ashes. 
Ezekiel27:31 They will shave their heads in sorrow for you and dress themselves in sackcloth. They will weep bitterly for you, Tyre, with heartfelt sorrow over your fall." 
Ezekiel27:32 Grieving, they will chant a funeral song for you, and wail with lament over you, saying: “Who is like Tyre, like a tower surrounded by the sea?”" 
Ezekiel27:33 When you exported your goods to satisfy so many peoples with the abundance of your wealth and wares you enriched the kings of the earth. 
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are broken to pieces by the waves; you lie at the bottom of the sea. Your cargo and all your crew have foundered with you. 
Ezekiel27:35 Those who dwell on the coast and the islands are stunned at your fate. Horror is written on the faces of their kings, and their hair stands on end." 
Ezekiel27:36 Your trading partners hiss at you in scorn, for you have come to a dreadful end. You are gone forever, never to return!’ ”" 
Ezekiel28:1 Yahweh spoke to me and said," 
Ezekiel28:2 “Son of man, say to the prince of Tyre, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: “ ‘Because your heart is swollen with pride, you have said, “I am a god. I sit in the seat of a god, enthroned in the heart of the sea.” You think you are as wise as a god, but you’re nothing but a man, not a god at all!" 
Ezekiel28:3 So, you really think you are wiser than Daniel, and no mystery baffles you?" 
Ezekiel28:3 So, you really think you are wiser than Daniel, and no mystery baffles you?" 
Ezekiel28:4 By your wisdom and vast understanding you have amassed a great fortune! You have accumulated gold and silver and placed them into your royal treasuries. 
Ezekiel28:5 Through your great wisdom in trading, your fortune has continued to increase, but it has made your heart grow prouder." 
Ezekiel28:6 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says to you: Do you really think your heart is like the heart of God?" 
Ezekiel28:7 See for yourself as I bring ruthless enemies against you, the most brutal of the nations! They will unleash their swords against all that your marvelous wisdom has built, and they will strike down your splendor!" 
Ezekiel28:8 You will die a violent death in the heart of the sea. They will throw you down into the abyss! 
Ezekiel28:9 Will you still say in the presence of your killers, “ Don’t you know I’m a god?” But you will be merely a mortal man, and not a god, when you are in the clutches of the ones who strike you down!" 
Ezekiel28:10 You will die like an outcast, a shameful death at the hand of foreigners. I, Lord Yahweh, have decreed it!’ ”" 
Ezekiel28:11 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel28:12 “Son of man, mourn for the king of Tyre. Say to him, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: “ ‘You were once the consummate model of perfection. You were full of great wisdom, and your beauty seemed perfect." 
Ezekiel28:13 You lived in Eden, in the very garden of God. Every precious jewel formed your mantle. You were dressed in splendor with diamonds, carnelian, topaz, chrysolite, beryl, onyx, sapphire, turquoise, and emeralds; each gem engraved in settings of gold. I, Yahweh, made them for you on the day I created you." 
Ezekiel28:14 I placed you with an anointed guardian cherub. You were on the holy mountain of God where you walked among the fiery stones. 
Ezekiel28:15 Your ways were blameless from the day I created you, until wickedness first appeared in you." 
Ezekiel28:16 Your vast wealth filled you with violence and sin. So, I have cast you down in disgrace from the mountain of God. I banished you, O guardian cherub, from among the stones of fire." 
Ezekiel28:17 Your heart became swollen with pride because of your beauty; your thirst for glory corrupted your wisdom. So, I hurled you down to the earth, and displayed you as a warning to other kings." 
Ezekiel28:18 So great was your sin— the injustice of your buying and selling. You defiled your sanctuaries. So, I set a fire inside of you, and it consumed you entirely! I reduced you to ashes on the ground before the eyes of all who saw you." 
Ezekiel28:19 All who once knew you are stunned at your fate. You have come to a terrible, dreadful end, and will be no more!’ ”" 
Ezekiel28:20 Yahweh spoke to me and said," 
Ezekiel28:21 “Son of man, turn toward Sidon and prophesy against her" 
Ezekiel28:22 with these words: ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: “ ‘Behold, I am against you, Sidon; I will manifest my glory in you when I bring my judgment upon you! Everyone will see my display of holiness and they will know that I am Yahweh!" 
Ezekiel28:23 For I will send a plague, and there will be blood in her streets. Enemies will attack her on all sides by the sword raised up against her, and the dead will fall in heaps within the city. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.’ ”" 
Ezekiel28:24 “As for the people of Israel, there will no longer be any hostile nation surrounding them like thorns or briars that would hurt or pierce them. Then people will know that I am Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel28:25 Lord Yahweh says: “When I regather the people of Israel from every place where they have been scattered, I will display my holiness among them for all the nations to see. They will live in the land that I gave to my servant Jacob." 
Ezekiel28:26 And they will live there securely, building houses and planting vineyards. When I execute judgment on all the mocking, hostile nations surrounding them, they will live in safety, and they will know that I am Yahweh, the God they worship.”" 
Ezekiel29:1 In the tenth year of our exile, on the twelfth day of the tenth month, Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel29:2 “Son of man, turn toward Hophra king of Egypt and prophesy against him and against all Egypt." 
Ezekiel29:3 Tell them what I, Lord Yahweh, say: “ ‘Behold, I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt— the great monster wallowing in its waterways. You boasted: “I made the Nile River for myself, and I own it!”" 
Ezekiel29:4 I will put hooks through your jaws, and make the fish of the Nile stick to your scales. Then I will drag you out of your waterways with all your fish clinging to your scales." 
Ezekiel29:5 I will leave you and all your fish on the ground to die in the desert. You will die in the open field, and I will feed you to the wild animals and the birds of heaven, and no one will collect your bones or bury them." 
Ezekiel29:6 Then all the inhabitants of Egypt will know that I am Yahweh. “ ‘ The Lord says to Egypt: You have been no help whatsoever to my people, Israel; you are nothing more than a crutch of flimsy reeds!" 
Ezekiel29:7 Wherever they grasped you, you broke in their hands and splinters pierced their hands. Whenever they leaned on you, you collapsed, and sent them sprawling." 
Ezekiel29:8 “ ‘So, Lord Yahweh says to you: I will bring war against you to eliminate both people and animals." 
Ezekiel29:9 The land of Egypt will become an empty desert, and they will know that I am Yahweh. Because you boasted, “The Nile is mine, I made it. It’s all mine,”" 
Ezekiel29:10 therefore, I am your enemy and the enemy of your waterways. I will reduce Egypt to a desolate, empty desert, from Migdol in the north to Syene in the south, as far as the border of Ethiopia." 
Ezekiel29:11 No person or animal will pass through it. For forty years it will remain uninhabited. 
Ezekiel29:12 I will turn Egypt into the most desolate of lands, and for forty years its cities will be the most desolate of ruined cities. And I will scatter your people among the nations and disperse them among foreign lands." 
Ezekiel29:13 “ ‘However, Lord Yahweh says this: After forty years, I will gather the Egyptians back from the nations where I scattered them." 
Ezekiel29:14 I will bring the Egyptian captives back and restore them to the way they were before in their homeland of Pathros. There they will be an insignificant kingdom. 
Ezekiel29:15 Egypt will be the weakest kingdom of all and no longer dominate other nations. For I will reduce it so that it will never again rule over the nations. 
Ezekiel29:16 It will never again be strong enough for the people of Israel to trust in. Its fate will be a reminder of Israel’s sin when they put their confidence in Egypt to help them. Then they will know that I am Lord Yahweh.’ ” 
Ezekiel29:17 In the twenty-seventh year of our exile, on the first day of the first month, Yahweh spoke to me, saying:" 
Ezekiel29:18 “Son of man, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has taken his army and launched an attack against Tyre and inflicted a heavy toll upon his soldiers. Their helmets have rubbed their heads bare, and their heavy loads made their shoulders raw and chafed, yet neither Nebuchadnezzar nor his army gained any reward from the campaign he led against Tyre." 
Ezekiel29:19 I, Lord Yahweh, will hand Egypt over to Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon. He will carry off its riches and loot the wealth of Egypt to pay the wages of his army." 
Ezekiel29:20 I am giving him Egypt as a reward for the labor he has undertaken on my behalf. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken!" 
Ezekiel29:21 “At that time, I will raise up a new day of power for the people of Israel. And Ezekiel, I will open your mouth, and when you speak, the people will listen to you. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel30:1 Yahweh spoke to me again, saying," 
Ezekiel30:2 “Son of man, prophesy and announce what I, Lord Yahweh, say to Egypt: “ ‘Weep and wail, for the day of disaster is here!" 
Ezekiel30:3 Yes, the day is near, the day of Yahweh is near! It will be a day dark with clouds, a time of trouble for the nations.’" 
Ezekiel30:4 “War will come upon Egypt, and terror will invade the land of Ethiopia. Egypt’s foundations will be leveled, multitudes will be slaughtered, and her riches will be carried away." 
Ezekiel30:5 Egypt and all her allies, Ethiopia, Libya, and Lydia, all Arabia, and Kub —they will all fall by the sword!”" 
Ezekiel30:6 Yahweh says, “Egypt’s allies will fall, and the pride of Egypt’s might will crumble. They will fall by the sword from Migdol in the north to Syene in the south. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel30:7 “They will be the most desolate in the midst of wasted countries, and their cities the most ruined among ruined cities." 
Ezekiel30:8 Then they will know that I am Yahweh when I set fire to Egypt and shatter all who support her. 
Ezekiel30:9 “At that time, I will dispatch messengers by river ships to terrify the unsuspecting Ethiopians. They will panic on the day of Egypt’s doom. Judgment is sure to come!" 
Ezekiel30:10 Lord Yahweh says: I will use the armies of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon to destroy the huge population of Egypt. 
Ezekiel30:11 I will bring him and his savage armies to ravage the country. They will wage war against Egypt and fill the country with corpses. 
Ezekiel30:12 I will dry up the waterways of the Nile and hand over the country to evil men. I will cause the land to be destroyed and everything in it, at the hand of foreigners. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel30:13 Lord Yahweh says: “I will demolish the idols and destroy the false gods from Noph. Egypt will be left without a ruler and a plague of fear will sweep over Egypt. 
Ezekiel30:14 I will make southern Egypt deserted, burn Zoan to the ground, and punish Thebes." 
Ezekiel30:15 I will pour out my fury on Pelusium, the stronghold of Egypt. I will wipe out the great population of Thebes." 
Ezekiel30:16 I will set Egypt on fire! Pelusium will be seized with great agony! The walls of Thebes will be torn down, and Noph will face distress daily from her enemies." 
Ezekiel30:17 And the cities of Heliopolis and Bubastis will go into captivity and their young soldiers will fall by the sword. 
Ezekiel30:18 At Tahpanhes, I will turn day to darkness when I shatter Egypt’s strength and silence the boast of her might. I will blanket Egypt with dark clouds, and her people will go into captivity." 
Ezekiel30:19 That is how I will punish Egypt, and then they will acknowledge that I am Yahweh.”" 
Ezekiel30:20 In the eleventh year of our captivity, on the seventh day of the first month, Yahweh spoke to me, saying:" 
Ezekiel30:21 “Son of man, I have broken the powerful arm of Pharaoh king of Egypt. Behold, you can see that no one has set the bone to make it heal. No one has wrapped it to help it heal and get strong enough to hold a sword again." 
Ezekiel30:22 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says, ‘Behold, I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt; I will break both his arms, the strong one and the broken one, and I will make him drop the sword from his hand." 
Ezekiel30:23 I will scatter the Egyptians to the wind and disperse them among the nations. 
Ezekiel30:24 I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon and put my sword in his hand. I will break Pharaoh’s arms, and he will moan in pain like a dying man before the Babylonian king." 
Ezekiel30:25 I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon, but I will make the arms of Pharaoh hang limp. And they will know that I am Yahweh when I put my sword into the hands of the king of Babylon and he wields it against Egypt." 
Ezekiel30:26 I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them among foreign lands. And then the people of Egypt will acknowledge that I am Yahweh.’ ” 
Ezekiel31:1 In the eleventh year of our captivity, on the first day of the third month, Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel31:2 “Son of man, say to Pharaoh king of Egypt and his multitude: ‘Who is comparable to you in your greatness?" 
Ezekiel31:3 I know who: Assyria! Once it was like a cedar tree in Lebanon, with its noble branches, its forest shade, and its lofty height, its treetop among the clouds." 
Ezekiel31:4 Plenty of water has made it grow, deep springs have made it tall. Streams flow all around where it was planted, sending rivulets to all the other trees." 
Ezekiel31:5 So it towered high, much higher than the other trees of the forest. Because of the plentiful waters making it grow, its branches increased in number and spread out very far." 
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of heaven nested in its strong branches, and wild animals gave birth under its shade. Many, many people sat in its shadow." 
Ezekiel31:7 It was beautiful in its greatness, tall, with such large branches! For its roots were planted in plentiful waters." 
Ezekiel31:8 The mighty cedar tree was unrivaled in God’s garden. No cypress compares to such lovely branches as these; No sycamore could match its splendor. No tree in the garden of God could match its beauty. 
Ezekiel31:9 I made it so lovely with its many branches. It was the envy of every tree in Eden, the garden of God.’ ”" 
Ezekiel31:10 Therefore, Lord Yahweh says: “Because it towered so high with its top into the clouds and its heart was proud about its height," 
Ezekiel31:11 I have rejected it! I have handed it over to the mightiest of the nations to be punished as its wickedness deserves. 
Ezekiel31:12 The most ruthless of nations cut down the cedar and left it deserted. Its broken branches littered the mountains, the valleys, and every ravine throughout the country. Those who once sat in its shadow have fled and abandoned it." 
Ezekiel31:13 All the birds of heaven settled in the fallen tree, and all the wild animals lay among its branches." 
Ezekiel31:14 “So from now on, let no tree, no matter how well-watered, grow that high again with its top pushing into the clouds. And let no watered tree grow that tall again. For all of them are doomed to death, and they will join those in the depths of the underworld of mortals with those who go down to the pit.”" 
Ezekiel31:15 Lord Yahweh says: “The day I cast it down to Sheol, I caused it to mourn, covering it with the deep springs. For I stopped its underground rivers, and I reduced the flow of the once plentiful waters. I clothed Lebanon with gloom as they mourned for the cedar tree, and all the trees withered because of it." 
Ezekiel31:16 I made the nations quake at the crashing sound of its fall. They trembled as I hurled it down to Sheol with those who sink into the pit. In the depths of the underworld, all the trees of Eden, the finest well-watered trees of Lebanon, were comforted when the cedar tree joined them." 
Ezekiel31:17 And the nations who sat in its shadow went down to Sheol with it to join those slaughtered by the sword. 
Ezekiel31:18 “Which of Eden’s trees could be compared to you in glory and greatness? Yet you have been hurled down with the trees of Eden to the depths of the underworld. You will lie among the uncircumcised and with those who have been slaughtered by the sword. So much for Pharaoh and all his multitudes! I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Ezekiel32:1 In the twelfth year of our captivity, on the first day of the twelfth month, Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel32:2 “Son of man, give a solemn warning to Pharaoh king of Egypt. Say to him: “ ‘You are like a young lion prowling among the nations, or like a sea monster lurking in your lagoons! You churn up the water with your trampling and muddy their streams." 
Ezekiel32:3 Lord Yahweh says to you: I will throw my net over you using a large group of nations. They will drag you to shore in my net, and I will leave you high and dry." 
Ezekiel32:4 I will fling you onto the open field and abandon you on the ground. I will gather all the birds of the air to feast on your flesh, and all the beasts of the earth will gorge themselves on you." 
Ezekiel32:5 I will scatter bits and pieces of your flesh on the hills and fill the valleys with your rotting corpse. 
Ezekiel32:6 I will drench the land with your oozing blood until it fills the ravines and spreads to the base of the mountains. 
Ezekiel32:7 When I snuff you out, I will hide the skies and darken the stars. I will cover the sun with clouds and turn off the moonlight." 
Ezekiel32:8 Heaven’s bright lights above you I will darken and shroud your land in darkness. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel32:9 “ ‘I will trouble the hearts of many peoples when I spread the news of your destruction to nations and lands unknown to you. 
Ezekiel32:10 What I do to you will stun many nations. When I brandish my sword before them all, their kings will shudder with horror at your fate. On the day they see you fall, each will tremble in terror, fearing your fate might become theirs!" 
Ezekiel32:11 “ ‘For Lord Yahweh says to Egypt: The king of Babylon is coming to attack you! 
Ezekiel32:12 I will cause your great army to fall by the swords of mighty warriors. They are the most barbaric of nations. They will slice the pride of Egypt into pieces, and all your people will be destroyed." 
Ezekiel32:13 I will also slaughter your livestock grazing beside the plentiful waters. No person or animal will muddy the water in the watering places with their feet and hooves. 
Ezekiel32:14 Then I will settle their waters and make their rivers flow as smoothly as oil. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Ezekiel32:15 “ ‘When I reduce Egypt to a desolate wasteland and strip the land of all it contains, when I annihilate all who live there, then they will acknowledge that I am Yahweh." 
Ezekiel32:16 “ ‘This is the funeral song that people will sing in mourning for Egypt. Women of other nations will sing in lament for it. They will utter a lamentation for the land of Egypt and all its many people. I, Lord Yahweh, decree it!’ ”" 
Ezekiel32:17 In the twelfth year of our captivity, on the fifteenth day of the twelfth month, Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel32:18 “Son of man, weep and mourn for the vast population of Egypt, and for the other mighty nations. For I will send them down to the world below with all who sink into the pit." 
Ezekiel32:19 “ Tell them, ‘Egypt, are you more favored than the other nations? Down with you! Go down and make your bed with the uncircumcised dead," 
Ezekiel32:20 with those who have been slaughtered by the sword! I will hand you and all your people over to the sword, and they will drag you away." 
Ezekiel32:21 From the depths of Sheol, the mightiest heroes, your allies, will shout to you, “Now they have come down. They have joined us among the uncircumcised dead, slaughtered by the sword.”" 
Ezekiel32:22 “ ‘Assyria is there with all her hordes, with the graves of her soldiers all around her. They fell on the battlefield, slaughtered by the sword." 
Ezekiel32:23 They made their graves in the deepest part of the pit. Her hordes surround her grave; they were all slaughtered! Those who once spread their terror throughout the world of the living now lie silent, slaughtered by the sword." 
Ezekiel32:24 “ ‘Elam is there, along with the graves of all her hordes. They were slaughtered—fallen by the sword. Those who once spread their terror throughout the land of the living have now joined the uncircumcised dead in the depths of the underworld. They have been disgraced and ashamed with those who sink into the pit." 
Ezekiel32:25 They have made a grave for her among her slaughtered multitudes, surrounded by all their tombs, all of them uncircumcised, slaughtered by the sword for having spread terror throughout the land of the living. They have carried their shame with them, with all those who sink into the pit. They have been placed among the slaughtered." 
Ezekiel32:26 “ ‘Meshech and Tubal are in the graveyard with their hordes all around their graves. They are all the uncircumcised dead, slaughtered by the sword for having spread their terror throughout the land of the living." 
Ezekiel32:27 They were not buried with the great warriors of ancient times. When the mighty warriors died, they went into Sheol wearing all their armor. When people buried them, they placed their swords under their heads and their shields on top of their bodies because these valiant heroes once terrorized the land of the living." 
Ezekiel32:28 And you, Pharaoh, will be broken and lie among the uncircumcised, with those slaughtered by the sword." 
Ezekiel32:29 “ ‘Edom is there, her kings and all her rulers, who, despite their might, have been buried with those slaughtered by the sword. They lie with the uncircumcised, with those who sink into the pit." 
Ezekiel32:30 “ ‘All the princes of the north and all the Sidonians are there. They have gone down in disgrace with the slaughtered because their powerful armies once terrified their enemies. They lie among the uncircumcised dead, those slaughtered by the sword who bear their shame with those who sink into the pit." 
Ezekiel32:31 “ ‘When Pharaoh and all his army see them, it will bring him some consolation at the sight of all his hordes killed by the sword. I, Yahweh, decree it!" 
Ezekiel32:32 Although I made him spread terror throughout the land of the living, he and all his hordes will be buried with the uncircumcised dead who were slaughtered by the sword. I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken.’ ”" 
Ezekiel33:1 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel33:2 “Son of man, give your people this message: ‘If I bring war to your nation, you must select a man and post him as a watchman." 
Ezekiel33:3 When he sees the enemy coming to attack, he must sound his shofar to warn the people." 
Ezekiel33:4 If people hear the warning but pay no attention and the sword overtakes them and kills them, they will bear the responsibility for their own deaths." 
Ezekiel33:5 Because they heard the sound of the shofar and paid no attention to the warning, their deaths are their own faults. But if they had listened to the warning, they could have saved their lives." 
Ezekiel33:6 “ ‘If, however, the watchman sees the enemy approaching but has not blown his shofar to warn the people and the sword overtakes them and kills a single one of them, even though that person was a sinner, I will still hold the watchman responsible for that person’s death.’" 
Ezekiel33:7 “Son of man, I have appointed you as watchman for the people of Israel. When you hear me speak a warning, give them my warning." 
Ezekiel33:8 If I say to the wicked, ‘Evildoers, you are about to die,’ and you fail to warn them to renounce their evil ways, they will die for their guilt, but I will hold you responsible for their deaths." 
Ezekiel33:9 But if you warn the wicked to change their evil behavior and they refuse to change, the wicked will die for their sins, but you will have saved your own life.”" 
Ezekiel33:10 “Son of man, say to the people of Israel, ‘You continually say: Our rebellious acts and our sins have caught up with us, and we are wasting away because of them. How then can we live?’" 
Ezekiel33:11 “Say to them, ‘As surely as I, Lord Yahweh, exist—I take no delight in the death of the wicked, but I delight in seeing them turn from their ways so they can live. So, repent and turn from your evil ways. People of Israel, why would you want to die? Turn and come back to me! ’" 
Ezekiel33:12 “Son of man, say to your people, ‘All the good things a good person has done will not be any help if that one decides to turn to sin. And all the sins of an evil person will not destroy him if the evildoer decides to repent and turn from sin. If the righteous turns to sin, the righteous person will not be able to live because of past righteous deeds.’" 
Ezekiel33:13 Suppose I say to a righteous person: ‘You will certainly live,’ but then he becomes confident in his righteousness and turns away and willfully sins; none of the good things he has done in the past will be remembered. He will die in his sins." 
Ezekiel33:14 If, however, I say to a wicked person: ‘You will certainly die,’ but then the wicked turns away from sin and does what is just and right" 
Ezekiel33:15 by returning what has been stolen and returning the borrower’s pledge and by following the laws that give life and no longer doing wrong, that person will live and will not die." 
Ezekiel33:16 None of his previous sins will be counted against him, for he has done what is just and right—therefore, he will certainly live." 
Ezekiel33:17 “Yet your people say: ‘The ways of the Lord are not right.’ But it is their ways that are not right! 
Ezekiel33:18 When an upright person turns away from what is right and does what is evil, that person will die for it." 
Ezekiel33:19 And when a wicked person turns from being wicked and does what is just and right, that person will live." 
Ezekiel33:20 Yet you say: ‘The ways of the Lord are not right.’ Listen, people of Israel, I will judge each of you according to your ways.”" 
Ezekiel33:21 In the eleventh year of our captivity, on the fifth day of the tenth month, one who escaped from Jerusalem arrived and informed me: “The city has been taken.”" 
Ezekiel33:22 Now the evening before the survivor arrived, the mighty hand of Yahweh had rested on me, and my speech was fully restored before he came to me in the morning—I could speak again." 
Ezekiel33:23 Then Yahweh said to me," 
Ezekiel33:24 “Son of man, the people who are living in the ruins in the land of Israel are saying, ‘Abraham was only one man when he took possession of the land, but we are many. Certainly, we can possess this land and rebuild.’" 
Ezekiel33:25 “Now give the people this message, ‘Lord Yahweh says to you: Do you really think you can take ownership of the land? You are evildoers! You murder others, you eat meat with blood, and you worship your foul idols." 
Ezekiel33:26 You rely on your swords, you engage in despicable pagan practices, and you are all adulterers. Do you really think I will give the ownership of the land to you?’" 
Ezekiel33:27 “Tell them that I, Lord Yahweh, solemnly warn them: As surely as I exist, those living in the ruined towns will fall by the sword, those in the countryside I will give to the wild animals for them to eat, and those among the crags and in caves will die from disease." 
Ezekiel33:28 I will turn the land into a total wasteland, and I will put an end to their arrogant power. Israel’s mountains will be so deserted that no one will pass that way again." 
Ezekiel33:29 Then they will know that I am Yahweh, when I turn the land into a barren desert because of all the filthy things they have done.”" 
Ezekiel33:30 “As for you, son of man, your fellow citizens are talking about you beside the walls and in the doorways of their homes. They keep saying to one another, ‘Let’s go hear what Yahweh has to say.’" 
Ezekiel33:31 They all flock to you to sit at your feet as my people. They listen to your words, but they will not act on them. Instead, they express genuine devotion to Yahweh, but their hearts are set on cheating others." 
Ezekiel33:32 As far as they are concerned, you are like an entertainer who sings a love song with a beautiful voice to a good musical accompaniment. They hear what you say, but no one will do it." 
Ezekiel33:33 When all your prophecies come true —and they are beginning to be fulfilled now—they will know that there has been a true prophet among them.” 
Ezekiel34:1 Yahweh spoke to me, saying," 
Ezekiel34:2 “Son of man, prophesy against the leaders of Israel; prophesy and say to them, ‘Listen, you leaders, Lord Yahweh has a message for you: Disaster is in store for the shepherds of Israel whose only concern is to feed themselves. Aren’t shepherds supposed to feed the sheep?" 
Ezekiel34:3 Yet you eat the fat, you shear the sheep to make your clothes, you slaughter the fattest sheep for your feasts, but you never feed the flock." 
Ezekiel34:4 You haven’t cared for the weak ones, brought healing to the sick, nor bandaged those who are injured. You haven’t looked for the strays nor brought the lost back to the fold. Instead, you have ruled over them cruelly and treated them harshly." 
Ezekiel34:5 Because there was no shepherd to care for the sheep, they were scattered and were eaten by every wild beast."
