Hosea8:7 “Since they sow the wind, they will reap the whirlwind. They will reap headless stalks of grain that produce no flour, and even if they did produce grain, foreigners would swallow it." 
Hosea8:8 Israel has already been swallowed up and is now powerless among the nations. Israel, you are like a useless piece of broken pottery." 
Hosea8:9 For indeed, you have sought assistance from Assyria, like a wild donkey wandering alone. Ephraim has sold herself to other lovers." 
Hosea8:10 Although they have hired allies from among the nations, I will round them up for judgment. They are already wasting away under the oppression of the king of princes.”" 
Hosea8:11 “The more altars Ephraim builds, the more places the people have to sin— altars for sinning and not for sin offerings." 
Hosea8:12 Although many times I have written many rules for my people, Ephraim regards them as something strange and foreign." 
Hosea8:13 They love to offer sacrifices to me so that they can eat the meat. But I, Yahweh, take no pleasure in them. I will not forget their guilt, and I will punish them for their sins. They will have to go back to Egypt." 
Hosea8:14 Israel has built palaces but has forgotten his Maker. Judah keeps on building fortified towns, but I will rain fire down on his cities to devour the strongholds with flames.”" 
Hosea9:1 Stop rejoicing, people of Israel! Stop rejoicing as you celebrate your festivals like pagans, for you have deserted your God and committed fornication with other gods. You have loved the prostitute’s pay on every threshing floor as if Baal were the one who could give you good harvests." 
Hosea9:2 You will not be able to enjoy the bounty of your harvest— for others will consume your wheat, oil, and wine." 
Hosea9:3 Ephraim will not remain in Yahweh ’s land; they will have to go back to Egypt or eat Assyria’s polluted food. 
Hosea9:4 Their sacrifices will not be pleasing to Yahweh. They will not be able to offer their firstfruits nor pour out drink offerings of wine to him, for their food will be like funeral fare— food given to those in mourning. Whoever eats it will become unclean. They will only have sufficient food to satisfy their hunger and not enough to offer to Yahweh in his house." 
Hosea9:5 What will you do on the appointed feast days, on the day you gather for Yahweh ’s festivals?" 
Hosea9:6 Look! Even if you flee from destruction, Egypt will round you up. Your gathering together will be for your burial in Memphis. Your silver treasures will be lost among the weeds, and thornbushes will overgrow your homes." 
Hosea9:7 Your day of punishment has come, and the time for recompense has arrived, and Israel will know it. You say, “The prophet is a fool. This man of the Spirit is crazy!” But your guilt is great, even greater than all your deep-seated hatred." 
Hosea9:8 This prophet of my God is a watchman for Israel. Yet, everywhere he goes, the people set traps for him. He is considered an enemy in the house of his God." 
Hosea9:9 Israel has sunk so deeply into depravity— just as you did at Gibeah. God will not overlook the guilt of your actions; he will punish you for your sins. 
Hosea9:10 Yahweh says, “When I found you, Israel, it was as pleasing as finding ripe grapes in the desert. When I saw your ancestors, it was as delightful as seeing the early ripe figs on a new fig tree in its first productive season. But when they reached Baal Peor they devoted themselves to the shameful god, Baal, and became as shameful as the things they loved." 
Hosea9:11 The glory of Israel will fly away from you like a bird, leaving you infertile—no pregnancy, no conception." 
Hosea9:12 Even if they raise their children, I will remove them, and not one will be left alive. Even worse than that will be when I desert you." 
Hosea9:13 Ephraim, I once saw you like the city of Tyre planted in a pleasant setting, but now you must send out your sons to be slaughtered in battle.”" 
Hosea9:14 Yahweh, give them . . . Yes, what punishment should I ask you to give? Give them wombs that miscarry and breasts with no milk." 
Hosea9:15 Yahweh says, “All of Ephraim’s evil began at Gilgal. It was there that I started to become their enemy. I will drive them out of my house because of the evil they have done. I will no longer love them because all their leaders are rebels." 
Hosea9:16 Ephraim, you’re finished! Your root has dried up, and you will bear no more fruit. Even if the women do give birth, I will put their beloved children to death.”" 
Hosea9:17 My God will disown you because you have not listened to him. You will become wanderers among the nations. 
Hosea10:1 Israel was once like a lush vine that produced plenty of grapes. Yet the more his fruit increased, the more pagan altars he built. The more abundantly his land increased, the more abundant were his sacred fertility pillars." 
Hosea10:2 Israel’s devotion is false and divided; soon the people must pay the penalty for their guilt. Yahweh will demolish their altars and shatter their sacred pillars. 
Hosea10:3 Then they will say, “We have lost our king because we have not feared Yahweh, but what could a human king do for us anyway?”" 
Hosea10:4 All they do is make empty speeches, phony promises, and useless treaties. Their so-called justice spreads like poisonous weeds among the furrows of the fields." 
Hosea10:5 Samaria’s citizens will tremble and mourn over the loss of their sacred calves of Bethel. Everyone will wail over its loss along with their idolatrous priests because their “glory” has gone into exile. 
Hosea10:6 They will carry the calf-idol to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Ephraim will reap the shame. What a disgrace for Israel! 
Hosea10:7 Samaria—your king has had his day. Your king will disappear like a twig on the surface of the waters. 
Hosea10:8 The hilltop shrines, the vanity and sin of Israel, will be demolished. Thorns and thistles will overgrow and cover their altars. Then they will say to the mountains, “Bury us!” and to the hills, “Fall on us!”" 
Hosea10:9 “Israel, since the days of Gibeah, you have not stopped sinning. From then until now, you have persisted in your ways. Were not the evildoers overtaken by war in Gibeah?" 
Hosea10:10 When I am ready, I will discipline these wayward people, and armies will be gathered against them to put them in bonds for their double sins.”" 
Hosea10:11 Yahweh says, “Ephraim was once obedient like a well-trained cow that loved to thresh the grain. Seeing her fair neck, I thought I would put Ephraim in the yoke. Judah will have to plow; Jacob must level the soil." 
Hosea10:12 Sow seeds of righteousness for yourselves, and you will reap a harvest of God’s unending love. Break up the hard, fallow ground of your heart. For it is time to seek Yahweh ’s favor until he comes to pour out righteousness on you like falling rain." 
Hosea10:13 But instead, you have cultivated lawlessness and reaped a harvest of injustice. You have eaten the fruit of your lies. Because you have trusted in your own strength and in your vast, mighty army," 
Hosea10:14 war cries will break out against your people, and all your fortified cities will be destroyed. Just as Shalman destroyed Beth Arbel on the day of battle, when children and their mothers were dashed to death on the ground," 
Hosea10:15 so it will be done to you, Bethel, because of your evil stacked upon evil. At dawn, as the battle begins, your kingship will be no more.”" 
Hosea11:1 “I have deeply loved Israel from his very beginning. From Egypt, I called my son." 
Hosea11:2 The more the people of Israel were invited to come to me, the more they turned away from me. They sacrificed to the Baal-idols and burned incense to pagan images" 
Hosea11:3 even though I was the one who taught Israel to walk. I took them up in my arms and carried them, yet they did not acknowledge that I was their healer." 
Hosea11:4 I gently pulled them to my heart with the cords of affectionate love. I stooped down to care for them and gently fed them. I showed them the same kind of tender love as one who picks up an infant and holds it to his cheek.” 
Hosea11:5 “Israel will return to the land of Egypt, and Assyria will rule over them because they refused to return to me." 
Hosea11:6 The whirlwind of war will break down the bars of their gates and ravage their cities in return for all their schemes. 
Hosea11:7 My people are bent on turning away from me, and together they call upon Baal, who will not help them at all.”" 
Hosea11:8 “Ephraim, how could I ever give up on you? Israel, how could I ever let you go? How can I treat you like Admah or deal with you like Zeboiim— cities I destroyed? My heart beats for you, and my compassion rekindles a deep love for you within me." 
Hosea11:9 I will certainly not punish you in my burning anger, nor will I turn around and destroy Israel again, for I am God and not a mere human being. I am the Holy One in the midst of you; I will not come to you in my wrath.”" 
Hosea11:10 “When I, Yahweh, roar like a lion, my people will march behind me. Yes, I will roar like a lion to gather my own around me! Indeed, when I roar, my children will come trembling from the west." 
Hosea11:11 They will return trembling like a bird from Egypt, like a dove from Assyria, and I will settle them in their homes. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Hosea12:1 Ephraim follows fantasies, like feeding on the wind. They chase the desert wind all day long; they multiply their lies and violence. They make a treaty with Assyria, and at the same time, they send a gift of olive oil to Egypt.”" 
Hosea12:2 Yahweh is bringing charges against Judah. He will punish Jacob as his conduct deserves; he will repay the people for what they have done. 
Hosea12:3 In his mother’s womb, Jacob grabbed his twin brother’s heel; when he grew to be a man, he struggled with God." 
Hosea12:4 He wrestled with the angel and won; he wept and sought his blessing. God met him at Bethel, and there he spoke to us." 
Hosea12:5 Yes, Yahweh is the God of Angel Armies. Yahweh —remember his name!" 
Hosea12:6 And you, to your God you must return. Stand guard over divine love and justice and always put your trust in him as you wait patiently for your God." 
Hosea12:7 Israel, you are like merchants using fraudulent scales. How you love to cheat your customers!" 
Hosea12:8 Ephraim boasts, “Look how rich I have become! I’ve made a fortune all by myself. There’s nothing wrong with the profits I have made. Is there anything sinful about that?”" 
Hosea12:9 “I, Yahweh, have been your true God ever since your days in Egypt. Yet, I will make you live in tents again as you did when we first met in the Tent of Meeting.”" 
Hosea12:10 “I am the One who speaks through the prophets, and reveals to them vision after vision. Through the ministry of prophets, I reveal my message in parables.”" 
Hosea12:11 “Gilead is full of nothing but iniquity! Therefore, its people will be wiped out. Do they sacrifice bulls in Gilgal? That is why I will make their altars nothing but heaps of stones on the furrows of the plowed fields.”" 
Hosea12:12 Jacob fled to the land of Aram, where Israel became a servant to win a wife. He took care of another man’s sheep to pay the bride-price." 
Hosea12:13 By a prophet, Yahweh brought Israel out of Egypt; By a prophet named Moses, he cared for his people, Israel." 
Hosea12:14 But the people of Israel have provoked him to bitter anger. Yahweh will hold the Israelites responsible for the crimes of bloodshed they have committed. Their master will make them pay for all the disgraceful things they have done. 
Hosea13:1 When Ephraim used to speak, his words commanded great respect. He once had great authority in Israel, but he became guilty of Baal worship and signed his own death warrant." 
Hosea13:2 And now they sin more and more by making silver idols for themselves, cleverly crafted idols of their own invention— each skillfully made by human hands. “Sacrifice to them,” they say. Why would men kiss idols of calves?" 
Hosea13:3 Therefore, the entire tribe of Ephraim will vanish like the morning mist, like the dew that quickly disappears, like the chaff blown by the wind from the threshing floor, like smoke rising from a chimney." 
Hosea13:4 “I, Yahweh, have been your true God ever since your days in Egypt. You must know no other god but me. There is no other god who can save you." 
Hosea13:5 I tenderly cared for you in the desert, in the land of dreadful drought." 
Hosea13:6 When I pastured you, you were full, but when you were satisfied, your hearts grew proud, and you completely forgot about me." 
Hosea13:7 So now I will be like a male lion to you, like a leopard lurking beside the path." 
Hosea13:8 I will attack you like a charging bear robbed of her cubs, and will rip open your chest. Like a lioness, I will devour you on the spot; like a wild beast I will tear you to shreds!" 
Hosea13:9 Israel, you have destroyed yourself, because you are against me, your only help.”" 
Hosea13:10 “Where is your king now, who could save you in all your cities? Where are your rulers, of whom you said, ‘Give us a king and leaders’?" 
Hosea13:11 In my anger, I gave you kings, and in my wrath, I have taken them away." 
Hosea13:12 “Ephraim’s sin is recorded; his guilt is on record and kept in store. 
Hosea13:13 “You are like a senseless baby at the point of childbirth; labor pains have begun, and his time is due, but he refuses to leave the womb." 
Hosea13:14 Shall I save my people from the clutches of Sheol? Shall I ransom them from Death’s firm grip? Hey, Death! Tell me, where is your punishment? Hey, Grave! Tell me, where is your sting? Revenge will be hidden from my eyes.”" 
Hosea13:15 Though Ephraim bears more fruit than his brothers, Yahweh ’s breath will blow from the desert. The scorching wind will come from the east; it will dry up his springs, and his wells will run dry. The wind will come to plunder his treasury, robbing it of every precious thing." 
Hosea13:16 Samaria will be punished because she has rebelled against her God. Her people will fall by the sword; even her little ones will be dashed to pieces and her pregnant women split open. 
Hosea14:1 Israel, return to Yahweh! Come back to your true God, for your sins have become your downfall." 
Hosea14:2 Repent! Choose the words of your confession and come back to Yahweh. Say to him, “Take all my guilt away; be gracious to us and receive us. Instead of animal sacrifices, we offer to you the praises of our lips." 
Hosea14:3 Assyria will not be our savior; you will. No longer will we trust in our military might. We will not call our handmade idols ‘our god.’ For in you, the orphan finds tender mercy.”" 
Hosea14:4 “I will heal you of your unfaithfulness. I will love you with all my heart, for my anger has turned away from you." 
Hosea14:5 My presence will fall like refreshing dew on Israel. My people will blossom as the fair lily and become firmly rooted as the cedars of Lebanon. 
Hosea14:6 My people will spread out like the beautiful branches of olive trees and carry the desirable scent of Lebanon’s cedars. 
Hosea14:7 They will come back to live beneath the shadow of my blessing. They will flourish like a sunlit field of wheat and thrive as a blossoming vine. Their wine will be as famous as the wine of Lebanon. 
Hosea14:8 Ephraim will say, ‘Why would I ever want to worship idols anymore?’ I, Yahweh, am the one who hears, responds, and watches over him. For I will shelter him under my branches like an evergreen cypress. Ephraim, you owe your fruitfulness to me.”" 
Hosea14:9 Do you consider yourself wise? Then you will understand these words. Are you discerning? Then you will discover their meaning. For Yahweh ’s paths are smooth for walking, and the righteous will walk in them, but the rebellious sinners will stumble in them." 
Joel1:1 These are the words of Yahweh that came to Joel son of Pethuel. 
Joel1:2 Listen to this, you leaders in the land; everybody everywhere, pay attention! Has anything like this ever happened in your lifetime or in the days of your ancestors?" 
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it! And tell them to tell their children and to tell their children to tell the next generation. 
Joel1:4 What the cutting-locust has left, the swarming-locust has eaten; what the swarming-locust has left, the hopping-locust has eaten; what the hopping-locust has left, the destroying-locust has eaten." 
Joel1:5 Wake up, you drunkards, and weep! Wail, all you wine-drinkers! For the new wine has been snatched from your lips." 
Joel1:6 For a nation has invaded my land, powerful and innumerable, with teeth like a lion’s teeth, and with the fangs of a lioness." 
Joel1:7 They have completely destroyed my vineyards and reduced my fig trees to splinters; they have stripped off the bark and thrown it away, leaving their branches white." 
Joel1:8 Lament like a young bride-to-be dressed in sackcloth for the bridegroom of her youth. 
Joel1:9 Yahweh ’s ministers, the priests, are in mourning because there is no more grain and wine to offer in Yahweh ’s temple." 
Joel1:10 The fields are stripped bare, and the ground is plunged into sadness over the ruined harvest. The grapes have dried up, and there is no new wine; only a trickle of olive oil remains." 
Joel1:11 Be ashamed, you farmers, for there is no harvest of wheat and barley. You vinedressers, cry out in despair, for the harvest of the fields has been lost." 
Joel1:12 The vine has withered, and the fig tree wilts away. In fact, every tree of the field has withered— pomegranate, date palm, and apple tree. And in the same way, the people’s joy and gladness has withered too." 
Joel1:13 You priests, put on sackcloth and weep. You ministers who serve at the altar, mourn and wail! Go, spend the night in the temple, you servants of my God, because there is no more grain or wine offerings to bring to the house of your God." 
Joel1:14 Declare a holy fast. Call a solemn assembly. Gather the leaders and all the people of the land to the house of Yahweh your God. Cry out with all your hearts to Yahweh! 
Joel1:15 How awful that time will be! Look! The day of Yahweh is certainly coming— the day when the Divine Destroyer brings destruction. 
Joel1:16 Has not the food supply been cut off before our very eyes? Have not the joyful celebrations been silenced in the temple of our God? 
Joel1:17 The seeds shrivel under the dry clods. The storehouses are empty; the barns are bare and broken down because the harvest has disappeared. 
Joel1:18 How loudly the cattle groan because they have no pasture! The herds wander bewildered. Not even the flocks of sheep are able to graze. 
Joel1:19 Yahweh, I cry out to you with all my heart, for fire has devoured the open pastures, and flame has scorched all the trees of the field." 
Joel1:20 The sources of water have run dry and fire has devoured the pasturelands, leaving the wild animals panting loudly for you." 
Joel2:1 Blow the shofar in Zion, sound the alarm on my mountain of holiness! Shake the people awake, for the day of Yahweh is coming. Yes, it is almost here." 
Joel2:2 It will be a day of darkness and gloom, a black and cloudy day. As the dawning light spreads across the mountains, so will a great and powerful people advance, an army such as has never been seen before and such as will never be seen again, not even in all the ages to come." 
Joel2:3 Fire blazes before them, and behind them the flame consumes. Before their eyes, the earth is like the garden of Eden, and behind them they leave a devastated wilderness. Nothing escapes them unscathed." 
Joel2:4 Their appearance is like horses prepared for battle; like war horses, they charge ahead." 
Joel2:5 They rumble like war chariots; they leap over the mountaintops. They devour the land, with the sound of a wildfire devouring stubble. They are a mighty people who are prepared for the battle." 
Joel2:6 At the sight of this army, nations panic, all their faces grow pale." 
Joel2:7 They press forward like mighty warriors, and scale walls like elite soldiers, each marching straight ahead, not swerving from his path." 
Joel2:8 They never jostle each other, for each one stays on his own path. They storm the city and burst through its defenses. Together they are unstoppable." 
Joel2:9 They rush against the city and scale its walls. They climb up into the houses and enter through the windows like thieves. 
Joel2:10 As that army advances, the earth shakes, and the heavens tremble. The sun and moon grow dark, and the stars no longer shine." 
Joel2:11 Yahweh ’s voice thunders as he leads his valiant troops. Numberless are the mighty ones who obey his commands. Yes, the day of Yahweh is great and astonishing! Who can withstand it?" 
Joel2:12 “But it’s not too late,” declares Yahweh. “Come back to me now with all your heart, with fasting, weeping, and mourning." 
Joel2:13 Tearing your clothes in sorrow is not enough— tear open your hearts!” Come back to Yahweh your God, for he is gracious to welcome you home and compassionate to embrace you. Yahweh is slow to anger and so rich in extravagant love for you. He is always ready to forgive and cancel judgment." 
Joel2:14 Who knows if he will not turn around and show pity, leaving behind a blessing. Then you can bring offerings of grain and wine to be presented to Yahweh your God." 
Joel2:15 Blow the shofar in Zion! Call for a fast and proclaim a solemn assembly. 
Joel2:16 Gather the people together, and prepare them for a sacred meeting. From the oldest to the youngest, assemble the community, even the nursing infants. Let the newly wedded bride and bridegroom come from their chamber." 
Joel2:17 With everyone gathered in the courtyard, the priests, the ministers of Yahweh, must stand weeping between the portico and the altar of burnt offering. Let them face the sanctuary and intercede, saying, “Spare your people! Have pity, Yahweh! Do not expose your very own people to the contempt and sarcasm of the nations. Why give the nations cause to say, ‘Where is their God now?’ ”" 
Joel2:18 Then Yahweh showed his great love for the land and showered compassion upon his people. 
Joel2:19 Yahweh answered their cry and said, “Behold, now your fast is over. I am providing you with plenty of wheat, wine, and olive oil until you are satisfied. Never again will I make you an object of scorn among the nations." 
Joel2:20 I will remove your northern enemy far from you and drive him into a dry, desolate land, with his front ranks to the Dead Sea and his rear guard to the Mediterranean Sea. I will destroy them for their boastful deeds, and people will smell the stench of their dead bodies.” Truly, he has done great things!" 
Joel2:21 My land, do not fear, for your restoration has come! Be glad and rejoice, you people, for Yahweh has done marvelous things for you." 
Joel2:22 Animals of the field, do not be afraid, for the pastures of the wilderness are green again, the trees bear their fruit, and the vine and fig tree yield their richness." 
Joel2:23 Children of Zion, be glad! Celebrate the mighty acts of Yahweh your God, for he has given you a teacher of righteousness whose teaching is like the refreshing spring rains. And he will pour down the rains for you, the spring and autumn rains as before." 
Joel2:24 “The threshing floors will be full of grain, the pits beside the presses will overflow with new wine and fresh oil." 
Joel2:25 I will make up for the years the swarms of locusts ruined your harvest— the hopping-locusts, the destroying-locusts, and the cutting-locusts— my great army that I sent against you." 
Joel2:26 You will eat to your heart’s content and be satisfied. You will gloriously praise the name of Yahweh your God, who has treated you so wonderfully. And never will my people be despised again." 
Joel2:27 Then, Israel, you will know without question that I am dwelling among you. I am Yahweh your God, and no one else. I say again: no more will I surrender my people to shame.”" 
Joel2:28 “Then afterward, I will pour out my Spirit on everyone. Your sons and your daughters will boldly prophesy, your elderly will have supernatural dreams, and your youth will experience ecstatic visions." 
Joel2:29 And I will even pour out my Spirit in those days upon every servant, both male and female." 
Joel2:30 “And I will display amazing wonders in the heavens and on the earth, blood and fire and columns of smoke." 
Joel2:31 The sun will be darkened, and the moon will appear as red as blood. These events will occur before the great and awesome day of Yahweh comes." 
Joel2:32 And everyone who sincerely worships Yahweh will be saved. For in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there will be a remnant who are spared, as I, Yahweh, have promised. They will be the chosen survivors whom I, Yahweh, will call.”" 
Joel3:1 “Behold! In those days, when I restore the fortunes of Judah and Jerusalem," 
Joel3:2 I will gather all the nations together. I will bring them to the Valley of Yahweh ’s Judgment. I will take them to court there for all they have done against Israel— my people and my inheritance. I will execute judgment upon them because they have scattered my people among the nations and divided my land among themselves. 
Joel3:3 They cast lots for my people and sold them into slavery. They traded a boy to pay for a prostitute and trafficked a girl for a drink of wine. 
Joel3:4 And what have you done to me, Tyre and Sidon and all you regions of Philistia? Are you trying to take revenge on me? If you are, I will swiftly and speedily pay you back and return your revenge so that it lands squarely on your own heads." 
Joel3:5 For you have stolen my silver and gold and carried off my valuable treasures into your temples. 
Joel3:6 You have sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks to be taken as slaves far from their homeland. 
Joel3:7 Behold! I will rouse them to return and come back from the places to which you have sold them. I will do to you the very thing that you have done to them. 
Joel3:8 Your sons and daughters will be sold as slaves to the sons of Judah, who in turn will sell them to the Sabaeans, who live in a distant land. I, Yahweh, have spoken!”" 
Joel3:9 Proclaim this message among the nations: “Prepare for war!” Awaken the champions! Let all you soldiers gather, advance, and march." 
Joel3:10 Hammer your plowshares into swords and your pruning hooks into spears. Let the weakling say, “I am a champion!”" 
Joel3:11 Hurry and join the battle, all you surrounding nations. Come and assemble there. Yahweh, send down your champions, your warring angels!" 
Joel3:12 “Let the nations stir themselves up and march to the Valley of Yahweh ’s Judgment. There I will sit in judgment on all the surrounding nations. 
Joel3:13 “Swing the sickle, for the harvest is ripe for judgment. Come and tread the grapes of wrath. The winepress is full and the vats are overflowing for their wickedness is so great!”" 
Joel3:14 Multitudes! So many multitudes in the Valley of Decision! For the day of Yahweh is near in the Valley of the Verdict. 
Joel3:15 The sun and moon grow dark, and the stars no longer shine." 
Joel3:16 Yahweh roars from Zion, and his voice thunders from Jerusalem; heaven and earth tremble. But Yahweh will be a shelter for his people, a faithful fortress for Israel." 
Joel3:17 “Then you will know that I am Yahweh your God, who dwells in Zion, my holy mountain. Jerusalem will be a sanctuary, and foreigners will never conquer it again." 
Joel3:18 A time is coming when the mountains will drip with new wine and the hills will flow with milk, and all the arid streambeds of Judah will run with water year-round. A fountain will spring from Yahweh ’s temple and descend to the valley of acacia trees." 
Joel3:19 “Egypt will become a desert and Edom a desolate wilderness because of the atrocious acts of violence they committed against the people of Judah, whose innocent blood they shed in their land." 
Joel3:20 I will avenge their blood and not spare the guilty, but Judah will be inhabited forever, Jerusalem from generation to generation, and Yahweh will dwell in Zion.”" 
Joel3:21  
Amos1:1 There was a prophet named Amos, a herdsman from the town of Tekoa. Two years before the great earthquake, when Uzziah was king of Judah, and Jeroboam son of Jehoash was king of Israel, God revealed these prophetic visions to Amos about Israel." 
Amos1:2 Amos said: “ Yahweh comes roaring out of Zion; from Jerusalem he comes with his thunderous voice. The shepherds’ lush pastures are dried up, and the trees on top of Mount Carmel wither.”" 
Amos1:3 Here is the message of Yahweh: “For the three terrible crimes of the people of Damascus— no, make that four — I will not turn back my wrath. For they have treated the people of Gilead with savage cruelty, running over them with a threshing sledge equipped with iron teeth." 
Amos1:4 So I will send fire down on the dynasty of Hazael to devour the royal palaces defended by Ben-Hadad. 
Amos1:5 I will smash the city gates of Damascus, I will cut down the ruler who sits in the Valley of Wickedness, the one who holds the royal scepter in the Paradise of Pleasure. And the people of Syria will go back to where they came from—to Kir,” declares Yahweh." 
Amos1:6 Here is the message of Yahweh: “For the three terrible crimes of the people of Gaza— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath. They have deported an entire community of exiles as slaves to Edom." 
Amos1:7 I will send fire down on the walls of Gaza to consume its fortresses. 
Amos1:8 I will cut off the ruler of Ashdod and the one who holds the scepter in Ashkelon. I will strike my blows against the city of Ekron, and what’s left of the Philistines will perish,” says the Lord Yahweh." 
Amos1:9 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of the people of Tyre— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath. For they deported entire people groups into exile in the land of Edom. In so doing, they broke the covenant of brotherhood." 
Amos1:10 So I will send fire down on the city walls of Tyre to devour her fortresses.” 
Amos1:11 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of the Edomites— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath. For Edom hunted down his relatives, the Israelites, with the sword, and he refused to show mercy. His anger never died down, and his rage continued to the end." 
Amos1:12 I will set the city of Teman on fire to burn up the fortresses of Bozrah.” 
Amos1:13 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of the Ammonites— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath. In order to seize more land for themselves, they ripped open the pregnant women of Gilead." 
Amos1:14 Therefore I will burn down the city walls of Rabbah to consume its fortresses. War cries will be heard on the day of battle; like a whirlwind, war will ravage the land." 
Amos1:15 Ammon’s king will be taken into captivity, he and his officials with him,” says Yahweh." 
Amos2:1 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of the Moabites— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath. They burned the bones of the king of Edom to lime." 
Amos2:2 So I will send fire down into Moab to devour the fortresses of Kerioth. Moab will perish in the heat of battle amid war cries and the blaring of shofars. 
Amos2:3 I will destroy Moab’s ruler and slaughter all officials there with him,” says Yahweh." 
Amos2:4 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of Judah— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath because they have despised Yahweh ’s instructions and not kept his commandments. Their lies, which their ancestors followed, have led them astray." 
Amos2:5 I will send fire down on Judah to devour the fortresses of Jerusalem.” 
Amos2:6 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of Israel— no, make that four— I will not turn back my wrath. For a few pieces of silver, they sold honest people into slavery. They even sold the poor, who couldn’t pay their debts, for a pair of sandals." 
Amos2:7 They trample the poor and helpless into the dirt and shove aside the rights of the destitute. A son and his father have sex with the same woman, and by doing so, they profane my holy name." 
Amos2:8 They lay themselves down beside every altar on garments taken as security for debts, and in the house of God they drink the wine they got from those who have been fined." 
Amos2:9 “Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorites before your very eyes. They stood as tall as cedar trees and as strong as oaks. I destroyed them root and branch. 
Amos2:10 It was I who brought you up from Egypt and led you for forty years through the desert to possess the land of the Amorites. 
Amos2:11 I raised up prophets from your sons and Nazirites from your young men. People of Israel, is this not true? I, Yahweh, have spoken!" 
Amos2:12 “But you made the Nazirites drink wine and break their vow. You ordered the prophets, ‘You’d better not prophesy.’" 
Amos2:13 Very well! I will press you down like a cart overloaded with grain. 
Amos2:14 Even the swiftest runner will not escape, physical strength will not be enough to protect the strong, nor will the mighty warrior be able to save himself." 
Amos2:15 The archer will not stand his ground, the fastest runners will not escape, nor will the one who flees on horseback save his life." 
Amos2:16 In that day, even the bravest of warriors will throw down his arms and run away. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Amos3:1 Israelites, listen to this prophecy that Yahweh pronounces against you, against all the people I rescued from Egypt." 
Amos3:2 “Of all the nations of the earth, you are the only one I have intimately known and have always cared for. That is why I will punish you for all your wrongdoings.”" 
Amos3:3 Do two walk together unless they have agreed to do so? 
Amos3:4 Does the lion roar in the bush if it has no prey? Does the lion growl in his lair if it has seized nothing? 
Amos3:5 Does a bird alight on a trap on the ground if there is no bait inside? Does the net spring up from the ground if it has caught nothing? 
Amos3:6 Does the war trumpet sound in the city without the people being frightened? Does calamity come to a city if it has not passed through Yahweh ’s hands? 
Amos3:7 Indeed, Lord Yahweh does nothing without revealing his secrets to his servants the prophets." 
Amos3:8 The lion has roared! Who is not afraid? Lord Yahweh has spoken! Who will not prophesy? 
Amos3:9 Proclaim to the strongholds of Assyria and Egypt, and say to them, “Gather on the hills around Samaria; take a good, hard look at the rampant disorder within the city and see how oppressed the people are.”" 
Amos3:10 “They don’t even know how to do right,” declares Yahweh. “They hoard in their palaces the spoils of their violence and extortion." 
Amos3:11 Therefore, I, Lord Yahweh, say: an enemy will soon overrun the land. He will topple your strongholds and plunder your palaces.”" 
Amos3:12 This is what Yahweh says: “As a shepherd can only rescue two leg bones or a piece of an ear out of the jaws of a lion, so the Israelites who live in Samaria will only be able to salvage one leg of a bed and a piece of a headboard from their ravaged city.”" 
Amos3:13 “I, Lord Yahweh, the Mighty God of Angel Armies, say: Listen! Warn Jacob’s descendants." 
Amos3:14 On the day when I punish Israel for her crimes, I will also destroy the altars of Bethel. The horns of the altar will be hacked off and fall to the ground." 
Amos3:15 I will tear down the winter house of the wealthy along with your summer house. Your luxury homes decorated with ivory and your lovely mansions will likewise be destroyed. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Amos4:1 Listen to this, you women of Samaria! You’re like the fattened, well-fed cows of Bashan grazing on the fertile mountain of Samaria. You women who exploit the weak and crush the poor and then command your husbands: “Bring us some drinks!”" 
Amos4:2 Lord Yahweh has sworn by his holiness: “Behold, the day is soon coming when they will use hooks to drag you away, every last one of you with fishhooks." 
Amos4:3 They will force you like cattle in a chute through the gaps of the broken walls and herd you off toward Hermon. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Amos4:4 “Go to Bethel and sin! Go to Gilgal and sin some more! You sin when you bring your sacrifices each morning. You sin when you bring your tithes every third day 
Amos4:5 and burn your thank offerings of bread made with yeast. Brag about your extra offerings, people of Israel, for this is what makes you happy! I, Lord Yahweh, have spoken." 
Amos4:6 “In every town, I left you hungry — a food shortage in all your villages— and still, you would not hunger for me. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Amos4:7 “I even withheld the rain from you three months short of harvest. I sent rain to fall on one town but not on another. One field had plenty of rain, and another became dry as dust." 
Amos4:8 People would stagger from city to city, longing for a drink of water yet never quenching their thirst. And still you had no thirst for me. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Amos4:9 “I struck your crops with blight and disease and dried up your many gardens and vineyards. Locusts kept devouring your fig trees and olive trees, and still, you would not return to me. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Amos4:10 “I sent plagues against you like I did against Egypt. I killed your young men with the sword and stole away all your war horses. I filled your nostrils with the stench of your unburied corpses, and still, you would not return to me. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Amos4:11 “I destroyed some of you like I destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah. Your survivors were like burning sticks plucked from a fire, and still, you would not return to me. I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Amos4:12 “So here is what I plan to do to you, Israel, and because of what I am planning, prepare to meet your God.”" 
Amos4:13 Behold, the Mountain-Maker and Wind-Creator, who reveals his intimate thoughts to people, who changes the dawn into darkness and moves over the mountains of the earth. His name is Yahweh, the God of Angel Armies." 
Amos5:1 Listen to this funeral song that I sing over you, people of Israel:" 
Amos5:2 “Virgin Israel has fallen, never to rise again. There she lies, abandoned on her own soil, with no one to lift her up.”" 
Amos5:3 Lord Yahweh says this: “The city that deployed a thousand soldiers will only have a hundred come back, and the city that deployed a hundred soldiers will only have ten survivors.”" 
Amos5:4 For Yahweh says this to the people of Israel: “ Come, seek me, and you will live." 
Amos5:5 But don’t go down to seek me at Beersheba. Don’t try to find me at Bethel’s shrines or at the altars at Gilgal. For Gilgal will be taken into exile, and Bethel will be brought to nothing.”" 
Amos5:6 Come back to Yahweh and live, or he will sweep down like fire upon the people of Israel. The fire will burn at Bethel with no one there to put out the flames." 
Amos5:7 They turn justice into a bitter pill to swallow and trample righteousness into the mud. 
Amos5:8 Creator of Constellations—Maker of the Pleiades and Orion, you turn the deepest darkness into dawn and daylight into the darkest night. You draw up drops of water distilled from the sea to drench the earth with pouring rain. Yahweh is your name." 
Amos5:9 You destroy the mighty with a blinding flash and overwhelm their strongholds with certain destruction. 
Amos5:10 They hate the one who challenges injustice and despise the one who declares the whole truth in court. 
Amos5:11 For I know how outrageous are your many sins and how many crimes you have committed. You persecute the righteous, trample on the rights of the poor, and seize their grain through extortion. By taking bribes, you prevent them from getting justice in the courts. This is why you will not live in the fine stone houses you build or enjoy the fruits of your luxury. You will not drink wine from the lush vineyards you plant, for your judgment is imminent." 
Amos5:12  
Amos5:13 Yes, the smartest thing for the prudent is to remain silent in such an evil time as this." 
Amos5:14 Devote yourself to what is good, not evil, so that you may live. And Yahweh, the God of Angel Armies, will really be with you, just as you claim he is." 
Amos5:15 Hate what is evil, love what is right, and let justice prevail in the courts. It may be that Yahweh, God of Angel Armies, will show mercy to the remnant of Israel." 
Amos5:16 Because of Israel’s sins, Lord Yahweh, God of Angel Armies, says this: “Wailing and sorrow everywhere! In every street they will mourn the dead. Those who work in the fields will be expected to mourn along with the professional mourners." 
Amos5:17 There will be wailing in all the vineyards, for I will pass through your midst to punish you,” declares Yahweh." 
Amos5:18 Woe to you who long for the day of Yahweh! Why do you long for the day of Yahweh? Its arrival will mean darkness, not light." 
Amos5:19 You will be seized like someone who runs from a lion and a bear charges at him. Then he hides indoors and leans his hand against the wall only to have a venomous snake bite him. 
Amos5:20 Will not the day of Yahweh bring darkness, not light— a day of gloom without a ray of light?" 
Amos5:21 “I hate your religious festivals; I absolutely despise them! Your pious gatherings make me sick! 
Amos5:22 Even though you offer me your burnt offerings and grain offerings, I will not accept them. And though you bring me choice peace offerings of fattened animals, I won’t even look at them." 
Amos5:23 Spare me the monotonous melodies of your ‘worship.’ I don’t want to hear your strumming guitars anymore. It’s just noise to me. 
Amos5:24 However, I delight in a land where justice flows like a river and where true righteousness rolls like a steady flowing stream." 
Amos5:25 Listen, people of Israel! Didn’t you worship me with your offerings and sacrifices during those forty years in the wilderness?" 
Amos5:26 Now you carry around the pagan idols that you made to worship your phony king, Sakkuth, and your very own star-god, Kaiwan." 
Amos5:27 Therefore, you can carry them all the way beyond Damascus, where I am about to drive you into captivity,” says Yahweh. God of Angel Armies is his name!" 
Amos6:1 Woe to you living so comfortably in Zion! Woe to you who live with a false sense of security on Mount Samaria, the elite of this foremost nation, on whom Israel’s people pin their hopes." 
Amos6:2 Travel to the city of Calneh and see for yourself what happened. Then go on from there to Hamath, the great capital city of the Hittites. Make your way down to Gath in Philistia, and ask yourself — are you better than these kingdoms? Is their territory larger than yours?" 
Amos6:3 You refuse to consider the day when disaster will come, yet all your evil only brings that day closer." 
Amos6:4 Lying on luxurious beds inlaid with ivory and sprawled out on your couches, you feast on lambs from the flock and stall-fattened veal." 
Amos6:5 You compose songs like David and strum away on your harps. 
Amos6:6 You guzzle wine by the bucketful, and slather yourselves with the finest lotions. Yet you do not grieve over Israel’s impending doom." 
Amos6:7 That is why they will be the first to go into captivity. Their lying around while enjoying the good life will come to an end. 
Amos6:8 Lord Yahweh has sworn by his own being — Yahweh, God of Angel Armies, declares: “I hate the pride of the people of Jacob, and I despise their strongholds, I will hand over their capital city to their enemies and everything in it.”" 
Amos6:9 Even if there are ten hiding in a house, they will all die." 
Amos6:10 If a relative who is to light a memorial fire for the dead comes to carry the bodies out of the house for burial and asks, “Is anyone else inside the house with you?” and that person answers, “No,” then the one who asked will say, “Quiet! We must not mention the name of Yahweh.”" 
Amos6:11 Behold, Yahweh strikes with his command: “Houses large and small will be smashed to pieces!”" 
Amos6:12 Can horses gallop over rocky cliffs? Can boulder-strewn hills be plowed with oxen? Of course not! Yet you have managed to pervert justice into poison and the sweet fruits of righteousness into bitter fruit. 
Amos6:13 You rejoice over conquering the city of Lo Debar, saying, “Wasn’t it by our own strength that we captured Karnaim for ourselves?”" 
Amos6:14 But look, house of Israel, Yahweh, God of Angel Armies, declares to you: “I will raise up a mighty nation to come against you, and they will oppress you and overrun your land from the northern to the southern borders of your kingdom.”" 
Amos7:1 This is what Lord Yahweh revealed to me in a vision: Behold! Just after the hay had been cut for the king, when the new young grass started to grow again, I saw Yahweh forming a swarm of mature locusts." 
Amos7:2 I watched as they began eating all the vegetation in the land, so I pleaded with God, saying, “Lord Yahweh, please forgive us, I beg you, or we will not survive! Our nation is so helpless.”" 
Amos7:3 My prayer moved God’s heart to compassion. Yahweh said: “It will not happen.” 
Amos7:4 In another vision, Yahweh revealed this to me: Behold! He was calling for punishment by fire. The scorching heat dried up the deep sources of water beneath the land and made the farmlands like dust." 
Amos7:5 So I pleaded with God, saying, “Lord Yahweh, please don’t do this, I beg you, or we will not survive! Our nation is so helpless.”" 
Amos7:6 Because of my prayer, God’s heart was stirred with compassion. “This also will not happen,” said Yahweh." 
Amos7:7 Lord Yahweh revealed this to me in another vision: I saw the Lord standing by a wall built true to plumb, holding a plumb line in his hand." 
Amos7:8 “Amos, what do you see?” Yahweh asked me. “A plumb line,” I replied. Then the Lord said, “Behold, I am about to set a plumb line up against my people Israel to test them. Never again will I look the other way when they sin." 
Amos7:9 I will destroy the high places where Isaac’s descendants worship, and I will ruin the holy places of Israel. With my sword in hand, I will attack the dynasty of Jeroboam.”" 
Amos7:10 Then Amaziah the chief priest of Bethel sent the following message to Jeroboam king of Israel: “Amos has conspired against you in the very heart of Israel. We cannot stand his speeches! 
Amos7:11 For Amos says, ‘Jeroboam is going to die by the sword, and Israel will go into captivity far from its native land.’ ”" 
Amos7:12 Amaziah said to Amos, “Go away, you seer. Why don’t you escape to Judah while there is time? You can always prophesy there and make your living." 
Amos7:13 But don’t stay around here and prophesy at Bethel anymore. This is the national temple, the place where the king worships.”" 
Amos7:14 Amos replied, “I am not a prophet, nor am I one of the sons of the prophets. I am merely a rancher, and I cultivate fig trees." 
Amos7:15 But one day, Yahweh called me as I was managing my flocks and said to me, ‘Go and prophesy to my people Israel.’" 
Amos7:16 “So now, Amaziah, listen to what Yahweh says. You say: ‘Do not prophesy against Israel, and do not prophesy doom on Isaac’s descendants!’" 
Amos7:17 Very well. This is what Yahweh says: ‘Your wife will become a prostitute in the streets, and your sons and daughters will fall in battle. Your land will be measured, divided up, and given to others. And you yourself will die in a pagan land, and Israel will go into captivity far from its own land!’ ”" 
Amos8:1 Lord Yahweh revealed this to me in another vision: a basket of ripe fruit. 
Amos8:2 “Amos, what do you see?” he asked. “A basket of ripe fruit,” I replied. Then Yahweh said, “The time is ripe for my people Israel. I will not continue to overlook their sins." 
Amos8:3 On that day, those singing songs in the temple will wail instead,” declares the Lord Yahweh. “There will be corpses piled up everywhere. They will be cast out in silence.”" 
Amos8:4 Listen to this, all you who trample on the poor and reduce the needy to nothing!" 
Amos8:5 You say to yourselves, “We can’t wait for these holy days to be over so that we can sell our corn!” And, “When will this Sabbath be over so that we can open the storage and sell our wheat? Then we can overcharge our customers, shortchange them, and deceive them by tampering with the scales." 
Amos8:6 We can even sell the worthless chaff we sweep up for silver. We can buy the poor with silver and make them our slaves because they cannot pay their debts, even a debt so small as that of a pair of sandals.”" 
Amos8:7 Yahweh, the Majesty of Jacob, has sworn an oath: “I will never forget all the evil they have done." 
Amos8:8 That is why the earth will tremble for this, and all who live on it mourn. All of the land will rise up like the river Nile— inundated and stirred up by floodwaters— and then subside, just like the river of Egypt!”" 
Amos8:9 “On that day,” declares Lord Yahweh, “I will make the sun set at noon and darken the earth in the middle of the day." 
Amos8:10 I will turn your festivals into funerals, and all your lively singing into funeral dirges. I will make you all wear sackcloth and shave your heads, as if you were mourning the death of an only child. That day will be bitter to the end.”" 
Amos8:11 “Behold, the days are coming,” declares Lord Yahweh, “when I will send a famine on the land. People will not hunger for food nor thirst for water but will starve for a word from Yahweh." 
Amos8:12 People will stagger from sea to sea and wander from the north to the east searching for a revelation from Yahweh, but they will not hear from me." 
Amos8:13 On that day, even your beautiful young women and strong young men will collapse from thirst." 
Amos8:14 Those who swear by Ashimah, the idol of Samaria, who say, ‘Long live your golden calf-god of Dan!’ and say, ‘As surely as the god of Beersheba lives!’— they will all fall, never to rise again.”" 
Amos9:1 I saw the Lord standing over the altar, and he gave the command: “Smash the top of the pillars so that the foundations shake! Shatter the pillars and make them crumble upon the heads of all the worshipers. And any survivors I will kill by the sword. Whoever runs away will not go far. Whoever tries to escape will not make it." 
Amos9:2 Even if they were to dig their way down to the underworld, my hand would haul them out. Even if they were to climb up to heaven, I would drag them down from there." 
Amos9:3 If they were to hide on top of Mount Carmel, I would hunt them down and snatch them. If they were to try to hide from my eyes at the bottom of the sea, I would order the serpent there to bite them." 
Amos9:4 If their enemies were to herd them into captivity, even there I would order the sword to kill them. Yes, I will fix my eyes on them to destroy them and not to help them.”" 
Amos9:5 Lord Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, you touch the earth and it melts, and everyone on earth mourns. All of the land will rise up like the river Nile— inundated and stirred up by floodwaters— and then subside, just like the river of Egypt." 
Amos9:6 You build your lofty stairway in the heavens and suspend your superdome over the earth. You summon the waters of the sea and pour them over the face of the earth: Yahweh is your name! 
Amos9:7 “Israel, I think as much of the Ethiopians as I do of you; you’re all the same to me,” declares Yahweh. “ Yes, I brought you out from Egypt. But didn’t I also bring out the Philistines from Crete and the Syrians from Kir?" 
Amos9:8 Behold, the eyes of Lord Yahweh are on the sinful kingdom of Israel. I will wipe it off the face of the earth. Yet I will not completely destroy the descendants of Jacob,” declares Yahweh." 
Amos9:9 “Behold, I will speak the command to scatter the people of Israel among all the nations. I will sift them the way sand is shaken in a sieve, through which not a single pebble falls to the ground." 
Amos9:10 All the sinners of my people will die by the sword who say, ‘Nothing bad could ever happen to us.’ ”" 
Amos9:11 “One day I will restore David’s tabernacle that has fallen. I will repair its broken places and raise up its ruins and fix it up like new. I will rebuild it and make it what it was long ago. 
Amos9:12 As a result, David’s people will conquer the nations over which they once ruled for me, down to the last remnant of Edom." 
Amos9:13 Behold, I, Yahweh, make this decree: The days of acceleration are coming, when the harvest will be so great that those plowing the field will overtake those still reaping the harvest, and the ones treading the grapes will overtake those who are planting. Blessings will stream forth like new wine pouring from every mountain, and every hill will flow with favor." 
Amos9:14 I will restore prosperity and good fortune to the captives of Israel. They will rebuild the ruined cities to live in. They will plant vineyards to produce their wine. And plant lush gardens and orchards to eat their produce. 
Amos9:15 And I will plant them in their own land, never to be uprooted again from the land that I have given them. I, Yahweh, your God, have spoken.”" 
Obadiah1:1 This is the vision that Lord Yahweh revealed to Obadiah concerning Edom. We have heard a report from Yahweh: “I have sent a messenger throughout the nations, saying, ‘Rise up! Let us march against this people. Prepare for war!’" 
Obadiah1:2 “Behold, I have reduced you to the least among the nations. You are now utterly despised." 
Obadiah1:3 Your proud heart has deceived you; you who are perched in the hidden places of the rock, whose home is in the heights. You think to yourself, ‘Who can ever topple me to the ground?’" 
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar like an eagle, and your nest is nestled among the stars, I am more than able to bring you down from there. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Obadiah1:5 “When thieves come at night to rob your home, wouldn’t they only steal the things they wanted? When people gather grapes, wouldn’t they miss a few and leave some gleanings behind? But you, Edom—your enemies will totally destroy you!" 
Obadiah1:6 How Esau’s descendants will be looted, their hidden treasures sought out and ransacked!" 
Obadiah1:7 Your allies will deceive you. They will overpower you and chase you off your homeland. Your ‘trusted friends’ will lay a trap for you, and you’ll have no clue it’s coming." 
Obadiah1:8 In that day, will I not destroy all the wise men of Edom and erase all their pseudo -intelligence from the mountains of Esau? I, Yahweh, have spoken." 
Obadiah1:9 Teman, all your brave warriors will be shattered so that everyone will be destroyed from Esau’s mountain.”" 
Obadiah1:10 “Shame will cover you and you will be annihilated because you violently slaughtered your relatives, the Israelites." 
Obadiah1:11 You deserted Israel at her time of need. On that day of infamy, you stood aloof and did nothing as foreigners broke through the city gates of Jerusalem and divided the city among themselves by casting lots. Strangers carried off all her treasures, and you were as one with the invaders." 
Obadiah1:12 Do not gloat over your relatives on the day of their misfortune. Do not rejoice over the people of Judah on the day of their ruin. Do not boast that they suffered such anguish. 
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered my people’s city on their day of calamity. You should not have feasted your eyes on their suffering on their day of misery. You should not have seized their wealth on their day of sorrow. 
Obadiah1:14 You should not have waited at the crossroads to cut off those who tried to escape. Why did you hand over their survivors on the day of their distress?” 
Obadiah1:15 “The day of Yahweh is drawing near for all the nations. As you have done to others, so will it be done to you: you will be paid back for all that your deeds deserve." 
Obadiah1:16 “Just as you have drunk and caroused on my sacred hill, so all the surrounding nations will drink continually from my bitter cup of judgment. They will drink and drink and gulp it down, but they will stagger and be destroyed.”" 
Obadiah1:17 “But on Mount Zion a remnant will escape, for that place will be a reconsecrated sanctuary. And Jacob’s tribes will possess what is rightfully theirs by inheritance." 
Obadiah1:18 Then the people of Judah will be a fire, the people of Israel a flame, and the people of Esau like stubble. They will set them on fire and consume them, and there will be no survivors from Esau’s descendants. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Obadiah1:19 People from southern Judah will capture Edom; those from the western foothills will occupy Philistia; Israelites will take back the territory of Ephraim and Samaria; and the people of Benjamin will possess Gilead. 
Obadiah1:20 The company of exiles from among the people of Israel will possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath while the exiles from Jerusalem now in Sardis will occupy the cities of southern Judah. 
Obadiah1:21 Victorious deliverers will go out from Mount Zion to rule over the land of Edom, and the kingdom will be Yahweh ’s." 
Jonah1:1 One day Yahweh revealed to Jonah, Amittai’s son, this message:" 
Jonah1:2 “Arise! Leave at once for the great city of Nineveh and proclaim judgment against her, for the wickedness of her people is staring me in the face.”" 
Jonah1:3 But Jonah bolted and ran in the opposite direction, determined to run away from the presence of Yahweh. He went down to the coastal city of Joppa and found a ship bound for Tarshish. He paid his fare, went down into the hold of the ship, and set sail for Tarshish to flee from the presence of Yahweh." 
Jonah1:4 But Yahweh unleashed a powerful, violent wind on the sea. The ship was caught in such a great tempest that it was in jeopardy of shattering." 
Jonah1:5 The sailors were terrified. Each of them began to call on his own god for help. In order to lighten the ship they hurled the cargo and tackle overboard. Jonah, however, had gone down below and lain down in the hold and was fast asleep." 
Jonah1:6 The captain went below and asked Jonah, “How can you be sleeping so soundly at a time like this? Get up! Call on your god! Maybe he will feel sorry for us and not leave us to die.”" 
Jonah1:7 Then the sailors said to each other, “Come on. Let’s cast lots to find out who is to blame for bringing us this calamity.” They cast lots, and the lot pointed to Jonah." 
Jonah1:8 So they interrogated him. “Tell us,” they said, “what is the purpose of your journey? Where do you come from? What is your country, and who are your people?”" 
Jonah1:9 “I’m a Hebrew,” he replied. “I worship the God of heaven, the Creator of both sea and dry land. His name is Yahweh.”" 
Jonah1:10 Jonah added, “ I am his prophet, but I’m trying to escape from him.” Hearing this, the sailors were gripped with fear and said, “What terrible thing have you done?" 
Jonah1:11 What can we do to you to make the sea calm down for us?” Even as they spoke, the storm and waves were getting worse and worse." 
Jonah1:12 Then Jonah said, “It’s all my fault that you are caught in this violent storm. So just pick me up and throw me into the sea, and it will calm down for you.”" 
Jonah1:13 Reluctant to sacrifice Jonah, the sailors rowed frantically trying to return to shore, but they got nowhere. All the while, the sea was raging even wilder than before." 
Jonah1:14 Losing hope, they began to pray to Yahweh and said, “Please, Yahweh, God of Jonah, don’t let us perish on account of Jonah. And don’t hold it against us for throwing this man overboard. For you, Yahweh, have acted in accordance with your will.”" 
Jonah1:15 So the sailors took hold of Jonah and tossed him into the foaming sea. Then all at once, the sea stopped raging." 
Jonah1:16 Awestruck, the men were gripped with the fear of Yahweh, and they made vows and offered sacrifices to Jonah’s God, Yahweh." 
Jonah1:17 Meanwhile, Yahweh directed a huge fish to be at that very spot in order to swallow Jonah. And Jonah was kept alive in the stomach of the fish for three days and three nights." 
Jonah2:1 This is Jonah’s prayer to Yahweh, his God, from out of the darkness inside the huge fish:" 
Jonah2:2 “I called out to Yahweh from a place of total desperation and he answered me. From inside the belly of Sheol I cried out, and you heard my voice." 
Jonah2:3 The waters closed in all around me; all your mighty waves and currents crashed over me. For you cast me into the deep, and I sank down to the bottom of the sea." 
Jonah2:4 I actually assumed that you had banished me from your sight. Yet, I know that I will one day gaze again upon your holy temple." 
Jonah2:5 The waters rose to choke me to death. The sea was closing in around me— seaweed tightened around my head. 
Jonah2:6 I sank down into the abyss, to the roots of the mountains, where the barred gates of that realm would lock me in forever. But you, Yahweh, are my God; you raised my life from the pit." 
Jonah2:7 When my life was ebbing away, my thoughts turned toward you. My cries rose before you in your holy, heavenly temple, and my prayer entered your heart, Yahweh." 
Jonah2:8 It’s true —some abandon faithful love to you by worshiping worthless idols. 
Jonah2:9 But I will sing songs of praise and offer my sacrifices to you. I will keep every vow I’ve made to you, Yahweh, for salvation comes only from you.”" 
Jonah2:10 So Yahweh spoke to the fish, and it vomited Jonah onto the dry land." 
Jonah3:1 Yahweh spoke to the prophet Jonah for the second time. 
Jonah3:2 “Arise!” he said. “Leave at once for the great city of Nineveh and preach to the people the message I give you.” 
Jonah3:3 This time, Jonah set out for Nineveh in obedience to the word of Yahweh. Now Nineveh was a very important city to God. It took three days to traverse the entire city." 
Jonah3:4 When Jonah entered the city, he walked a day’s journey and began to preach: “ God will destroy this city in forty days!”" 
Jonah3:5 The people of Nineveh believed Jonah’s message and turned to God. To show they had repented, they called for a fast and put on sackcloth, from the greatest to the least of them." 
Jonah3:6 When the news reached the king of Nineveh, he arose from his throne, took off his royal robe, put on sackcloth, and sat down in ashes." 
Jonah3:7 He then issued this proclamation: “People of Nineveh, by decree of the king and his nobles, no person or animal, herd or flock, may eat anything; they must not consume any food or water." 
Jonah3:8 Each of you must cover yourself and your livestock with burlap and call on God with all your might. Everyone must turn away from his or her evil ways and violent behavior. 
Jonah3:9 Who knows? Perhaps God may show us mercy and be willing to change his mind. Perhaps he will relent and turn from unleashing his fierce anger so that we will not die.” 
Jonah3:10 When God saw their sincere efforts to turn from their wicked ways, he decided not to punish the people of Nineveh and did not bring upon them the disaster he said would come." 
Jonah4:1 Jonah was extremely upset that God would show mercy to Nineveh. Indignant, he fell into a rage." 
Jonah4:2 He prayed to Yahweh and said, “God, I knew this is what would happen even before I left home. For I know that you are a tender, compassionate God, who is extremely patient, rich in faithful love. And I know you are always ready to relent from inflicting disaster. That was why I attempted to flee to Tarshish." 
Jonah4:3 So now, Yahweh, please take my life, for death seems better to me than life.”" 
Jonah4:4 Yahweh replied, “What good reason do you have to be so angry?”" 
Jonah4:5 Then Jonah went out east of Nineveh, where he made himself a makeshift shelter. He sat there under its shade waiting to see what would happen to the city." 
Jonah4:6 Now Lord Yahweh arranged for a gourd plant to grow up over Jonah to provide him shade to rest in and to soothe his sour demeanor. Jonah was thrilled with the plant. 
Jonah4:7 But at dawn the next day, God sent a worm to chew through the plant—and it withered." 
Jonah4:8 As the sun rose into the sky, God sent a scorching east wind. The wind and blazing sun beat down so hard on Jonah’s head that he was starting to faint. So he begged God to end his life, saying, “I’m better off dead than alive.”" 
Jonah4:9 God said to Jonah, “Are you right to be so angry about the plant that gave you shade?” He replied, “I have every right to be angry, angry enough to die!”" 
Jonah4:10 Yahweh replied, “You are concerned for the vine, which you did not plant, nor did you do anything to make it grow. It sprang up one night and died the next." 
Jonah4:11 Should I not have compassion for the great city of Nineveh, in which there are more than 120,000 people who cannot tell their right hand from their left, to say nothing of all the animals?”" 
Micah1:1 This is the prophetic message Yahweh revealed to Micah of Moresheth. He delivered these prophecies during the reigns of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah. These are his supernatural visions concerning Samaria and Jerusalem." 
Micah1:2 Listen, you peoples—all of you! Earth, pay attention —and everyone on it. From his holy temple, the Lord Yahweh himself will testify against you." 
Micah1:3 For behold, Yahweh is leaving his dwelling place. Here he comes to tread upon the high places of the land." 
Micah1:4 Mountains melt beneath his feet, like wax melting near a raging fire. Valleys are split wide open, like water pouring down a steep slope." 
Micah1:5 All of this is because of the rebellion of Jacob’s tribes, the sin of the people of Israel. So who is to blame for the rebellion of Jacob? Is it not the people of Samaria? Who is to blame for the idolatry of the high places of Judah? Is it not the people of Jerusalem?" 
Micah1:6 “So I will wrestle Samaria to the ground, and she will become nothing more than an open field for planting vines. I will roll the stones of her buildings down into the valley until I have laid bare her foundations." 
Micah1:7 I will hammer to pieces all her gilded pagan idols, all the gifts given to her sacred prostitutes will be scorched by fire. I will turn all her precious idols into a pile of rubble. For she—Samaria—gathered her treasured idols as a prostitute collects her fees, and now these idols will become a prostitute’s wages again.”" 
Micah1:8 I will mourn and wail because of this. I will walk around barefoot and bare to show my sorrow. I will howl like a jackal and mourn like an owl. 
Micah1:9 For her wound is incurable, and her infection is spreading to Judah. It has even reached the gateway of my people, to Jerusalem itself." 
Micah1:10 Show no tears in Showtown (Gath). People of Dusty House (Beth Leaphrah), wallow in the dust of your despair!" 
Micah1:11 You’ll take a different path, Glamour-Girl-Town (Shaphir); you’ll go into exile naked and ashamed. You citizens of Outbound City (Zaanan) are stuck at home. Your next-door neighbor (Beth Ezel) is in mourning and will no longer be able to help you." 
Micah1:12 The women of Bitterville (Maroth) writhed in labor pains, hoping for good to come. Instead, Yahweh sent down disaster all the way to the gates of Jerusalem itself." 
Micah1:13 Start up your chariots, you people of Ponytown (Lachish), for the sin of the daughter of Zion can be traced back to you, where Israel’s sin began." 
Micah1:14 People of Judah, give gifts to Moresheth Gath, as you would to a daughter who is leaving home to be married. The Domiciles of Deception (Beth Achzib) will deceive the kings of Israel." 
Micah1:15 People of Mareshah, Yahweh will again bring a conqueror against you, and the glory of Israel will flee to the cave of Adullam." 
Micah1:16 Cut off your hair and make yourselves bald, for your delightful children are taken away. Make yourselves as bald as a vulture, for your children have been taken away from you into exile." 
Micah2:1 Disaster is ahead for those who plot evil. They’re as good as dead! Disaster is ahead for those who scheme as they lie on their bed. No sooner is it dawn than they act on their plan just because they can. 
Micah2:2 They covet someone’s field and seize it for themselves, and they take over his house as well. They defraud a man of his home and another man of his inheritance." 
Micah2:3 So here’s what Yahweh has to say about that: “Now I am the one plotting a disaster for those who plot to defraud others, a disaster from which you cannot escape. You will no longer walk with heads held high, for a catastrophe will bring you low." 
Micah2:4 And when that time comes, people will adopt a taunting proverb about you and sing this mournful song to mock your sad experience: ‘We have lost everything! God has divided up our people’s lands with no one left to restore it to our hands. Our property has been seized and given to our captors—the traitors.’ ”" 
Micah2:5 Because of this, when Yahweh restores the land to his people, those corrupt leaders will have no stake in Yahweh ’s assembly nor have any part in the distribution of land." 
Micah2:6 They berate me continually. “Don’t prophesy,” they say to me. “Don’t keep prophesying on and on about these things! Disgrace will not overtake us.”" 
Micah2:7 Should Jacob’s descendants even be asking, “Is the Spirit of Yahweh really so short-tempered that he would do such things to us?” “When I speak, don’t my words produce good within the one who obeys them?" 
Micah2:8 But you wealthy oppressors of the poor have become like enemies attacking my people. When men return home from war and think they are finally safe at home, there you are, ready to snatch the coats off their backs as security for the money they owe you." 
Micah2:9 You wrongly evict the women of my people from the very homes they love, forever depriving their children of the honor of their inheritance." 
Micah2:10 Get up and get out! This is no longer your resting place. Because of this defilement, I will completely destroy you, and your destruction will be severe beyond measure." 
Micah2:11 If a lying windbag comes to deceive you, saying, ‘I prophesy wine and liquor will flow for you,’ he would be the ideal prophet for a people like this.”" 
Micah2:12 “I will regather the tribes of Jacob. Yes, I will regather the remnant of Israel. I will bring you together like sheep in a fold, like a flock within their pasture, a noisy, excited multitude." 
Micah2:13 “The Barrier-Breaker will lead them out into freedom. They will all break out, passing through the enemy’s gates. Their king will advance before their eyes with Yahweh himself leading the way!”" 
Micah3:1 Then I said, “Listen, you leaders of the tribes of Jacob, you princes among the people of Israel. Is it not your duty to see that justice prevails?" 
Micah3:2 Yet you hate what is good and love what is evil. You skin my people alive, pulling the flesh off their bones." 
Micah3:3 Like butchers, you tear the skin off my people. You break their bones and chop them up like stew meat for the pot. Like wild animals, you rip the flesh off their bones and devour my people." 
Micah3:4 Then you will cry out to him, but he will not answer your prayers. Yahweh will hide his face from you rulers because you have totally misused your power.”" 
Micah3:5 Here is what Yahweh says against the prophets who lead my people so far from the truth: “So long as they pay you, you prophesy peace. But if they don’t, you threaten war." 
Micah3:6 So, darkness will close in around you, and you will receive no prophetic visions. A shroud of darkness will bring your predictions to an end. The sun will set for the prophets, and the daylight will go dark for them." 
Micah3:7 Then shame will silence the seers. Confusion will cover those who predict the future. Humiliated, they will all put their hands over their mouths because there is no word from God.”" 
Micah3:8 Not so with me, for I am full of power! Yahweh ’s breath fills me with a sense of justice and courage to expose the rebellion of Jacob’s tribes and the sin of the people of Israel." 
Micah3:9 Now hear this, you leaders of the tribes of Jacob, you princes of the people of Israel: You hate justice and distort right into wrong." 
Micah3:10 Indeed, you build up Zion and Jerusalem but upon an immoral foundation of murder and malice." 
Micah3:11 Her judges render verdicts for the highest bidder, her priests preach for a price, her prophets prophesy for a fee, and yet they pretend to depend upon Yahweh. “Isn’t Yahweh on our side?” they say. “How could any disaster come upon us?”" 
Micah3:12 That is why, thanks to you, Zion will return to farmland, Jerusalem to a pile of rubble, and the Temple Mount will be deserted — overgrown with thickets." 
Micah4:1 In the future, the mountain of Yahweh ’s temple will be raised up as the head of the mountains, towering over all the hills. A sparkling stream of every nation will flow into it." 
Micah4:2 Many peoples will come and say, “Everyone, come! Let’s go up higher to Yahweh ’s mountain, to the house of Jacob’s God; then he can teach us his ways, and we can walk in his paths.” Zion will be the center of instruction, and the word of Yahweh will go out from Jerusalem." 
Micah4:3 He will judge fairly between strong, distant nations and settle disputes among many peoples. The swords they used against each other they will beat into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks. No nation will take up weapons against another, nor will they prepare for war anymore." 
Micah4:4 But everyone will relax under their own fig trees and vineyards with no one to trouble them. For Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, has decreed it." 
Micah4:5 Though all the nations walk in the name of their own gods, we will walk in the name of Yahweh, our God, forever and ever." 
Micah4:6 I, Yahweh, declare: “In that day of hope I will gather the lame and bring together the wandering outcasts and those whom I have bruised." 
Micah4:7 I will make a new beginning with those who are crippled and far from home. My remnant will be transformed into a mighty nation. And I, Yahweh, will reign over them on Mount Zion from now and throughout eternity." 
Micah4:8 “And to you, Tower of the Flock, where the daughter of Zion is lifted up, your royal dominion will arrive. His kingship will come to you, Daughter Jerusalem.”" 
Micah4:9 Why are you wailing? Why are you writhing like a woman in labor? Have you no king to help you? And your wise leader, has he perished?" 
Micah4:10 Groan and writhe in anguish, like a woman in hard labor, daughter of Zion, for now, you must leave the city and live out in the open field. Off to Babylon you must go! Yet, one day you will be rescued from there, for Yahweh will deliver you from the clutches of your enemies." 
Micah4:11 Many nations have now gathered to attack you. They say, “Let’s destroy Jerusalem so that we can gloat over capturing Zion.”" 
Micah4:12 But they do not know Yahweh ’s plans, and they do not understand his strategy: he has brought them together to punish them, like grain is brought to be beaten on the threshing floor to separate the good from the worthless." 
Micah4:13 “Arise, start your threshing, daughter of Zion, for I will make you strong as a bull with iron horns and bronze hooves so that you can crush many peoples. And you must devote to Yahweh what they have stolen and bring their wealth to me, the Lord of the whole earth.”" 
Micah5:1 Now you are walled in. They have laid siege against us. They smack the ruler of Israel’s face with a rod. 
Micah5:2 “But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, so small among the clans of Judah, from your people will come a ruler who will rule Israel for me. He will burst forth from the first sunrise from the days of eternity!”" 
Micah5:3 Therefore, Yahweh will hand Israel over to her enemies until the labor pains are over and the child is born. And then the remnant of his brothers in exile will be reunited with the Israelites." 
Micah5:4 He will take his stand and will lead and feed his flock with the strength that comes from Yahweh and with the majesty of the name of his God. With this shepherd, they will live in safety, for his greatness will extend to the ends of the earth." 
Micah5:5 He himself will be their peace. When the Assyrian army sets foot on our soil to invade our land, we will raise up more than enough mighty captains to conquer them." 
Micah5:6 They will shepherd the Assyrians with the sword, the land of Nimrod with the drawn sword. And Yahweh will deliver us from the Assyrians, when they invade our land and tread on our territory." 
Micah5:7 Then the remnant of Jacob will be scattered among many peoples. They will be like the refreshing dew from Yahweh, like raindrops on the meadows, which do not wait on man’s command and are beyond human control." 
Micah5:8 Then the scattered remnant of Jacob will live among the nations. They will be like lions among the animals of the forest, like fierce lions among flocks of sheep, they will pounce on their prey with no one to rescue." 
Micah5:9 You will be victorious over your foes, and all your enemies will be torn to pieces." 
Micah5:10 “When that day comes,” declares Yahweh, “I will destroy your horses and demolish your chariots." 
Micah5:11 I will destroy the cities of your country and tear down all your strongholds. 
Micah5:12 I will dismantle the witchcraft in your land, and you will no longer cast spells." 
Micah5:13 I shall tear away your idols and your sacred stone pillars from among you. You will no longer worship things that your own hands have made. 
Micah5:14 I will uproot your sacred poles and level your cities. 
Micah5:15 I will punish with furious anger the nations who have disobeyed me.” 
Micah6:1 Now listen to what Yahweh says: “Stand up and state your case. The mountains will be your witnesses, so let the hills hear your defense." 
Micah6:2 Listen, mountains, to Yahweh ’s accusation; pay attention, you enduring foundations of the earth, for I, Yahweh, have an indictment against my people, and I will prosecute my case against Israel." 
Micah6:3 My people, what have I done to deserve your neglect? How have I made you tired of me? Answer me!" 
Micah6:4 Did I not rescue you from Egypt and ransom you from slavery? I sent Moses, Aaron, and Miriam to lead you." 
Micah6:5 Please remember this, my people: King Balak of Moab planned to curse you, and how did the prophet Balaam son of Beor answer him? He spoke Yahweh ’s blessing over you! Remember how I parted the Jordan so that you could cross over from Acacia to Gilgal. Now you should know through all my saving acts that I have treated you fairly.”" 
Micah6:6 What should I bring when I enter Yahweh ’s presence and bow down before God Most High? Should I approach him with burnt offerings, with year-old calves to sacrifice?" 
Micah6:7 Will he be pleased with thousands of rams and with endless streams of olive oil? Should I offer him my firstborn for my wrongdoing, my own offspring for the sins of my life?" 
Micah6:8 No! Listen, O Adam’s offspring, Yahweh has made it plain to you what is best. He has shown you what he really is longing for from you— only to promote justice, to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God." 
Micah6:9 Yahweh ’s voice is calling out to the city. Wise is the one who fears the name of Yahweh. “So pay attention, O tribe, to the one who called you together!" 
Micah6:10 Can I overlook the treasures that fill the homes of evil people who cheated others? They use false measures, which I despise." 
Micah6:11 Do you really expect me to forgive those who cheat others with a bag of wrong weights and dishonest scales? 
Micah6:12 The wealthy are steeped in violence, and the people are liars and speak deceitfully." 
Micah6:13 As for me, I will deal you a crippling blow to ruin you for your sins." 
Micah6:14 You will eat but not be satisfied; you will still be hungry. You will acquire but save nothing because what you save, I will send your enemies to destroy."
