Micah6:15 You will plant crops but not reap a harvest, press olives for its oil and never be able to rub it on yourselves, and tread grapes but never drink the wine." 
Micah6:16 For you live by the standards of the dynasty of Omri and follow the evil ways of Ahab and his household. Because you walk after their example, you force me to make an example out of you! I will hand you over to ruin and make you a laughingstock among the nations. People everywhere will treat you with contempt.”" 
Micah7:1 I’m so depressed and disappointed, like a hungry man who went to the field after the harvest had been gathered and found nothing remaining, not even the gleanings— nor a single cluster of grapes nor any of those firstfruit figs I love." 
Micah7:2 The godly have vanished from the land: there is not a decent person left. They are all out for each other’s blood, they hunt each other with a net." 
Micah7:3 Concerning evil, both hands do it well. Officials and judges ask for bribes, the man in power pronounces whatever he pleases, and the prominent plot as one." 
Micah7:4 The best and brightest is like a briar bush, the most honest of them like a thorn hedge. Now here comes the punishment your prophets proclaimed. Confusion confronts you!" 
Micah7:5 Don’t dare to trust in a neighbor or place your confidence in a friend. Watch your words even with the wife you love. 
Micah7:6 For the son insults his father, the daughter rebels against her mother, and the daughter-in-law quarrels with her mother-in-law. One’s enemies come from within your own household." 
Micah7:7 But as for me, I will keep watching for Yahweh to break through. I will wait for the God who will save me, and I know my God will hear my cry." 
Micah7:8 Listen, my enemy. Don’t gloat over me when I fall, for I will get back up even stronger. Whenever I feel darkness around me, Yahweh himself will be the light that surrounds me." 
Micah7:9 I must endure Yahweh ’s anger for a while because I have sinned against him. Yet he will still defend my cause and right every wrong done to me. He will expose it all and bring me out into the light, and I will experience firsthand his vindication." 
Micah7:10 When my enemy sees this, she will be disgraced and covered with shame— she who sneered, “Where is this God of yours, Yahweh?” I’ll see it with my own eyes as she is trampled down like mud in the streets." 
Micah7:11 What a day that will be! A day for rebuilding your walls! A day for expanding your borders! 
Micah7:12 A day when people come to you from everywhere— from the cities of Assyria, Egypt, and Tyre and all the way from the river Euphrates, from across the seas, and from every mountainous land." 
Micah7:13 Those lands will become desolate because of the corruption of their inhabitants. 
Micah7:14 Our kind Shepherd, watch over us, the flock that is your special inheritance. With your staff, lead your people to pasture. For we are like a flock confined to live in a forest with meadowland all around us. Let us graze again in the fertile fields of Bashan and Gilead as in the days of old." 
Micah7:15 “I will show you mighty miracles as in the days when you came out of Egypt.” 
Micah7:16 When the nations see your miracles, they will be ashamed, seeing that their power counts for nothing. They will put their hands over their mouths, too dumbfounded by your might to even speak, and their ears will become deaf." 
Micah7:17 Let them lick the dust like snakes, like the crawling things of the earth. Let them come slinking out of their strongholds, trembling in terror before you, Yahweh." 
Micah7:18 Who is a merciful God like you? Your grace wipes the slate clean of guilt and pardons the rebellion of the remnant of your people. You don’t hold your anger against us forever, but you delight in showing faithful, tender love." 
Micah7:19 Once more we ask: have tender mercy on us, tread our iniquities under your feet, and hurl all our sins to the bottom of the sea." 
Micah7:20 You will demonstrate endless faithfulness to Jacob’s tribes and to Abraham’s descendants your constant love, just like you swore to our ancestors in ancient times." 
Nahum1:1 This is the book of the vision of Nahum who was from the village of Elkosh. A prophecy concerning Nineveh: 
Nahum1:2 Yahweh is a God who tolerates no rivals. Lord Yahweh will take vengeance on his foes. He punishes his adversaries and keeps wrath for his enemies. 
Nahum1:3 Yahweh is very slow to anger but so great in power; Yahweh will not let the guilty go unpunished. He marches out in the whirlwind and storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet." 
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and dries it up and makes all the rivers run dry. At his rebuke, the lush meadows of Bashan and Carmel wither; the flowering fields of Lebanon fade." 
Nahum1:5 In his presence, the mountains quake and the hills melt away. When he appears, the earth heaves, and the world and all who dwell in it are laid waste." 
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before an angry God? Who can face his fiery fury? When his wrath erupts, it pours out like lava, and boulders are broken to bits as he approaches." 
Nahum1:7 The goodness of Yahweh is beyond belief! For when trouble comes, we run into his heart and hide. He always cares for those who seek refuge in him." 
Nahum1:8 But with an overwhelming flood, he will make a complete end of Nineveh, and darkness will overtake his enemies." 
Nahum1:9 Whatever evil plot you hatch against Yahweh, he will so completely thwart that it will not rise up a second time." 
Nahum1:10 They will be like drunkards from their carousing— like entangled thorns, they will be consumed as dry stubble." 
Nahum1:11 From you, Nineveh, came one who plotted evil against Yahweh, a wicked, worthless strategist." 
Nahum1:12 Yahweh says, “Although your enemies are many and secure in their alliances, they will nonetheless be mowed down and pass away. Though I have afflicted you, my people, I will not do it again." 
Nahum1:13 And now I will break his yoke of bondage from your neck and tear away your shackles of Assyrian oppression.” 
Nahum1:14 Yahweh has decreed against you, Nineveh: “Your dynasty is over! Every idol in the temple of your gods I will destroy, your carved and engraved idols. I will dig your grave, for you are nothing to me.”" 
Nahum1:15 What a beautiful sight to behold— the precious feet of the messenger coming over the mountains to announce good news and to proclaim peace! People of Judah, celebrate your feasts, keep your solemn promises to God, for the wicked one will never pass through you again; he is completely cut off." 
Nahum2:1 Nineveh, an attacker is poised to advance against you. Place your guards along the top of the walls; set lookouts on the road. Go ahead, get ready for battle; gather all your forces, but it will be in vain." 
Nahum2:2 For Yahweh is about to restore the majesty of Jacob and Israel her glory. For you have plundered everything and ruined their vineyards. 
Nahum2:3 The invading soldiers’ shields are scarlet, each warrior robed in red. The metal of the Babylonian chariots gleams like fire on the day they are made ready for battle, and the wooden spears are brandished in anticipation." 
Nahum2:4 The chariots race madly through the streets of Nineveh; they dash wildly through the public squares, gleaming like torches, darting like lightning." 
Nahum2:5 The king of Assyria summons his elite commanders, but they stumble while attempting to advance. The attackers run swiftly to the city wall; the protective canopy for the battering ram is set up." 
Nahum2:6 The floodgates are opened; the royal palace is inundated by the surging torrent and collapses. 
Nahum2:7 Nineveh is stripped, taken captive, and carried into exile. Her maidservants moan like doves and beat their breasts in sorrow." 
Nahum2:8 The people rush out of Nineveh like water pours out of a broken dam. The officers shout, “Stop! Stop!” but no one turns back." 
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver, plunder the gold! Look! There is no end of the treasure — heaps of every precious thing." 
Nahum2:10 Desolation! Devastation! Destruction! Hearts melting, knees knocking, stomachs churning, faces turning pale." 
Nahum2:11 What has happened now to the lions’ den where the fierce lions were fed, where the lion and lioness once lived, where the cubs frolicked, cozy and fearless?" 
Nahum2:12 Like a lion, Assyria once killed his prey and tore it to pieces, filling his cave with meat for his mate and her cubs." 
Nahum2:13 “Behold, I am your enemy,” declares Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. “I will burn your chariots to ashes, and the sword will slaughter your young lions. No longer will you prey upon the land, and your messengers will announce no more victories.”" 
Nahum3:1 Doomed is the city of bloodshed, embodiment of treachery and thievery, where the murdering never stops!" 
Nahum3:2 Listen to the crack of the whip, the rumble of the chariot wheels, the thunder of horses’ hooves, and charging chariots." 
Nahum3:3 Horsemen advancing, gleaming swords and glistening spears, hosts of slain, heaps of corpses, dead bodies, too many to count. People stumble over the carcasses." 
Nahum3:4 This is because you lustfully lured the nations like a wanton whore. You acted so gracefully, yet you are a mistress of witchcraft. You sold entire nations into the sex trade and bewitched whole families into slavery." 
Nahum3:5 Behold, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declares to you: “I am your enemy! I will lift up your skirt over your face, and I will make nations gawk at your nakedness and kingdoms at your shame." 
Nahum3:6 I will pelt you with filth and make you an object of scorn and will treat you with contempt. 
Nahum3:7 And all who look at you will turn their backs on you and say, ‘Poor Nineveh is a pile of rubble; who will grieve for you? Who will have sympathy for you?’ ”" 
Nahum3:8 Are you any better than Thebes, which once sat by the Nile surrounded by water, her moat of defense, a mighty river?" 
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia and Egypt were her endless strength; Somalia and Libya were her helpful allies. 
Nahum3:10 Yet the people of Thebes became exiles and were carried off into captivity. Their infants were dashed to pieces on every street corner. They cast lots for her honored men— all the best and brightest were bound in chains and taken captive. 
Nahum3:11 You will fall into a drunken stupor. You will go into hiding and seek refuge from the enemy. 
Nahum3:12 All your fortified cities are like fig trees with first-ripe figs: shake the tree and they fall right into the open mouth of the eater. 
Nahum3:13 Behold, all who remain in the midst of your cities are the women. The borders of your land are wide open to your enemies. Fire will devour your fortresses." 
Nahum3:14 Go ahead and draw plenty of water and prepare for the siege. Strengthen your defenses. Get mud, trample the clay, make more bricks." 
Nahum3:15 Yet the fire will devour you there. You will be slaughtered by the sword. It will consume you like locusts. You multiplied like hopping locusts and like swarming grasshoppers. 
Nahum3:16 Your merchants multiplied until they were more than the stars in the sky. Like locusts they stripped the land, spread their wings, and flew away." 
Nahum3:17 Your officials were as numerous as grasshoppers, your scribes like a swarm of locusts. On a cold day, they settle on the stone fences, but when the sun appears, they abandon you and fly away; no one knows where they are." 
Nahum3:18 Emperor of Assyria, your shepherds slumber, your nobles nod off to sleep soundly. All your people are like sheep scattered on the mountains with no one to bring them home." 
Nahum3:19 There is no remedy for your injuries; your wound is fatal. All who hear the news about you applaud for joy over your downfall. For whoever was untouched by your endless evil? 
Habakkuk1:1 This is the vision of Habakkuk the prophet: 
Habakkuk1:2 Yahweh, how long must I cry for help and you turn a deaf ear? My heart cries out, “Save us from this brutality!” but you do not save." 
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you make me stare at injustice? Why do you tolerate such trouble and wrongdoing? Destruction and violence surround me; strife and conflict are everywhere. 
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore, the law is not enforced, and so justice never prevails. The wicked overwhelm the righteous so that justice is perverted." 
Habakkuk1:5 “Look at the nations and observe— you’ll be stunned by what you see. For while you watch, I am about to do something so surprising in your days that you would not believe it even if you were told." 
Habakkuk1:6 I am raising up the Babylonians to punish you — that fierce and fearsome people, who sweep across the whole earth to seize houses they do not own." 
Habakkuk1:7 They are a feared and dreaded people. In their autocratic arrogance, they become a law unto themselves." 
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards, fiercer than wolves on the prowl at dusk. Their cavalry charges headlong; their horsemen come from afar. They soar swiftly like an eagle swooping to devour." 
Habakkuk1:9 Their armies invade, intent on violent conquest. Their terrifying hordes advance like a desert wind and gather prisoners as numerous as grains of sand." 
Habakkuk1:10 They are a people who mock kings and scoff at those in authority. They laugh at every stronghold; they pile up earthen siege ramps and capture them. 
Habakkuk1:11 Then they hurry on, like the wind that blows by and disappears. They are guilty men who worship their own power as if it were a god.”" 
Habakkuk1:12 Yahweh, have you not always been the everlasting God? You are my God, my Holy One, who will not die. Yahweh, you have appointed the Babylonians to execute judgment; my Rock, you have raised them up so they can punish us." 
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are so pure that you cannot stand the sight of evil; nor do you look on wickedness with favor. Why then do you tolerate the treacherous? Why are you so silent while sinners devour those more righteous than themselves? 
Habakkuk1:14 Have you only made us like fish, to be caught and killed by the wicked; like swarming schools of fish with no ruler?" 
Habakkuk1:15 Then the Babylonians pull them up with fishhooks and drag them in with their nets. Gleefully, they gather them up in their dragnets, shouting for joy over their catch." 
Habakkuk1:16 They even offer sacrifices and burn incense in honor of their nets, as if these were gods. For their nets enable them to live luxuriously and eat the finest food." 
Habakkuk1:17 How long will you allow them to fill and empty their throw nets? Will they forever destroy nations without mercy? 
Habakkuk2:1 I will wait at my watchpost and station myself on the tower. I’ll keep watching to see what he’ll say to me and consider the reply when he answers my argument. 
Habakkuk2:2 Then Yahweh replied: “Write down the revelation; write it legibly on clay tablets so that whoever reads it may run and explain it to others. 
Habakkuk2:3 The vision still awaits an appointed time; it speaks of the end and will not prove to be false. When the appointed time comes, it will happen with breathless haste. Although it may seem slow, just be patient and wait. For it will surely come right on time." 
Habakkuk2:4 “Look at the proud, puffed up with pride! They are crooked to the core. But the righteous will live by his faith.”" 
Habakkuk2:5 “Wealth is deceitful. The proud are full of themselves but have empty souls. They are as greedy as the grave. And just like death itself, they are never satisfied. They gather and grasp for themselves all the wealth of the nations and take all the people captive." 
Habakkuk2:6 “All these conquered nations will one day taunt them, saying, ‘You are doomed, you who pile up stolen goods! How long will you amass wealth by extortion?’" 
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your creditors suddenly arise? Will they not wake up and terrify you? Before you know it, you will become their prey." 
Habakkuk2:8 Since you have plundered many nations, the peoples who are left will plunder you. For you have killed many people; you have acted violently against lands and cities and everyone within them." 
Habakkuk2:9 “You are doomed, you who enrich your family by cheating others! You build your luxurious home on high thinking you’ll be safe from harm’s reach." 
Habakkuk2:10 Your schemes to ruin the lives and homes of others bring shame upon your own house. You are as good as dead for doing this. 
Habakkuk2:11 The stones of the wall will cry out to accuse you, and your wooden rafters will echo it." 
Habakkuk2:12 “You are doomed, you who build a city with bloodshed and establish it upon evil!" 
Habakkuk2:13 I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have decreed: The efforts of the nations are nothing but fuel for the fire, for all they have built will go up in smoke." 
Habakkuk2:14 Yet the entire earth is being filled with the revelation of the glory of Yahweh, just as waters fill the sea." 
Habakkuk2:15 “You are doomed, you who delight to mix drinks for neighbors, pouring wine from pitchers until they are dead drunk just so that you can gaze on their naked bodies!" 
Habakkuk2:16 Now you will be drunk with shame instead of honor. Now it is your turn! Drink your fill and let your nakedness be exposed. The cup of punishment that Yahweh holds in his right hand is now coming around to you. Your former glory will be covered with your vomit of disgrace. 
Habakkuk2:17 Your violent acts against Lebanon will violently overwhelm you. Since you killed its animals, animals will terrify you. For you have killed many people and acted violently against lands and cities and everyone in them." 
Habakkuk2:18 “You are doomed, you who say to a piece of dead wood, ‘Come to life!’ Or to lifeless stone, ‘Get up!’ Can something dead give revelation? Even though it is covered with gold and silver, there is no breath in it. Of what use is an idol carved by a craftsman? Or a metal image that gives misleading oracles? For the one who makes it trusts the work of human hands; a god that cannot even talk.”" 
Habakkuk2:19  
Habakkuk2:20 Yahweh is in his holy temple; let everyone on earth be silent before him. 
Habakkuk3:1 This is the passionate prayer of Habakkuk the prophet. 
Habakkuk3:2 Yahweh, I know the things that have made you famous. I am stunned when I consider your miracles. Do them again in our day! Let this generation experience your mighty deeds. Yahweh, show us your mercy even in this time of turmoil." 
Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman, the Holy One from Mount Paran. The heavens blazed with the brightness of his glory while the earth echoed his praises." 
Habakkuk3:4 The brilliance of his glory is bright as the sun; twin bolts of lightning flash from his hand — there is the hiding place of his power. 
Habakkuk3:5 A plague marched in front of him; a consuming pestilence followed at his heels. 
Habakkuk3:6 When he stood up, the earth shook. At his glance, nations trembled, mountains crumbled, and age-old hills collapsed, but his paths are everlasting." 
Habakkuk3:7 In my vision, I saw the tents of Cushan trembling in distress and the tent curtains of the Midianites shaking in anguish." 
Habakkuk3:8 Yahweh, was it the rivers that made you angry or stirred up your rage? Was it the sea that made you furious when you rode your horses and chariots of salvation?" 
Habakkuk3:9 Your bow is strung and ready for action, and with a word, you commission your arrows to hit their mark. You carved open the earth with rivers." 
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw you and trembled. Torrential rains flooded the earth; the ocean depths roared and lifted their waves on high. 
Habakkuk3:11 Sun and moon stood still in the sky when your speeding arrows flashed and your glittering spear gleamed. 
Habakkuk3:12 In your fury you marched across the earth, and in your anger you trampled the nations." 
Habakkuk3:13 You came to save your people, to win the victory with your Messiah. You crushed the head of the house of wickedness and stripped him from head to toe." 
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced his head with his own arrows when his warriors stormed out to scatter us, gloating as they slaughtered their wretched victims in hiding." 
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with your horses, churning the great waters." 
Habakkuk3:16 I hear all this, and my heart pounds; my teeth chatter with fear at the sound. My body goes limp, and my legs tremble. Yet my heart quietly rests. I know the day of trouble is ahead for the people invading us." 
Habakkuk3:17 Even if my fig trees do not blossom and my vines grow no grapes, if my olive crop fails and my fields produce no harvest, and even if all my sheep die and I’m left with no cattle in my barn," 
Habakkuk3:18 I still have Yahweh, and I will rejoice in him. Yes, I will dance with joy no matter what, for I have a Savior-God!" 
Habakkuk3:19 Yahweh is the Lord of my strength; he makes my feet sure-footed as a deer bounding upon the high places. These are the lyrics for the conductor of my string ensemble. 
Zephaniah1:1 This is the prophetic message Yahweh gave to Zephaniah son of Cushi, grandson of Gedaliah, great-grandson of Amariah, great-great-grandson of Hezekiah. Zephaniah received this message during the time when Josiah, the son of Amon, ruled Judah." 
Zephaniah1:2 “I, Yahweh, declare that I will certainly sweep away every living thing from the land— animals and humans, birds and fish. I will topple the wicked and their idols and sweep away man from the land.”" 
Zephaniah1:3  
Zephaniah1:4 “I will punish Judah and all who live in Jerusalem. I will wipe out every trace of Baal from the land along with the names of the pagan priests or any priest who has turned away from me. 
Zephaniah1:5 I will punish those who bow down on their rooftops to worship the sun, moon, and stars and those with divided loyalties who bow before Yahweh and swear allegiance to me but also swear their oaths in the name of their king." 
Zephaniah1:6 I, Yahweh, will punish those who have turned their back on me, who do not seek me nor pray for me to guide them.”" 
Zephaniah1:7 Be silent before the Lord God, for the day of Yahweh is near. “I have prepared a sacrifice and have consecrated my invited guests." 
Zephaniah1:8 On the day of Yahweh ’s sacrifice, I will punish the royal family, your officials, and all who dress like pagan priests." 
Zephaniah1:9 At that time, I will punish all who worship Dagon and fill the palace of their rulers with violence and deception." 
Zephaniah1:10 “I, Yahweh, declare: A cry for help will be heard from the Fish Gate in Jerusalem, wailing from the wealthier, newer part of the city. A loud crash will echo from the hills." 
Zephaniah1:11 Wail, you who live in the market area because all the merchants will be dead, and those who weigh out silver will be wiped out." 
Zephaniah1:12 “When that time comes, I will search Jerusalem with lamps and punish those stagnating in their self-satisfied smugness. They say in their hearts, ‘ Yahweh is aloof and can do nothing for us, either good or bad.’" 
Zephaniah1:13 Yet all their hoarded wealth will be looted and their houses leveled. They will build houses but never move in, plant vineyards but never drink the wine.”" 
Zephaniah1:14 The great day of Yahweh is near, right around the corner, closer than you think and coming fast. How bitter will be the sound of the day of Yahweh, when even the brave warriors weep in anguish." 
Zephaniah1:15 It will be a day of wrath, a day of terror and trouble, a day of desolation and distress, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness," 
Zephaniah1:16 a day of shofar blasts and battle shouts against fortified cities and all the lofty towers. 
Zephaniah1:17 “I will bring such distress on people that they will grope their way like the blind for having sinned against me. Their blood will be poured out like dust, yes, their flesh like dung." 
Zephaniah1:18 On that day, I will display my great anger, and none of their silver or gold will save them. My avenging zeal will burn like a fire on that day, to consume the entire land. For I will bring a terrible and sudden end that will destroy everyone living in the land.”" 
Zephaniah2:1 Go ahead and gather yourselves together, you shameless people! Gather yourselves together and turn back to Yahweh" 
Zephaniah2:2 before the appointed time arrives. Repent before you are blown away like chaff, which is swept away in a day, before Yahweh pours out on you his fiery anger— the day of Yahweh ’s burning fury." 
Zephaniah2:3 All who are meek and humble on earth, seek the mercy of Yahweh. All who obey his commands, pursue righteousness and discover true humility. For maybe, just maybe, Yahweh will shelter you on the day of his anger." 
Zephaniah2:4 “For Gaza will be deserted, and Ashkelon devastated. Ashdod will be driven out in broad daylight, and Ekron left desolate." 
Zephaniah2:5 Doom to you who live on the seacoast, to the nation of Cherethites! And Canaan, land of the Philistines, the word of Yahweh is bad news for you. I will destroy and annihilate you." 
Zephaniah2:6 Your coastal lands will be reduced to pastureland, to grazing grounds for shepherds and folds for flocks." 
Zephaniah2:7 Your land will be occupied by the remnant of the people of Judah. During the day, they will find pasture by the sea, and at night, they will rest in the houses of Ashkelon. Yahweh their God will care for them, and make them prosper again.”" 
Zephaniah2:8 “I, Yahweh, have heard the crude taunts of Moab and the vile insults of the Ammonites as they scorned my people and boasted that they would annex the land of Judah." 
Zephaniah2:9 For as surely as I live, I, Yahweh, God of Israel, Commander of Angel Armies, decree: Moab will become like Sodom and the Ammonites like Gomorrah. The remnant of my people will plunder them; the survivors of my nation will possess them. Their lands will become barren forever, a land of nothing but salt pits and piles of weeds." 
Zephaniah2:10 This will be the price of their pride, of having taunted and boasted against the people of Yahweh, who commands angel armies." 
Zephaniah2:11 I, Yahweh, will be terrifying to them, for I will make all the gods of the earth vanish. And the people of every nation will bow down and worship me wherever they live.”" 
Zephaniah2:12 “You Ethiopians, too, will be killed by my sword.”" 
Zephaniah2:13 “I will attack the northern land of Assyria and destroy it all. Nineveh will be left as desolate and dry as a desert. 
Zephaniah2:14 It will be nothing more than a resting place for flocks and herds, along with all creatures of every kind. The vulture and owl will nest at night in its ruins. Owls will hoot from the windows, and crows will caw in the doorways— for all their once-fancy cedar woodwork will be exposed to the elements." 
Zephaniah2:15 This is the once-proud city, living so happy and carefree and thinking to herself, ‘I am so amazing, and I have no rival!’ Yet look at her now! What has she become? Nothing but a pile of rubble and a lair for wild beasts. Everyone who passes by will hiss with scorn and shake their fists.”" 
Zephaniah3:1 Doomed is the filthy and foul city— the city of oppression! 
Zephaniah3:2 Her people do not listen to the voice of God and will not accept correction. They do not trust in Yahweh anymore and do not want a close relationship with their God. 
Zephaniah3:3 Her rulers are roaring lions, and her judges are ravenous wolves who hunt in the evening, too greedy to leave even a bone till the morning." 
Zephaniah3:4 Her prophets are proud, reckless impostors. Her priests profane the holy place and openly break the Law." 
Zephaniah3:5 In spite of all this, Yahweh the Righteous resides in Jerusalem, and all he does is just and true. Morning by morning, he brings justice to light, and day by day, he never fails. Yet the wicked are shameless." 
Zephaniah3:6 “I have destroyed proud nations and toppled their high towers. I filled their streets with rubble so no one walks through them. Their cities have been demolished. Everyone is gone. Not one is left. 
Zephaniah3:7 I thought, ‘Certainly, now you will revere me. Now you will yield to my correction.’ Then I would not need to punish them again and destroy their homes. But no, they were just as eager to do whatever was evil." 
Zephaniah3:8 “So wait patiently for me,” declares Yahweh. “Wait for the day when I rise to seize the prey. For I have determined to gather the nations and assemble the kingdoms so that they will experience my fury and know how great my anger is. For the land will be consumed by the fire of my avenging zeal.”" 
Zephaniah3:9 “For then I will change the impure speech of all the people to the pure speech of praise so that all may worship my name together and serve me shoulder to shoulder. 
Zephaniah3:10 From beyond the rivers of Ethiopia, my worshipers from distant lands will bring me gifts.”" 
Zephaniah3:11 “When that day comes, you will never again be put to shame over your former acts of rebellion. For I will remove from your midst all who are arrogant and who boast in their self-importance. You will never again strut about in pride on my holy mountain." 
Zephaniah3:12 But I will leave a remnant among you: a humble and lowly people who will find their hiding place in the beautiful name of Yahweh. 
Zephaniah3:13 They will do no wrong and only speak the truth, nor will they use any form of deceit. They will be content like grazing sheep. They will rest, and no one will frighten them.”" 
Zephaniah3:14 Go ahead, sing for joy, daughter of Zion. People of Israel, get excited and shout aloud. Rejoice, let blissful praise fill your heart, fair daughter of Jerusalem." 
Zephaniah3:15 Yahweh has canceled out his judgments against you. He has turned your accuser away. Yahweh, your King, is here within you, Israel; you have nothing more to fear." 
Zephaniah3:16 On that day, the message for Jerusalem will be: Zion, do not yield to fear. Don’t let your heart be discouraged." 
Zephaniah3:17 Yahweh, your God, is inside you. He is the Warrior-Savior who takes such delight in you that it will make him leap for joy and shout with great gladness. Yes, he will soothe you with his love. He will sing over you his song of praise." 
Zephaniah3:18 “I will remove the sadness over your appointed feasts, and you will not be ashamed about them again." 
Zephaniah3:19 Behold, at the right time, I will deal with those who mistreat you. I will rescue the weak and regather the scattered ones. I will turn their shame into praise when I restore their honor across the land." 
Zephaniah3:20 On that day, I will be your guide to bring you home; at that time, I will gather you together. You can be sure of this: I will give you a good name, and you will be admired among all the peoples of the earth as I restore your fortunes before your own eyes. I, Yahweh, have spoken.”" 
Haggai1:1 On the first day of the month of Elul during the second year Darius was emperor of Persia, the word of Yahweh was spoken through the prophet Haggai. He delivered this prophecy to the appointed governor of Judah, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and to the high priest, Joshua son of Jehozadak." 
Haggai1:1 On the first day of the month of Elul during the second year Darius was emperor of Persia, the word of Yahweh was spoken through the prophet Haggai. He delivered this prophecy to the appointed governor of Judah, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and to the high priest, Joshua son of Jehozadak." 
Haggai1:2 “ Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, says this: ‘These people procrastinate by saying that this is not the right time to finish rebuilding Yahweh ’s house.’ ”" 
Haggai1:3 Then Yahweh spoke the following message to the people using Haggai as his mouthpiece: 
Haggai1:4 “ Really? Is this the right time for you to be living in your expensive houses while my house lies in ruins? 
Haggai1:5 So now, I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you: You had better be intentional about the path you choose." 
Haggai1:6 You have planted much but harvested very little. You fill your plates, but you are never full. You drink but are still thirsty. You put on clothes but not enough to keep warm. You earn wages, but your money burns a hole in your pocket and quickly runs out." 
Haggai1:7 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, repeat: Be intentional about the path you take." 
Haggai1:8 Now, go up the mountain, cut some timber, and rebuild my house, and I will take pleasure in it and manifest my glory there. It is I, Yahweh, who say so." 
Haggai1:9 “You hoped for abundant crops, but instead you gathered little. When you brought in your meager harvest, I caused it to spoil. It is I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, asking you, ‘Why?’ It is because my house lies in ruins while each of you runs to his own house." 
Haggai1:10 That is why the sky has withheld the rain and the ground its crops. 
Haggai1:11 I have called down drought on the land and hills—your grain, oil, and new wine. It has affected both people and animals and spoiled all your attempts to grow food.”" 
Haggai1:12 Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, Joshua son of Jehozadak the high priest, and the entire remnant of the people obeyed the voice of Yahweh their God. They recognized that Yahweh their God had sent Haggai to them, so they obeyed his words. And the people were filled with holy awe toward Yahweh." 
Haggai1:12 Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, Joshua son of Jehozadak the high priest, and the entire remnant of the people obeyed the voice of Yahweh their God. They recognized that Yahweh their God had sent Haggai to them, so they obeyed his words. And the people were filled with holy awe toward Yahweh." 
Haggai1:13 Then the messenger of Yahweh, Haggai, spoke Yahweh ’s promise to the people: “I am with you to bless you —declares Yahweh.”" 
Haggai1:14 And Yahweh stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel governor of Judah, the spirit of Joshua son of Jehozadak the high priest, and the spirit of the entire remnant of the people, and they came together to rebuild the temple of Yahweh, their God, the Commander of Angel Armies." 
Haggai1:14 And Yahweh stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel governor of Judah, the spirit of Joshua son of Jehozadak the high priest, and the spirit of the entire remnant of the people, and they came together to rebuild the temple of Yahweh, their God, the Commander of Angel Armies." 
Haggai1:15 They began their work on the twenty-fourth day of the sixth month. 
Haggai2:1 In the second year of King Darius, the twenty-first day of the seventh month, the word of Yahweh happened through the prophet Haggai as follows," 
Haggai2:2 “Go and give this urgent message to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and the high priest Joshua son of Jehozadak, and all the remnant of the people:" 
Haggai2:2 “Go and give this urgent message to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and the high priest Joshua son of Jehozadak, and all the remnant of the people:" 
Haggai2:3 “Did anyone from among you see the magnificent glory of the original temple? And how does it look to you now? Does it not seem as nothing in your eyes? 
Haggai2:4 “I, Yahweh say to you, Zerubbabel, be courageous! Joshua, son of Jehozadak, high priest, be courageous! All you living in the land, take heart! Go to work, for I am with you, says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Haggai2:4 “I, Yahweh say to you, Zerubbabel, be courageous! Joshua, son of Jehozadak, high priest, be courageous! All you living in the land, take heart! Go to work, for I am with you, says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Haggai2:5 I, Yahweh, will keep the covenant that I made with you when you came out of Egypt. My Spirit is still present among you, so be fearless." 
Haggai2:6 “For I declare to you: Very soon, I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, will once again shake the heavens and earth, the land and sea." 
Haggai2:7 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, will shake every nation, and the treasures of the nations will flow in, and I will fill this temple with glory." 
Haggai2:8 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declare to you, all the silver and gold is mine." 
Haggai2:9 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declare to you, the glory of this new temple will surpass the glory of the original, and I will grant peace and prosperity in this place. “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have spoken.”" 
Haggai2:10 In the second year of Darius, on the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, the word of Yahweh was spoken to the prophet Haggai as follows:" 
Haggai2:11 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you: ‘Ask the priests for their ruling on this:" 
Haggai2:12 If someone holds consecrated meat from a sacrifice and carries it in the fold of his priestly robe, could his robe then pass on holiness if it comes into contact with bread, stew, wine, olive oil, or any kind of food at all?’ ” “No,” the priests replied." 
Haggai2:13 Haggai then said, “If anyone is ritually unclean by contact with a corpse and touches any of these things, would it become unclean?” The priests replied, “Yes, it is unclean.”" 
Haggai2:14 Then Haggai said, “So it is the same with this people. Yahweh declares: ‘The nation is unclean in my eyes. Everything they produce is unclean to me, and everything they offer at the altar is also unclean.’ ”" 
Haggai2:15 “Now consider carefully your recent past before you started rebuilding Yahweh ’s temple. 
Haggai2:16 Stop and remember what condition you were in. You would come expecting to scoop twenty measures of grain but find only ten. You would come expecting to draw fifty measures of wine from a vat but find only twenty. 
Haggai2:17 I struck the produce of your labor by sending hail, hot desert winds to wither your grain, and damp winds to make it rot. Yet you still would not return to me —declares Yahweh." 
Haggai2:18 “Today is the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, the day the people resumed the building of my temple. So be intentional from today forward." 
Haggai2:19 The seed is still in the storehouse, isn’t it? Until now, the grapevines, fig trees, pomegranate and olive trees have not produced a harvest, but from now on, I will certainly bless you, and your harvest will be plentiful.”" 
Haggai2:20 On that same day, the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, the word of Yahweh happened a second time to Haggai, as follows:" 
Haggai2:21 “Tell these words to Zerubbabel governor of Judah: ‘I am going to shake the heavens and the earth. 
Haggai2:22 I will topple thrones and destroy the dynasties of foreign kingdoms. I will crash their chariots and their riders. Horses and their riders will die, for their riders will kill one another with the sword." 
Haggai2:23 I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declare, when that day comes, I will take you, my servant Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and make you like a signet ring. For I have chosen you to be mine. I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies have spoken.’ ”" 
Zechariah1:1 In October of the second year that Darius was emperor of Persia, Yahweh spoke to the prophet Zechariah son of Berechiah, son of Iddo, saying," 
Zechariah1:2 “ I, Yahweh, was intensely angry with your ancestors." 
Zechariah1:3 So give the people this message: ‘ Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, says to you: “Come back to me, and I will come back to you,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Zechariah1:4 Do not be like your ancestors. Long ago my prophets warned them, saying, “Turn your lives around completely and end your evil, wicked ways.” But they refused to listen to me or obey me." 
Zechariah1:5 And where did they end up? And the prophets, did they live forever?" 
Zechariah1:6 But my words and warnings, which I gave through my servants the prophets, proved to be true. Your ancestors ignored them, and so they were punished. As a result, they repented, saying, “ Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, has punished us as he warned he would do and as our sinful ways have deserved.” ’ ”" 
Zechariah1:7 On February 15 (the month of Shvat), in the second year of Darius’ reign, Yahweh spoke to the prophet Zechariah son of Berechiah, son of Iddo." 
Zechariah1:8 That night I had a vision. Behold, I saw a man sitting on a reddish-brown horse in the shadows of a grove of myrtles. And behind him I saw other horses—some brown, tan, and white with riders upon them." 
Zechariah1:9 There was another angel there who talked with me, and I asked him, “What do these represent, my lord?” And the angel who was talking to me said, “Let me show you.”" 
Zechariah1:10 Then the man standing among the myrtles spoke up and said, “These are Yahweh ’s agents sent to patrol the earth.”" 
Zechariah1:11 Then the horsemen reported to the angel of Yahweh who stood among the myrtles, “We have been patrolling the land, and indeed, all is peacefully at rest.”" 
Zechariah1:12 Yahweh ’s angel then spoke and said, “ Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, you have inflicted your anger on Jerusalem and the cities of Judah for the past seventy years. How many more years will pass before you once again show them that you love them?”" 
Zechariah1:13 Yahweh then replied to the angel who was talking to me with kind and comforting words. 
Zechariah1:14 So the angel told me to proclaim these words of Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies: “My heart burns with passion for Jerusalem and Zion," 
Zechariah1:15 but I am deeply angry with the careless and complacent nations. I was only mildly angry with my people, but the nations I used to punish them acted too severely." 
Zechariah1:16 Therefore, I have returned to Jerusalem with mercy and kindness. I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have decreed to rebuild my house there. Surveyors will stretch my measuring line over Jerusalem to rebuild the city.”" 
Zechariah1:17 The angel also told me to proclaim, “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, decree that my cities will once more overflow with prosperity. I will comfort Zion once again and claim Jerusalem as my own.”" 
Zechariah1:18 Then I looked up and had a vision. Behold, I saw four horns." 
Zechariah1:19 I asked the angel who was talking to me, “What do these represent?” And he answered, “These are the four horns that scattered Jerusalem, Judah, and Israel.”" 
Zechariah1:20 Then Yahweh opened my eyes to see four conquering craftsmen. 
Zechariah1:21 And I asked, “What are they coming to do?” He replied, “They have come to terrify and hammer out justice among the nations who lifted their horns to completely crush the land of Judah and scatter its inhabitants.”" 
Zechariah2:1 Then I looked up again and had another vision. Behold, I saw a man with a measuring line in his hand." 
Zechariah2:2 I asked him, “Where are you going with that?” “To measure Jerusalem,” he replied, “to calculate her width and length.”" 
Zechariah2:3 And behold, the angel who was talking with me left, and another angel came out to meet him." 
Zechariah2:4 He said to him, “Run, and tell the surveyor to stop measuring, for Jerusalem will be too full of people and animals to measure or be enclosed by walls." 
Zechariah2:5 So, I, Yahweh, will be a protective wall of fire surrounding her and a radiant glory within her.”" 
Zechariah2:6 “Look out! Look out! Flee from Babylon,” declares Yahweh, “for like the four winds of heaven I have scattered you in every direction,” declares Yahweh." 
Zechariah2:7 “Escape, Zion, you who live within the fair city of Babylon!”" 
Zechariah2:8 For Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, sent me after glory. And he warns the nations who plundered you: “Whoever touches you, my precious ones, touches the apple of my eye." 
Zechariah2:9 Now behold, I will raise my fist against them to crush them, and those whom they enslaved will plunder them. Then you will know that Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, has sent me.”" 
Zechariah2:10 I, Yahweh, declare to you: “It’s time to sing out and rejoice, my daughter Zion, for now I am coming to live among you.”" 
Zechariah2:11 At that time, people from many nations will be converted to Yahweh and become his people, and he will live among them. Then you will know that Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, has sent me to you." 
Zechariah2:12 The people of Judah will become Yahweh ’s special people, his inheritance in the holy land, and he will again choose Jerusalem." 
Zechariah2:13 Let everyone be silent before Yahweh, for now he is stirring from his holy dwelling place." 
Zechariah3:1 Then I was shown another vision. I saw Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of Yahweh dressed in filthy clothes. On Joshua’s right was the accuser, standing there ready to prosecute him. The angel of Yahweh said to Satan, “ Yahweh rebukes you, Satan! Yes, Yahweh rebukes you, for he has chosen Jerusalem. And he has rescued Joshua, like a stick snatched out of the fire.”"
