Zechariah4:1 Then the angel who had been explaining the visions came back and roused me as someone awakened from sleep. 
Zechariah4:2 And he asked me, “What do you see?” As I focused my eyes, I replied, “I see a solid gold lampstand with a bowl for the oil on top. It has seven lamps, and each lamp has a golden spout for the wicks." 
Zechariah4:3 And I also see two olive trees overshadowing the lampstand, one on the right and one on the left.”" 
Zechariah4:4 So I asked the angel who was talking to me, “My lord, what do these things represent?”" 
Zechariah4:5 The angel answered, “Do you not know what they are?” “No, my lord,” I said." 
Zechariah4:6 Here is the message of Yahweh to Zerubbabel: “ You will not succeed by military might nor by your own strength but by my Spirit,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Zechariah4:7 “And what are you, O mighty mountain? Before you, Zerubbabel, this mountain of obstacles will crumble. Then he will set the capstone in place to shouts of ‘Grace and beauty be upon it!’ ”" 
Zechariah4:8 Then another message from Yahweh came to me: 
Zechariah4:9 “With his own hands, Zerubbabel has laid the foundation of this temple, and with his own hands he will finish it. Then you will know that Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, has sent me to you." 
Zechariah4:10 “Those who despise the day of small beginnings will rejoice when they see Zerubbabel holding the plumb line in his hands. “The seven lamps represent the seven eyes of Yahweh that keep a close watch over everything on earth.” 
Zechariah4:11 Then I asked the angel two questions: “What about these two olive trees on each side of the lampstand? 
Zechariah4:12 And what are the two branches from which golden oil flows through the two golden pipes?” 
Zechariah4:13 He replied, “Don’t you know what these are?” “No, my lord,” I answered." 
Zechariah4:14 So he explained, “These branches are the two sons of oil who serve the Lord of all the earth.”" 
Zechariah5:1 Then I had another vision. I looked up and saw a flying scroll. 
Zechariah5:2 The angel asked me, “What do you see?” I replied, “I see a flying scroll. It looks about thirty-five feet long and seventeen feet wide.”" 
Zechariah5:3 Then he explained to me its meaning: “This scroll contains God’s curse against evildoers sweeping over the whole land. On one side of the scroll, it says that the Lord will banish every thief. On the other side, it says that he will banish everyone who lies using his name." 
Zechariah5:4 Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declares, ‘I will release this curse, and it will enter the house of the thief and of anyone who lies using my name. The curse will remain in his house until it consumes it—walls, beams, and all.’ ”" 
Zechariah5:5 Then the angel who was talking to me appeared and said, “Look at what is coming.”" 
Zechariah5:6 I asked, “What is it?” He replied, “It is a barrel, and it is carrying the guilt of all the people of the land of Judah.”" 
Zechariah5:7 As I looked, the angel removed the heavy lid made of lead from the barrel, and a woman was seated inside." 
Zechariah5:8 The angel said, “This is Lady Wickedness.” And he shoved her back into the barrel and clamped the lead lid over her." 
Zechariah5:9 I looked up and suddenly two women appeared, soaring swiftly in the sky with outstretched wings like those of a stork. They lifted the barrel high in the sky and flew away with it." 
Zechariah5:10 So I asked the angel who was talking to me, “Where are they taking the barrel?”" 
Zechariah5:11 He replied, “To the wicked land of Babylon, where they will build a temple and make a pedestal for it. They will place it there and worship it.”" 
Zechariah6:1 Then I looked once more and had another vision. I saw four chariots coming out between two mountains of bronze. 
Zechariah6:2 The first chariot was pulled by reddish-brown horses, the second by black horses," 
Zechariah6:3 the third by white horses, and the fourth by dappled horses—all of them mighty." 
Zechariah6:4 I asked the angel who was talking to me, “What do these represent, my lord?”" 
Zechariah6:5 The angel explained, “These are the four winds of heaven. They are being dispatched after standing before the Lord of all the earth." 
Zechariah6:6 The black horses are leaving for the land of the north, the white ones for the west, and the dappled for the land of the south.”" 
Zechariah6:7 The strong horses came out eager to patrol the world. He said, “Go and patrol the earth,” and off they went." 
Zechariah6:8 Then he called to me and said, “Watch, the ones going to the land of the north are going to make my Spirit descend upon that region.”" 
Zechariah6:9 Then another message from Yahweh came to me: 
Zechariah6:10 “Go at once to the home of Josiah the son of Zephaniah. There you will receive gifts from Heldai, Tobijah, and Jedaiah, who have just arrived among the exiles in Babylon." 
Zechariah6:10 “Go at once to the home of Josiah the son of Zephaniah. There you will receive gifts from Heldai, Tobijah, and Jedaiah, who have just arrived among the exiles in Babylon." 
Zechariah6:11 Accept their gifts of gold and silver and make a crown to place on the head of the high priest Joshua son of Jehozadak. 
Zechariah6:11 Accept their gifts of gold and silver and make a crown to place on the head of the high priest Joshua son of Jehozadak. 
Zechariah6:12 And say this to him, ‘This is what Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, says: “Behold the man whose name is the Branch! He will branch out and flourish from his place, and he will build Yahweh ’s sanctuary." 
Zechariah6:13 Yes, he will build Yahweh ’s sanctuary and will be clothed with majesty. He will sit on his throne and rule. He will be a priest ruling on his throne. These two offices will flow together with perfect harmony." 
Zechariah6:14 Joshua’s crown will be placed in my temple as a memorial to the donors Heldai, Tobijah, Jedaiah, and in recognition of the generosity of Josiah the son of Zephaniah." 
Zechariah6:14 Joshua’s crown will be placed in my temple as a memorial to the donors Heldai, Tobijah, Jedaiah, and in recognition of the generosity of Josiah the son of Zephaniah." 
Zechariah6:14 Joshua’s crown will be placed in my temple as a memorial to the donors Heldai, Tobijah, Jedaiah, and in recognition of the generosity of Josiah the son of Zephaniah." 
Zechariah6:15 And those in distant lands will come to assist the building of Yahweh ’s sanctuary.” “ ‘When this happens, you will know for certain that Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, has sent me to you. This will happen if you faithfully obey the voice of Yahweh your God.’ ”" 
Zechariah7:1 In the fourth year of Emperor Darius, on the seventh of December, Yahweh spoke again to Zechariah." 
Zechariah7:2 Now the people of Bethel had sent Sharezer and Regem-Melech with a delegation to Jerusalem to pray for the favor of Yahweh. 
Zechariah7:3 So they went to the prophets and priests who were at the Temple of Yahweh and asked them: “For many years, each July we have mourned and fasted. Should we continue to do this?”" 
Zechariah7:4 Then Yahweh spoke this message to me: 
Zechariah7:5 “Ask all the priests and the people of the land: ‘For nearly seventy years, you have been fasting and mourning in the fifth and seventh months. Have you truly been doing this to honor me?" 
Zechariah7:6 And when you ate and drank, were you not doing it for your own pleasure?" 
Zechariah7:7 Did not Yahweh speak these things to you through the prophets in the past? Have you forgotten what he told you when Jerusalem and the surrounding towns were prosperous and secure and when the southland and western foothills were inhabited?’ ” 
Zechariah7:8 Yahweh spoke again to Zechariah, saying," 
Zechariah7:9 “Here is the message of Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies: “Apply the law fairly. “Demonstrate tender love and compassion toward all." 
Zechariah7:10 “Never oppress the widow and the fatherless, the foreigner and the poor. “And do not secretly plot to harm someone.”" 
Zechariah7:11 But they would not listen; they turned their backs on him, and they stopped up their ears rather than stopping to hear." 
Zechariah7:12 Their hearts became as hard as a rock. They refused to listen to the teaching of Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. And they would not hear the words he spoke by his Spirit through the earlier prophets. So Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, became very angry." 
Zechariah7:13 Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, says, “Even when I called for them, they would not listen. Therefore, I would not listen when they prayed to me for help." 
Zechariah7:14 Like a whirlwind, I swept them away to live in distant lands as foreigners.” After they were scattered, the country was left so desolate that it became impassable. What once was a land of delights they turned into a desert of desolation." 
Zechariah8:1 Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, spoke to me, saying," 
Zechariah8:2 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you: My heart burns with passion for Zion, and my jealousy for her makes me red-hot with anger toward her enemies." 
Zechariah8:3 “I, Yahweh, have this to say: I will return to Jerusalem and live there on Mount Zion. Then Jerusalem will be known as my faithful city and Zion as my holy mountain, the mountain of Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Zechariah8:4 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you: Men and women will once again sit in the parks and squares of Jerusalem, each with a staff to lean on because of their great age." 
Zechariah8:5 And the parks and squares of the city will bustle with boys and girls playing outside. 
Zechariah8:6 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have this to say: If this seems too marvelous for the remnant of my people to believe today, remember this—is there anything too marvelous for me? For I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have spoken." 
Zechariah8:7 “Here is what I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you: Behold, I will rescue my people from the countries of the east and the west." 
Zechariah8:8 I will bring them back to live in the heart of Jerusalem, and they will be my beloved people, and I will be their faithful God." 
Zechariah8:9 “This is what I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have to say: Be encouraged, you who now hear these words spoken by the prophets who were there when the foundation was laid for the house of Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. May your hearts be brave so that the temple may be built." 
Zechariah8:10 For up till now, there was no money to hire men or animals; neither has there been security for people to come and go because I had turned them against each other." 
Zechariah8:11 But now, I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, will not treat the remnant of this people as I have in the past." 
Zechariah8:12 For now they will plant in peace. Fresh dew will fall from the sky, and the soil will be fertile. Their vineyards will be lush and fruitful. I will grant the remnant of my people all these things." 
Zechariah8:13 Judah and Jerusalem, your names have been used as curses among all the nations, but now you will become the symbol of a blessing. Yes, I will save you to be a blessing, so do not be afraid. Let your hearts be brave!" 
Zechariah8:14 “For I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you: Just as I determined to bring calamity when your ancestors made me angry and I showed no pity," 
Zechariah8:15 so now I have determined to treat Judah and Jerusalem well and make them prosperous. So do not be afraid! 
Zechariah8:16 “Here is what you must do: Speak the truth to one another. Pursue justice in your courts, and hand down verdicts that are sound." 
Zechariah8:17 Have no evil scheme in your hearts against one another. Do not take delight in committing perjury, for these are things that I hate, declares Yahweh.”" 
Zechariah8:18 Then Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, spoke these words to me:" 
Zechariah8:19 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say this to you: Instead of having days of fasting in the fourth, fifth, seventh, and tenth months, the people of Judah are to have cheerful, joy-filled festivals. Simply live together peacefully and love truth.”" 
Zechariah8:20 Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, says this, “In the future, people from many nations and cities will come streaming to Jerusalem." 
Zechariah8:21 Citizens of one city will say to the people of another city: ‘We must certainly go and worship Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, and seek his blessing. I am determined to go.’ ”" 
Zechariah8:22 Yes, many peoples and many nations will worship Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, in Jerusalem and seek his blessing." 
Zechariah8:23 Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, says this, “In that day, there will be ten men who take hold of the robe of a Jew. They will come from all nations, speaking every language, and will say: ‘We have heard that God is with you! We want to go with you.’ ”" 
Zechariah9:1 A prophecy: The weighty message of Yahweh pertains to the region of Hadrach, and it will come to rest upon Damascus. “ Yahweh keeps an eye on every person and on all the tribes of Israel" 
Zechariah9:2 and even on Hamath, which borders Damascus, and on Tyre and Sidon with all their great wisdom." 
Zechariah9:3 The people of Tyre have built for themselves a fortress. They have swept up piles of silver like dust and gathered mounds of gold like the dirt of the streets. 
Zechariah9:4 Behold! The Lord will take away all she has, burn her city to the ground, and sink her warships into the sea." 
Zechariah9:5 Ashkelon will see it and be afraid; Gaza will see it and writhe in agony. Ekron will witness the loss of her hopes; the king of Gaza will perish, and Ashkelon will be deserted." 
Zechariah9:6 Foreigners will live in Ashdod. Yes, I will destroy the pride of the Philistine people." 
Zechariah9:7 I will stop them from eating meat with its blood and take the forbidden food from between their teeth. But their remnant, too, will become a part of the people of God, like a clan in Judah. And the people of Ekron will also become a part of God’s people as did the Jebusites." 
Zechariah9:8 I will stand guard before my temple to defend it from all who march against it. No oppressor will overrun them ever again, for now, I am constantly on the alert.”" 
Zechariah9:9 “My daughter Zion, let your heart and soul rejoice! Rejoice, my daughter Jerusalem, and shout your cheerful praises! Look! Your King comes to you. He is righteous and fully qualified to save you. Yet he comes to you so humbly, riding on a donkey; sitting meekly on a colt, a young foal of a donkey." 
Zechariah9:10 I, the Lord, will banish war chariots and horses from Israel and Jerusalem. I will break the bows used in war. Your king will proclaim peace and well-being to the nations. His dominion will stretch from sea to sea, from the river to the ends of the earth." 
Zechariah9:11 As for you, Zion, because of my covenant with you sealed with blood, I have set your captives free from the waterless pit of their prison.”" 
Zechariah9:12 “Come running back home to your fortress, you hope-filled prisoners! This very day, I vow I will restore to you double blessing for your trouble." 
Zechariah9:13 My people will be my weapons, for I have bent Judah as my bow and made Ephraim my arrow. I will awaken and stir up your sons, Zion, against the sons of Greece. I will use you like a warrior’s sword.”" 
Zechariah9:14 Then Yahweh will appear above them, and his arrow will flash like lightning. The Lord Yahweh will sound the shofar and advance in the great storm of his power." 
Zechariah9:15 And Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, will be their shield. Their slinging stones shall conquer and subdue the enemy, spilling their blood like wine from an overfilled bowl, like the blood that stains the corners of the altar." 
Zechariah9:16 On that day, Yahweh their God will rescue them and make a way. For they are like a beloved flock of sheep to him, yes, precious like the gemstones of a diadem that will sparkle throughout his land." 
Zechariah9:17 How beautiful and splendid they will be! Young men will flourish by eating the grain, and the maidens by drinking the new wine." 
Zechariah10:1 Ask Yahweh for rain in the season of the latter rain. For Yahweh is the Thunderstorm-Maker. He will give them the outpouring of rain, causing the grain of the field to flourish." 
Zechariah10:2 The household gods babble nonsense, the fortune tellers see false visions, dreamers peddle delusions and give comfort in vain. Therefore, the people have strayed like scattered, suffering, lost sheep because they have no shepherd." 
Zechariah10:3 “I am enraged at the so-called shepherds, and I will vent my anger and punish the proud leaders. I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, care deeply for my flock, the people of Judah. I will transform them from a shepherdless flock of sheep into brave battle horses of splendor." 
Zechariah10:4 Out of them will emerge the cornerstone and the tent peg. From them will emerge battle bows and warrior captains. Together 
Zechariah10:5 they will be like warriors trampling the foe in battle like mud in the streets. They will fight because Yahweh fights with them; they will defeat and humiliate the horsemen. 
Zechariah10:6 “I will make the people of Judah mighty and the descendants of Joseph victorious. And I will bring them back home because I have compassion on them. They will be as though I had never rejected them, for I am Yahweh their God, and I will answer their prayers." 
Zechariah10:7 The people of Israel will be like warriors. Their hearts will be cheered as though by wine. Their children will see this and be full of joy, and their hearts will celebrate and rejoice in Yahweh." 
Zechariah10:8 “I will whistle to call them home as a shepherd gathers his sheep, for I have redeemed them. And they will be as numerous as ever before." 
Zechariah10:9 Though I sowed my people as seed among the nations, they will remember me in those faraway lands. They will all return and rear their children." 
Zechariah10:10 For I will bring them home from Egypt and gather them back from Assyria; I will lead them back into the land of Gilead and Lebanon, yet even there they will not have enough space." 
Zechariah10:11 “I will cross the turbulent sea of trouble and calm its waves. All the depths of the Nile will dry up. I will humble arrogant Assyria and dismantle the power of Egypt. 
Zechariah10:12 I will make my people mighty in me, and they will walk in my name. I, Yahweh, declare it.”" 
Zechariah11:1 Open your borders, Lebanon. Let fire consume your cedar trees." 
Zechariah11:2 Cry in sorrow, cypress, for the towering cedar trees have toppled— the high and mighty ones are destroyed. Wail, oaks of Bashan, for the thick forest has been felled." 
Zechariah11:3 Listen! It’s the sound of the wailing of shepherds. Their glory is gone, their rich pastures destroyed. Listen! It’s the sound of the young lions roaring. The thickets of the Jordan have been ravaged." 
Zechariah11:4 This is what my God, Yahweh, says: “Shepherd the inhabitants of this land, for they are like a flock doomed for slaughter." 
Zechariah11:5 Those who buy them slaughter them without remorse. And the merchants who sell them say, ‘Blessed be Yahweh, I have become rich!’ Even the hirelings—their own shepherds—show no pity for the sheep. I will no longer show mercy to the inhabitants of the land,” declares Yahweh. “Instead, I will cause each one to fall into the clutches of his neighbor and into the clutches of his king. They will devastate the land, and I will not rescue anyone from their power.”" 
Zechariah11:6  
Zechariah11:7 So I became a shepherd for the sheep doomed for slaughter, especially for those sheep who had been trafficked. I took two staffs in my hand; one I named “Affection,” and the other I named “Union,” and I cared for the sheep myself." 
Zechariah11:8 So I removed three shepherds in one month, for I ran out of patience with the sheep-traffickers, and they hated me." 
Zechariah11:9 And I told them, “I am not going to be your shepherd anymore. Those doomed to die can die; those doomed for destruction can perish; and the rest can devour one another.”" 
Zechariah11:10 Then I took my staff, “Affection,” and broke it in half to revoke the covenant that I had made with the whole nation of Israel." 
Zechariah11:11 So it was annulled on that day, and the sheep-traffickers, who were watching me, realized that Yahweh was speaking through me." 
Zechariah11:12 Then I said to them, “If it seems right to you, give me my wages; if not, never mind.” So they weighed out my wages: thirty shekels of silver." 
Zechariah11:13 Yahweh said to me, “Now, hand your silver over to the treasury, these thirty pieces of silver—this wonderful sum at which they valued me.” So I took the thirty shekels of silver and handed them over to the treasury in the house of Yahweh." 
Zechariah11:14 Then I broke in half my second staff, “Union,” to symbolize the breaking of brotherly bonds between Judah and Israel." 
Zechariah11:15 Next, Yahweh said to me, “Once again, act the part of a worthless shepherd and take with you the shepherd’s gear." 
Zechariah11:16 For behold, I am now raising up a shepherd in the land who will not care about the lost, who will not go in search of the stray, who will not heal the injured, who will not feed the healthy sheep, but instead, he will eat the meat of the fattest sheep and even tear off their hooves." 
Zechariah11:17 “Doom is ahead for the worthless shepherd who deserts his flock! May the sword strike his arm and gouge his right eye! May his arm shrivel completely and his right eye be totally blind!” 
Zechariah12:1 A prophecy. A message of Yahweh to Israel. “I, Yahweh, the Heaven-Unfolder, the Earth-Foundation-Layer, and the Spirit-Shaper of every human spirit, announce to you:" 
Zechariah12:2 “Behold! I am about to make Jerusalem an intoxicating cup filled with the wine of my wrath. Any nation around her who dares to drink from it will stagger and fall. And a siege will be laid against Judah and Jerusalem. 
Zechariah12:3 “In that day, when all the nations join forces against her, I will make Jerusalem like a heavy stone. All who lift it will severely injure themselves." 
Zechariah12:4 “In that day, I will confuse every horse with panic and their riders with madness,” says Yahweh. “ Yes, I will strike the horses of the enemies with blindness, but I will watch over the people of Judah." 
Zechariah12:5 Then the leaders of Judah will say to themselves, ‘The people of Jerusalem are strong because Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, is their God.’" 
Zechariah12:6 “In that day, I will make Judah’s leaders like a blazing pot in a pile of dry brush, like a flaming torch in straw. They will destroy in all directions the surrounding nations. The people of Jerusalem will continue to live safely in their own city." 
Zechariah12:7 I, Yahweh, will save the tents of Judah first so that the descendants of David and the people of Jerusalem do not receive more honor than the rest of Judah." 
Zechariah12:8 “In that day, my wraparound presence will protect the people of Jerusalem. And in that day I will transform the weakest among them to become as mighty as David, and the dynasty of David will be like God, like the angel of Yahweh going before them.”" 
Zechariah12:9 “I am determined in that day to destroy all the nations who attack Jerusalem. 
Zechariah12:10 But I will pour out over the rulers and people of Jerusalem the Spirit of Grace and the Spirit of Prayer. And they will gaze upon me, the Pierced One. They will mourn for the one they pierced as though for an only child and weep bitterly for him as people weep for a firstborn child." 
Zechariah12:11 In that day, the mourning in Jerusalem will be as great as the mourning for Hadad Rimmon in the Plain of Megiddo." 
Zechariah12:12 And the people of the land will mourn each clan by itself and their women by themselves: The clan of David’s family and their women, the clan of Nathan’s family and their women," 
Zechariah12:13 the clan of Levi’s family and their women, the clan of Shimei’s family and their women," 
Zechariah12:14 and all the rest of the clans and their women.” 
Zechariah13:1 “In that day, I will tear open a flowing fountain for the family of David and the people of Jerusalem—a fountain to wash away sin and impurity.”" 
Zechariah13:2 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declare: In that day, I will eliminate idolatry from the land, and no one will remember the names of idols anymore. I will also rid the land of false prophets and unclean spirits." 
Zechariah13:3 Therefore, if anyone continues prophesying falsely, his parents who gave him life will say to him, ‘You must forfeit your life since you speak lies in Yahweh ’s name.’ And even while he is prophesying, his parents who gave him life will pierce him through." 
Zechariah13:4 “In that day, every false prophet will be ashamed to prophesy and share their so-called visions. No longer will false prophets wear the coarse robe to deceive people into thinking they are genuine prophets." 
Zechariah13:5 Instead, they will admit, ‘I am not a prophet. I have been living off the land as a farmer ever since my youth.’" 
Zechariah13:6 And if anyone asks him, ‘What are those scars on your back?’ he will reply, ‘I was wounded in the house of my dear friends.’ ”" 
Zechariah13:7 “Awake my sword! Strike my shepherd! Strike the man who stands close to me,” declares Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. “If you strike the shepherd, the sheep will scatter. I will even set myself against the young ones." 
Zechariah13:8 “So it will be, throughout the land,” declares Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. “Two-thirds of the people will be struck down, and the other third will remain." 
Zechariah13:9 I will cause the remaining third to pass through the fire, to refine them as silver is refined. And I will test them as gold is tested. They will call on my name, and I will answer them; I will say to them, ‘You are my people,’ and they will say, ‘ Yahweh is our God!’ ”" 
Zechariah14:1 Behold, Yahweh, will have his day! And Jerusalem, when that day comes, your plunder will be divided in your midst." 
Zechariah14:2 For I will gather all the nations to Jerusalem for battle. The city will be captured, the houses ransacked, and the women raped. Half the people will go into exile, but the rest will be allowed to remain in the city." 
Zechariah14:3 Then Yahweh will intervene and fight against those nations as when he fights on a day of battle. 
Zechariah14:4 On that day, his feet will stand on the Mount of Olives, east of Jerusalem. Then the Mount of Olives will be divided down the middle, forming a wide valley that runs from east to west. So half the Mount will move backward to the north and the other half to the south." 
Zechariah14:5 When this happens, you will escape by way of my newly formed valley, which will extend as far as Azal. You will flee as your ancestors fled from the earthquake in the days of Uzziah king of Judah. And Yahweh your God will come and all the holy ones with him." 
Zechariah14:6 On that day, there will be no light, neither cold nor frost." 
Zechariah14:7 It will be a unique day, a day known only to Yahweh. There will be no more day and night, and even at evening, it will remain light." 
Zechariah14:8 On that day, living waters will flow from Jerusalem, half toward the Dead Sea, half toward the Mediterranean Sea. And they will flow in the summer and winter." 
Zechariah14:9 Then King Yahweh will rule over the whole world. When that day comes, Yahweh will be the one and only God, and everyone will acknowledge him by one single name." 
Zechariah14:10 The entire land of Judah will be transformed into a fertile plain, from Geba in the north to Rimmon south of Jerusalem. But Jerusalem will remain standing high above the plain from the Benjamin Gate to the site of the first gate, to the Corner Gate, and from the Tower of Hananel to the king’s winepresses." 
Zechariah14:11 People will live there securely, for the city will never again be doomed to destruction. Jerusalem will be safe to live in." 
Zechariah14:12 And Yahweh will bring a devastating plague upon all the nations who have fought against Jerusalem. Their flesh will rot while they stand on their feet. Their eyes will rot in their sockets, and their tongues will rot in their mouths." 
Zechariah14:13 And on that day, Yahweh will cause their hearts to panic. Each one will seize his neighbor and strike him. Fighting will break out one against another" 
Zechariah14:14 until even the people of Judah will fight against the people of Jerusalem. The people of Jerusalem will gather all the riches of the surrounding nations: vast amounts of gold, silver, and clothing." 
Zechariah14:15 And a similar plague will afflict the horses, mules, camels, donkeys, and all the other animals in the camp of their enemies." 
Zechariah14:16 Afterward, the survivors of all the nations that have attacked the city will return to Jerusalem each year to worship the King of the world, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, and to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles." 
Zechariah14:17 And if any of the people groups of the world do not go up to Jerusalem to worship the King of the world, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, no rain will fall upon their land." 
Zechariah14:18 And if the Egyptian people do not make this pilgrimage, then Yahweh will send the same plague he sent the other nations that did not celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles." 
Zechariah14:19 This will be the punishment for Egypt and the punishment for all the nations that fail to come up to keep the Feast of Tabernacles. 
Zechariah14:20 When that day comes, the very bells on the horses will have inscribed on them, “Holy to Yahweh.” Even the cooking pots of the house of Yahweh will be as sacred as the sprinkling bowls set before the altar." 
Zechariah14:21 Yes, every cooking pot in Jerusalem and in Judah will be holy to Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, and acceptable to use for anyone who comes to offer a sacrifice. And at that time there will be no merchant in the house of Yahweh, who commands the armies of heaven." 
Malachi1:1 This is divine revelation —the message Yahweh gave to Israel through Malachi. 
Malachi1:2 “I, Yahweh, say to you: I have always loved you. “But you ask, ‘How have you shown us your love?’ “I, Yahweh, say to you: Were not Esau and Jacob twins? Yet I have loved Jacob and his descendants" 
Malachi1:3 more than Esau and his descendants, whom I rejected. And I have turned the hill country of Esau into a barren wasteland and left his land to the desert jackals." 
Malachi1:4 “The people of Edom vow, ‘Though we have been shattered, we will rebuild the ruins.’ “But this is what I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say: You may build, but I will demolish it again. People will call you ‘The Wicked Land’ and ‘The People under the Perpetual Anger of Yahweh.’" 
Malachi1:5 “ Israel, you will witness this with your own eyes and say, ‘The greatness of Yahweh extends even beyond our own borders.’ ”" 
Malachi1:6 “I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, say to you priests, ‘Sons honor their fathers, and servants respect their masters. Yet if I am your father, why don’t you honor me? If I am your master, why don’t you respect me? You have despised my glorious name.’ “But you ask, ‘How have we despised your name?’" 
Malachi1:7 “You place defiled food on my sacred altar. “Still you ask, ‘How have we defiled you?’ “By treating Yahweh ’s table as though it deserves no respect." 
Malachi1:8 Is it not wrong to offer your blind, lame, and sick animals as sacrifices to me? Try offering them to your governor! Would he be pleased with you? Would he show you favor?” asks Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Malachi1:9 “Now try flattering me with inappropriate sacrifices that I may be gracious to you. When you offer me such sacrifices, do you expect me to show you favor?” asks Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Malachi1:10 “It would be far better to halt temple worship altogether and one of you shut the temple gates so that you would not keep lighting worthless fires on my altar. I am not pleased with you, and I will not accept any offering from your hands,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Malachi1:11 “From one end of the world to the other, my name will be great among the nations. In every place, incense and a pure offering will be offered to my name. All over the world my name will receive honor,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Malachi1:12 “But your actions dishonor me. You profane my name by being bored with the Lord’s table and considering it to be common. You say that what you offer me has no worth. 
Malachi1:13 You also say, ‘We’re fed up with all this!’ And you turn up your nose in disgust,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. “You offer me injured, crippled, or diseased animals, but I won’t accept your sacrifices. This is Yahweh speaking to you." 
Malachi1:14 “A curse rests on the cheat who has a perfect male offering in his flock and vows to give it but then sacrifices a puny, imperfect animal to me. For I am a great King,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, “and my name is to be formidable among the nations.”" 
Malachi2:1 “And now, this is my warning for you priests:" 
Malachi2:2 I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, am speaking to you. If you do not listen and fervently devote your heart to honor my name, then I will send the curse upon you and convert your blessings into curses. Yes, I have already done so since you have not dedicated your heart to honor me." 
Malachi2:3 “Behold! I will rebuke your descendants. I will smear feces on your faces—the dung from your festival sacrifices—and you priests will be tossed away with the animal waste. 
Malachi2:4 And then you will know that I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, have sent you this commandment so that my covenant with Levi may endure." 
Malachi2:5 My covenant with his tribe was a covenant to give them a full, peaceful life. All this I gave to him so that he would revere me, and indeed, he honored me and stood in awe of my name." 
Malachi2:6 He gave the people solid instruction and spoke nothing false. He walked with me in peace and integrity and turned many from evil. 
Malachi2:7 “Listen, you priests, descendants of Levi. Your lips should be a gateway to sacred truths when people come to you for instruction because you are the messengers of Yahweh, who rules over all." 
Malachi2:8 But you have turned from my path, and your teachings have caused many to stumble. You have corrupted the covenant with Levi. So I have caused you to be despised and humiliated before all the people. For you have not followed my ways but have shown partiality in matters of the law. This is Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, speaking to you.”" 
Malachi2:9  
Malachi2:10 Have we not all one Father, the one God who created us? So why do we cheat one another and violate the covenant that God made with our ancestors?" 
Malachi2:11 The people of Judah have been unfaithful. A horrible atrocity has been committed throughout the land and even in Jerusalem. They have married foreign women who worship their strange gods and in this way have defiled Yahweh ’s temple, which he loves." 
Malachi2:12 May Yahweh punish the one who has done such a despicable thing, whoever he may be, even though he brings offerings to the Lord Almighty. May Yahweh banish his entire family from the community of Israel!" 
Malachi2:13 Another thing you do: You drown Yahweh ’s altar with tears. You weep and wail because he no longer values your offerings or accepts with pleasure what you bring him. 
Malachi2:14 “Why not?” you ask. It is because you promised before Yahweh to be faithful to the wife you chose in your youth. She is your partner, and you are bound to her by covenant, yet you have betrayed her." 
Malachi2:15 Has God not made them one with a shared Spirit in their union? And what does he desire but godly children from your union? So guard your spirit and do not be disloyal to the wife of your youth. 
Malachi2:16 “I, Yahweh, the God of Israel, say to you: ‘He who does not love his wife but hates her and divorces her overwhelms her with cruelty. So guard your spirit and do not abandon her.’ This is Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, speaking to you.”" 
Malachi2:17 You weary Yahweh with your words. Yet you ask, “How have we wearied him?” By saying, “Evildoers are good in the eyes of Yahweh, and he is pleased with them.” And you ask, “Where is the God of justice?”" 
Malachi3:1 “Behold! I am sending my messenger, who will prepare the way before me. Then suddenly, the Lord whom you long for will enter his temple. The messenger of the covenant, your soul’s delight, will certainly come,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Malachi3:2 But who can endure the day of his coming? Who will not fall down in worship when the Lord is unveiled? For he will carry out a purifying process. He will be like a refiner’s fire or a launderer’s bleach. 
Malachi3:3 He will be as a silversmith who sits refining and purifying silver. He will purify the Levites, refining his priests until they are like pure gold and fine silver. Then I, Yahweh, will have those who bring me offerings in righteousness." 
Malachi3:4 And the offerings of Judah and Jerusalem will be pleasing to me, as in former times and years past." 
Malachi3:5 “So I will come near to you and put you on trial. I will be quick to testify against sorcerers, adulterers, and those who lie under oath, against those who cheat workers of their wages, who oppress the widows and orphans and ignore the immigrants but who do not live in awe of me. This is Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, speaking to you." 
Malachi3:6 Truly, I, Yahweh, have not changed. And you, children of Jacob, you haven’t changed either.”" 
Malachi3:7 “Ever since the time of your ancestors, you have turned away from my laws and evaded them. Return to me, and I will return to you,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. “But you ask, ‘How do we need to return?’" 
Malachi3:8 “Is it right for people to rob God? Yet you rob me. “But you ask, ‘How could we possibly rob you?’ “You withhold your tithes and offerings." 
Malachi3:9 You are under a curse—the whole lot of you are guilty of robbing me. 
Malachi3:10 Bring the full amount of your tithes into the temple treasury so that there may be ample provisions in my house. Test me and see if I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, will fling open the floodgates of heaven for you and pour down upon you an overflowing blessing that you will not be able to contain." 
Malachi3:11 I will defeat the devourer so that it will not destroy the fruits of your fields, and your vines will be full of grapes,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies." 
Malachi3:12 “Then all the nations will call you blessed, for you will be a land in which God takes delight. I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, promise you this.”" 
Malachi3:13 “Your harsh, rebellious words are in defiance of me,” says Yahweh. “But you ask, ‘What words are you talking about?’" 
Malachi3:14 “You have said, ‘It’s no use serving God. What do we gain by keeping his requirements and observing these mourning ceremonies before Yahweh?" 
Malachi3:15 But now, we will call proud people blessed. Even evildoers prosper, and those who provoke God escape punishment.’ ”" 
Malachi3:16 Then those who lived in awe of Yahweh began discussing these things, and Yahweh noticed and listened. A scroll of remembrance was written in the presence of Yahweh. It included the names of those who lived in awe of Yahweh and honored his name." 
Malachi3:17 “They will be mine,” says Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies. “And on the day when I make up my treasured possession, I will show them mercy, just as a compassionate father is gentle with his faithful son." 
Malachi3:18 And you will once again see that I make a distinction between the righteous and the wicked, between those who serve me and those who do not.”" 
Malachi4:1 “You see, there is a day coming, raging like a fiery furnace. On that day, all the proud and wicked people will burn up like straw thrown into a furnace. Neither stalks nor roots will remain. I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, declare it." 
Malachi4:2 “But for you who stand in awe at the sound of my name, a new dawn is coming. For the sun of righteousness will rise with healing radiating from its wings. And you will be free and leap for joy like calves released from the stall." 
Malachi4:3 In the day that I, Yahweh, Commander of Angel Armies, am preparing, you will overcome and trample down the wicked like they were ashes under your feet.”" 
Malachi4:4 “Remember the law of my servant Moses, the commands and instructions I gave him at Mount Sinai for all Israel to obey." 
Malachi4:5 “Keep watching, for I am sending you the prophet Elijah before that great and fearful day of Yahweh arrives." 
Malachi4:6 He will return the fathers’ hearts to their children and the children’s hearts to their fathers so that I can come and bless and not strike the land with a curse.”

Matthew3:3 Isaiah was referring to John when he prophesied: A thunderous voice! One will be crying out in the wilderness, “Prepare yourself for the Lord’s coming and level a straight path inside your hearts for him.”" 
Matthew4:14 He did this to make the prophecy of Isaiah come true: 
Matthew8:17 In doing this, Jesus fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah: He put upon himself our weaknesses, and he carried away our diseases and made us well." 
Matthew12:17 in order to fulfill the prophecy of Isaiah: 
Matthew12:39 Jesus replied, “Only evil people who are unfaithful to God would demand a sign. There will be no sign given to you except the sign of the prophet Jonah." 
Matthew12:40 For as Jonah was in the belly of the huge sea creature for three days and three nights, so the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights." 
Matthew12:41 The people of Nineveh will also rise up on the day of judgment to condemn this generation. For they all repented when they heard the preaching of Jonah. And there is one greater than Jonah here preaching to you today! 
Matthew13:14 The prophecy of Isaiah describes them perfectly: Although they listen carefully to everything I speak, they don’t understand a thing I say. They look and pretend to see, but the eyes of their hearts are closed." 
Matthew15:7 Frauds and hypocrites! Isaiah described you perfectly when he said: 
Matthew16:4 A wicked and wayward generation always asks for signs, but the only sign I provide for you will be the sign of Jonah the prophet.” Then he turned away and left them." 
Matthew16:14 They answered, “Some are convinced you are John the Baptizer, others say you are Elijah reincarnated, or Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.”" 
Matthew16:17 Jesus replied, “You are favored and privileged Simeon, son of Jonah! For you didn’t discover this on your own, but my Father in heaven has supernaturally revealed it to you." 
Matthew23:35 As your penalty, you will be held responsible for the righteous blood spilled and the murders of every godly person throughout your history—from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah, son of Jehoiada, whom you killed as he stood in the temple between the brazen altar and the Holy Place."

Matthew27:9 This fulfilled the prophecy of Zechariah: They took the thirty pieces of silver, the price at which he was valued by the people of Israel, the price of a precious man," 
Mark1:2 It starts with Isaiah the prophet, who wrote: Listen! I am sending my messenger ahead of you and he will prepare your way!" 
Mark7:6 Jesus replied, “You are hypocrites! How accurately did Isaiah prophesy about you phonies when he said: ‘These people honor me with their words while their hearts run far away from me!" 
Mark13:14 “When you witness what Daniel prophesied, ‘the disgusting destroyer,’ standing where it must not be [Let the reader learn what it means], then those in the land of Judah must escape to higher ground." 
Luke1:5 During the reign of King Herod the Great of Judea, there was a Jewish priest named Zechariah who served in the temple as part of the priestly order of Abijah. His wife, Elizabeth, was also from a family of priests, and was a descendant of Aaron." 
Luke1:8 One day, Zechariah’s priestly order was on duty and he was serving as priest. He was chosen by the casting of lots (according to the custom of the priesthood) so the honor fell upon Zechariah to enter the Holy Place and burn incense before the Lord." 
Luke1:12 Zechariah was startled and overwhelmed with fear. 
Luke1:13 But the angel reassured him, saying, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God is showing grace to you. For I have come to tell you that your prayer for a child has been answered. Your wife, Elizabeth, will bear you a son and you are to name him John." 
Luke1:18 Zechariah asked the angel, “How do you expect me to believe this? I’m an old man and my wife is too old to give me a child. What sign can you give me to prove this will happen? ”" 
Luke1:21 Meanwhile, the crowds outside kept expecting him to come out. They were amazed over Zechariah’s delay, wondering what could have happened inside the sanctuary." 
Luke1:39 Afterward, Mary arose and hurried off to the hill country of Judea, to the village where Zechariah and Elizabeth lived." 
Luke1:59 When the baby was eight days old, according to their custom, all the family and friends came together for the circumcision ceremony. Everyone assumed that the parents would name the baby Zechariah, after his father," 
Luke1:64 Instantly Zechariah could speak again. And his first words were praises to the Lord. 
Luke1:67 Then Zechariah was filled to overflowing with the Holy Spirit and he prophesied, saying:" 
Luke3:1 A powerful message from God came to John, Zechariah’s son, when he was living out in the lonely wilderness. This prophetic commission came to John during the fifteenth year of the reign of Emperor Tiberius, son of Caesar. Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea at that time. Antipas, son of Herod, was governor over Galilee, Herod’s brother Philip was over the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was over Abilene. This happened during the days of two high priests, Annas and Caiaphas." 
Luke3:4 fulfilling what was written in the book of the prophet Isaiah: “Listen! You will hear a thunderous voice in the lonely wilderness telling you to wake up and get your heart ready for the coming of the Lord Yahweh. Make straight every twisted thing in your lives. 
Luke4:16 When he came to Nazareth, where he had grown up, he went into the synagogue, as he always did on the Sabbath. When Jesus came to the front to read the Scriptures, he was handed the scroll of the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found where it is written," 
Luke11:29 As the crowds continued to swell, Jesus went on to say, “How evil is this generation! When you demand a mighty display of power simply to prove who I am, you demonstrate your unbelief. The only sign you will see will be a repeat of the miracle of Jonah." 
Luke11:30 In the same way Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Son of Man will be a sign to this generation."
